## 2026-07-10 - ENG-93089: implement parallel-contention guard + contention reclassify/retry (A+B+C)
Context: Implemented the ADR (spec/adr/adr-create-app-section-parallel-guard.md) for ENG-93089 on branch feature/ENG-93089-create-app-section-parallel-guard. All 5 BMAD stories in one branch.
Decision: (A) New ISectionCreateSerializationGuard/SectionCreateSerializationGuard (clio/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateSerializationGuard.cs) — DI SINGLETON (AddSingleton in BindingsModule.cs ~L290) owning a ConcurrentDictionary<string,SemaphoreSlim> keyed env+app-code (lower-cased, joined by U+241F); Run<T>(env,appCode,waitTimeout,work) does a bounded Wait, degrades to best-effort+WriteWarning on timeout, releases in finally. Injected into ApplicationSectionCreateService (extra ctor param; S107 note updated). CreateSection now wraps ONLY insert→readback in guard.Run(request.ApplicationCode, TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(insertTimeoutMs), ...); prep reads stay outside. (B) New enum member ApplicationSectionCreateFailureClass.Contention + ToWireValue "contention". Replaced EnsureInsertSucceeded with ClassifyInsertResponse returning Success|Contention (throws terminal ServerError for non-JSON/empty/DETAILED rejection); IsDetailLessInsertRejection = null/empty or "InsertQuery failed"(.). New InsertWithContentionRecovery: first TryInsertAttempt → on contention, TryVerifySectionExists (existing helper, Id-matched) → true=readback(no retry), null=throw Contention(sectionCreated=null), false=Thread.Sleep(PollDelay 2s)+one more TryInsertAttempt → still contention → throw Contention(sectionCreated=false). BuildContentionFailure + ContentionRetryGuidance constant. (C) [Description] on ApplicationTool.ApplicationSectionCreate + args; AppModeling/ExistingAppMaintenance guidance error-class bullets (+sequential bullet); ToolContractGetTool.cs error-class field enum; help/en + docs/commands/create-app-section.md (Concurrency section + contention row/troubleshooting); docs/McpCapabilityMap.md. clio.mcp.e2e concurrent-create scenario (McpE2E.Sandbox, AllowDestructive+seeded-env gated, 3 parallel calls one session, asserts no error-class=contention / no "InsertQuery failed" / all succeed + cleanup).
Discovery: (1) Flipped 3 existing ApplicationSectionCreateServiceTests (detail-less body was ServerError→now Contention); the "Rejected"/detailed-message tests correctly STAY ServerError (SetUpInsertTimeoutCaptureMocks returns "Rejected" = detailed = terminal, so budget-capture tests unaffected). (2) SelectQueryHelper.ExecuteSelectQuery THROWS on null/empty response → in flipped tests the unconfigured verify readback → TryVerifySectionExists returns null → no retry, no 2s sleep (only AC-04/AC-06 tests, which configure empty-rows readback, incur the 2s PollDelay). (3) Build/test MUST use `-o <temp> -f net10.0` — clio/bin/Debug/net10.0/clio.dll is locked by the live plugin MCP server (.NET Host); from the temp dir, ReadmeChecker (AppContext.BaseDirectory/../../../../clio/Commands.md) + VisibleCommands/HelpDirectory/DocsDirectory canonical-artifact + materialization/workspace fixture tests all fail on path resolution — a PRE-EXISTING temp-dir artifact set (same before my changes), NOT real. Everything else green: 3871 passed incl. 78 new/changed (guard 7, contention/retry/ToWireValue, flipped 3) + guidance content assertions.
Files: clio/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateSerializationGuard.cs (new), ApplicationSectionCreateCommand.cs, ApplicationSectionCreateFailure.cs, BindingsModule.cs, Command/McpServer/Tools/{ApplicationTool.cs,ToolContractGetTool.cs}, Command/McpServer/Resources/{AppModelingGuidanceResource,ExistingAppMaintenanceGuidanceResource}.cs, help/en/create-app-section.txt, docs/commands/create-app-section.md, Commands.md(unchanged-index), docs/McpCapabilityMap.md; tests: clio.tests/Command/{ApplicationSectionCreateServiceTests,SectionCreateSerializationGuardTests(new)}.cs, Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/ApplicationSectionToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Validated `dotnet test -f net10.0 -o <temp> --no-build --filter "Category=Unit&(Module=Command|Module=McpServer)"` = 3871 passed, 0 real failures (path-artifact tests excluded require a normal-bin/CI run — blocked locally by the MCP-server lock). NOT yet committed (awaiting user approval). Pre-PR agentic 3-lens review still owed before opening the PR. clio.mcp.e2e concurrent test is NOT in CI (manual/seeded-stand).

## 2026-07-10 - ENG-93089: session analysis — create-app-section "InsertQuery failed" under parallel/contention
Context: Analyzed two Codex Desktop rollout JSONL sessions (2026-05-27, toolkit ai-driven-app-creation) in the context of ENG-93089. Task = prevent/recover from opaque "InsertQuery failed." when create-app-section runs in parallel. No code changes yet — scoping only.
Discovery: (1) REPRO confirmed in session 019e6982 (user a.shykova): 3 create-app-section calls against ONE app (UsrClient) started within 52ms of each other (13:24:02.235/.286/.287) — all three returned `{"success":false,"error":"InsertQuery failed."}` after only 36/36/55s. (2) RECOVERY: same payloads re-run SEQUENTIALLY ~27min later (13:51:06, 13:52:46) both SUCCEEDED in 93.3s / 102.6s. So parallel→fail, sequential→success = lock/contention, not bad input; ~93-102s/section makes overlap likely. (3) Failed calls returned FASTER (36-55s) than successful (93-103s) — contention aborts insert mid-way; usable signal for retry heuristic. (4) Sibling session 019e68d6 (a.voloshchenko) created UsrCustomer but never reached parallel section creation → no repro (clean control). CODE FINDINGS (master): (a) NO serialization/lock anywhere in ApplicationSectionCreateCommand.cs; no SemaphoreSlim/mutex. (b) The async MCP tool ApplicationTool.ApplicationSectionCreate runs via McpProgressHeartbeat.RunWithProgressAndDeadlineAsync and does NOT go through BaseTool's `lock(CommandExecutionLock)` — so the global McpToolExecutionLock never serializes section creation (and couldn't: lock can't span await). (c) Even if it did, the toolkit calls clio via scripts/mcp_client.py = fresh `clio mcp-server` PROCESS per call → a static in-process lock is useless across processes. (d) MISCLASSIFICATION: detail-less `InsertQuery failed` → EnsureInsertSucceeded throws ApplicationSectionCreateException with class=ServerError + ServerErrorRetryGuidance ("retrying will most likely fail again") — the OPPOSITE of the evidence (serialized retry works). ClassifyInsertFailure never sees it (it's a success=false body, not an exception). TryVerifySectionExists helper already exists → retry-with-verify is feasible.
Files: (analysis only) sessions in ~/Downloads/rollout-2026-05-27T{08-57-14-019e6982,12-49-20-019e68d6}*.jsonl; code read: clio/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateCommand.cs, ApplicationSectionCreateFailure.cs, McpServer/Tools/{ApplicationTool.cs,BaseTool.cs,McpToolExecutionLock.cs}
Impact: Fix design fork for ENG-93089 — (A) in-process async serialization (SemaphoreSlim keyed by env+application-code) fixes the common single-server parallel-batch repro but NOT cross-process; (B) auto-retry-once-with-verify on detail-less InsertQuery failed is the robust cross-process fallback but must re-check existence to avoid duplicate/"already bound"; (C) mandatory [Description]/guidance "do not create sections in parallel" + clio.mcp.e2e scenario. Recommend A+C as primary, B as complementary. BMAD: non-trivial → ADR before code.
## 2026-07-14 – ENG-93088 odata-read masks Web API routing errors as success
Context: rollout 2026-05-27 session (a studio sandbox) showed `odata-read` on a freshly-created custom lookup (`UsrCustomerStatus`) returning `success:true, count:1, value:{Message, MessageDetail}` — Creatio served an ASP.NET Web API 404 routing body with HTTP 200, and `ParseODataResponse` fell through to the single-entity path (no `value` wrapper) and wrapped the error as data.
Decision: extend `ODataResponseError.TryDetect` (shared by odata-read + odata-create) with a THIRD branch after the OData-v4 `error` and ASP.NET `ExceptionType` branches: fire when root is `{Message(+MessageDetail)}` and has NO other member (`HasNonRoutingErrorMembers`). Append `UnregisteredEntityHint` (compile+restart guidance) ONLY when `MessageDetail` is present (the routing discriminator) — a bare `{Message}` is surfaced verbatim to avoid misattributing an unrelated error. Also added a guidance anti-pattern to `ToolContractGetTool.BuildODataRead()`.
Discovery: (1) branch is false-positive-safe because odata-read always hits the collection endpoint (`odata/{e}?$top=`), so a real body always carries `@odata.context`/`value` → `HasNonRoutingErrorMembers` true. Load-bearing assumption: default OData metadata (NOT `metadata=none`); pinned in a code comment. (2) E2E: extended `RuntimeDetectionStubServer` with `ODataRoutingErrorEntity` to serve the masked 200 body; registered env via reg-web-app (auto-detects .NET Framework → `/0/odata/` prefix) then called odata-read through the real mcp-server. Category `McpE2E.Sandbox` (needs node; not in CI).
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ODataResponseError.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ToolContractGetTool.cs (BuildODataRead anti-pattern), clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ODataReadToolTests.cs (+3), clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ODataCreateToolTests.cs (+1), clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Creatio/RuntimeDetectionStubServer.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/ODataReadRoutingErrorE2ETests.cs (new).
Verify: OData read+create unit = 31 pass (net8+net10); ToolContractGetTool = 103 pass; routing E2E = 1 pass (net10). No CLI docs (odata-read is MCP-only). ClioRing does not consume odata-read (grep clean).
Impact: odata-* verification failures are no longer masked as success; a freshly-created-but-uncompiled lookup now returns success:false with a compile/restart hint instead of reading as a data gap. Reuse the `RuntimeDetectionStubServer.ODataRoutingErrorEntity` pattern for future OData response-parsing E2E.

## 2026-07-13 - ENG-93208 PR #830: master merge + 5 reviewer Major findings + 5 Sonar
Context: drive-pr-green on PR #830 (mcp-http credential passthrough). Branch was CONFLICTING (spec/ reorg + clio-ring landed on master) and had CHANGES_REQUESTED (5 Major findings from m-dymytrova) + 5 Sonar smells.
Decision (merge): kept both diary/sprint-status blocks (append-only union); BaseTool.cs conflict = master's [RequiresCreatioVersion] gate (exit 78) + branch's M2 out-tenantKey; relocated feature prd/stories/test-plans into per-feature spec/mcp-http-credential-passthrough/ to match master's new layout.
Decision (findings): #1 gate now fail-closed on EVERY request when passthrough enabled (no-credential-header requests were reaching the full tool surface unauthenticated on a non-loopback bind); behavior change — a key-configured -e caller must now send the Bearer key. #2 SessionContainerCache builds the container OUTSIDE _sync + double-checked insert (disposes the same-key race loser) — the ~455-reg ValidateOnBuild build no longer serializes all tenants on one lock. #3 TenantExecutionLockProvider now PINS at hand-out inside GetLock (removed provider.MarkInUse; facade MarkInUse marks only the session cache, MarkAvailable releases both) — closes the GetLock→MarkInUse eviction window under map saturation. #5 InternalExecute<TCommand> reserves the lock+in-flight guard via ResolveTenantLockKey(GetTenantKey) BEFORE ResolveCommand's Acquire (symmetric with the typed path); split ExecuteLocked into RunCommandUnderHeldLock. #4 = the CwdLock invariant comment was FALSE (only ~6 tools take it; transitive Environment.CurrentDirectory readers don't) → corrected the comment to state the real scope + flag-gating (incubation, off by default) + follow-up (thread explicit cwd); no layering inversion.
Decision (Sonar): S2325 DbOperationLogging — made the AsyncLocal fields INSTANCE (singleton ⇒ identical isolation, no unused-field cascade) rather than static methods. S1075 BindingsModule localhost fallback composed from Uri.UriSchemeHttp. S125 ×2 = rephrased prose comments (arrows/quotes/`;` tripped the commented-code heuristic).
Discovery: #5 linchpin verified — GetTenantKey == Acquire's cacheKey (both ResolveSettingsAndKey/BuildPassthroughCacheKey), so reserving up-front keys the same entry (costs one extra settings.Fill, M2 bypassed). CredentialPassthroughSecretHygieneTests mocked only LastResolvedTenantKey; #5 keys off GetTenantKey now → stubbed both in the harness. DeployCreatioDefaultsResolverTests (3) use `F:\...` Windows paths + MockFileSystem → fail on macOS local, GREEN on the Windows self-hosted CI (build.yml runs-on: self-hosted); pre-existing on master, unrelated, out of scope.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/{McpHttpServerCommand,SessionContainerCache,CredentialHeaderParser}.cs, Tools/{BaseTool,TenantExecutionLockProvider,McpToolExecutionLock}.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/Common/DbOperationLogging.cs; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{CredentialPassthroughMiddlewareTests,SessionContainerCacheTests,TenantExecutionLockProviderTests,BaseToolTests,CredentialPassthroughSecretHygieneTests}.cs.
Impact: full Unit net8.0 5659/3 (the 3 = macOS-only Windows-path site-name tests). ClioRing compatibility reviewed — no Ring-consumed MCP contract changed (internal execution-lock/cache/gate wiring only; no tool name/args/schema/stage-event change). e2e NOT in CI — concurrency cases stay manual.

## 2026-07-09 - ENG-93208 review batch: SSRF trailing-dot + exit-code + cache-key + middleware tests
Context: Applied a batch of verified Medium/Low review findings + test gaps on mcp-http credential passthrough (did NOT touch B1 bearer child-container logic).
Decision: (1) TargetUrlValidator.TryGetIpLiteral now strips exactly one trailing dot and re-parses as an IP so `http://169.254.169.254./` / `http://127.0.0.1./` hit the baseline blocks (SSRF M1); stale class-remark corrected. (2) ToolCommandResolver.ResolvePassthrough wraps TargetUrlNotAllowedException → EnvironmentResolutionException (exit 1 not -1). (3) BuildPassthroughCacheKey uses the FULL SHA-256 hex (dropped `[..16]`) and is now internal for direct testing. (4) Divergent loopback predicates renamed to make intent visible: validator IsLoopbackHost→IsLoopbackIpOrLocalhost (broad: 127/8), host-command IsLoopbackHost→IsLoopbackAlias (narrow: fixed alias literals) — no behavior change. (5) EnforcePlatformApiKeyGate/CaptureCredentialContext promoted private→internal static + new CredentialPassthroughMiddlewareTests.cs (DefaultHttpContext). (6) New CommaSet helper (shared split/trim/drop-empty) + AuthenticationScheme.Bearer const in Clio.Common.
Discovery: Verified empirically on .NET 10 that decimal/hex/octal integer hosts canonicalize to HostNameType=IPv4 (already blocked) — the prior comment claiming they were DNS residuals was wrong; only a trailing dot classifies as Dns and slipped through. RFC1918 (10/8, 192.168/16) is a deliberate v1 residual, locked by two permit-tests.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/TargetUrlValidator.cs, McpHttpServerCommand.cs, PlatformApiKeyGate.cs, Tools/ToolCommandResolver.cs, CommaSet.cs (new); clio/Common/ApplicationClientFactory.cs, AuthenticationScheme.cs (new); clio/Environment/ConfigurationOptions.cs; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{TargetUrlValidatorTests,ToolCommandResolverTests,CredentialPassthroughMiddlewareTests(new)}.cs
Impact: Closes the SSRF trailing-dot bypass, correct exit-code class for blocked URLs, no cache-key truncation collision, and the first unit coverage of the HTTP middleware pipeline. Full Unit suite green 5096/0/35.

## 2026-07-09 - ENG-93208 B1: bearer passthrough dropped in child-container connection sites
Context: Adversarial review of mcp-http credential-passthrough (stories 3-7) found a confirmed cross-identity/privilege-escalation bug. ToolCommandResolver.ResolvePassthrough builds an ephemeral bearer EnvironmentSettings (AccessToken set, Login/Password/ClientId null) and a child container via `new BindingsModule().Register(settings)`, but every Creatio-connection site in RegisterActiveEnvironmentServices (and the SysSettingsManager Func factory just above) read ONLY Login/Password/ClientId and defaulted null creds to "Supervisor"/"Supervisor" — so a bearer request silently authenticated as Supervisor and NoReauthExecutor was never used.
Decision: Added a bearer-first, fail-closed branch (keyed on `!string.IsNullOrEmpty(AccessToken)`) at all 4 child-container sites in BindingsModule.cs: (1) SysSettingsManager Func factory data provider (~733); (2) IDataProvider/LazyDataProvider RemoteDataProvider (~750); (3) lazyCreatioClient — covers both the CreatioClient singleton and the IApplicationClient adapter (~756); (4) IApplicationClient adapter now `sp =>` and wires `sp.GetRequiredService<IReauthExecutor>()` (the DI'd NoReauthExecutor) on the bearer leg only. Bearer ctors used: `new RemoteDataProvider(uri, accessToken, isNetCore)` and `new CreatioClient(uri, accessToken, isNetCore)`. Non-bearer login/password/OAuth branches preserved byte-for-byte; Supervisor default stays reachable only for the no-credential bootstrap case. ApplicationClientFactory (story 3) untouched — NOT redundant overall (it builds clients for explicit foreign environments passed at call time, e.g. ApplicationManager cross-env, package install); its bearer branch is bypassed only for the child-container direct-injection path.
Discovery: (1) `Creatio.Client.CreatioClient` fields for identity assertions: `_oauthToken` (bearer), `_userName`/`_userPassword` (basic). Bearer ctor is `.ctor(string,string,bool)`. (2) ATF `RemoteDataProvider` bearer ctor `.ctor(string,string,bool)` DOES wrap a reachable `Creatio.Client.CreatioClient` — a reflective type-navigated graph walk (not hardcoded ATF names) finds it, so the token is observable end-to-end. (3) CLIO005 shows as a warning building each project alone (0 errors) but the RTK-wrapped combined build once mislabeled warnings as errors — spurious.
Files: clio/BindingsModule.cs (4 sites), clio.tests/Command/McpServer/CredentialPassthroughClientIdentityTests.cs (new, 3 fail-first tests)
Impact: Multi-tenant isolation now enforced at the composition root; the fail-first test locks it (all 3 fail pre-fix reproducing Supervisor/token-drop, pass post-fix). Full Unit suite green 5080/0/35. Residual: nested-ternary sites may draw Sonar S3358 in CI.

## 2026-07-06 – ENG-91842 data-source filters (Option B): describe read-back + negatives + e2e
Context: user asked to implement the remaining Option-B filter work in the ENG-91842 branches (clio + clioprocessbuilder). Delivered the fully locally-verifiable core; macros/date-parts/aggregation + date-format deferred pending a live-stand decision (design doc requires live verification for those).
Decision: pure static decoder `FilterDescriptorReader` (exact inverse of the `ProcessFilterService` encoder; reads the lean `dataSourceFilters` rep) so NO DI/ctor ripple. `ProcessDescriber` emits `DescribeProcessElement.filter` — signal `EntityFilters` (gated on `HasEntityFilters`) / data-element `DataSourceFilters` (Source==ConstValue). clio adds `DescribedFilter`/`DescribedFilterGroup`/`DescribedFilterCondition` + `DescribedElement.filter` (else System.Text.Json drops the field on re-serialize). Negatives (notContains 12 / notStartWith 10 / notEndWith 14) added to encoder+decoder+constants. A parameter reference surfaces as the raw meta-path `expression` token (reversing token->friendly name deferred). Guidance now says `setFilter` REPLACES the whole filter → to add a condition, read-back then resend whole.
Discovery: package is C# 7.3 (no switch expressions / using-declarations). The test project references NO JSON lib, so read-back tests round-trip through the encoder (build wrapper -> `FilterDescriptorReader.Read` -> assert on the FilterDescriptor object, no JSON parsing). Process filter runtime is `Terrasoft.Nui...ProcessFilterFactory` — it MAY accept a different shape than the ESQ envelope `LocalEsqFilterBuilder` emits, so macros/date-parts/date-format need LIVE verification, not just round-trip unit tests.
Files: package — Filters/FilterDescriptorReader.cs (new), Contracts/DescribeContracts.cs, Describe/ProcessDescriber.cs, ProcessDesignConstants.cs, Contracts/FilterContracts.cs, Filters/ProcessFilterService.cs, tests/FilterDescriptorReaderTests.cs (new). clio — Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/CreateBusinessProcessToolE2ETests.cs, Resources/ProcessDesigner/ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/ProcessModel/ServerProcessDescriberTests.cs. Commits: package b3323c6 + 3ad835c; clio 8ca327ca + a4c92f37 + 702aa8a2.
Verify: package filter tests 28 pass (dev-nf); clio ProcessModel 90 + McpServer 1549 pass (net8); clio.mcp.e2e compiles (env-gated). e2e + macro/date serialization still need a stand.
Impact: describe now round-trips a filter, so fetch-modify-save is possible; negatives complete the comparison set. Remaining Option-B vocabulary (macros/date-parts/aggregation) + date-format are reference-ports from clio `LocalEsqFilterBuilder` that MUST be live-verified — do them against a reachable stand.

## 2026-07-06 – Merge master/main into ENG-91842 branches (clio + clioprocessbuilder)
Context: merged origin/master into clio and origin/main into the clioprocessbuilder package on feature/ENG-91842-data-source-filters (50 / 26 commits behind). Both merges conflicted in the exact process-designer files under active work.
Decision: additive union — master's process-designer refactor (rename `id`->`name` / `elementId`->`elementName`, `describe-process`->`describe-business-process`, setParameter/removeParameter ops, ProcessParameterService/ProcessMappingService now take UserConnection via `Connection(sp)`, ProcessDesigner gained IProcessDescriber, commands gated behind `[FeatureToggle("process-designer")]` so their docs/help were deleted from the committed baseline) COMBINED WITH the branch's filter work (setFilter/clearFilter, element `filter?`, IProcessFilterService). Accepted master's doc/help deletion (gated). Diary = union. Kept local clio/appsettings.json `features.process-designer:true` UNCOMMITTED.
Discovery: (1) master's id->name rename silently broke branch code on lines git AUTO-MERGED (no conflict): ProcessDesigner.ApplyFilter passed `element.Id`, and `ModifyOperation.ElementId` no longer exists (renamed ElementName) — only a build caught it. ALWAYS build after a large merge even with zero remaining conflict markers. (2) The branch's setFilter reuses `ElementName`; the filter's OWN FilterElementParameterRef legitimately keeps wire key `elementId` (different concept — leave it). (3) ProcessFilterService must register via `sp => new ProcessFilterService(Connection(sp))` (master's pattern); plain `AddScoped<IProcessFilterService, ProcessFilterService>()` cannot resolve UserConnection. (4) The widened ctors (IProcessDescriber + IProcessFilterService) forced updating 4 auto-merged PB test call sites.
Files: clio — Command/McpServer/{Tools,Prompts,Resources}/ProcessDesigner/*, clio.mcp.e2e/{Create,Describe,ListUserTasks,Modify}*E2ETests.cs, docs+help create/modify-business-process (deleted), .codex/workspace-diary.md. package — Contracts/ModifyContracts.cs, Operations/ProcessOperationExecutor.cs, ProcessDesigner.cs, src/ClioProcessBuilderApp.cs, tests/{ProcessOperationExecutor,ProcessOperationExecutorGraphOps,ProcessDesignerRoundTrip,ProcessDesignerOrchestrator,ProcessDesignerSecurity}Tests.cs. Merge commits: clio 0c7cfe3f, package 3c8b5e0.
Verify: clio McpServer unit = 1549 passed (net8); package = 254/255 (the 1 fail, ProcessMappingServiceTests.ApplyMapping_ShouldAssignConstantValue_WhenValueProvided, is PRE-EXISTING on origin/main — bare net472 harness lacks a populated DataValueTypeManager; merged files byte-identical to main). 2 CLIO005 warnings also pre-existing on master. Neither branch pushed.
Impact: future master merges on this branch should expect the id->name / describe-business-process renames and MUST compile-check — auto-merge hides rename breakages that leave no conflict markers.

## 2026-06-19 - Confluence "Backend designer" page: added Build & modify contract diagram
Context: Asked to add a process create/modify contract to the TER Confluence page 4752572418 ("Backend designer — architecture & delivery options"); chose a PlantUML class diagram + a routes/verbs mini-table, placed as a new section 3 after the sequence diagram (sections 3-8 renumbered to 4-9).
Decision: Render the contract as a UML class diagram in the same `plantumlcloud` (puml4cc) macro the page already uses, so it stays editable on-page and matches the existing sequence diagram's style. Contract fields sourced from clio/Command/CreateBusinessProcessCommand.cs + ModifyBusinessProcessCommand.cs (descriptor: elements/flows/parameters/mappings; modify ops: addElement/removeElement/addFlow/removeFlow/addParameter/addMapping; routes from ServiceUrlBuilder KnownRoute.BuildProcess/ModifyProcess).
Discovery: The Confluence `plantumlcloud` macro stores its diagram in `data-parameters` macroParams.data.value as **Base64( raw-DEFLATE( encodeURIComponent(plantuml-text) ) )** with `compressed:"true"` (NOT PlantUML.com's 6-bit alphabet, which uses `~1`/HUFFMAN and a different charset). Verified by decoding the page's existing sequence-diagram blob with .NET DeflateStream (raw, nowrap) -> got `%40startuml...`. To author a new one: build PlantUML, `[Uri]::EscapeDataString`, raw-deflate, `[Convert]::ToBase64String`. Validate PlantUML *syntax* separately via plantuml.com using its native 6-bit encoder on the BARE `/svg/<enc>` path (the `~1` prefix routes to the wrong decoder and errors "does not look like HUFFMAN data"). MCP `updateConfluencePage` replaces the WHOLE body and is provided inline by the model -> long base64 blobs and large prose are an easy place to slip; I duplicated a section on the first publish. Mitigation that worked: assemble body in a scratch file, decode both macro blobs from the file to confirm before publishing, then re-verify by re-fetching the live page.
Files: Confluence page 4752572418 (v13); no repo files changed. Source of truth read: clio/Command/CreateBusinessProcessCommand.cs, clio/Command/ModifyBusinessProcessCommand.cs, clio/Common/ServiceUrlBuilder.cs, clio/docs/commands/{create,modify}-business-process.md.
Impact: Recipe + validation/verification flow for embedding/editing PlantUML in Creatio Confluence pages is reusable for the other ENG-90883 research pages. Key gotcha: macro encoding is base64+deflate+urlencode, distinct from plantuml.com's encoding.

## 2026-06-30 - ENG-91555: new-container guidance in page-modification (+ PR #789 review correction)
Context: ENG-91555 asked to document the "inline items in new container" constraint in the page-modification MCP guide (root case: session 7ca711a1, runtime `Item "X" is not a container for other items` after inserting new ExpansionPanels). Delivered as PR #789. PR review (tetiana-moshon, Major/CHANGES_REQUESTED) then flagged the first cut as wrong.
Decision: Reviewer was RIGHT — corrected the block. Verified authoritatively: related-list RelatedListGuidanceResource.cs:188 + :48-54 attribute that exact runtime error to a MISSING `items: []` slot on an inserted container ("#1 detail footgun"); get-component-info crt.ExpansionPanel's canonical example inserts the container via parentName WITH `items: []` initialized. So the true root cause is the un-initialized content slot, NOT "chain-nesting", and separate parentName child inserts ARE supported when the container initializes its slot.
Discovery: Final block: heading "Inserting a NEW container — its content slot MUST be initialized"; removed the false chain-nesting mechanism and the blanket prohibition on separate parentName inserts; WRONG example = container inserted without its slot; CORRECT(inline) + CORRECT(separate) both valid; cross-ref related-list / get-component-info composite; config-node clarification (RC-1, Alexandr-Kravchuk) folded into prose. clio carries NO component descriptions — type/slot/shape deferred to get-component-info. The plugin clio MCP server is launched as bare `clio mcp-server` from .mcp.json (active plugin version 1.3.0 cache copy), which resolves via an npm shim to a different repo (C:\Projects\adaclio ... net8.0) — so a freshly-built local clio is NOT served until that .mcp.json is repointed to the absolute net10.0 dll AND Claude Code is restarted; get-guidance page-modification returning "Unknown guidance" is the tell that the wrong binary is serving.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/PageModificationGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/GuidanceGetToolTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Validated dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&Module=McpServer" (1460 passed). Lesson: a guide that names a runtime error MUST agree with every other guide naming the same error — cross-check the error string across GuidanceCatalog resources before asserting a root cause.
## 2026-07-08 - Exclude process-designer E2E fixtures from the TeamCity plan via category filter
Context: The 5 ProcessDesignerE2EGate-gated fixtures (CreateBusinessProcess/DescribeProcess/ModifyBusinessProcess/ListUserTasks/ValidateProcessGraph ToolE2ETests) showed up as ~28 Ignored tests on every Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests run (feature ships with clioprocessbuilder, off on the default stand). Request: stop them from running on the plan at all (DataForgeToolE2ETests stays as-is — its skip logic is service-report-driven, not feature-gated).
Decision: Two-sided exclusion. (1) Repo: new public const ProcessDesignerE2EGate.CategoryName = "McpE2E.ProcessDesigner" + [Category(ProcessDesignerE2EGate.CategoryName)] on the 5 fixture classes (follows the existing McpE2E.NoEnvironment / McpE2E.Sandbox naming). (2) TeamCity: appended --filter "TestCategory!=McpE2E.ProcessDesigner" to the Run_MCP_e2e_tests step args via REST PUT /app/rest/buildTypes/id:Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests/steps/Run_MCP_e2e_tests/parameters/args (teamcity CLI has no step-edit subcommand; `teamcity api -X PUT -H "Content-Type: text/plain" --input file` works). Kept the Assert.Ignore gate as defense-in-depth for local runs.
Discovery: (1) The runner-level filter excludes tests from DISCOVERY (they no longer appear even as Ignored), unlike Assert.Ignore/[Explicit]. (2) The TeamCity args change is live immediately for ALL BranchNameClio branches; branches without the attribute are unaffected (filter matches nothing) — no breakage window, but the exclusion only takes effect per-branch once this commit is in that branch. (3) clio.mcp.e2e has no explicit `using NUnit.Framework;` in fixtures — it comes via csproj global usings.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/ProcessDesignerE2EGate.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/{CreateBusinessProcess,DescribeProcess,ListUserTasks,ModifyBusinessProcess,ValidateProcessGraph}ToolE2ETests.cs + TeamCity job Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests step Run_MCP_e2e_tests (args)
Impact: Plan runs no longer discover the process-designer E2E fixtures (28 tests). Verified locally: build clean; `dotnet test --list-tests --filter "TestCategory=McpE2E.ProcessDesigner"` returns exactly the 28 tests. To re-enable on a dedicated lane later: run with --filter "TestCategory=McpE2E.ProcessDesigner" on a stand with clioprocessbuilder + the feature enabled.

## 2026-07-08 - Investigation: Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests master-branch failures
Context: Asked to investigate failures on TeamCity job Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests, focus on runs where the build param BranchNameClio = master. (VCS branch column shows "trunk" for all; the clio branch under test is the BranchNameClio resulting-property, checked via /app/rest/builds/id:<id>/resulting-properties/BranchNameClio.)
Discovery: Four recent master failures split into TWO distinct modes. MODE A — test-host HANG: runs 15688937 & 15688144 (~2026-07-06) both aborted identically with "The active test run was aborted. Reason: Test host process crashed" after --blame-hang-timeout 3m. Hang-dump process tree is testhost -> cmd -> clio in BOTH: an orphaned `clio` CLI/MCP subprocess (child of a cmd child of testhost) never exits, so after 3 min inactivity the whole test host is killed and only ~247-250/408 tests run (rest never execute). The stall lands right after the PageBusinessRuleToolE2ETests `BusinessRuleCreate_Should_Bind_*_And_Report_Invalid_Environment` batch (methods themselves Passed in 3-40ms; the leaked process is what hangs) — the *_Report_Invalid_Environment tests spawn a clio process against an invalid env that apparently never times out/terminates. This is the real recurring bug. MODE B — assertion failures (flaky/env), the two MOST RECENT runs 15693316 & 15693193 (~2026-07-07): different tests fail each run -> environmental, not one regression. 15693316: ApplicationGetInfo_..._After_SchemaSync (sync-schemas success=False) + PageCreateTool_..._Dashboard_With_Optional_Properties (get-page Bundle null). 15693193: CreateWorkspace_..._Invalid_Environment_Key + _Directory_Is_Omitted (create-workspace exit code 1, both new). Each run is a different clio commit (moving master), reinforcing flaky-not-regression for Mode B.
Files: (investigation only) logs saved to scratchpad log15688937.txt / log15688144.txt
Impact: Master is currently RED (latest run 15693316 failed). Priority fix = Mode A orphaned-clio-process hang (deterministic host crash, blocks whole suite): make the *_Report_Invalid_Environment E2E paths enforce a process timeout / guaranteed kill+dispose of the spawned clio child so it can't outlive the test. Mode B needs per-test retry/quarantine or root-causing the flaky env (schema-sync / create-workspace against the shared stand).

## 2026-07-06 - ENG-92127: platform interpretation-validation pre-save gate (clioprocessbuilder)
Context: Manual designer testing of ENG-92127 found a self-referential parameter mapping (Parameter 2 = [#Parameter 2#]) is correctly rejected on the ContractProcessSchema save ("circular dependency for the parameter"); clioprocessbuilder's own build/modify path did NOT run that validation, so an AI-built process could bypass it. Work is in the ProcessBuilder repo (C:\Projects\workspace\ProcessBuilder), NOT clio.
Decision: Added a pre-save gate. New IProcessSchemaValidator/ProcessSchemaValidator (namespace ClioProcessBuilder) calls the PUBLIC platform seam ProcessSchemaManager.GetProcessValidationResult(schema, userConnection) and throws on HasErrors (joined ErrorMessages); the existing orchestrator catch surfaces it as response.ErrorMessage (build rolls back; modify releases the design session). Wired into ProcessDesigner.BuildProcess (before Save) and ModifyProcess (before SaveEdited). AUGMENTS (does not replace) the per-mapping ParameterTypeCompatibility guard: the platform type rule (ValidateParameterBindingValueTypes) is .NET-ValueType + implicit-cast based (accepts Email↔Phone, Date↔Time, Integer→Money, lookup-with-different-object) — laxer than the designer mapping-window rule the custom guard mirrors — and our-accept ⊆ platform-accept, so they layer cleanly (user chose augment after I confirmed this).
Discovery: (1) GetProcessValidationResult is NOT called by SaveSchema — the designer's ContractProcessSchema layer calls it separately, so the direct SaveSchema path must call it explicitly; the ProcessSchemaManager override short-circuits IsEmbedded → empty result. (2) For a Script source (clio's `expression`) the platform runs ValidateExpression at save (compile check), so once DEPLOYED a malformed expression fails at save, not just runtime — the ProcessModelingGuidanceResource "expression UNVALIDATED / RUNTIME-only" wording (line ~197) becomes wrong; deferred that reword to the package release (guidance describes deployed-package behavior). (3) Package sources under Files/src/cs are SDK-auto-globbed (Files/-rooted); LangVersion 7.3; ProcessDesigner is built via ActivatorUtilities (new ctor dep auto-resolved) but three TEST sites use `new ProcessDesigner(...)` and needed the arg. (4) ProcessMappingServiceTests.ApplyMapping_ShouldAssignConstantValue_WhenValueProvided is a PRE-EXISTING FLAKY test (shared/order-dependent DataValueTypeManager via ProcessParameterValueValidator.ValidateConstantValue): failed once then passed 5× on identical source and passes on master — spawned a task chip to stabilize it.
Files: packages/clioprocessbuilder/Files/src/cs/Validation/IProcessSchemaValidator.cs (new) + ProcessSchemaValidator.cs (new), packages/clioprocessbuilder/Files/src/ClioProcessBuilderApp.cs, packages/clioprocessbuilder/Files/src/cs/ProcessDesigner.cs, tests/clioprocessbuilder/{ProcessDesignerOrchestratorTests,ProcessDesignerSecurityTests,ProcessDesignerRoundTripTests,ClioProcessBuilderAppTests}.cs, spec/eng-92127-server-validation/eng-92127-server-validation-plan.md (new) — all in the ProcessBuilder repo.
Impact: clioprocessbuilder build/modify now enforces the same interpretation validation the designer runs on save. Build clean; 248/248 ProcessBuilder unit tests green. MCP reviewed: tools already relay the platform ErrorMessage (contracts unchanged) so no tool change; guidance reword deferred to package release. STILL PENDING: E2E on a stand (deploy the rebuilt package; verify a self-map/cycle is rejected and a valid process still builds).

## 2026-07-02 - ENG-92127: make modify-business-process signalStart E2E portable
Context: ModifyBusinessProcess_Should_EditExistingProcess (clio.mcp.e2e) hardcoded the signalStart signal entity "UsrTestRunButton" in BuildOperations(); that custom object exists on only one specific stand, so on any other environment the addElement op fails and the whole batch atomically rolls back — the test was non-portable (the describe read-back, not the weak success assertions, is what caught it).
Decision: Swap the signal entity to the base "Contact" object (present on every Creatio), keeping "on":"save". A signalStart binds to a real EntitySchema, so the referenced object must exist in the target SysSchema; Contact makes the fixture environment-independent. Left the illustrative "e.g. UsrTestRunButton" in ProcessModelingGuidanceResource as-is (it is a doc example, not an executable reference).
Discovery: (1) Runtime settings — registered environments AND the `features` map — load from %LOCALAPPDATA%\creatio\clio\appsettings.json (confirmed via `clio info --settings-file`), NOT the repo clio/appsettings.json; the repo file is only the shipped defaults/seed, so a local `features.process-designer:true` there must not be committed (it would flip the experimental default ON). (2) The E2E resolves the sibling clio\bin\<cfg>\<tfm>\clio.exe; when the live MCP-server clio locks the Debug binary, build test-only with `-p:BuildProjectReferences=false` and run `--no-build` to reuse the existing binary instead of killing the process. (3) ProcessDesignerE2EGate skips (Assert.Ignore, not fail) unless features.process-designer is enabled in the home appsettings.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/ModifyBusinessProcessToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Live-verified passing on a reachable stand with the process-designer package in both Release and Debug/net10.0. Future process-designer E2E fixtures should use base entities (Contact) for signal/reference schemas to stay portable across stands.

## 2026-06-25 - Scope chart-widget validation to the environment platform version
Context: ChartWidgetValidation hardcoded ComponentRegistryClient.LatestVersion when loading the catalog; the registry-driven crt.ChartWidget validation should run against the target environment's component set, not always 'latest'.
Decision: Split by whether the tool has an environment. ChartWidgetValidation.ValidateAsync/ResolveTypeDefinitionsAsync now take a requestedVersion, normalise it (blank/unparseable -> latest, else 3-part Major.Minor.Patch) and pass it to catalog.LoadAsync instead of the LatestVersion constant (fail-open preserved). validate-page (no environment) gets an explicit optional `version` MCP arg — the agent passes get-component-info's resolvedTargetVersion. sync-pages and update-page (already have environment-name) do NOT get a version arg; instead they resolve the platform version FROM their environment via IPlatformVersionResolverFactory + ISettingsRepository (the same probe get-component-info uses) and feed it to the validator. NOTE (design correction mid-task): an earlier pass added the `version` arg to all three tools; the user corrected it — the save tools must derive the version from the environment they already target, not take a redundant arg. The two resolver deps were added as OPTIONAL ctor params (= null) on PageSyncTool/PageUpdateTool so DI still injects the registered services in production while ~15 existing unit-test constructions stay untouched; the resolution helper is fail-soft (null deps / bad env / probe failure -> null -> ChartWidgetValidation maps to latest). sync-pages skips the probe entirely when validate:false; update-page skips it for mobile bodies (chart validation is web-only).
Discovery: (1) "Latest fallback for a missing per-version registry" is already handled by ComponentRegistryClient.GetAsync (404 -> latest), so only the version string had to be threaded — no client change. (2) validate-page/sync-pages are MCP-only (no [Verb]); update-page is a CLI verb but the `version` arg lives on PageUpdateArgs (MCP), not PageUpdateOptions (CLI), so the CLI verb + its help/docs are unchanged. (3) ToolContractGetTool has no curated arg contract for these three tools. (4) Local build/test is blocked by the live `clio mcp-server` process holding clio\bin\Debug\net10.0 DLLs; build/test into a temp `-o` dir AND pin a single TFM (`-f net10.0`) — clio.tests/clio.mcp.e2e multi-target net8.0;net10.0 and a shared `-o` dir makes the two frameworks clobber each other (this caused 518 phantom test failures until I pinned the TFM).
Docs: deliberately NONE. sync/update version determination is deterministic + internal (the tool derives it from the environment it already targets; the agent neither chooses nor needs to know it), so it warrants no documentation — per the user's explicit call. The only new agent-facing surface, validate-page's optional `version` arg, is self-documented by its `[Description]`. An earlier pass added a sync-pages.md paragraph + a page-modification guidance bullet; both were reverted (net-zero on those files).
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ChartWidgetValidation.cs, PageValidateTool.cs, PageSyncTool.cs, PageUpdateTool.cs; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ChartWidgetValidationTests.cs (new), ValidatePageToolTests.cs, PageSyncToolTests.cs, PageUpdateToolBaselineTests.cs; clio.mcp.e2e/PageValidateToolE2ETests.cs, PageSyncToolE2ETests.cs, PageUpdateToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: To validate a clio change while the MCP server is running, use `dotnet test ... -f net10.0 -o <temp>` to dodge both the bin lock and the multi-TFM `-o` collision. Agents should forward get-component-info's resolvedTargetVersion to the page tools' `version` arg so pre-flight (validate-page) and save-time (update-page/sync-pages) chart validation agree on the environment's component set.

## 2026-06-18 - ENG-91828: Refresh drifted MCP e2e contract assertions
Context: SDK upgrade 1.3.0→1.4.0 + apply-static-filter action + masking/guidance changes drifted 5 clusters of MCP e2e assertions.
Decision: Updated test assertions across 7 files to match current production behavior. For Cluster 5 (Content enumeration), replaced `callResult.Content!.Select(...)` with null-safe `(callResult.Content ?? []).Select(...)` pattern to match SDK 1.4.0 binding failure behavior.
Discovery: (1) `apply-static-filter` was added as JsonDerivedType for both entity and page business rule tools (anyOf count 6→7). (2) PageSchemaHandlersGuidanceResource reversed recommendation: HandlerChainService is now preferred over executeRequest. (3) McpToolArgumentSupport.BuildLegacyAliasError centralized alias errors as `"Rename: 'alias' -> 'canonical'"` format. (4) ShowWebAppListTool changed to maskSensitiveData=true (masked value is "****"). (5) SDK 1.4.0 binding failures: Content is null/empty rather than having TextContentBlock with error.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/EntityBusinessRuleToolE2ETests.cs, PageBusinessRuleToolE2ETests.cs, McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, PageGetToolE2ETests.cs, SchemaSyncToolE2ETests.cs, ShowWebAppListToolE2ETests.cs, ToolContractGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: When adding new JsonDerivedType action subtypes, also bump the anyOf count assertion in the corresponding e2e test. SDK binding failures do not populate Content in 1.4.0 — use `(callResult.Content ?? [])` pattern.

## 2026-06-16 - Feature-toggle MCP findings 3-6 + latent registration regression
Context: Fix four IMPORTANT review findings on the experimental feature-toggle system; uncovered a blocking MCP registration bug.
Decision: Did NOT add abstract/open-generic exclusion to McpFeatureToggleFilter - the ModelContextProtocol 1.4.0 SDK INCLUDES such types in its *FromAssembly scan (it iterates methods, not types), so excluding them would BREAK parity. BaseTool<T> carries [McpServerToolType] but has no [McpServerTool] method -> contributes 0 registered tools on both sides. ExperimentalCommand now reuses McpFeatureToggleFilter for all 3 markers (inherit:true) instead of forked inherit:false 2-marker helper. Wired --list (precedence: --list wins, lists and returns). Single-sourced MCP registration in McpFeatureToggleFilter.RegisterEnabledPrimitives, called by both BindingsModule and the parity test.
Discovery: BLOCKING latent bug - BindingsModule's `.WithResources(Type[])`/`.WithTools(Type[], opts)`/`.WithPrompts(Type[], opts)` all bound to the SDK's GENERIC With*<TType>(TType target,...) overload (identity conversion on a Type[] arg beats Type[]->IEnumerable<Type> reference conversion, decided before the generic-vs-nongeneric tiebreaker), registering 0 tools/0 resources/0 prompts. Probe confirmed: PROD shape 0/0/0 vs cast shape 120/86/67. Fix: type the lists as IEnumerable<Type>. The whole MCP surface was silently dead in this feature branch.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpFeatureToggleFilter.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs (~632-645), clio/Command/ExperimentalCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpFeatureToggleFilterTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ExperimentalToolTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/ExperimentalToolE2ETests.cs, clio/help/en/experimental.txt, clio/docs/commands/experimental.md
Impact: Future MCP registration must pass IEnumerable<Type> (never Type[]) to With*; the parity test now drives the real production seam so the cast cannot be dropped again. The IEnumerable<Type>-overload-binding hazard is the #1 gotcha when touching MCP type-list registration.

## 2026-02-22 - Canonical command-doc instructions
Context: Align duplicated command documentation guidance used by Codex skills and GitHub Copilot prompts.
Decision: Introduce a single canonical instruction file and convert both agent-specific files into thin wrappers with Canonical-Source and Canonical-Version markers.
Discovery: A lightweight PowerShell verifier can enforce wrapper-to-canonical sync and prevent silent drift.
Files: docs/agent-instructions/document-command.md, .codex/skills/document-command/SKILL.md, .github/prompts/doc-command.prompt.md, scripts/verify-agent-docs.ps1, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Future updates to command documentation guidance can be made once in canonical docs and validated automatically for both agent surfaces.
## 2026-02-22 - Document verifier inline help
Context: Improve maintainability of agent-doc drift checker by documenting script purpose and parameters.
Decision: Add PowerShell comment-based help and concise inline comments in verify-agent-docs script.
Discovery: Script remains CI-friendly; exit behavior and validation output unchanged after documentation updates.
Files: scripts/verify-agent-docs.ps1, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Team members can discover usage via Get-Help and understand script intent without reading full implementation.
## 2026-02-22 - Add DI instance-creation policy
Context: Establish repository-wide guidance for service instantiation and dependency injection consistency.
Decision: Add AGENTS.md directive to resolve behavior classes from DI, require interfaces for behavior classes, and allow `new` only for simple DTO/value carriers (prefer records).
Discovery: Clear exceptions are needed to avoid over-constraining value objects and data-only models.
Files: AGENTS.md, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Reduces ad-hoc object creation, improves testability, and enforces consistent dependency management.
## 2026-02-22 - Add CLIO001 DI manual-construction analyzer
Context: Enforce repository policy to avoid `new` for DI-registered behavior classes.
Decision: Create `Clio.Analyzers` project with `CLIO001` analyzer, wire it as Analyzer reference in `clio` and `cliogate`, and add analyzer project to `clio.slnx`.
Discovery: Analyzer infers registered implementations from Autofac and Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection registration calls, and intentionally skips record types and test assemblies.
Files: Clio.Analyzers/Clio.Analyzers.csproj, Clio.Analyzers/DependencyInjectionManualConstructionAnalyzer.cs, clio/clio.csproj, cliogate/cliogate.csproj, clio.slnx, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Builds now flag manual `new` construction of DI-registered types with diagnostic `CLIO001`.
## 2026-02-22 - Migrate clio DI and JSON stack
Context: Replace Autofac with Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection and move off System.Json in clio and clio.tests.
Decision: Ported the existing remove-autofac migration with targeted reconciliation on current branch, then completed remaining DI conversion callsites manually.
Discovery: Keeping BuildServiceProvider validation with ValidateOnBuild=false avoids premature DI graph failures while preserving runtime resolution behavior; test fixture overrides map cleanly to IServiceCollection + GetRequiredService patterns.
Files: clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/Program.cs, clio/Command/CloneEnvironmentCommand.cs, clio/Command/ShowDiffEnvironmentsCommand.cs, clio/Command/ScenarioRunnerCommand.cs, clio/AppUpdater.cs, clio/ComposableApplication/ComposableApplicationManager.cs, clio/Package/PackageConverter.cs, clio/ExceptionReadableMessageExtension.cs, clio/clio.csproj, clio.tests/Command/BaseClioModuleTests.cs, clio.tests/GlobalUsings.cs, clio.tests/clio.tests.csproj
Impact: clio now uses Microsoft DI container APIs end-to-end, System.Json dependency is removed, and full clio.tests suite passes on net8.
## 2026-02-22 - Retarget CLIO001 analyzer to Microsoft DI
Context: After migrating clio away from Autofac, analyzer registration detection still included Autofac methods/namespaces.
Decision: Restrict CLIO001 registration-source detection to Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection APIs and add support for TryAdd*/Replace method names.
Discovery: Existing type extraction logic for Add* generic overloads still works for IServiceCollection patterns; no Autofac-specific extraction paths are needed.
Files: Clio.Analyzers/DependencyInjectionManualConstructionAnalyzer.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: CLIO001 now reflects the current DI stack and avoids Autofac-based assumptions/false positives.
## 2026-02-22 - Stabilize CLIO001 matches after MS DI migration
Context: Manual `new` in `Command/AddSchema.cs` was not reported even though CLIO001 analyzer assembly was loaded and executed.
Decision: Extended CLIO001 registration matching from symbol-only to symbol+name fallback, preserving registration discovery from Microsoft DI methods while handling cases where type binding is not symbol-resolvable.
Discovery: `dotnet build` with analyzer reporting confirmed analyzer execution; diagnostics were missed by symbol-only matching in this code path and restored by name fallback.
Files: Clio.Analyzers/DependencyInjectionManualConstructionAnalyzer.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: CLIO001 now reports manual construction in AddSchema and other DI-registered usages more reliably under current MS DI setup.
## 2026-02-22 - Validate CLIO001 on RfsEnvironment
Context: User requested validation of CLIO001 warnings in `Command/RfsEnvironment.cs` after analyzer retargeting to MS DI.
Decision: Classified warnings by exact object-creation site: outer `new Clio.Common.FileSystem(...)` is a true positive, inner `new System.IO.Abstractions.FileSystem()` is a false positive.
Discovery: Name-based fallback in CLIO001 can collide on short type names (`FileSystem`) across namespaces, producing duplicate warnings on the same line/statement.
Files: clio/Command/RfsEnvironment.cs, Clio.Analyzers/DependencyInjectionManualConstructionAnalyzer.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Future analyzer tightening should prefer fully-qualified type matching (or short-name matching only for unbound symbols) to avoid namespace-collision false positives.
## 2026-02-22 - Tighten CLIO001 matching and validate RfsEnvironment warnings
Context: User asked to proceed with analyzer tightening and validate CLIO001 findings in `Command/RfsEnvironment.cs`.
Decision: Updated CLIO001 to prioritize symbol/FQN matching, limit simple-name fallback to unresolved simple syntax, and include anonymous-method factory extraction; also switched registration collection to non-cancelled pass for stability.
Discovery: `BindingsModule` aliases `FileSystem` to `System.IO.Abstractions.FileSystem` and registers it (`AddTransient<IFileSystem, FileSystem>`), so inner `new System.IO.Abstractions.FileSystem()` warnings in RfsEnvironment are semantically consistent with current analyzer policy.
Files: Clio.Analyzers/DependencyInjectionManualConstructionAnalyzer.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/Command/RfsEnvironment.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: CLIO001 behavior is more deterministic, but RfsEnvironment still reports four warnings because both concrete `FileSystem` types are currently DI-registered/treated as services.
## 2026-02-22 - Refactor RfsEnvironment to DI-managed filesystem
Context: User requested removing manual filesystem construction in `RfsEnvironment` and consuming `Clio.Common.FileSystem` through DI.
Decision: Converted `RfsEnvironment` to an injectable service with constructor dependencies `IFileSystem` and `IPackageUtilities`; replaced static `new FileSystem(...)`/`new PackageUtilities(...)` with injected services; updated link commands and wiring to resolve through DI.
Discovery: `Link2RepoCommand` was created via `CreateCommand` and had to be switched to `Resolve` in `Program` so constructor injection works.
Files: clio/Command/RfsEnvironment.cs, clio/Command/Link2RepoCommand.cs, clio/Command/Link4RepoCommand.cs, clio/Program.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: `RfsEnvironment` no longer manually constructs filesystem helpers, and CLIO001 warnings for `RfsEnvironment.cs` are eliminated.
## 2026-02-22 - Add target-typed new support to CLIO001
Context: Analyzer reported `new EnvironmentSettings()` but missed `new()` for the same type.
Decision: Extended CLIO001 to analyze both `ObjectCreationExpression` and `ImplicitObjectCreationExpression` and resolve created type from semantic model for both forms.
Discovery: Enabling implicit object creation analysis surfaced additional existing manual-construction sites (many previously hidden due target-typed syntax).
Files: Clio.Analyzers/DependencyInjectionManualConstructionAnalyzer.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: CLIO001 now consistently detects DI manual construction for explicit and target-typed `new`.
## 2026-02-22 - Add CLIO001 attention directive to AGENTS
Context: User requested stronger repository guidance to prioritize DI and treat manual instance creation as last resort.
Decision: Added a dedicated `CLIO001 handling` section in `AGENTS.md` emphasizing DI-first, last-resort `new` usage, and justification for suppressions.
Discovery: Existing DI policy was broad; explicit CLIO001 guidance reduces ambiguity during warning triage.
Files: AGENTS.md, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Future work should consistently treat CLIO001 warnings as actionable and avoid normalizing manual construction patterns.
## 2026-02-22 - Add CLIO002 analyzer for Console output
Context: User requested a new analyzer to discourage direct `Console` output and encourage `ConsoleLogger` usage.
Decision: Added `CLIO002` analyzer that reports `System.Console.Write`/`WriteLine` invocations, excluding `Clio.*.ConsoleLogger` implementation.
Discovery: Running build immediately surfaces many existing direct console writes across command and utility classes, which confirms analyzer coverage.
Files: Clio.Analyzers/ConsoleOutputAnalyzer.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Repository now has automatic enforcement pressure toward `ILogger`/`ConsoleLogger` instead of direct console printing.
## 2026-02-22 - Introduce CLIO severity policy in .editorconfig
Context: User requested scalable analyzer governance without documenting every rule in AGENTS.
Decision: Added `clio/.editorconfig` with CLIO severity tiers and explicit per-ID settings (`CLIO001` warning, `CLIO002` suggestion), and updated AGENTS to make `.editorconfig` the source of truth.
Discovery: Numeric ID ranges cannot be expressed directly in .editorconfig, so each new CLIO rule must be added explicitly while preserving the tier convention.
Files: clio/.editorconfig, AGENTS.md, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Analyzer severity management is centralized and scalable as CLIO analyzer set grows.

## 2026-02-22 - CLIO002 cleanup in LoadPackagesToFileSystemCommand
Context: User requested addressing CLIO002 in LoadPackagesToFileSystemCommand.
Decision: Replaced direct Console usage with injected ILogger in the command and updated the direct-construction test.
Discovery: Only one unit test instantiated this command directly (TurnFsmCommand login-retry test) and required constructor update.
Files: clio/Command/LoadPackagesToFileSystemCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/TurnFsmCommand.LoginRetry.Tests.cs
Impact: CLIO002 is removed from this command while keeping behavior and tests intact.

## 2026-02-22 - Refactor NugetPackagesProvider logging, DI HttpClient, and JSON parser
Context: User requested fixing CLIO002 warnings in NugetPackagesProvider and migrating its HTTP/JSON flow to DI + System.Text.Json.
Decision: Replaced direct Console output with ILogger, added constructor injection for HttpClient/ILogger, switched versions parsing from Newtonsoft JObject/JToken to System.Text.Json model binding, and registered typed client via AddHttpClient<INugetPackagesProvider, NugetPackagesProvider>.
Discovery: Registering plain HttpClient globally introduced broad CLIO001 fallout, so typed HttpClient registration was used to scope DI creation to INugetPackagesProvider.
Files: clio/Package/NuGet/NugetPackagesProvider.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/clio.csproj, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: The targeted CLIO002 warnings are resolved, NugetPackagesProvider now follows DI for HttpClient, and Newtonsoft dependency is removed from this provider path.

## 2026-02-22 - Add NugetPackagesProvider tests with DelegatingHandler and DI wiring
Context: User requested coverage for NugetPackagesProvider with mocked DI and DelegatingHandler-based HTTP responses.
Decision: Added a dedicated NUnit fixture that registers INugetPackagesProvider via AddHttpClient and injects a custom DelegatingHandler from DI to control responses; covered success, no-versions warning, and HTTP-error logging paths.
Discovery: Existing PackageVersion ordering treats "-rc" as greater than empty suffix, so Last resolves to rc in current implementation and tests were aligned to current behavior.
Files: clio.tests/Package/NuGet/NugetPackagesProvider.Tests.cs, clio/Package/NuGet/NugetPackagesProvider.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: NugetPackagesProvider behavior is now regression-protected and demonstrates project-preferred DI+HttpClient testing approach.

## 2026-02-22 - Remove Task covariance in NuGet provider
Context: Address compiler warning about co-variant array conversion in NugetPackagesProvider.
Decision: Replaced Task.WaitAll(Task<T>[]) usage with Task.WhenAll(tasks).GetAwaiter().GetResult() to keep strong typing.
Discovery: Task.WaitAll requires Task[], which triggers array covariance from Task<T>[]; Task.WhenAll avoids that path.
Files: clio/Package/NuGet/NugetPackagesProvider.cs
Impact: Removes potential runtime array type mismatch risk and clears analyzer/compiler warning.

## 2026-02-22 - Convert NuGet provider API to async/await
Context: User requested replacing blocking .GetAwaiter().GetResult() in NugetPackagesProvider with async/await.
Decision: Changed INugetPackagesProvider methods to Task-based signatures and updated provider implementation to wait Task.WhenAll(tasks).
Discovery: Synchronous call sites in NuGet manager/restorer now bridge via .Result until broader async propagation is done.
Files: clio/Package/NuGet/INugetPackagesProvider.cs, clio/Package/NuGet/NugetPackagesProvider.cs, clio/Package/NuGet/NuGetManager.cs, clio/Package/NuGet/NugetPackageRestorer.cs, clio.tests/Package/NuGet/NugetPackagesProvider.Tests.cs
Impact: Provider no longer blocks with GetAwaiter().GetResult() and keeps async flow where version aggregation happens.

## 2026-02-23 - Add CLIO003 direct System.IO usage analyzer with test harness
Context: User requested analyzer enforcement to discourage direct System.IO usage and guide code toward System.IO.Abstractions for testability.
Decision: Added CLIO003 analyzer that reports direct usage of core System.IO types (File, Directory, Path, FileInfo, DirectoryInfo, DriveInfo, FileStream), excludes test assemblies, and avoids invocation/member-access double-reporting for the same expression; implemented diagnostics-only iteration without code fix.

## 2026-02-23 - Expand ProcessExecutor with launch/capture/realtime APIs
Context: User requested ProcessExecutor capabilities for fire-and-forget launch, output capture, and realtime output streaming.
Decision: Reworked ProcessExecutor to add async APIs (`FireAndForgetAsync`, `ExecuteAndCaptureAsync`, `ExecuteWithRealtimeOutputAsync`) with structured options/results, while preserving the legacy `Execute(...)` compatibility path.
Discovery: Realtime callback behavior is reliably validated via stdout-focused command execution; platform-specific mixed stdout/stderr shell syntax can be flaky in tests.

## 2026-03-19 00:00 – Fix l4r relative env package path on Unix
Context: User reported that `link-from-repository` treated a relative `--envPkgPath` as an environment name on macOS and failed with a Windows-only error.

## 2026-02-23 - Expand CLIO002 to detect Console.Out and Console.OutputEncoding usage
Context: User reported ConsoleOutputAnalyzer missed Console.Out-based async writes and output stream/encoding direct access.
Decision: Extended ConsoleOutputAnalyzer to inspect member access and invocation chains so it now reports CLIO002 for Console.Out, Console.OutputEncoding, and Console.Out.* invocations (e.g., WriteAsync), while preserving test-assembly and Clio.ConsoleLogger exclusions.
Discovery: Invocation-only matching on methods declared by System.Console misses Console.Out.WriteAsync because the method is declared on TextWriter.
Files: Clio.Analyzers/ConsoleOutputAnalyzer.cs, Clio.Analyzers.Tests/ConsoleOutputAnalyzerTests.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: CLIO002 now enforces logger-only output policy for additional real-world console patterns previously slipping through.

## 2026-03-19 00:00 – Push workspace backup investigation
Context: Investigated why `pushw --Safe true` still performs package backup before install on a development environment.
Decision: No code change; documented the actual call chain and current behavior.
Discovery: `PushWorkspaceCommand` always routes through `WorkspaceInstaller` to `BasePackageInstaller.InstallPackedPackage`, which unconditionally calls `CreateBackupPackage` before installation. The `--Safe` option is not consulted there; current `Safe` handling in `EnvironmentSettings.Fill` only prompts based on the stored environment setting `this.Safe`, not the command-line `options.Safe` value.
Files: clio/Command/PushWorkspaceCommand.cs, clio/Workspace/WorkspaceInstaller.cs, clio/Package/BasePackageInstaller.cs, clio/Environment/ConfigurationOptions.cs
Impact: Future fixes should make backup behavior explicit/configurable and should not rely on `--Safe` for install-time backup control.

## 2026-03-23 00:00 – Document prod Docker cache strategy guidance
Context: User asked whether the prod Dockerfile can "export everything up to line 19" to speed up image rebuilds.
Decision: Advised that Docker already caches lines 1-19 as stable layers when the daemon cache is preserved, and recommended either a named base stage / separately tagged intermediate image or BuildKit cache export/import for environments with ephemeral caches.
Discovery: `BuildDockerImageService.CreateBuildContext` always copies the template and `source/` into a temporary context, so repository-root `.dockerignore` rules do not control that staged context; the main invalidation point in the template is `COPY source/ ./`, not the apt/entrypoint layers.
Files: C:\Projects\clio\clio\tpl\docker-templates\prod\Dockerfile, C:\Projects\clio\clio\Command\BuildDockerImageService.cs, C:\Projects\clio\.codex\workspace-diary.md
Impact: Future Docker build-performance questions can start from the correct cache boundary in the prod template and distinguish between local layer reuse versus exported remote/intermediate cache strategies.

## 2026-03-23 16:45 – Reuse creatio-base stage in dev Docker template
Context: User requested aligning the dev Docker image with the prod cache strategy by reusing a shared `creatio-base` layer.
Decision: Split the dev Dockerfile into a `creatio-base` stage for the common .NET SDK and base OS dependencies, then kept SSH, code-server, and the global `clio` tool in the dev-only final stage.
Discovery: The dev template still carried the older fragile XML `sed` expression; switching it to the same delimiter-safe form as prod removed a latent build failure while preserving behavior.
Files: C:\Projects\clio\clio\tpl\docker-templates\dev\Dockerfile, C:\Projects\clio\.codex\workspace-diary.md
Impact: Dev and prod templates now share the same cached foundation, which reduces duplicated base setup work and keeps future dependency changes easier to reason about.

## 2026-03-23 18:15 – Add deep-link environment registration with login/password
Context: User needed a generic external-link flow to register a new environment with URI, login, password, runtime flags, and environment path.
Decision: Added a new `clio://RegisterEnvironment` handler that parses the requested query arguments and forwards them into the existing `RegAppCommand`/`RegAppOptions` flow instead of creating a separate registration path.
Discovery: `RegisterOAuthCredentials` was the only existing deep-link registration flow; `RegAppOptions` already supported `Uri`, `Login`, `Password`, `IsNetCore`, `Safe`, and `EnvironmentPath`, so the missing piece was only a generic external-link handler plus validator/test coverage. When no explicit environment name is supplied, deriving it from the URI host and port gives a stable default.
Files: C:\Projects\clio\clio\Requests\RegisterEnvironment.cs, C:\Projects\clio\clio.tests\Requests\RegisterEnvironmentHandlerTests.cs, C:\Projects\clio\clio.tests\Validators\ExternalLinkOptionsValidator.Tests.cs, C:\Projects\clio\.codex\workspace-diary.md
Impact: Future tooling can register local or password-based environments through a single deep link without relying on the OAuth-only registration path.

## 2026-03-23 18:35 – Add deep-link environment unregister by name
Context: User requested a matching external-link flow to remove a registered environment by its name.
Decision: Added `clio://UnregisterEnvironment?name=...` and mapped it directly to the existing `UnregAppCommand` so environment removal reuses the current repository deletion behavior instead of introducing a second unregister implementation.
Discovery: `ExternalLink` validation only needed the new request type name to exist plus a valid query shape; the simplest reliable test for the handler is asserting the underlying `ISettingsRepository.RemoveEnvironment(name)` side effect through the real `UnregAppCommand`.
Files: C:\Projects\clio\clio\Requests\UnregisterEnvironment.cs, C:\Projects\clio\clio.tests\Requests\UnregisterEnvironmentHandlerTests.cs, C:\Projects\clio\clio.tests\Validators\ExternalLinkOptionsValidator.Tests.cs, C:\Projects\clio\.codex\workspace-diary.md
Impact: Future tooling can both add and remove environments through deep links without shelling out to separate explicit CLI commands.

## 2026-03-23 18:50 – Fix MediatR container validation after adding unregister deep link
Context: After adding `UnregisterEnvironmentHandler`, unrelated tests such as `SetApplicationIconCommand_CallsComposableAppmanager` started failing during test fixture setup.
Decision: Registered `UnregAppCommand` in the main DI container so MediatR-discovered request handlers depending on it can be constructed during service-provider validation.
Discovery: The failures were not specific to the application-icon command; `BindingsModule` scans the whole assembly for MediatR handlers, and the new `UnregisterEnvironmentHandler` introduced a constructor dependency on `UnregAppCommand` that was never previously registered. Any test that built the full container failed before reaching its own assertions.
Files: C:\Projects\clio\clio\BindingsModule.cs, C:\Projects\clio\.codex\workspace-diary.md
Impact: Full-container tests are stable again, and future MediatR handler additions that depend on commands need corresponding DI registrations or interface-based dependencies.

## 2026-03-09 20:05 – Identify Windows register tool packaging mismatch
Context: User reported `clio register` failing on Windows with `DirectoryNotFoundException` for `.dotnet\tools\.store\...\tools\net8.0\any\img`.
Decision: Traced the failure to `RegisterCommand` assuming an `img` folder exists under `AppContext.BaseDirectory`, then verified the built `clio.8.0.2.16.nupkg` packs `reg` assets but not `img`.
Discovery: `clio/Command/RegisterCommand.cs` unconditionally calls `imgFolder.GetFiles()` on `<base>/img`, while `clio/clio.csproj` includes `reg`, `tpl`, `cliogate`, `Wiki`, and `help` as content but omits `img`; existing register tests manually add `img` to the mock filesystem, so they miss the packaged-tool layout.
Files: clio/Command/RegisterCommand.cs, clio/clio.csproj, clio.tests/Command/RegisterCommand.Tests.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Future fixes should either pack `img` with the tool or make `register` tolerate a missing icon directory, and add a regression test that reflects the real .NET tool installation structure.

Decision: Added a thin MCP tool with separate methods for registered-environment and direct-environment-package-path modes, paired prompt helpers, and mapping-focused unit coverage without E2E because the command creates filesystem symlinks.
Discovery: `Link4RepoCommand` already owns both target-resolution paths, so the MCP adapter does not need `IToolCommandResolver`; isolated test output paths remain necessary in this repo because the default `clio.exe` debug output can be locked by a running process.

## 2026-03-09 21:15 – Make MCP E2E coverage mandatory in repo guidance

## 2026-03-09 20:17 – Review LinkFromRepository MCP additions
Context: User requested a focused code-quality/correctness review of the new `link-from-repository` MCP tool, prompt, and unit tests.

## 2026-03-09 21:25 – Add aggregate assert-infrastructure MCP tool
Context: User requested one MCP call that runs the full infrastructure assert sweep and returns complete machine-readable results for Kubernetes, local infrastructure, filesystem, and deployment database candidates.

## 2026-03-09 21:40 – Return structured unmasked show-webApp-list MCP payload
Context: User wanted the `show-webApp-list` MCP tool to stop returning raw logger output and instead expose the same structured shape as the command JSON output, but without masking sensitive values.
Decision: Kept the CLI command projection reusable by adding a structured environment DTO path on `ShowAppListCommand`, updated the MCP tool to return that structured result directly with `maskSensitiveData: false`, and tightened the E2E parser so it only accepts meaningful environment payloads instead of MCP wrapper objects.

## 2026-03-09 23:07 – Reduce deploy-creatio MCP inputs to five fields
Context: User wanted the MCP-facing `deploy-creatio` tool to accept only `db-server-name`, `redis-server-name`, `ZipFile`, `SiteName`, and `SitePort`, with all other MCP arguments disabled.
Decision: Reduced `DeployCreatioArgs` to the five approved fields, kept `IsSilent = true` plus `RedisDb = -1` internally, and trimmed `show-passing-infrastructure` recommendation bundles so they only advertise MCP arguments that `deploy-creatio` still accepts.
Discovery: The underlying installer does not need an MCP `db` argument when `ZipFile` is supplied because it detects the database type from the unpacked build; the real MCP server advertises the deploy input schema under an `args` wrapper, so E2E validation must inspect nested properties rather than expecting flattened tool parameters.

## 2026-03-09 23:20 – Fix deploy-creatio MCP AutoRun null regression
Context: A real deploy-creatio MCP request failed after successful unzip and PostgreSQL restore with `Nullable object must have a value.`
Decision: Set `AutoRun = true` in the deploy-creatio MCP wrapper and changed the installer service to use a null-safe `options.AutoRun == true` check.
Discovery: The reduced five-argument MCP wrapper manually constructs `PfInstallerOptions`, so CLI parser defaults are bypassed and nullable options must be assigned explicitly.

## 2026-03-09 23:55 – Add hardened find-empty-iis-port MCP tool
Context: User needed an MCP tool that helps agents choose a safe local IIS `sitePort` in the fixed `40000-42000` range before `deploy-creatio`.
Decision: Added a read-only MCP tool plus IIS port discovery services, then hardened the result contract to expose only the recommended port and occupancy counts while failing closed on scan errors.
Discovery: Returning full bound-port lists and raw exception text leaked unnecessary host topology, and cross-platform unit tests required injected platform detection because IIS discovery is Windows-only.

## 2026-03-10 00:04 – Restore disable-reset-password default in deploy-creatio MCP
Context: User reported that password-reset disabling no longer worked through the reduced five-argument `deploy-creatio` MCP tool.
Decision: Set `DisableResetPassword = true` explicitly in the MCP wrapper so it matches the CLI parser default that is bypassed by manual `PfInstallerOptions` construction, and locked that behavior with a unit test.
Discovery: The CLI option default lives on `PfInstallerOptions`, but the MCP wrapper builds the options object directly, so any omitted boolean defaults silently fall back to `false` unless they are assigned in code.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/InstallerCommandTool.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/InstallerCommandToolTests.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: `deploy-creatio` MCP now preserves the existing CLI behavior for forced-password-reset disabling instead of silently skipping it.

## 2026-03-10 01:22 – Add dconf MCP tools for environment and build flows
Context: User needed MCP support for `dconf -e` and `dconf --build` so agents can download configuration into a chosen workspace.
Decision: Added separate `download-configuration-by-environment` and `download-configuration-by-build` MCP tools plus aligned prompt guidance, required absolute `workspace-path` for both modes, enforced absolute `build-path` for build mode, and serialized MCP command execution in `BaseTool` to avoid concurrent working-directory leakage.
Discovery: `DownloadConfigurationCommand` depends on the current working directory to target the workspace, Windows drive-relative paths can bypass a simple rooted-path check, and the extracted-build flow works in E2E when the workspace includes `.clio/workspaceSettings.json`.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/BaseTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/DownloadConfigurationTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/DownloadConfigurationPrompt.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/DownloadConfigurationToolTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/DownloadConfigurationToolE2ETests.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: MCP clients can now invoke both supported `dconf` flows with a clear contract and passing unit/E2E coverage, while malformed paths and concurrent tool execution are handled more safely.

## 2026-03-10 15:09 – Add FSM mode and compile MCP tools

## 2026-03-10 16:08 – Add DB operation log artifacts for deploy and restore

Decision: Added per-invocation DB log sessions, surfaced `log-file-path` in MCP results, introduced restore-db MCP tools, and routed DB-native restore output plus scoped logger output into a shared synchronized artifact writer.

## 2026-03-10 17:06 – Add remote entity schema column commands

## 2026-03-11 17:02 – Add get-pkg-list MCP tool and workspace happy-path coverage
Context: User asked to expose `get-pkg-list` through MCP and to add real happy-path push/restore coverage using arranged workspaces and packages.
Decision: Added a structured `get-pkg-list` MCP tool plus prompt and unit/E2E coverage, changed workspace E2E to arrange real packages with `create-workspace` and `add-package`, asserted push success through MCP `get-pkg-list`, and verified restore by deleting the local package directory then restoring into the same workspace so existing workspace settings drive the package download.
Discovery: `CallToolResult.Content` for structured non-command MCP tools arrives as text-content wrappers containing JSON strings, so E2E parsers must reject wrapper objects before deserializing payload rows; `install-gate` can fail even when cliogate is already present, so the harness now falls back to verifying `get-pkg-list --Json true` success instead of assuming install must always be the successful path.

## 2026-03-11 17:25 – Research E2E test filtering options
Context: User asked whether the `clio.mcp.e2e/run-e2e-tests.ps1` filter input can be made more specific, ideally around `AllureFeature`.
Decision: Reviewed the local runner and test attributes, then checked official `.NET`, NUnit adapter, and Allure NUnit documentation to see which metadata is actually filterable during execution.
Discovery: In the current VSTest-based `dotnet test` flow, NUnit filtering is limited to `FullyQualifiedName`, `Name`, `Category`, and `Priority`; `AllureFeature` is reporting metadata and is not a native test-selection property. More precise selection would need either standardized NUnit categories or NUnit's `NUnit.Where` filtering against custom NUnit properties.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/run-e2e-tests.ps1, clio.mcp.e2e/clio.mcp.e2e.csproj, Directory.Packages.props, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Future cleanup of the E2E runner should standardize on explicit NUnit metadata for test selection instead of overloading free-form filter input and class-name conventions.
