Errors: R1/R2(+missing-node)/R3/R10/R11/R13/R14/R15/UNKNOWN; Warnings (never error): R7/R9/R12/R17.
Discovery: DI needs NO explicit BindingsModule line — RegisterAssemblyInterfaceTypes (BindingsModule.cs:709) auto-registers every non-abstract class against every Clio.*-namespaced I* interface as AddTransient (exclusions: IDbOperationLogSession, IReauthExecutor, ConsoleLogger). An explicit AddTransient<IProcessGraphValidator,...> would DUPLICATE the registration. So BindingsModule was NOT edited; full suite still run to be safe.
Files: clio/Command/ProcessModel/{IProcessGraphValidator.cs(new),ProcessGraphValidator.cs(new)}; clio.tests/Command/ProcessModel/ProcessGraphValidatorTests.cs(16); sprint-status (4 -> in-progress) + story-4 Dev Agent Record.

## 2026-06-12 – ENG-90883 Story 1 implemented (ICdpSession extraction + launcher refactor)
Decision: Extracted the CDP frame-pump from AuthenticatedBrowserLauncher into ICdpSession/CdpSession (ConnectAsync/SendAsync/EvaluateAsync) with an ICdpConnection transport seam (ClientWebSocketCdpConnection) so Send/Evaluate are unit-testable with a fake connection. Launcher now consumes ICdpSession (dropped IHttpClientFactory; kept ReadDevToolsPortAsync), and gained LaunchAndKeepOpenAsync returning LaunchResult{DevToolsPort} (LaunchAsync delegates to it) so Story 6's driver can re-attach to the same browser. open-web-app --authenticated behavior unchanged.
Discovery: (1) CdpSession public ctor cannot take an internal ICdpConnection (CS0051) → made ICdpConnection PUBLIC (impl ClientWebSocketCdpConnection stays internal); keeps DI ctor-injection (no new → no CLIO001) and matches ICdpSession being public. (2) ClientWebSocket.SendAsync(byte[],...) binds to the Task-returning ArraySegment overload, so `.AsTask()` is a compile error — use new ArraySegment<byte>(...) and return the Task directly. (3) DI: ICdpSession+ICdpConnection auto-registered by RegisterAssemblyInterfaceTypes (no explicit BindingsModule line; explicit add would duplicate). (4) NSubstitute auto-returns completed Task/Task<T>/ValueTask for ICdpSession members → launcher happy-path test needs no Returns setup; assert via Received().
Files: clio/Common/BrowserSession/{ICdpSession.cs(new),CdpConnection.cs(new: ICdpConnection+ClientWebSocketCdpConnection),CdpSession.cs(new),AuthenticatedBrowserLauncher.cs(refactor),IAuthenticatedBrowserLauncher.cs(+LaunchAndKeepOpenAsync,+LaunchResult)}; clio.tests/Common/BrowserSession/{CdpSessionTests.cs(new,7),AuthenticatedBrowserLauncherTests.cs(reworked to mock ICdpSession)}; sprint-status (1 -> in-progress) + story-1 Dev Agent Record.
Impact: Story 1 done + verified — full Unit suite 3881 passed, 0 failed, 20 skipped; shipped launcher path preserved (manual live-nav E2E gate from story-browser-session-handoff-9 still applies, R-05). Stories done: 1,2,3,4. Remaining: 5 (validate-process-graph tool; unblocked), 6 (driver; needs 1 -> now unblocked), 7 (process-add-element; needs 1,4,6), 8 (describe-process; needs 2,3 -> unblocked). Not committed/PR'd (on master).

## 2026-06-12 – ENG-90883 Story 5 implemented (validate-process-graph MCP tool)
Decision: ValidateProcessGraphTool is a standalone [McpServerToolType] (like GuidanceGetTool), NOT BaseTool — it is pure in-memory, injects IProcessGraphValidator, maps kebab args (nodes:[{id,type}], edges:[{source,target,flow-kind}]) to ProcessGraph, returns ValidateProcessGraphResponse {success, has-errors, findings:[{severity,rule-id,message,node-id,source,target}]}. Safety flags ReadOnly=true/Destructive=false/Idempotent=true/OpenWorld=false; MCP-only (no Program.cs verb). Added ValidateProcessGraphPrompt (process-design-guidance: read process-modeling -> plan -> validate -> drive). Registered AddTransient<ValidateProcessGraphTool>() in BindingsModule (consistent with the explicit tool-registration block). McpCapabilityMap section 11 added.
Discovery: (1) The Story said "BaseTool" but AC-07 (direct IProcessGraphValidator injection, no IToolCommandResolver) means a standalone tool is correct — BaseTool<T> wraps a Command<T>, which a pure in-memory validator has none of. (2) First full-suite run showed 1 transient failure (a PostgreSQL/[::1]:5432 or timing test) that did NOT reproduce on re-run (3888 passed, 0 failed) — flaky, unrelated to this change. (3) e2e is hermetic (no environment) since validation is in-memory; CallToolAsync wraps the graph under ["args"].
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ValidateProcessGraphTool.cs(new); clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/ValidateProcessGraphPrompt.cs(new); clio/BindingsModule.cs (+AddTransient); clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ValidateProcessGraphToolTests.cs(7); clio.mcp.e2e/ValidateProcessGraphToolE2ETests.cs(3, not in CI); docs/McpCapabilityMap.md (section 11).
Impact: Story 5 done + verified (Module=McpServer 1116 passed; full Unit 3888 passed, 0 failed; e2e project builds). Stories done: 1,2,3,4,5. Remaining: 6 (CDP driver; unblocked by 1), 7 (process-add-element; needs 6), 8 (describe-process; unblocked). Not committed/PR'd.

## 2026-06-12 – ENG-90883 Story 6 implemented (ProcessDesignerDriver + embedded recipe)
Decision: Created clio/Common/ProcessDesigner/{IProcessDesignerDriver.cs(+ProcessAddElementRequest/Result records), ProcessDesignerDriver.cs, ProcessDesignerRecipes.cs, Recipes/read-data-element.recipe.js}. Driver orchestrates over ICdpSession: ConnectAsync(port) -> Page.navigate(designer url) -> poll 'prepare' until canvas ready -> TRUSTED Input.dispatchMouseEvent to select Start -> 'append' (untrusted QA-proven drag) -> 'fillObject' + trusted click on dropdown option -> 'setCaption' -> 'checkValid' (abort w/o save on .djs-validate-outline) -> trusted SAVE click -> 'saveResult' (poll "Successfully saved" + read SchemaUId). Recipe phases return JSON objects (append/fillObject/saveResult return Promises; EvaluateAsync awaitPromise=true). Params JSON-escaped via JsonSerializer.Serialize (AC-09). Recipe shipped as <EmbeddedResource> in clio.csproj; ProcessDesignerRecipes.Get suffix-matches the manifest name and caches.
Discovery: (1) Runtime.evaluate is UNTRUSTED, so canvas selection / dropdown / SAVE must use CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent (trusted) — the driver gets coords from a recipe phase, then SendAsync('Input.dispatchMouseEvent', mousePressed+mouseReleased). The append stays untrusted (QA-proven). (2) ICdpSession.SendAsync already accepts arbitrary CDP methods, so Input.dispatchMouseEvent needs no new plumbing. (3) IProcessDesignerDriver/ProcessDesignerRecipes auto-register (no BindingsModule edit); driver has public DI ctor + internal test-seam ctor (short render timeout). (4) Embedded-resource manifest suffix-match avoids RootNamespace guessing; the recipe-cache unit test proves it resolves.
Files: clio/Common/ProcessDesigner/{IProcessDesignerDriver.cs,ProcessDesignerDriver.cs,ProcessDesignerRecipes.cs,Recipes/read-data-element.recipe.js}(new); clio/clio.csproj (EmbeddedResource); clio.tests/Common/ProcessDesigner/ProcessDesignerDriverTests.cs(8).
Impact: Story 6 done + verified (full Unit 3896 passed, 0 failed, 20 skipped). Recipe LIVE correctness deferred to Story 7 e2e on test-stand (channel LOCKED). Stories done: 1,2,3,4,5,6. Remaining: 7 (process-add-element command+MCP tool — needs 1,4,6; the headline), 8 (describe-process — read/explain). Not committed/PR'd.

## 2026-06-12 – ENG-90883 Story 8 implemented (describe-process — read & explain)
Decision: Created clio/Command/ProcessModel/{IProcessGraphExtractor.cs(+ProcessDescription/Element/Flow/Parameter records), ProcessGraphExtractor.cs, IProcessSchemaReader.cs(+ProcessIdentity), ProcessSchemaReader.cs}; clio/Command/{DescribeProcessCommand.cs(+DescribeProcessOptions)}; clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/DescribeProcessTool.cs(+Args); clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/DescribeProcessPrompt.cs. Extractor projects the ALREADY-parsed ProcessSchemaResponse: non-flow FlowElements -> elements {id=UId, dataId=EventType, type=ResolveRole, label=Captions[culture]??Name, parameters}; flow-typed (SequenceFlow/ConditionalFlow/DefFlow) -> flows {source=SourceRefUId, target=TargetRefUId, kind}; MetaDataSchema.Parameters -> process parameters. Reader resolves identity by code(Name)/uid(UId)/caption via VwProcessLib then fetches via ProcessSchemaRequest (same path as ProcessModelGenerator; generate-process-model untouched). Command: kebab options, exactly-one-identity guard, JSON to stdout. Tool: env-aware BaseTool InternalExecute<DescribeProcessCommand>, ReadOnly/non-destructive/idempotent. Wired Program.cs + BindingsModule (command+tool explicit; reader+extractor auto). Docs: help/en, docs/commands, Commands.md, WikiAnchors.txt (ReadmeChecker 4-doc gate for the CLI verb), McpCapabilityMap §11.
Discovery: (1) BaseCommandTests<DescribeProcessOptions> enforces ReadmeChecker → the CLI verb needs all 4 docs (Commands.md link + help/en + docs/commands + WikiAnchors line) or the fixture fails. (2) FluentAssertions OnlyContain FAILS on an empty collection ("but the collection is empty") — use NotContain(<invalid predicate>) for "all valid OR empty". (3) A single-$ interpolated raw string ($"""...""") cannot contain literal `{{` (CS9006) — use $$ or avoid literal braces (used parentheses). (4) Schema.MetaDataSchema has a private setter (set via the metaData string), so the extractor must be tested via FromJson + the ProcessSchema/*.json fixtures, not a hand-built object.
Files: clio/Command/ProcessModel/{IProcessGraphExtractor,ProcessGraphExtractor,IProcessSchemaReader,ProcessSchemaReader}.cs; clio/Command/DescribeProcessCommand.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/DescribeProcessTool.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/DescribeProcessPrompt.cs; clio/{Program.cs,BindingsModule.cs,Commands.md,Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt,help/en/describe-process.txt,docs/commands/describe-process.md}; docs/McpCapabilityMap.md; clio.tests/Command/ProcessModel/ProcessGraphExtractorTests.cs(4); clio.tests/Command/DescribeProcessCommandTests.cs(4); clio.tests/Command/McpServer/DescribeProcessToolTests.cs(4); clio.mcp.e2e/DescribeProcessToolE2ETests.cs(2).
Impact: Story 8 done + verified (full Unit 3909 passed, 0 failed, 20 skipped; e2e builds). ALL 8 stories of the thin slice now implemented EXCEPT Story 7 (process-add-element command+MCP tool — the headline build verb that wires get-browser-session/launcher → IProcessDesignerDriver → readback; needs live e2e on test-stand). Stories done: 1,2,3,4,5,6,8. Remaining: 7. Not committed/PR'd (on master). Checkpoint commit 73746bd8 on branch feature/ENG-90883-ai-business-process-generation (stories 1-6,8).

## 2026-06-12 – ENG-90883 Story 7 implemented (process-add-element — the headline build verb)
Decision: Created clio/Command/ProcessDesigner/ProcessAddElementCommand.cs (+ProcessAddElementOptions, +ProcessAddElementOutput record) and clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ProcessAddElementTool.cs (+Args) + Prompts/ProcessAddElementPrompt.cs. Command orchestrates: read-data guard -> ISettingsRepository.GetEnvironment -> IProcessGraphValidator.Validate(Start->readDataUserTask->End) ABORT-before-browser on error -> IBrowserSessionService.GetSessionPathAsync (CreatioAuthenticationException -> "forms-auth ... required" Error) -> IAuthenticatedBrowserLauncher.LaunchAndKeepOpenAsync (ChromiumNotFoundException -> Error) -> IProcessDesignerDriver.AddReadDataElementAsync(request{env,port,processId,readObject,caption}) -> WriteInfo JSON {success,code,uId,caption} on success / WriteError "Error:" on failure (never a false-positive save). Caption auto-gen clio-pae-<utc>-<6hex>. Env-aware MCP tool (BaseTool InternalExecute<ProcessAddElementCommand>; ReadOnly=false/Idempotent=false; Destructive=false static default per OQ-01 + documented). Async collaborators blocked via .GetAwaiter().GetResult() (same as GetBrowserSessionCommand).
Discovery: (1) ProcessAddElementCommand/Options namespace = Clio.Command.ProcessDesigner (folder-matched), so BindingsModule.cs + Program.cs needed `using Clio.Command.ProcessDesigner;` (DescribeProcessCommand was Clio.Command and needed none) — CS0246 until added. (2) Same single-$ raw-string `{{}}` brace trap in the prompt (CS9006) — avoid literal braces. (3) NSubstitute: GetSessionPathAsync/LaunchAndKeepOpenAsync return Task; throw via Task.FromException<T>(...); both exceptions have a (string message) ctor. DidNotReceiveWithAnyArgs() to assert a collaborator wasn't reached.
Files: clio/Command/ProcessDesigner/ProcessAddElementCommand.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/{Tools/ProcessAddElementTool.cs,Prompts/ProcessAddElementPrompt.cs}; clio/{Program.cs,BindingsModule.cs,Commands.md,Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt,help/en/process-add-element.txt,docs/commands/process-add-element.md}; docs/McpCapabilityMap.md; clio.tests/Command/ProcessDesigner/ProcessAddElementCommandTests.cs(7); clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ProcessAddElementToolTests.cs(3); clio.mcp.e2e/ProcessAddElementToolE2ETests.cs(2).
Impact: Story 7 done (unit) + verified — full Unit 3920 passed, 0 failed, 20 skipped; e2e builds. ALL 8 stories of the thin slice now implemented. Remaining gate: the LIVE build-and-readback e2e on test-stand (not in CI) — where the Story-6 driver recipe is tuned against the real designer (channel LOCKED, PoC-proven). Story 7 not yet committed (the checkpoint commit covered 1-6,8). [committed e3cb049c after.]

## 2026-06-12 – ENG-90883 LIVE e2e on test-stand: process-add-element run + driver-recipe tuning
Context: User rebuilt+restarted the clio MCP server, exposing the new tools (mcp__clio__{process-add-element,describe-process,validate-process-graph}). Ran process-add-element live, then tuned the Story-6 recipe against the real designer.
Decision: First live run of process-add-element (test-stand, read-object Contact) — auth+launch+navigate SUCCEEDED ("injected 8 session cookie(s)") but the recipe failed at append: "append drag did not start." Tuned the recipe phase-by-phase in a controllable chrome-devtools browser and applied 3 fixes to clio/Common/ProcessDesigner/Recipes/read-data-element.recipe.js.
Discovery (recipe fixes, all live-validated): (1) APPEND: .djs-drag-group only materializes AFTER an initial mousemove on the SOURCE element — sequence is dragstart(add.serviceTask) -> mousemove(start shape center) -> grab last .djs-drag-group -> mousemove/mouseover/mousemove/mouseup at target. (2) OBJECT LOOKUP: the "object to read" field is input[id*="EntitySchemaSelect"] (full id ReadDataUserTaskPropertiesPageEntitySchemaSelectComboBoxEdit-el); untrusted value-set+input/keyup opens the .listview; click the exact-text option (trusted). Read-mode = ...DataReadMode..., element caption = ...captionTextEdit-el. (3) SAVE: #schema-designer-save-btn-textEl (DOM text "Save", CSS-uppercased — exact ==='SAVE' missed it). Process caption = #schema-designer-caption-el. Save signal = .message-panel "Successfully saved" (confirmed). SchemaUId read from Ext.ComponentManager.get('schema-designer').model.values returned null -> identity resolved by caption (OQ-04). (4) describe-process types the Read data element as Unknown/Other because the data-op managerItemUId isn't in ManagerMap's GUID map -> future refinement (map data-task manager UIds); element still read with label+params.
Validation: built Start->Read data(Contact)->End live via the tuned recipe (manual phase replay) -> "Successfully saved" -> clio describe-process read it back = UsrProcess_6ddeb29 ("AI live e2e read contact tuned"): StartEvent/ReadDataUserTask1/TerminateEvent + 2 sequence flows. describe-process (Story 8) and validate-process-graph (Story 5) both confirmed live via the MCP tools.
Files: clio/Common/ProcessDesigner/Recipes/read-data-element.recipe.js (3 fixes); spec/ai-business-process-generation/ai-bp-ui-playbook.md (§7 live tuning + confirmed selectors). Common unit suite 572 passed (recipe embeds fine).
Impact: Recipe is fixed + the phases proven against the live designer. NEXT: rebuild+restart the clio MCP server (embedded recipe refresh), then re-run process-add-element to confirm the FULL command path end-to-end with the fixed recipe. PoC/live processes UsrProcess_493d4c9 + UsrProcess_6ddeb29 left on test-stand.

## 2026-06-12 – ENG-90883 LIVE e2e GREEN: process-add-element end-to-end via clio MCP
Context: Iterated the driver recipe over 3 live rebuild/restart cycles on test-stand until process-add-element passed end-to-end through the clio MCP tool.
Iteration log: run1 -> "append drag did not start" (fixed: append needs an initial mousemove on the source to materialize .djs-drag-group). run2/run3 -> "object lookup field (EntitySchemaSelect) did not render" within 6s (the Read data setup card renders async after selection). FIX (run4): (a) append now returns the appended element's center coords; (b) the DRIVER trusted-clicks those coords to EXPLICITLY select the element (don't rely on auto-selection) so the card opens; (c) fillObject field-poll bumped to ~12s + diagnostic on failure.
Result (run4): exit 0, {"success":true,"caption":"AI e2e read contact v4"} -> "Opened an authenticated browser session (injected 8 cookies)" + saved. describe-process read it back = UsrProcess_c13f45c: StartEvent -> ReadDataUserTask1 -> TerminateEvent + 2 sequence flows, Read data params (ResultEntity/DataSourceFilters/OrderInfo/...). The headline "AI designs a process via clio MCP" goal is validated live end-to-end.
Discovery: the appended element is NOT reliably auto-selected on the cold driver browser (or the property-card module loads >6s) — explicit trusted-click on the new element + a generous poll is the robust pattern. code/uId come back null from the recipe (SchemaUId not exposed on model.values) so the command returns success+caption; identity is resolved by caption via describe-process/execute-esq (OQ-04).

## 2026-06-15 – ENG-90883 / ENG-91447: split the monobranch into common-core + per-approach branches
Context: All AI-BP-generation work (8 stories) lived on one branch. Split so the channel-independent core is reusable by BOTH the browser approach (Approach 3 = ENG-91447) and the upcoming backend non-visual designer (Approach 1). User framing: the common part is "the documentation/knowledge that explains to the AI what a BP is + which elements exist".
Decision: 3 stacked branches.
- feature/ENG-90883-process-common-core (off master) = AI BPMN knowledge (ProcessModelingGuidanceResource + get-guidance process-modeling), element catalog/connection-rules docs, element typing (Schema.cs ResolveDataId/ResolveRole), graph validator (R1-R17), describe-process + validate-process-graph, full BMAD record (prd/adr/stories/test-plan/sprint-status). NO browser code.
- feature/ENG-90883-approach3-ui-designer (stacked on core) = CDP infra (ICdpSession/CdpConnection/CdpSession + launcher refactor) + designer driver + read-data recipe + process-add-element command/tool. Live e2e GREEN on test-stand.
- feature/ENG-90883-approach1-backend-designer (off core) = clean starting point for the backend ProcessDesignService research (no browser baggage).
Discovery: the 5 shared composition/doc files (Program.cs, BindingsModule.cs, Commands.md, WikiAnchors.txt, docs/McpCapabilityMap.md) carry BOTH the describe/validate (common) and process-add-element (approach3) edits. Stacking (NOT parallel-from-master) makes those edits purely additive and conflict-free: common registers describe/validate; approach3 appends process-add-element. Each branch builds (Release) and passes its module tests independently (common 1188+1511; approach3 3282).
Verified lossless: `git diff archive/ENG-90883-monobranch approach3-ui-designer` empty (approach3 reconstructs the monobranch exactly); `git diff common-core approach1-backend-designer` empty (clean base). Backup: tag archive/ENG-90883-monobranch (1dc70481) + old branch feature/ENG-90883-ai-business-process-generation (local only).
PR stacking: common-core -> base master; approach3 + approach1 -> base feature/ENG-90883-process-common-core.
Branch links recorded in Jira ENG-91447 (comment) and research page TER/4702928908 (Approach 3 "Implementation branches" panel, v19).
Files: branch reorganization only (no code change); this diary entry on the common-core base.
Impact: Approach 1 research starts clean on feature/ENG-90883-approach1-backend-designer with the validator + BPMN guidance + describe-process already available and zero browser code. NEXT (best done in a NEW Claude session): checkout approach1 branch, read research page TER/4702928908 (Approach 1 section) + project-context.md, reuse the validator + guidance from common-core, run /bmad for the backend command-driven ProcessDesignService (PRD -> ADR -> stories -> test plan).

## 2026-06-15 – ENG-91447 Approach 1: BuildProcess mappings[] (process-param -> element-param) implemented
Context: ProcessDesignService.BuildProcess already added process-level params (FJ1) but could not bind them into element params. The one blocker was the exact value-expression syntax to reference a process parameter; resolved via a designer capture (UsrProcess_7e74a2a on test-stand, schema JSON pasted in-session).
Decision: Do NOT parse FSD metadata.json (storage is the server package's internal concern; clio only sends commands). Capture/verify go through the server: extended DescribeProcess to read back parameter SourceValue over REST.
Discovery (authoritative, from Terrasoft.Core SOURCE in the checkout C:\Projects\Creatio\TSBpm\Src\Lib\Terrasoft.Core\Process\*.cs):
- Process-param reference token = "[#" + ProcessSchemaParameter.GetMetaPath() + "#]" = [#[IsOwnerSchema:false].[IsSchema:false].[Parameter:{uid}]#]. Source code for the binding = Script(3) (designer never used Mapping(2) for process-param refs in the capture).
- ProcessSchemaParameter.SourceValue SETTER auto-syncs the schema-level Mappings (BK15) entry via Mappings.FindByTargetUId(UId) -> ASSIGN a new SourceValue (don't mutate in place). Element params + mirror Mappings are auto-created when userTask.SchemaUId is set.
- ProcessSchemaParameterValue.Value setter routes to the GS2/metadata store only when IsFormula (Source in {Script,Mapping}) -> set Source BEFORE Value.
- enum confirmed: None0 ConstValue1 Mapping2 Script3 SystemValue4 SystemSetting5 EntityMapping6 SamplingEntityMapping7. Codes: ProcessSchemaParameterValue GS1=Source GS2=Value GS4=MetaPath GS5=ModifiedInSchemaUId; ProcessSchemaParameter.SourceValue=L8; ProcessSchemaMapping GT1..GT5.
Changes: ProcessDesignContracts.cs (BuildProcessRequest.mappings[] + ProcessMappingDescriptor{elementId,elementParameter,processParameter|expression|value}; DescribeProcessElement.parameters[] + DescribeProcessResponse.parameters[] + DescribeProcessParameter{name,uid,source,value}). ProcessDesignService.cs (ApplyMappings/ResolveElementParameter/BuildSourceValue; DescribeProcess now dumps SourceValue via ReadElementParameters/ReadProcessParameters/ToDescribeParameter).
Build: dotnet build clioprocessbuilder.csproj -c Debug = 0 warn / 0 err. NOT yet loaded on the stand (deploy loop: user manually restarts test-stand, then verify via REST).
Files: C:\Projects\Creatio\TSBpm\Src\Lib\Terrasoft.WebApp.Loader\Terrasoft.WebApp\Terrasoft.Configuration\Pkg\clioprocessbuilder\Files\cs\{ProcessDesignContracts.cs,ProcessDesignService.cs}; spec/process-design-service/process-design-service-state.md.
Impact: Mapping front unblocked end-to-end at the server layer. NEXT: after restart, build start->performTask->end with a Text process param + mapping into a text element param, DescribeProcess to confirm element param {source:"Script", value:"[#...[Parameter:{uid}]...#]"} and uid==process param; then move to clio Layer-2 TODO list. [DONE+VERIFIED later same day.]

ProcessDesignService is WCF BodyStyle=Wrapped, so request wrapped as {"request":...} and response parsed via an envelope DTO {"<Method>Result":...}. create-business-process takes --descriptor <FILE> (raw BuildProcessRequest JSON, wrapped via System.Text.Json.Nodes) + optional --package-name override; list-user-tasks posts "{}".

STILL PENDING (Phase B, mandated by repo policy, NOT done yet): MCP tool+prompt(+resources) + clio.mcp.e2e for both commands; unit tests via BaseCommandTests<TOptions> ([Category("Unit")]); docs (help/en/*.txt, docs/commands/*.md, Commands.md, Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt). ## 2026-06-16 – ENG-91447 Approach 1: clio Layer-2 Phase B (MCP + tests + docs) for the two commands
Context: Phase A (routes + commands) verified. User chose to complete the mandated Phase B in full. Skills create-mcp-tool/test-mcp-tool/document-command referenced by CLAUDE.md are NOT loaded in this session's skill list, so did the work manually against the mapped conventions.
Decision: Mirror reference artifacts exactly. MCP tools derive BaseTool<TOptions> and use InternalExecute<TCommand>(options) (both options are EnvironmentOptions); tools/prompts auto-discovered via .WithToolsFromAssembly/.WithPromptsFromAssembly (BindingsModule.cs:612-613) — no extra DI, ActivatorUtilities builds them from registered ctor deps. Refactored create-business-process to also accept --descriptor-json (inline); service became content-based (parses JSON string), CLI command reads the file — so the MCP tool passes inline JSON with no temp-file dance. Un-sealed both command classes so Fake subclasses in MCP tool tests compile (mirrors FakeRedisCommand).
Discovery: BaseCommandTests<T> contains ReadmeChecker test Command_ShouldHave_DescriptionBlock_InReadmeFile that REQUIRES, per verb: a Commands.md index entry "(docs/commands/<verb>.md)", a help/en/<verb>.txt (renderable), a docs/commands/<verb>.md file, AND a Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt line "<verb>:...". So docs are a hard gate for command unit tests, not optional. Interpolated raw string literals ($"""...""") treat {{ }} as interpolation — removed literal braces from the CreateBusinessProcessPrompt text. clio.tests + clio.mcp.e2e are net10.0-only; the net10.0 clio.exe was locked by 4 running session clio MCP processes — stopped them (Get-Process clio | Stop-Process) to unblock the apphost copy.
Changes: Tools/{ListUserTasksTool,CreateBusinessProcessTool}.cs; Prompts/{ListUserTasksPrompt,CreateBusinessProcessPrompt}.cs; clio.tests/Command/{ListUserTasksCommandTests,CreateBusinessProcessCommandTests}.cs; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{ListUserTasksToolTests,CreateBusinessProcessToolTests}.cs; clio.mcp.e2e/{ListUserTasksToolE2ETests,CreateBusinessProcessToolE2ETests}.cs; help/en/{list-user-tasks,create-business-process}.txt; docs/commands/{list-user-tasks,create-business-process}.md; Commands.md; Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt; un-sealed ListUserTasksCommand + CreateBusinessProcessCommand; refactored CreateBusinessProcessCommand (inline descriptor + content-based service).
Build/verify: clio + clio.tests + clio.mcp.e2e build 0-err. New 15 unit tests pass (incl. both ReadmeChecker README-block checks). FULL unit suite 3908 passed / 0 failed / 20 skipped. e2e NOT run (manual-only, needs sandbox with ProcessDesignService). create-business-process re-verified via CLI inline path (--descriptor-json) -> UsrClioBpCliTest2 built.
Impact: Both Layer-2 commands are now complete per repo policy (CLI + MCP + unit + e2e + docs). NOT committed. NEXT remaining Layer-2 items: package delivery (ship clioprocessbuilder.gz like cliogate), pre-flight graph validator (R1-R17 common-core) before BuildProcess, describe-process server rework (item 5). Cleanup: test processes UsrClioBpMap213428 / UsrClioBpCliTest1 / UsrClioBpCliTest2 left in Custom on test-stand. Session clio MCP processes were stopped — restart Claude to restore mcp__clio__* (now including the two new tools if pointed at the rebuilt net10.0 clio).

## 2026-06-16 – ENG-91447 Approach 1: Layer-2 item 5 — describe-process moved to the server (ProcessDesignService)
Context: User decided to move process reading to the server package. Premise verified by git: DescribeProcessCommand.cs / IProcessSchemaReader.cs / ProcessGraphExtractor.cs are NOT on master (common-core, unmerged → free to rework); Schema.cs IS on master (keep — used by generate-process-model + R1-R17 validator).
Decision: Delegate the read to server-side DescribeProcess instead of the local ProcessSchemaRequest + ManagerMap(GUID->coarse-role) parse. The ONLY real gap of the local path was that managerItemUId for every user task is the generic UserTaskUId, so all user tasks collapsed to a coarse "Activity/UserTask" with no specific schema name; the server types from the real C# class and resolves the user-task schema name via the manager. (Note: the local gap was also closable locally via element.schemaUId lookup — but the user chose the server route for Approach-1 consistency.)
Discovery: blast radius — IProcessSchemaReader/IProcessGraphExtractor/ProcessGraphExtractor were used ONLY by DescribeProcessCommand (ProcessModelGenerator uses only Schema.cs's ProcessSchemaResponse.FromJson). DescribeProcessTool just runs the command via InternalExecute<DescribeProcessCommand>, so the tool + its args (DescribeProcessArgs) need NO change — only the [Description] was updated. KnownRoute.DescribeProcessSchema (44) was already registered in Phase A. Cost of the move: describe-process is now PACKAGE-DEPENDENT (needs clioprocessbuilder on the env) and the output contract changed (free, since common-core unmerged): top-level name/schemaUId (was code/uId); elements[].type = runtime class + userTaskName; params carry source+value.
Changes: NEW clio/Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs (ProcessIdentity moved here + ServerProcessDescriber + DescribeProcessResult/DescribedElement/DescribedFlow/DescribedParameter DTOs). DescribeProcessCommand.cs reworked (inject IProcessDescriber; output server result verbatim). DELETED IProcessSchemaReader.cs, IProcessGraphExtractor.cs, ProcessGraphExtractor.cs, clio.tests/.../ProcessGraphExtractorTests.cs. Reworked DescribeProcessCommandTests.cs (mock IProcessDescriber) + DescribeProcessToolTests.cs FakeDescribeProcessCommand ctor. Updated DescribeProcessTool [Description]; help/en/describe-process.txt; docs/commands/describe-process.md. No BindingsModule change (IProcessDescriber auto-registers; DescribeProcessCommand already registered).
Build/verify: clio (net8.0) + clio.tests (net10.0) build 0-err. describe-process + ProcessModel = 90 passed. FULL unit suite 3905 passed / 0 failed / 20 skipped. LIVE: describe-process --process-code UsrClioBpCliTest1 -e test-stand -> type:ProcessSchemaUserTask, userTaskName:ActivityUserTask, Recommendation source:Script value:[#...[Parameter:{...}]#], process param MyText. NOT committed. Killed session clio processes again to unblock the net10.0 build.
Impact: describe-process now universal (real types + specific user-task names + parameter value sources), consistent with BuildProcess round-trip. Remaining Layer-2: item 1 (package delivery — also the dependency that gates describe-process/create-business-process/list-user-tasks on arbitrary envs) and item 4 (pre-flight R1-R17 validator before BuildProcess).

## 2026-06-16 – ENG-91447 Approach 1: fix — performTask must produce the dedicated "Perform task" element (not generic "User task")
Context: User reported that an AI-built process ("Process test 1", b1fbff50-…) used the generic "User task" element (with a "Which user task to perform?" dropdown) instead of the dedicated "Perform task" element. Our create-business-process/CreateUserTask set only SchemaUId, leaving the ctor default ManagerItemUId = generic UserTaskUId.
Root cause + model (confirmed from Terrasoft.Core source AND the client designer): "User task" and "Perform task" are the SAME class ProcessSchemaUserTask; the designer picks the editor by ManagerItemUId. ProcessSchemaUserTask.Initialize() ALWAYS sets ManagerItemUId = ProcessSchemaElementManager.UserTaskUId (generic {1418E61A…}); the SchemaUId setter does NOT change it. The CLIENT designer (Resources/ui/Terrasoft/designers/process-schema-designer/process-schema-designer-view-model-new.js) creates an element with managerItemUId = getFlowElementSchemaManagerItemByTypeName(type).uId; palette user tasks are in FlowElementGroup.UserTask, loaded from SysProcessUserTask. So a dedicated user-task element has ManagerItemUId == the user-task SCHEMA UId (capture: ActivityUserTask element ManagerItemUId == SchemaUId == b5c726f2…).
KEY nuance (user correction): the generic "User task" element can run ANY user task; only a SUBSET have a dedicated element. CrtProcessDesigner has a generic UserTaskPropertiesPage editor + per-task dedicated editors (ActivityUserTask=Perform task, ReadData/AddData/ChangeData/DeleteData, Approval, SendEmail/Email/EmailTemplate, Call, Question, OpenEditPage/Preconfigured/AutoGenerated page, ChangeAdminRights, WebService, LinkEntityToProcess, *FileProcessing) + ProcessUnsupportedElement fallback. So setting ManagerItemUId=SchemaUId blindly for a NON-palette user task would render incorrectly.
Fix: CreateUserTask sets userTask.ManagerItemUId = taskSchema.UId ONLY when HasDedicatedPaletteElement(taskSchema.UId) — i.e. the task is in SysProcessUserTask; else keep the generic element. First attempt set it unconditionally (WRONG) — corrected after user feedback to look at the client model. ManagerItemUId is a public get/set on ProcessSchemaBaseElement.
Also: added managerItemUId to DescribeProcess element output (server ProcessDesignContracts.DescribeProcessElement + service + clio DescribedElement) so the specialized/generic distinction is verifiable over REST.
Changes (server pkg, NOT on master either way): Files/cs/ProcessDesignService.cs (CreateUserTask conditional + HasDedicatedPaletteElement; DescribeProcess populates ManagerItemUId); Files/cs/ProcessDesignContracts.cs (DescribeProcessElement.managerItemUId). clio: Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs (DescribedElement.ManagerItemUId).
Build/verify: package + clio build 0-err. User restarted test-stand. LIVE: create-business-process performTask -> describe-process shows element managerItemUId == b5c726f2… (ActivityUserTask, specialized), generic 1418e61a… absent, userTaskName == ActivityUserTask. (Process UsrClioBpPerform1 / b8ea752d… on test-stand.) NOT committed.
Impact: performTask (and any palette user task) now builds the dedicated specialized element; non-palette tasks correctly stay generic. Test processes on test-stand Custom: UsrClioBpMap213428, UsrClioBpCliTest1/2, UsrClioBpPerform1, + the user's Process test 1 (b1fbff50, old generic one).

## 2026-06-16 – ENG-91447 Approach 1: signalStart (record-triggered start) + ModifyProcess Phase 1 (edit existing process)
Context: AI fell back to a client crt.SaveRecordRequest handler for "run process on record save" because the backend designer (a) couldn't make a signal start and (b) could only CREATE, not EDIT, a process. Two capabilities added.
signalStart: capture obtained (UsrProcess_c502814) — ProcessSchemaStartSignalEvent with WaitingEntitySignal:true, WaitingRandomSignal:false, EntitySchemaUId (PRESENT in serialized schema), EntitySignal (EntityChangeType flags Inserted1/Updated2/Deleted4; save=3), HasEntityColumnChange/NewEntityChangedColumns, EntityFilters (FilterGroup json), auto-param RecordId, UseBackgroundMode:true, ManagerItemUId=StartSignalEventUId. Serialization is via the MetaType property path (NOT the element's WriteMetaData/DZ codes) — so assigning the C# properties + SaveSchema persists them (proven by the designer's own save). Impl: BuildProcess element type `signalStart` (descriptor `signal:{entity,on}`; on=added/modified/deleted/save[=added|modified default]); CreateSignalStart sets WaitingEntitySignal+EntitySchemaUId(ResolveEntitySchemaUId by name)+EntitySignal(ParseEntitySignal)+UseBackgroundMode. Filters/changed-columns deferred. DescribeProcess reads back a signal{entity,entitySchemaUId,on} (server + clio DescribedSignal).
ModifyProcess Phase 1: operation-list model, phased (user choice). Mechanism (SchemaManager.cs): DesignSchema(uc,uid) -> GetDesignInstance(uc,uid) editable ProcessSchema -> mutate -> SaveSchema(uid, removeDesignItem:true, uc, generateBundles:true). Ops: addElement (reuses PlaceNewElement/CreateElement incl signalStart + user-task specialization), removeElement (+ removes its sequence flows and schema.Mappings entries whose TargetMetaPath contains the element UId), addFlow/removeFlow ({source,target} by Name or UId via FindFlowNode). Refactored BuildGraph to share PlaceNewElement + AddSequenceFlow (BuildProcess behavior unchanged). Any op failure aborts (nothing saved). Phase 2 TODO: setElement (props), parameter ops, mapping ops.
Changes (server pkg): Files/cs/ProcessDesignContracts.cs (signal descriptor + DescribeSignalInfo; ModifyProcessRequest/ProcessOperationDescriptor/ModifyProcessResponse); Files/cs/ProcessDesignService.cs (CreateSignalStart/ResolveEntitySchemaUId/ParseEntitySignal; signalStart case; ReadSignal/DecodeEntitySignal; BuildGraph refactor -> PlaceNewElement/AddSequenceFlow; ModifyProcess + ResolveSchemaUId/GetPrimaryLane/FindFlowNode/ApplyOperation/RemoveElement/RemoveFlow). clio: Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs (DescribedSignal). New using Terrasoft.Core.Entities.
Build/verify: package + clio (net8.0) build 0-err. User restarted test-stand (deployed signalStart + ModifyProcess together). LIVE (REST): ModifyProcess on UsrClioBpPerform1 (removeElement StartEvent1 + addElement signalStart UsrTestRunButton/save + addFlow signalStart->task1) -> success, appliedOperations:3; DescribeProcess shows StartEvent1 gone, SignalStart1=ProcessSchemaStartSignalEvent signal=UsrTestRunButton/added|modified, flows signalStart->task1->EndEvent1. NOT committed.
Impact: backend designer can now start a process on a record event AND edit an existing process (add/remove elements + flows) — the proper alternative to a client save handler. UsrClioBpPerform1 now starts on save of UsrTestRunButton. NEXT: clio glue for both (KnownRoute already has BuildProcess/ListUserTasks/DescribeProcess; add ModifyProcess route + create-business-process already done; add modify-business-process command + MCP + tests + docs; signalStart is reachable via create-business-process already). ModifyProcess Phase 2 ops (setElement/params/mappings).

## 2026-06-16 – ENG-91447 Approach 1: signalStart designer-fidelity fixes (size / event / position)
Context: User opened the modified UsrClioBpPerform1 in the designer and reported 3 issues. Root causes + fixes (server pkg, Files/cs/ProcessDesignService.cs):
1. Signal drawn task-sized (69×55): GetDefaultSize didn't know signalStart -> default size. Fix: signalStart -> 31×31 (event).
2. "Which event should trigger the signal?" empty: the designer (BaseSignalEventPropertiesPage.CrtProcessDesigner.js getEntityChangeTypeList) keys the event dropdown by SINGLE EntityChangeType values (Inserted1/Updated2/Deleted4) via list[entitySignal]; our save->Inserted|Updated=3 is not a key -> undefined -> empty. Fix: ParseEntitySignal returns SINGLE values only; save/default -> Updated (modified). No combined value (the designer has no "added or modified" option).
3. signalStart overlapped EndEvent1: modify addElement placed by next index (X=420 = EndEvent1). Fix: ComputeNewElementX — start-type elements go left (minX-step), others right (maxX+step); PlaceNewElement now takes explicit X (BuildGraph behavior unchanged).

LIVE: fresh UsrClioBpSignal2 (create start->task->end, modify removeElement start + addElement signalStart save + addFlow) -> describe: SignalStart1 pos=60;185 (left, no overlap), signal.on=modified (single), flows SignalStart1->task1->EndEvent1. Size 31×31 visual (user confirms in designer). NOT committed.

## 2026-06-16 – ENG-91447 Approach 1: topological auto-layout (LayoutGraph) for build + modify
Context: User wants added elements readably placed — End at max X, flow-order left-to-right, no overlap. The per-add heuristic (ComputeNewElementX) couldn't guarantee "End rightmost" (a task added via maxX+step could land right of End).
Change (Files/cs/ProcessDesignService.cs): replaced per-element X placement with a single re-layout pass LayoutGraph(schema) run BEFORE save in both BuildGraph (end) and ModifyProcess (after the ops loop). PlaceNewElement no longer takes/computes X (placeholder position only); ComputeNewElementX removed; added const LayoutVerticalStep=90; helpers IsStartElement (is ProcessSchemaStartEvent — covers signal start) / IsEndElement (ProcessSchemaEndEvent || ProcessSchemaTerminateEvent). Algorithm: longest-path layering (Kahn) over sequence flows → column = distance from start; start events forced col 0 (leftmost), end/terminate forced col maxNonEnd+1 (strictly rightmost = max X); nodes in a column staggered vertically; cyclic/no-source degrades to col 0 + vertical spread. X = LayoutStartX + col*LayoutStepX. Re-lays out the whole diagram each build/modify (programmatic processes have no manual layout to keep).
Build/verify: package 0-err. User restarted test-stand. LIVE (UsrClioBpLayout1): build with elements listed END-FIRST + flows start->task->end -> describe X Start=60, task1=240, End=420 (end rightmost despite descriptor order). Modify insert task2 between task1 and end (addElement + removeFlow task1->end + addFlow task1->task2 + addFlow task2->end) -> X Start=60, task1=240, task2=420, End=600 (end stays rightmost, no overlap). NOT committed.
Impact: build + modify always produce a readable left-to-right diagram with End rightmost and no overlap, regardless of input order. NEXT unchanged (ModifyProcess Phase 2 setElement/params/mappings; Layer-2 item 1 package delivery + item 4 validator; commit).

## 2026-06-16 – ENG-91447 Approach 1: guidance fix — AI should trigger run-on-save via a process signalStart, not a page handler
Context: Second time an AI, asked to "create a process that runs on save of UsrTestRunButton_FormPage and shows a task", planned a client crt.SaveRecordRequest page handler instead of a Signal start element. The capability exists (signalStart) but the MCP GUIDANCE didn't tell the AI to use it; the page-handlers guidance actively teaches a "run logic after save" handler. So this is a knowledge/template gap, not a code gap.
Change (clio MCP guidance resources — compiled into clio.dll, so the clio MCP server / Claude must restart to serve the new text):
- ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs: added a prominent section "Trigger a process on a record event (run on save)" — it is a PROCESS trigger not page logic; make the process START with a signalStart element via create-business-process (signal:{entity, on:added|modified|deleted}, single event — no combined); convert an existing process via modify-business-process (removeElement start + addElement signalStart + addFlow). Explicit anti-pattern: do NOT add a crt.SaveRecordRequest/page handler to launch a process on save.
- PageSchemaHandlersGuidanceResource.cs: redirect note before the save-handler template — if the goal is to run a business process on save, use a process Signal start, not this handler.
Build: clio net8.0 + net10.0 0-err. NOT committed. Needs Claude/clio-MCP restart to take effect for the AI.
Known staleness flagged (separate follow-up, NOT fixed): the process-modeling guidance "Supported slice"/"Build recipe" still describe the approach-3 process-add-element (CDP) driver — not present on this approach-1 branch; should be re-aligned to the backend create/modify/describe/list-user-tasks tools so the AI's build recipe matches the actual tools.
Impact: once the MCP restarts, an AI reading the guidance for "run process on save" is steered to a signalStart element (the correct, platform-native trigger) instead of a fragile page handler. NEXT unchanged (ModifyProcess Phase 2; Layer-2 item 1 + 4; align the rest of the process-modeling guidance to the backend tools; commit).

## 2026-06-16 – ENG-91447 Approach 1: align process-modeling guidance to the backend tools (drop the CDP/process-add-element framing)
Context: The process-modeling guidance still described the approach-3 process-add-element (CDP) driver — not present on this approach-1 branch — so an AI's build recipe pointed at a non-existent tool. Aligned it to the backend tools.
Change (ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs Text): replaced "Determinism contract"/"Supported slice"/"Build recipe" CDP content (.djs-validate-outline, add.serviceTask, "what clio can DRIVE", "Simple/Signal/Timer start" slice) with: "How clio builds processes" (no LLM; package owns metadata; declarative; auto-layout; tool list list-user-tasks/create-business-process/modify-business-process/describe-process/validate-process-graph), "What you can build today" (startEvent/signalStart/endEvent/userTask + flows + parameters + mappings; gateways/conditional/timer/message/intermediate/sub-process/read-data-filters NOT yet), a "Descriptor (create-business-process)" JSON example, a new build recipe (translate -> validate-process-graph -> list-user-tasks -> create-business-process -> describe-process; modify-business-process to edit; FSD-not-runnable caveat), descriptor-based "Parameters / mapping / formulas" (parameters[]/mappings[] with processParameter|value|expression). Kept R1-R17 + quick can/can't + the run-on-save signalStart section. Element catalog kept but re-labelled as the data-id vocabulary for validate-process-graph/planning, with the type/userTaskName build mapping. Updated class summary + [Description].
Tests (ProcessModelingGuidanceResourceTests.cs): re-pinned 3 assertions to backend text (validate-process-graph + create-business-process; signalStart + Read data + "NOT yet buildable"; create-business-process instead of add.serviceTask) and added GetGuide_ShouldSteerRecordTriggerToSignalStart (signalStart + crt.SaveRecordRequest warning). Determinism + catalog (readDataUserTask) + R1/R17 assertions unchanged and still pass.
Build/verify: clio net8.0 + net10.0 0-err; guidance tests 7 passed; McpServer namespace 1196 passed. clio net10.0 (the MCP server build) is current. NOT committed. Needs Claude/clio-MCP restart for the AI to read the aligned guidance.
Impact: the process-modeling guide now matches the actual approach-1 backend tools end-to-end (build recipe + descriptor + run-on-save), so an AI builds with create-business-process / signalStart instead of reaching for a non-existent CDP driver or a page save handler. NEXT: ModifyProcess Phase 2; Layer-2 item 1 (package delivery) + item 4 (validator); commit.

## 2026-06-15 – ENG-91314 add-package-dependency: PR #698 review fixes
Context: drive-pr-green on PR #698. Sonar 0, CI green, but 3 unresolved Major threads from b-horodyskyi.
Decision:
- Load-path NRE: PackageDependencyManager.LoadPackageProperties no longer delegates to BasePackageOperation.ThrowsErrorIfUnsuccessfulResponseReceived (which dereferences response.ErrorInfo.Message). It now guards Success locally and falls back to `Could not read properties of package "<name>".` when ErrorInfo is null — mirrors the save path's BuildSaveErrorMessage guard.
- Command test fixture now resolves AddPackageDependencyCommand from the DI Container (registers IPackageDependencyManager substitute in AdditionalRegistrations, Logger set post-resolve, ClearReceivedCalls in teardown) instead of `new` — exercises the real BindingsModule wiring (IPackageDependencyManager is auto-bound via RegisterAssemblyInterfaceTypes).
- Manager tests now capture the GetPackageProperties request body via Arg.Do<string> and assert it equals JsonConvert.SerializeObject(targetUId) (bare quoted GUID), pinning the reverse-engineered wire contract; added load-failure tests for both null and non-null ErrorInfo.
Discovery: IPackageDependencyManager is not explicitly registered to its interface — it relies on BindingsModule.RegisterAssemblyInterfaceTypes (auto-binds Clio.* I-prefixed interfaces). additionalRegistrations runs after it (line 622 > 99) so test substitutes win.
Files: clio/Package/PackageDependencyManager.cs, clio.tests/Package/PackageDependencyManager.Tests.cs, clio.tests/Command/AddPackageDependencyCommand.Tests.cs
Impact: command no longer surfaces a bare NRE on a failed/permission-denied GetPackageProperties; tests now guard both the DI registration and the GET wire shape. docs/MCP reviewed, no update required (command contract, options, MCP tool args unchanged). Targeted: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&(Module=Package|Module=Command)" → 1545 passed, 0 failed.

## 2026-06-12 19:05 – PR #699 branch refresh, version bump, and test build
Context: User requested refreshing GitHub PR #699 (`feature/ENG-91317_detect-external-schema-changes`), bumping the local build version, and producing a testable build artifact.
Decision: Fetched the PR head as local branch `pr-699`, merged `origin/master` into it, resolved the only merge conflict by keeping both `.codex/workspace-diary.md` entries, then bumped `clio/clio.csproj` fallback `AssemblyVersion` from `8.1.0.57` to `8.1.0.58` and ran a Release build.
Discovery: The merge touched master changes in `Directory.Packages.props`, `BindingsModule`, `CreatioClientAdapter`, `ReauthExecutor`, and `ReauthExecutorTests`, but the only conflict was the diary. `dotnet build clio/clio.csproj -c Release --no-incremental` succeeded and produced `clio/bin/Release/clio.8.1.0.58.nupkg`; build still reports the pre-existing CS0168 warning in `clio/Package/PackageDownloader.cs:93` for both `net8.0` and `net10.0`.
Files: .codex/workspace-diary.md, clio/clio.csproj
Impact: PR #699 is now based on current `master`, has the next fallback version for testing, and has a fresh Release package ready for installation or manual validation.

## 2026-06-12 19:40 – PR #699 Sonar cleanup before push
Context: Drive PR #699 to green required fixing the 12 SonarCloud new-code issues that remained despite a passing quality gate.
Decision: Refactored page MCP helpers without changing behavior: collapsed PageBaselineStore refresh arguments into a baseline object, extracted baseline-building and pre-validation helpers from PageGetTool/PageUpdateTool/PageSyncTool to reduce complexity and nested ternaries, and replaced repeated schema metadata literals in PageSchemaMetadataHelper and PageUpdateCommand with constants.
