## 2026-06-16 – ENG-89424 telemetry: ship default production endpoint + operator opt-out (Option A)
Context: "when clio is updated on a machine where it already exists, how do we ensure it gets a default telemetry endpoint." Investigated: endpoint was resolved ONLY from CLIO_TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT or settings telemetry.endpoint (TelemetryFlushOptionsProvider.Resolve); ADR decision 4 said "no default endpoint ships"; appsettings.json is neither shipped nor seeded (persists across `dotnet tool update`), and SettingsBootstrapService stamps SettingsVersion on fresh-install so a migration-only seed would MISS new installs. Chose Option A: bake the prod endpoint into the binary as the lowest-precedence default (covers fresh + in-place-update uniformly; endpoint relocation = normal release; env/settings still override).
Decision: TelemetryFlushOptionsProvider new DefaultEndpoint const = https://caadt-telemetry.creatio.com/v1/logs (user-supplied prod host); precedence now env > settings.endpoint > DefaultEndpoint (FirstNonEmpty made params). Operator opt-out IsUploadingDisabled(): CLIO_TELEMETRY_ENABLED (bool, wins) over new TelemetrySettings.Enabled (bool?) — false => Resolve returns (null,null) => reuses flusher "endpoint-not-configured" skip; env=true re-enables over settings=false. Invalid configured endpoint still disables (does NOT fall back to default). Consent gate unchanged (no upload unless granted). Added Enabled to schema.json.tpl telemetrysettings + fixed stale "empty disables" descriptions. ADR decision 4 rewritten (reverses "no default ships"; documents opt-out + binary-as-delivery rationale). Docs: help/en/mcp-server.txt, docs/commands/mcp-server.md, McpServerInstructions.cs ("Once consent is granted ... uploaded").
Discovery: SendTelemetryToolE2ETests grants consent + sets CLIO_TELEMETRY_HOME but NO endpoint — with a shipped default the flusher would POST to the REAL prod collector from a test run; guarded with CLIO_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false set BEFORE RawMcpSession.Start so the subprocess inherits it. SendTelemetryFlushE2ETests already set an explicit stub endpoint => unaffected. TelemetryFlushServiceTests use a mock ITelemetryFlushOptionsProvider => real DefaultEndpoint never leaks in. MCP tool contracts (send-telemetry/get-telemetry-consent args/validators/flags) UNCHANGED — change is in the background flusher; MCP reviewed, only instruction wording updated; no new MCP E2E tool added (no tool changed); opt-out covered by provider unit tests.
Files: clio/Common/Telemetry/TelemetryFlushOptions.cs, clio/Environment/ConfigurationOptions.cs (TelemetrySettings.Enabled), clio/tpl/jsonschema/schema.json.tpl, clio/Command/McpServer/McpServerInstructions.cs, clio/help/en/mcp-server.txt, clio/docs/commands/mcp-server.md, spec/adr/adr-product-telemetry.md, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/TelemetryFlushOptionsProviderTests.cs (rewritten: hermetic SetUp/TearDown clears 3 CLIO_TELEMETRY_* vars; default-fallback, settings>default, settings opt-out, env opt-out, env-true-over-settings-false, value-pin tests), clio.mcp.e2e/SendTelemetryToolE2ETests.cs.
Impact: existing installs that update clio now upload to the prod collector once consent is granted, no per-machine config; ops can disable via CLIO_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=false or telemetry.enabled:false. Validated: TPL ConvertFrom-Json valid; build 0 errors; dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&Module=McpServer" 1154 passed; full "Category=Unit" 3862 passed/0 failed/20 skipped; clio.mcp.e2e compiles. Prod hostname caadt-telemetry.creatio.com per user; confirm DNS/TLS live before merge.
## 2026-06-15 21:29 – ENG-91583 [Solution E] version-enforcement: machine-readable confirmation flag
Context: Sub-task E of ENG-91134 decomposition (epic ENG-89871). The version head: ComponentInfoResolution already had 3 tiers + prose LatestFallbackWarning, but the agent skipped the prose (same passive-instruction failure as the discovery head). Alex chose to code directly (umbrella ADR is Proposed/uncommitted — BMAD gate waived for this well-specified sub-task).
Decision: Enforce, don't just surface. (1) Added computed `requiresVersionConfirmation: true` on ComponentInfoResponse (derived from ResolvedFrom==latest-fallback, same single-source pattern as VersionWarning) — emitted on every response shape, omitted on environment/environment-superset. (2) AC#3 transient-vs-permanent: new VersionFallbackReason enum on PlatformVersionResolution (None/NoActiveEnvironment/ProbeError/CoreVersionMissing/CoreVersionUnparseable); probe methods now return CoreVersionProbe(Version, TransientError) so a thrown probe → ProbeError (retry-worthy) is distinct from a usable-but-empty shape → CoreVersionMissing. Surfaced as kebab `resolvedFromReason` (latest-fallback only). Hard stop unchanged (AC#4) — reason only classifies. NO auto-retry (left to agent/user). (3) Added the version-resolution + communicate-unknown rule to the ACTIVE PagePrompt.GetPage (AC#2), plus aligned passive surfaces (McpServerInstructions, PageModificationGuidanceResource, tool [Description]). (4) CLI ComponentInfoCommand kept in lockstep (shares CreateDetailResponse + GetFallbackReason).
Discovery: AC#5 references [Solution C]'s labelled eval-harness, which does not exist yet (stories deferred). Resolved per the decomposition's adversarial-review note ("clio.mcp.e2e not in CI → deterministic [Category(Unit)] assertions; agent-comm is non-deterministic"): the labelled "version unknown → must communicate + confirm" case is a deterministic unit test over the response shape (flag + prose), exercised end-to-end via the existing real-MCP-server e2e test. No dependency on C/D code.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/PlatformVersionResolver.cs, ComponentInfoResolution.cs, ComponentInfoTool.cs; clio/Command/ComponentInfoCommand.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/PagePrompt.cs; McpServerInstructions.cs; Resources/PageModificationGuidanceResource.cs; clio/docs/commands/get-component-info.md; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{ComponentInfoToolTests,PlatformVersionResolverTests}.cs; clio.mcp.e2e/ComponentInfoToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: get-component-info now gives AI an enforceable, machine-readable version-unknown gate + a transient/stable reason. Future C/D work can reuse VersionFallbackReason and the resolvedFromReason wire token. Validated: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&(Module=McpServer|Module=Command)" (all green); clio + clio.mcp.e2e compile clean, 0 CLIO diagnostics.

## 2026-06-16 – ENG-91583 PR #706 drive-pr-green: b-horodyskyi review fixes (1 Major + 5 Minor)
Context: PR #706 уже CI-зелений, Sonar 0, але людський рев'ю b-horodyskyi (creatio-code-review Mode 2) — CHANGES_REQUESTED з 1 Major + 5 Minor, плюс гілка DIRTY (master зрушив, конфлікт лише в цьому diary). drive-pr-green закриває все перед re-review.
Decision: виправлено всі 6. (Major) Transient ProbeError кешувався на повний CacheTtl 5хв → мережевий блип пінив середовище в latest-fallback на 5хв, anti-AC#3. Додано `TransientCacheTtl=30s`; `ResolveAsync` гейтить TTL за reason (`Reason==ProbeError ? TransientCacheTtl : CacheTtl`) — лише transient клас отримує короткий TTL, стабільні (success/no-active-env/core-version-missing/unparseable) лишають 5хв. (M1) Додано тест mixed-precedence (`|=`): appInfo throws ⇒ cliogate empty ⇒ Reason==ProbeError (transient виграє над CoreVersionMissing). (M2) `GetFallbackReason` `_ => null` → explicit `None => null` + `_ => throw ArgumentOutOfRangeException` (нове enum-значення = test/runtime failure, не silent null) + enum-guard тест ітерує `Enum.GetValues<VersionFallbackReason>()`. (M3) Дубльований no-flags fallback (ComponentInfoTool:226 + ComponentInfoCommand:165) → shared `ComponentInfoResolution.CreateNoActiveEnvironmentFallback()`. (M4) `--pretty` тепер виносить машинні маркери на WARNING-рядок (`[requiresVersionConfirmation=true; resolvedFromReason=...]`) на latest-fallback → parity з JSON; docs get-component-info.md уточнено. (M5) AC#2 prompt-rule покрито новим `PageVersionConfirmationPromptTests` (assert PagePrompt.GetPage містить requiresVersionConfirmation + request explicit confirmation + resolvedFromReason).
Discovery: TransientCacheTtl 30s (не skip-cache) свідомо — короткий non-zero вікно шилдить down-середовище від probe-storm, але recovery-латентність низька. 2 тести підкріплюють Major: re-probe після 30s (recovered env резолвиться) + stable тримає повний TTL (advance 30s+30s within CacheTtl → cache hit, Received(1)). Wire-контракт MCP незмінний (requiresVersionConfirmation/resolvedFromReason поля вже в PR) → лише внутрішня cache-поведінка + pretty-render → MCP reviewed, no contract update required.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/{PlatformVersionResolver,ComponentInfoResolution,ComponentInfoTool,ComponentInfoPrettyRenderer}.cs; clio/Command/ComponentInfoCommand.cs; clio/docs/commands/get-component-info.md; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{PlatformVersionResolverTests,ComponentInfoToolTests,PageVersionConfirmationPromptTests}.cs
Impact: рев'ю-знахідки закрито; transient probe-error тепер реально retry-able у межах AI-сесії (Major AC#3); майбутні enum-reasons fail-fast; CLI/MCP no-flags fallback byte-identical; human --pretty має parity з JSON. Build clean (0 errors, 0 CLIO); Module=McpServer|Command 2780 passed/26 skipped/0 failed; 6 нових/змінених тестів 6/6.

## 2026-06-16 – PR #707 round 2: address b-horodyskyi CHANGES_REQUESTED + Sonar S125 (ENG-91591)
Context: drive-pr-green on PR #707 (ObjectDisposedException on clean EOF shutdown). Branch was DIRTY (diary conflict, merged origin/master keeping both append-only blocks). Sonar: 1 new S125 on McpServerCommand.cs:20. Human reviewer b-horodyskyi requested changes — Major: the 3 RequestShutdown unit tests cover the guard in isolation only; they stay green if the Execute detach-before-dispose fix is reverted → no automated guard for the user-visible EOF-exits-0 contract.
Decision: Major closed via reviewer's option A — added clio.mcp.e2e/McpServerShutdownE2ETests.cs that spawns the real `clio mcp-server`, closes stdin (EOF), asserts exit 0 + no ObjectDisposedException/"Unhandled exception" on stderr. Chose E2E over the injectable-seam unit refactor (option B): zero production-architecture change on a bug-fix PR, faithful repro, matches MCP AGENTS.md E2E-for-host-changes. S125 was a false positive (legit explanatory comment with code-like `;`/identifiers) → rephrased lines 19-23 as plain prose. Minor fixes: added dual-mitigation comments in finally (unsubscribe=deterministic fix, RequestShutdown catch=defense-in-depth; detaching CancelKeyPress before 10s drain means 2nd Ctrl+C hard-kills, intended); repurposed the near-duplicate disposed-source test to a distinct branch (live + already-cancelled). Declined AggregateException hardening (would mask genuine cancellation-callback faults on the live Ctrl+C path; observed bug is strictly ObjectDisposedException from Cancel-after-dispose).
Discovery: Verify-before-assert paid off — BOTH the detach AND the RequestShutdown ODE-catch independently prevent the EOF crash, so the E2E test only fails when the *contract* breaks (reverting both → exit 134 / SIGABRT from unhandled ObjectDisposedException, confirmed locally). The E2E suite is NOT in CI (manual), so it is a runnable guard, not a CI gate — honest caveat the reviewer already accepted. clio.mcp.e2e EOF repro needs no Creatio env (only ResolveFreshClioProcessPath → clio/bin/Debug/net10.0). ModelContextProtocol McpServer is abstract (McpServerImpl concrete) so option B was technically possible but more invasive.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpServerCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpServerCommandTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpServerShutdownE2ETests.cs
Impact: Build 0 errors; Module=McpServer 1185 passed/1 skip; new EOF E2E passes (731ms) and fails on reverted fix (proven). Sonar S125 rephrase pending server re-analysis after push. Pattern: for host-verb shutdown contracts, a real-binary EOF E2E is the faithful regression guard; isolated-helper unit tests do not lock the user-visible contract.

## 2026-06-16 – CLIO005 false-positive reduction (broadened consumption)
Context: CLIO005 (UnusedDiServiceAnalyzer) fired ~336 raw / ~168 unique on real clio build, mostly *Command/*Tool resolved indirectly via generic dispatch Resolve<T>() (type arg, not GetRequiredService<Concrete>).
Decision: broadened consumption in UnusedDiServiceAnalyzer.cs — non-registration generic invocation type-args + generic object-creation type-args (new Bar<Foo>() → Foo) + typeof(Foo) all now count as consumption. Add* registrations excluded from self-consumption (registration branch returns early; typeof inside Add* skipped via IsTypeOfInsideRegistration). Unbound type params skipped naturally (not INamedTypeSymbol).
Discovery: after fix, unique flagged services 168→71 (raw 336→284; clio multi-targets net8.0+net10.0 so raw doubles, 71 unique on either single or full build). Remaining 71 = 46 MCP *Tool (AddTransient<XTool>() in BindingsModule, instantiated by reflection/[McpServerToolAttribute] scan in McpToolSchemaCatalog — never a type-arg/typeof literal, so legitimately flagged), 11 *Validator, 14 other services. *Command flood gone (now seen via Resolve<T>() in Program.cs).
Files: Clio.Analyzers/UnusedDiServiceAnalyzer.cs, Clio.Analyzers.Tests/UnusedDiServiceAnalyzerTests.cs
Impact: analyzer no longer floods on indirect-generic-dispatch resolution; remaining list is the honest candidate set (reflection-resolved MCP tools dominate — would need [ResolvedDynamically] or a reflection-aware heuristic, deliberately NOT applied here).

## 2026-06-16 – CLIO005 MCP reflection heuristic + triage evidence
Context: CLIO005 flagged 71 services; 46 were MCP tools instantiated by reflection.
Decision: Generalized the ResolvedDynamically attribute check into a name set {ResolvedDynamicallyAttribute, McpServerToolAttribute}; check type-level AND declared-method-level attributes (McpServerTool sits on a tool METHOD, mirroring McpToolSchemaCatalog's DeclaredOnly assembly scan).
Discovery: BindingsModule.RegisterFluentValidators (typeof(IValidator<>) loop) and RegisterAssemblyInterfaceTypes (every Clio I* interface) auto-register interfaces reflectively — the analyzer cannot see these, so every explicit concrete AddTransient<XValidator>/<XAssertion>/<XService>() is REDUNDANT-CONCRETE when the type is consumed only via its interface. 71→25 after the MCP heuristic; the remaining 25 are all redundant-concrete (validators, assertions, interface-backed services) except IisScannerHandler concrete (used via static GetSites + alive via IIisScanner/IExternalLinkHandler).
Files: Clio.Analyzers/UnusedDiServiceAnalyzer.cs, Clio.Analyzers.Tests/UnusedDiServiceAnalyzerTests.cs
Impact: Heuristic suppresses all 46 MCP tools; triage table classifies the residual 25 for a future removal unit. No production removals in this unit.

## 2026-06-16 – PR #707 round 3: post-approval minor hardening (ENG-91591)
Context: drive-pr-green re-run. b-horodyskyi APPROVED (re-review of ae55854), tetiana-moshon (creatio-code-review) + m-dymytrova also APPROVED. Branch had drifted to DIRTY again (sibling CLIO005 PRs merged → diary conflict, kept both blocks). All remaining review items are Minor/non-blocking.
Decision: Reversed my earlier decline of the AggregateException hardening — two reviewers independently flagged it, and the reasoning flips on reflection: an exception escaping a Ctrl+C/ProcessExit handler does not "surface a bug", it CRASHES shutdown, so catching it makes graceful-shutdown consistent. Added `catch (AggregateException)` to RequestShutdown (type-specific, swallow with rationale) + XML-doc sentence + unit test RequestShutdown_ShouldNotThrow_WhenCancellationCallbackThrows (Token.Register throwing callback → Cancel() aggregates → swallowed; IsCancellationRequested still true). Clarity (tetiana #3): comment on `using var cts` warning future refactors not to narrow scope / dispose earlier (detach-before-dispose ordering), and refined the catch(OperationCanceledException) comment to say it is the Ctrl+C/ProcessExit path (EOF returns from RunAsync normally, no throw). Test robustness (tetiana #4): wrapped E2E Act/Assert in try/finally → TryKill so a failed assertion never leaks a child clio process. Finding #1 (CI guards guard-contract not detach-wiring): accepted the E2E-only caveat (reviewer-offered resolution) rather than adding an injectable seam — no production-architecture change on an approved bug-fix PR.
Discovery: Cancel() (parameterless) runs token callbacks synchronously and, with throwOnFirstException=false, aggregates callback exceptions into an AggregateException thrown AFTER all run — so IsCancellationRequested is set even when a callback faults (the new test asserts both). Pushing post-approval risks stale approvals if branch protection dismisses on push; re-request if so.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpServerCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpServerCommandTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpServerShutdownE2ETests.cs
Impact: Build 0 errors; Module=McpServer 1186 passed/1 skip (+1 AggregateException test); EOF E2E still passes (1s). Shutdown route now hardened against both ObjectDisposedException (disposed source) and AggregateException (throwing callback). Pattern: a reviewer-declined point is worth revisiting when a second reviewer raises it AND the original rationale inverts under scrutiny.

## 2026-06-17 14:50 – addParameter empty "Data type": friendly "Text" → ABSTRACT TextDataValueType
Context: clio modify-business-process addParameter created the process parameter but the Creatio visual designer showed "Data type" blank (test-stand, source/FSM stand C:\Projects\Creatio).
Discovery: the type WAS persisted (process metadata had DataValueType for the param) — so it was never a persistence bug. The value was wrong: ProcessDesigner.ResolveDataValueTypeUId did _userConnection.DataValueTypeManager.GetInstanceByName("Text"), and "Text" resolves to the ABSTRACT base TextDataValueTypeUId = 8b3f29bb-ea14-4ce5-a5c5-293a929b6ba2 (see Terrasoft.Core/DataValueType.cs:129). The designer's "Data type" picker only lists CONCRETE types (ShortText 325A73B8-0F47-44A0-8412-7606F78003AC, MediumText, LongText, Integer 6b6b74e2…, Boolean 90b65bf8…, Lookup b295071f…, Guid 23018567…), so the abstract base renders blank.
Decision: in ProcessDesigner, NormalizeParameterTypeName maps "Text"/"String" → "ShortText" (concrete), keep DataValueTypeUId (NOT the DataValueType object — assigning a shared manager instance is non-idiomatic and was an unrelated dead-end). DefaultParameterType stays "Text" but is normalized. Added describe  field (DataValueType?.Name).
Also: clio MCP modify required BOTH processName+processUid (non-nullable string → MCP-required) → omitting one gave generic "An error occurred invoking"; fixed by making them optional (string? = null).
CORRECTION — mistakes I made this session, do NOT repeat: the describe `type` field I first added read parameter.DataValueType?.Name, which is NULL on a read-back schema (only DataValueTypeUId is populated) → the field was always omitted. I then wrongly treated that always-empty field as "proof the old assembly is still loaded" and chased a phantom deploy problem (even tried compile-all, which is wrong for an assembly package). Reality on this stand: a pool restart DOES reload the package assembly — the user's FS-link + restart workflow is correct; the rebuilt assembly was loaded after the restart, and after the user compiled the package the Text→ShortText fix worked (designer showed "Text (50 characters)" = ShortText). Fixed the describe field to resolve the name via _userConnection.DataValueTypeManager.GetInstanceByUId(uid) (Manager.cs:354). The earlier modify-by-name "not found" / by-uid DesignSchema NPE were specific to certain processes' state, NOT a general "cold ProcessSchemaManager" rule. Lesson: verify deployed server behavior by the actual designer/metadata, not by a freshly-added describe field whose own correctness is unproven.
Files: ProcessBuilder packages/clioprocessbuilder/Files/src/cs/ProcessDesigner.cs (NormalizeParameterTypeName, ResolveDataValueTypeName), ProcessDesignContracts.cs, ProcessDesignConstants.cs (DefaultParameterType); clio Command/McpServer/Tools/ModifyBusinessProcessTool.cs
Impact: friendly process-parameter types must map to CONCRETE DataValueTypes (Text→ShortText) or the designer shows blank.

## 2026-06-16 – ENG-91317 reopened: CLI parity for page conflict detection
Context: ENG-91317 returned to In Progress. Kateryna: "Claude works correct, Codex continue rewrite changes." Codex reads via MCP get-page (which writes .clio-pages/{schema}/meta.json baseline) but saves via CLI `clio update-page --body-file …\.clio-pages\{schema}\body.js` WITHOUT --expected-checksum, so PageUpdateCommand.TryCheckForExternalModification short-circuited (PageUpdateOptions.cs:210) and overwrote the designer edit. Confirmed from the rollout jsonl: MCP get-page at 13:00 then CLI update-page at 13:02/13:07/13:20 for UsrPatient_FormPage.
Decision: Reversed ADR Option B's "command stays filesystem-free; CLI users pass --expected-checksum explicitly". Extracted baseline arm/refresh into shared DI service IPageBaselineGuard (clio/Command/PageBaselineGuard.cs) and get-page file output into IPageFileWriter (clio/Command/PageFileWriter.cs); both consumed by CLI verbs (PageUpdateCommand.Execute, PageGetCommand.Execute) AND the three MCP tools — this also de-duplicates the arm/refresh that PageUpdateTool and PageSyncTool each carried. Moved PageBaselineStore + PageOutputDirectoryResolver to namespace Clio.Command (no longer MCP-specific; tools still resolve them via nested namespace). CLI get-page gained kebab-case --output-directory. Backward-compat: an explicit --expected-checksum disarms on-disk arming (guard returns not-armed) so it is never overwritten.
Discovery: (1) PageGetTool.BuildBaseline returns null ONLY when response.Editable is null (FR-10 failed checksum capture); when Editable exists with EditableSchemaExists=false it still writes an absent-schema baseline (so update-page can detect external creation) — my first writer test got this wrong. (2) Test fixtures construct the tools target-typed (`Tool tool = new(...)`), so a `new PageXTool(` grep misses them — 48 ctor sites across PageToolsTests/PageSyncToolTests/PageUpdateToolRunProcessTests needed the new service args (fixed with a scripted regex pass). (3) sync-pages has no CLI verb (MCP-only) → out of CLI scope. (4) CLI update-page with no on-disk baseline arms nothing → zero extra SysSchema queries (no perf regression for plain CLI users).
Files: clio/Command/PageBaselineGuard.cs (new), clio/Command/PageFileWriter.cs (new), clio/Command/PageUpdateOptions.cs, clio/Command/PageGetOptions.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/{PageUpdateTool,PageSyncTool,PageGetTool,PageBaselineStore,PageOutputDirectoryResolver}.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio.tests/Command/{PageBaselineGuardTests,PageFileWriterTests,PageUpdateCommandBaselineTests,PageGetCommandFileWriterTests}.cs (new) + PageToolsTests/PageSyncToolTests/PageUpdateToolRunProcessTests/PageUpdateToolBaselineTests/PageSyncToolBaselineTests/PageOutputDirectoryResolverTests fixtures, clio/help/en/{update-page,get-page}.txt, clio/docs/commands/{update-page,get-page}.md, spec/adr/adr-detect-external-schema-changes.md (addendum), spec/sprint-status.yaml, spec/stories/story-detect-external-schema-changes-6.md (new).
Impact: One baseline-orchestration implementation now serves CLI + MCP (FR-11 extended to the CLI); AI-agent CLI flows (Codex) get the same protection as MCP flows. clio build 0/0 (no new CLIO*), clio.tests build 0/0; new unit tests 20/20 green; full Unit suite 4026 passed / 0 failed. Validated: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit". MCP external contract unchanged → existing clio.mcp.e2e page coverage still applies (not re-run; not in CI). Still pending: live verification on a dev stand.

## 2026-06-16 - Harden [RequiresPackage] gate (malformed version, CLI seam, dead dep)
Context: Code-review follow-ups on feature/requires-package-attribute after MCP critical fixed.
Decision: I1 - IsCompatible uses Version.TryParse (faithful to old new Version(); not PackageVersion.TryParseVersion which would silently accept "-beta" suffix); malformed non-empty version throws PackageRequirementException naming pkg+bad string. I2(b) - graceful catch-all lives in Program.TryGetPackageRequirementError (the seam existing tests hit), PRE-first ordering preserved, non-PRE -> GetReadableMessageException(IsDebugMode)+return true (caller maps to exit 1). Feature-toggle gate (TryGetDisabledFeatureName) untouched.
Discovery: Dispatch lambda ExecuteCommandWithOption is NOT unit-tested at exit-code level for either gate; both gates covered only at helper level - matched that. SqlScriptCommand.Execute fully overrides base, never reads ClioGateWay; IClioGateway still consumed by GetCreatioInfoCommand -> no CLIO005.
Files: clio/Common/RequiredPackageChecker.cs, clio/Program.cs, clio/Command/SqlScriptCommand.cs, clio.tests/Common/RequiredPackageCheckerTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/ProgramPackageRequirementGateTests.cs
Impact: [RequiresPackage] gate now fail-closed on malformed versions and unreachable environments at both CLI and MCP seams.

## 2026-06-16 – [RequiresPackage] attribute replaces inline cliogate checks
Context: Generalize the cliogate-only version gate (inline `IClioGateway.CheckCompatibleVersion` + implicit `ServicePath.Contains("rest/CreatioApiGateway")` trigger + `RemoteCommand.ClioGateMinVersion`) into a package-agnostic declarative attribute, ahead of a second required package.
Decision: New `[RequiresPackage("name","version",Hint=...)]` on the OPTIONS class (mirrors `[FeatureToggle]`). Enforced at the two dispatch chokepoints — `Program.ExecuteCommandWithOption` (CLI + scenario runner) and MCP `BaseTool.InternalExecute<TCommand>` — NOT in a base-class hook (the 4 target commands override Execute / aren't RemoteCommand, so a `RemoteCommand.Execute` hook would never fire). `IClioGateway` kept as the queryable API for graceful/conditional callers (get-info, ApplicationDownloader) and refactored to delegate to the new `IRequiredPackageChecker`.
Discovery: MCP enforcement MUST resolve `IRequiredPackageChecker` from the per-call env-keyed container (`ToolCommandResolver.Resolve<T>(options)`), NOT via constructor injection — a ctor-injected checker binds to the MCP startup/bootstrap container (placeholder http://localhost) and validates the WRONG environment; and an optional ctor param `=null` is never forwarded by derived tools, silently disabling the gate (same false-confidence class as the old `WithTools(Type[])` bug). `EnsureRequirements` early-returns with NO `GetPackages()` HTTP call when the type has no attribute (zero-cost path). Use a static `RequiresPackageAttribute.IsDefinedOn(Type)` pre-check before resolving the env checker so ungated tools touch no environment.
Files: clio/Common/RequiresPackageAttribute.cs, clio/Common/RequiredPackageChecker.cs, clio/Common/PackageRequirementException.cs, clio/Program.cs (TryGetPackageRequirementError), clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/BaseTool.cs (ResolveFromCallContainer<T>), clio/Common/ClioGateway.cs, clio/Command/RemoteCommand.cs, Lock/Unlock/RestoreWorkspace/SqlScript/ShowPackageFileContent + GetCreatioInfo commands.
Impact: To require any Creatio package for a command, decorate its options class — no inline plumbing. Graceful-degradation commands (get-info) stay imperative on IClioGateway; the attribute is hard-fail only.

## 2026-06-16 00:00 – [RequiresPackage] property-level (bool-true) triggers
Context: Extend class-only [RequiresPackage] to gate on a bool option flag (required only when true) and apply to the conditional cliogate commands.
Decision: AttributeUsage now Class|Property. `IRequiredPackageChecker.EnsureRequirements(Type)` → `EnsureRequirements(object instance)` (needs the value). Collect-then-enforce: class attrs always + property attrs where the bool value is true; if triggered list empty, return before any GetPackages() (zero-cost preserved). Non-bool decorated property → InvalidOperationException naming the property (fail-fast during the metadata scan, no HTTP). Read value off the CANONICAL property only (Async / UseFeatureWebService), never the hidden alias.
Discovery: `RequiresPackageAttribute.IsDefinedOn(Type)` had to also scan properties — a property-only decorated type was otherwise silently skipped by the MCP pre-check in BaseTool.EnforcePackageRequirements (same silent-no-op class as the WithTools(Type[]) bug). Guarded by IsDefinedOn_ShouldReturnTrue_WhenOnlyAPropertyIsDecorated. NSubstitute landmine: after the signature change, `typeof(X)` / `Arg.Any<Type>()` still compile as `object` but match a Type, so stubs silently miss — switched matchers to the instance / `Arg.Any<object>()`.
Applied: PullPkgOptions.Async (pull-pkg --async → cliogate PackagesGateway), FeatureOptions.UseFeatureWebService (set-feature --use-feature-web-service → FeatureStateService), ListenOptions class-level (listen → ATFLogService). All presence-only. Default paths (core GetZipPackages / ATF.Repository FeatureService) stay usable without cliogate.
Files: clio/Common/RequiresPackageAttribute.cs, clio/Common/RequiredPackageChecker.cs, clio/Program.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/BaseTool.cs, clio/Command/PackageCommand/DownloadPackageCommand.cs, clio/Command/FeatureCommand.cs, clio/Command/ListenCommand.cs, clio.tests/Common/RequiredPackageCheckerTests.cs, clio.tests/Common/RequiresPackageAttributeTests.cs (new), clio.tests/Command/ProgramPackageRequirementGateTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/BaseToolTests.cs
Impact: New user-facing refusal — `pull-pkg --async` / `set-feature --use-feature-web-service` / `listen` now fail early with an install-gate hint when cliogate is absent. No MCP tool exists for these three (gate is automatic via BaseTool if one is added later). No CLI arg/help change.

## 2026-06-16 – [RequiresPackage] part 2: full cliogate ServiceContract audit + property-level gating
Context: Review revealed the first pass only covered the CreatioApiGateway route. The cliogate package exposes FOUR [ServiceContract] classes (cliogate/Files/cs): CreatioApiGateway, PackagesGateway, FeatureStateService, ATFLogService — each at /rest/<ClassName>/. Commands hitting any of them need cliogate.
Decision: Extended [RequiresPackage] to bool PROPERTY-level (required only when flag is true): EnsureRequirements(Type)->(object instance); class attr = always, bool prop attr = when-true; non-bool decorated prop throws fast. Gated: listen (class, ATFLogService unconditional), push-workspace (class 2.0.0.0, WorkspaceInstaller.Install ResetSchemaChangeState unconditional), pull-pkg (prop Async, PackagesGateway only on --async), set-feature (prop UseFeatureWebService, FeatureStateService only on --use-feature-web-service deprecated). Decorate the CANONICAL property, not the hidden kebab/camel alias.
Discovery: Audit's "unconditional" claims must be VERIFIED — pull-pkg default path uses core PackageInstallerService (only --async hits cliogate); publish-app (alias publish-workspace) uses WorkspaceInstaller.PublishToFolder where the reset call is COMMENTED OUT (:212) so it is NOT cliogate-dependent — left ungated. Deliberately NOT gated (graceful/conditional, gating would regress): the SetFileDesignMode family (pkg-to-db/-to-file-system/set-fsm-config/turn-fsm/create-user-task/modify-user-task-parameters/link-from-repo all 404-degrade), download-configuration/add-item (inverse/combination), get-info (graceful). LANDMINE fixed: RequiresPackageAttribute.IsDefinedOn(Type) must scan PROPERTIES too, else a property-only-decorated command silently bypasses the MCP pre-check.
Files: clio/Common/RequiresPackageAttribute.cs, clio/Common/RequiredPackageChecker.cs, clio/Command/{ListenCommand,PushWorkspaceCommand,FeatureCommand,PackageCommand/DownloadPackageCommand}.cs, clio/Program.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/BaseTool.cs, docs help/en + docs/commands for listen/pull-pkg/set-feature/push-workspace.
Impact: To gate a command on a package conditionally, put [RequiresPackage] on the bool option that triggers the remote call. Always verify the default (flag-off) path is package-free before gating, and never gate a command that already degrades gracefully.

## 2026-06-17 – Docs-cache freshness: sync-revalidate stale component docs (ENG-91135 reopen)
Context: ENG-91135 reopened — an agent repairing an already-added crt.QuickFilter acted on a STALE cached component doc. clio docs-cache used stale-while-revalidate, so get-component-info served the pre-fix `.component.md` instantly (cached 9-11 Jun, before the doc fix landed on the CDN) while the background refresh arrived too late; the agent never saw §2.2 and tried wrong-path / wire-onto-DataTable repairs. Session export confirmed: CDN `latest` already had the fixed doc — the failure was clio-side cache delivery + the doc lacking a repair (not just add) recipe.
Decision: For DOCS only (not the registry payload), `ComponentRegistryDocsClient.GetDocAsync` now revalidates a stale hit SYNCHRONOUSLY against the CDN, bounded by `StaleRevalidateBudget = 5s`, and serves the stale bytes only as a fallback (stale-if-error). Removed the docs background refresh (`ScheduleBackgroundRefresh` / `_inFlightRefreshes`). `ComponentRegistryClient` (registry payload) intentionally keeps stale-while-revalidate — Alex scoped this to docs.
Discovery: the docs background refresh had no `DrainAsync` hook (unlike the registry's `PendingRefreshTasks`), so deleting it is safe. Budget cancellation relies on `TryFetchOnceAsync`'s `when (!cancellationToken.IsCancellationRequested)` guard: a budget-CTS timeout surfaces as `OperationCanceledException` and is caught in `TryRevalidateWithinBudgetAsync` (distinguished from a real caller-cancel) → returns null → stale fallback. A 4xx revalidation is permanent (no retry) → immediate stale fallback. Cold-cache miss keeps the unbounded fetch (no stale copy to fall back to).
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ComponentRegistryDocsClient.cs, ComponentRegistryDocsCacheStore.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/AGENTS.md, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ComponentRegistryDocsClientTests.cs. (The repair guidance — §2.3 — lives in creatio-ui `quick-filter.component.md`; CDN publish is the nightly Jenkins generator job, see memory `cdn-component-registry-nightly-deploy`.)
Impact: get-component-info now trades a few seconds on a stale doc for the CURRENT guide; an outdated cached doc no longer silently misleads agents. MCP tool contract unchanged (MCP reviewed, no update required). To extend the same policy to the registry payload, mirror the bounded revalidate in `ComponentRegistryClient.GetAsync`.

## 2026-06-17 – ENG-91317 PR #723 review follow-up (m-dymytrova) + master merge
Context: PR #723 was red on two counts — CONFLICTING/DIRTY (5 behind master) and CHANGES_REQUESTED. Merged origin/master (only conflict: append-only .codex/workspace-diary.md → kept BOTH blocks; BindingsModule auto-merged). m-dymytrova left 2 inline threads + 3 non-blocking top-level follow-ups.
Decision: (1 — bug) PageGetCommand.Execute now treats the .clio-pages baseline write as best-effort: a write failure (locked body.js / read-only dir) logs a warning and keeps the fetched page + exit 0 instead of discarding a successful read and exiting 1 (new test Execute_ShouldKeepFetchedPageAndExitZero_WhenBaselineWriteFails). (3a — doc) update-page.md notes the fail-safe stale-baseline edge when --expected-checksum is mixed with an auto-armed baseline. (3c — doc) Commands.md left unchanged: its get-page entry is a one-line index description; --output-directory already documented in docs/commands/get-page.md + help/en/get-page.txt. (3b) MCP e2e page coverage not in CI — acknowledged in reply.
Discovery: (2 — suggestion to make IPageBaselineGuard / IPageFileWriter required ctor params, not `= null`) DEFERRED to Alex: the reviewer's premise "test fixtures already pass real instances" is incomplete — several MCP-path unit fixtures (PageSyncToolTests CreateSuccessful/Failing*Command, PageToolsTests) deliberately construct the commands with the 4-arg overload and no guard/writer, because the MCP tools orchestrate the baseline themselves rather than via the CLI auto-arm. Making the params required forces a ctor reorder (required-before-optional) + updating ~6-10 positional fixtures; structural, so left for human confirmation per autonomous-run guardrails.
Files: clio/Command/PageGetOptions.cs, clio.tests/Command/PageGetCommandFileWriterTests.cs, clio/docs/commands/update-page.md
Impact: get-page is now resilient to baseline-write failures (read no longer fails on a write problem); docs capture the --expected-checksum/auto-arm interplay.

## 2026-06-17 – ENG-91317 PR #723 review follow-up (tetiana-moshon) — explicit --expected-checksum stale baseline
Context: tetiana-moshon Major thread on PageBaselineGuard.cs:64 — an explicit `--expected-checksum` made TryArm return `(null,false)`, so PageUpdateCommand.Execute skipped RefreshOrDrop and `.clio-pages/{schema}/meta.json` kept the PRE-save checksum. The next unpinned `update-page` auto-armed from that stale baseline and raised a false `checksum-mismatch` conflict against a value the user's own prior save had already superseded (regresses AC6).
Decision: TryArm no longer short-circuits on a pinned checksum. It now always resolves metaFilePath + reads the on-disk baseline. When a baseline matches the environment AND a checksum was pinned, it reports `(metaFilePath, true)` WITHOUT overwriting options.ExpectedChecksum — so the explicit checksum still wins the comparison, but RefreshOrDrop moves the on-disk baseline forward to response.NewChecksum after a successful non-dry-run save. No matching baseline (pinned) → still `(_, false)`, nothing to refresh. Execute's `baselineArmed && success && !DryRun → RefreshOrDrop` is unchanged.
Discovery: The behavior change is CLI-only — MCP callers (PageUpdateTool/PageSyncTool) always have ExpectedChecksum==null at TryArm, so callerPinnedChecksum is false and the path is identical to before. No MCP/doc change required. Updated the existing TryArm_ShouldRespectExplicitChecksum test to assert armed=true + untouched checksum, added TryArm_ShouldNotArm_WhenExplicitChecksumSetAndNoBaseline and an end-to-end TryArm→RefreshOrDrop test (pin checksum, save, assert meta.json == post-save checksum) per the reviewer's requested coverage.
Files: clio/Command/PageBaselineGuard.cs, clio.tests/Command/PageBaselineGuardTests.cs
Impact: Mixing pinned/unpinned page saves against the same `.clio-pages` baseline dir no longer leaves the on-disk baseline pointing at an overwritten checksum, so a legitimate follow-up save is not blocked by a false conflict.

## 2026-06-17 – ENG-91317 PR #723: conflict-detection services now required ctor deps (m-dymytrova)
Context: m-dymytrova's 🟡 thread asked to drop the `= null` defaults on the two data-loss-guard deps — `IPageBaselineGuard pageBaselineGuard` (PageUpdateCommand) and `IPageFileWriter pageFileWriter` (PageGetCommand). With `= null`, a broken/removed DI registration silently reverts to overwrite-without-checking (this ticket reopens invisibly). Alex confirmed: make them required now.
Decision: Both are now required ctor params. PageUpdateCommand reorders the guard ahead of the optional hierarchyClient/presenceNotifier (required-before-optional); PageGetCommand keeps pageFileWriter last (already trailing). Removed the now-dead `is not null` guards at the call sites (PageUpdateOptions Execute always arms via TryArm; PageGetOptions always writes on success). Updated ctor XML docs to state the fail-loud rationale.
Discovery: ~45 positional test fixtures broke — most are MCP-path `PageUpdateCommand X = new(client, urlBuilder, logger[, hierarchyClient])` target-typed `new(...)` (grep for `new PageUpdateCommand(` alone misses these). Fix: inject an inert `Substitute.For<IPageBaselineGuard>()` as the new 4th arg (guard only touched in CLI Execute, never in MCP TryUpdatePage, so substitute default `(null,false)` preserves prior behavior). PageGetCommand fixtures need a PASSTHROUGH writer (`Substitute.For<IPageFileWriter>()` returning its input PageGetResponse) — a bare substitute returns null and NPEs on the success write path; added a `CreatePassthroughPageFileWriter()` helper in the 3 affected get-fixtures.
Files: clio/Command/PageUpdateOptions.cs, clio/Command/PageGetOptions.cs, clio.tests/Command/{PageUpdateCommandBaselineTests,PageUpdateCommandConflictTests,PageUpdateCommandDesignerPresenceTests}.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{PageToolsTests,PageSyncToolTests,PageSyncToolBaselineTests,PageUpdateToolBaselineTests}.cs
Impact: A future wiring regression on the conflict-detection services now fails loudly at DI resolve time instead of silently disabling the data-loss guard. Validated: clio + clio.tests build 0/0; dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&(Module=Command|Module=McpServer)" → 2892 passed/0 failed/26 skip.

## 2026-06-17 – ENG-91317 PR #723 review follow-up (tetiana-moshon, 5-lens) — path-traversal hardening
Context: New reviewer tetiana-moshon CHANGES_REQUESTED with 1 Major + 5 Minor. Major (explicit --expected-checksum stale baseline) already fixed in 703bd2a5; Minor 4 (optional-nullable services) fixed in ba4c4b19. Addressing Minor 3 (security): PageFileWriter.WritePageFiles does `Directory.Delete(Path.Combine(rootDir, schemaName), recursive:true)` with no charset/containment check on schemaName — a defense-in-depth gap in a destructive shared service (repo mandates containment elsewhere, e.g. ComponentRegistryDocsPath).
Decision: Validate schemaName against `^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$` (compiled regex) at the top of WritePageFiles → fail-fast PageGetResponse before any FS mutation; PLUS a belt-and-suspenders GetFullPath containment assertion that the resolved schemaDir stays under `.clio-pages/`. Added a 6-case `[TestCase]` (../escape, a/b, a\b, .., "with space", "") asserting Success=false + no files written. Minors 2 (no get-page write opt-out) and 5 (asymmetric heavy-payload stripping) judged intentional design — answered with rationale in the PR reply, not changed. Minor 6 (CLI conflict round-trip coverage) already satisfied by Execute_ShouldReturnFailure_WhenArmedChecksumDiffers (exit 1 + no SaveSchema) + PageBaselineGuardTests + PageFileWriterTests.
Files: clio/Command/PageFileWriter.cs, clio.tests/Command/PageFileWriterTests.cs
Impact: A path-separator or `..` in a future non-platform schema name can no longer make the recursive delete escape the workspace. Validated: build 0/0 (no new warnings in page files); dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&Module=Command" → 1685 passed/0 failed/25 skip.
Follow-up (same day): the first cut ALSO added a GetFullPath containment assertion using `_fileSystem.Path.DirectorySeparatorChar`. That broke `PageGetTool_WhenDirectoryCreationFails_ReturnsError` — its bare `Substitute.For<IFileSystem>()` returns `'\0'` for the unstubbed DirectorySeparatorChar, so `StartsWith(fullRoot + '\0')` was always false and the guard fired before the test's CreateDirectory-throw path. Removed the containment assertion: the `^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$` regex is already airtight against traversal (a matching name has no separator/`..`/drive marker), so the assertion was redundant defense that only added a stubbed-fs false-negative. LESSON: production code that consumes `IFileSystem.Path.*` beyond Combine/GetFullPath can be falsified by partial NSubstitute fs mocks — prefer charset validation over separator-arithmetic for containment.

## 2026-06-17 – ENG-89424 telemetry: event_name single source of truth (clio + edge collector)
Context: The event name was stored 3x per event (OTLP body, attributes["event_name"], and the dedicated event_name field) and persisted into 3 ClickHouse columns (Body, LogAttributes['event_name'], EventName). User asked to collapse to a single source on the dedicated OTLP LogRecord event_name field (proto field 12), aligned across both repos.
Decision: clio emits the name ONLY on the dedicated field. TelemetryService.BuildLogEvent drops the event_name attribute and the body; OpenTelemetryLogEvent replaces its body field with a dedicated EventName field (and OtlpLogRecord drops its body field too); SchemaVersion deliberately kept at 1 (pre-release - no old-shape rows exist to discriminate, so the relocation ships as the initial shape). The flusher maps the dedicated EventName straight to the OTLP event_name field - no body emitted, no body/attribute fallback (pre-release, so no old-shape spool files exist; EventNameFor deleted). Edge collector (values-local/-rnd.yaml): filter keys on bare `event_name` (OTTL ottllog log context), event_name removed from the attribute keep-list, and body normalization set(log.body, attributes["event_name"]) -> set(log.body, "") (still mitigates threat I-01 oversized body). ClickHouse schema unchanged (exporter maps r.EventName() -> EventName column).
Discovery: OTTL `log.event_name` is a supported path at collector-contrib v0.144.0 (ottllog README at the tag) and the clickhouseexporter writes EventName from r.EventName() (exporter_logs.go) — both prerequisites for dropping the attribute. filterprocessor log_record uses BARE field names (so `event_name`, not `log.event_name`). The two ends MUST move together: drop the attribute client-side without moving the collector filter and every event is dropped server-side (event_name==nil). The local probe suite (deploy-caadt-local.ps1 TEST 1/2/3) is the verification gate for the bare-path filter and now asserts the EventName column + empty Body.
Files: clio/Common/Telemetry/{TelemetryModels,TelemetryService,TelemetryFlushService,OtlpModels}.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{SendTelemetryToolTests,TelemetryFlushServiceTests}.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/{SendTelemetryToolE2ETests,SendTelemetryFlushE2ETests}.cs, spec/adr/adr-product-telemetry.md (decision 9); metrics-installation/helm/caadt-telemetry/{values-local,values-rnd}.yaml + deploy-caadt-local.ps1 + README.md. MCP surface reviewed, no change (event_name stays a send-telemetry request input; the wire shape is internal). Validated: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&Module=McpServer" 1266 passed; clio.mcp.e2e compiles.
Impact: event_name now lives in exactly one ClickHouse column (EventName). Any future query/dashboard must read EventName, not Body or LogAttributes['event_name']. Adding a typed top-level OTLP field (vs an attribute) is the pattern: set it in OpenTelemetryLogEvent + BuildOtlpPayload and move the collector filter to the dedicated OTTL path in lockstep.

## 2026-06-16 23:33 – ENG-91691 CreateRecordRequest Add-button footgun in related-list guidance
Context: CAADT Sub-bug. An agent built the canonical detail composite (ExpansionPanel + DataGrid) and satisfied "Add button in the panel header" with a header `tools` button wired to `crt.CreateRecordRequest` for a standalone detail entity (UsrCP9RetryItem — created via create-entity-schema + create-page, NO section). Clicking it throws the runtime toast "There is no page for new or existing record" (console `_openEntityPage` -> `_showEntityNavigationError`) because CreateRecordRequest resolves the entity's REGISTERED navigation/edit page, which a section-less entity does not have. Root cause = misleading clio MCP guidance, not platform/CAADT code (same class as ENG-91134: weak/passive guidance steers the agent wrong).
Decision: Fix the two guidance articles the agent reads (Alex chose "inline-add + warning"). RelatedListGuidanceResource: replaced the buried "Editable vs read-only" bullet with an explicit "Adding records to the detail" section — INLINE grid add (features.editable.enable + itemsCreation) is the DEFAULT add affordance (new row inherits master FK via dependencies, needs NO page/navigation, works for ANY child incl. section-less), a header CreateRecordRequest button is a FOOTGUN, and if a header button is truly needed pass explicit entityPageName or register a section page + seed params.defaultValues; clarified the "+ New" add-button bullet; added a Common-mistakes entry. PageSchemaHandlersGuidanceResource: added a "crt.CreateRecordRequest page-resolution note" after the direct-request decision table (registered-page requirement + entityPageName escape + inline-add cross-ref).
Discovery: McpGuidanceResource content tests do EXACT-substring assertions, so a guidance phrase that WRAPS across a raw-string line is NOT a contiguous substring — my first run failed because "There is no page for new or existing record" was split "...new or\n         existing..."; the fix was to keep the whole quoted error on one (longer) line. Pattern for any future guidance test assertion: keep the asserted phrase unbroken on a single source line. Tool contracts (get-guidance/get-component-info) unchanged → docs (mcp-server.md/update-page.md/sync-pages.md/help) reference only the article NAMES, so no doc update; MCP reviewed, only article BODY extended + e2e coverage added.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/RelatedListGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/PageSchemaHandlersGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: get-guidance now teaches inline-add-first for detail records and names the exact "There is no page for new or existing record" failure mode + its two escape hatches, so agents stop emitting unclickable header CreateRecordRequest buttons for section-less detail entities. Validated: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&Module=McpServer" → 1209 passed/0 failed/1 skip; clio + clio.tests build 0 errors/0 warnings; clio.mcp.e2e compiles (e2e not in CI). csproj auto AssemblyVersion bump (8.1.0.58→59) reverted — version is owned by the release process.

## 2026-06-17 – ENG-91691 PR #725 review follow-up (m-dymytrova)
Context: Human reviewer m-dymytrova requested changes on PR #725 (2 inline threads + CHANGES_REQUESTED). (1) Inline "+ New" add on a section-less detail would not SAVE following the canonical payload — the FK column was omitted from the grid, so the dependencies relationship had no column to populate and the new row's parent FK stayed empty ("<FK caption> field must be filled in"). (2) The new page-schema-handlers page-resolution note had unit coverage but no e2e assertion (AGENTS.md: e2e required for changed MCP surface).
Decision: (1) RelatedListGuidanceResource — dropped the now-inaccurate "NO extra wiring" phrasing and added a REQUIRED-wiring bullet: the child FK column (the dependencies attributePath) MUST be one of the grid's columns/collection attributes for inline create to inherit the master FK and save (may be hidden; verify child ...Id == master Id via OData). (2) GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.GuidanceGet_Should_Return_Page_Schema_Handlers_Guide — added a Contain("There is no page for new or existing record") assertion mirroring the related-list e2e assertions.
Discovery: Reviewer threads are HUMAN-authored, so replied but did NOT resolve (skill: leave human threads for the human). Re-request review / merge / Jira deferred — autonomous run, needs Alex's confirmation.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/RelatedListGuidanceResource.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Inline-add guidance now states the FK-column requirement so agents stop emitting "+ New" grids whose rows can't save; the page-schema-handlers note is now e2e-asserted.
## 2026-06-16 17:10 – MCP guidance forcing: thin router + tool-channel triggers + 3 creatio-* guides
Context: ADR spec/adr/adr-mcp-guidance-forcing.md (Pt 1-4). Always-on McpServerInstructions overflowed (git HEAD = 9428 chars / 2199 cl100k tokens, truncated past the observed ~1000-token point so long-running/profile-culture/error-handling were lost), and get-guidance was passive (no forcing function).
Decision: Rewrote McpServerInstructions.cs as a thin pure-ASCII router (2249 chars / 542 tokens, -75%) — hard invariants on top (compile-not-required, long-running await, profile-culture, destructive, correlation-id) + a names-only routing table. Pushed forcing onto guaranteed channels (tool [Description]): analytics-clause (get-component-info crt.IndicatorWidget + dashboards + indicator-widget + analytics-widgets) on create-page/update-page/sync-pages/get-page; app-modeling+schema-naming on create/update-entity-schema; business-rules+schema-naming on create-lookup/modify-entity-schema-column; business-rule-filters added to create-entity-business-rule. Deterministic note:"compile-creatio not required" (nullable, JsonIgnore-when-null) on create-page (PageCreateResponse) + update-entity-schema (CommandExecutionResult) success paths. Pt-4: three thin pointer guides (schema-naming, ui-guidelines, analytics-widgets) as Resources/*GuidanceResource.cs registered in GuidanceCatalog (26->29 static); analytics-widgets see-also dashboards/indicator-widget by NAME (no content copy). Doc-sync bullet added to AGENTS.md.
Discovery: (1) resolvedFrom is NOT desynced — ComponentInfoResolution.cs already implements all 3 tiers (environment/environment-superset/latest-fallback) and instructions matched; the ADR pt-3 premise was wrong, no code fix. (2) get-component-info was in NO page-tool [Description] — only inside the indicator-widget guide (a 2-hop dependency behind get-guidance); the analytics-clause now closes that chain on the guaranteed channel. (3) Forcing was already ~80% present (create-page/update-page/sync-pages/get-page/entity tools/execute-esq all had triggers); the real gap was dashboards/indicator-widget (referenced nowhere) + create-lookup/modify-column. (4) Router 542 tokens > original 400 estimate: the 5 verbose must-survive invariants + 3 mandated creatio-* lines (+48 tokens) make 400 unrealistic without thinning invariants; accepted (still -75%, far under truncation); ADR §4.2 updated honestly + test ceiling set to 2400 chars. (5) recommendedGuidance field DEFERRED to iter 2 — body-scan analytics detection is mistimed (create-page body empty; update-page fires post-authoring) and lossy; primary observability = the get-guidance/get-component-info CALLS visible in the client log (zero code). (6) ProfileLanguageGuidanceTests pins exact tokens "ASK the user" + "once per session" (ENG-91044 AC-04/05) — restored verbatim in the router after an initial reword broke them. (7) UpdateEntitySchemaToolTests lacked [Property("Module","McpServer")] so it was invisible to the targeted filter; added it. (8) A stale `clio mcp-server` (PID) held bin DLL locks and blocked the build; stop the running mcp-server before building.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpServerInstructions.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/{SchemaNaming,UiGuidelines,AnalyticsWidgets}GuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/GuidanceCatalog.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/{PageCreateTool,PageUpdateTool,PageSyncTool,PageGetTool,EntitySchemaTool,BusinessRuleTool,CommandExecutionResult}.cs, clio/Command/PageModels.cs, AGENTS.md, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{McpGuidanceForcingTests,UpdateEntitySchemaToolTests}.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs, spec/adr/adr-mcp-guidance-forcing.md.
Impact: Always-on token budget -75% (2199->542); forcing now rides tool descriptions/results (guaranteed across Claude Code/Copilot/Codex) instead of the truncatable instructions block. Tests: Unit Module=McpServer 1204 pass; Module=Command 1628 pass/11 skip; guidance e2e 18 pass (incl. 3 new guides over real stdio). The live note e2e needs a real Creatio (the success path), so it is covered by a deterministic unit test (FakeUpdateEntitySchemaCommand, ExitCode 0) instead of a hermetic e2e.

## 2026-06-19 10:07 – Confluence PlantUML macro edit (ENG-90883 research page)
Context: asked to restack the "Build modify contract" class diagram on Confluence page 4752572418 (TER space) so MODIFY sits below BUILD.
Decision: PlantUML layout fix — added hidden directional link `BuildProcessResult -[hidden]down-> ModifyRequest` and switched the 3 cross-package `Operation ..> X` links to `.up.>` so they reinforce vertical stacking.
Discovery: the "PlantUML Diagrams for Confluence" (puml4cc/stratus) macro stores its source in the ADF extension node `data-parameters` JSON, key `data.value`, with `compressed:true`. Encoding chain (NOT PlantUML's own ~1 codec): PlantUML text -> percent-encode (urllib quote safe='') -> raw DEFLATE (zlib wbits=-15) -> STANDARD base64 (has +,/,= ). Decode is the inverse. Round-trip with a tiny py script; edit only the one `data.value` substring and re-send the whole body via mcp atlassian updateConfluencePage (whole-body replace; verified live by re-decoding both diagram blobs). Macro also supports `compressed:false` (uncompressed) per stratus docs, but base64 is what the editor produces.
Files: (no repo files) scratchpad puml.py/encode2.py/assemble.py; Confluence page 4752572418 now v14.
Impact: reusable recipe for programmatically editing puml4cc diagrams on Confluence without the native editor; avoids hand-corrupting the compressed payload by always round-trip-verifying via decode.

## 2026-06-19 – ENG-91842 Data source filters: research + design + branches
Context: ENG-91842 (Task 4, "the cost center"). User pasted a designer-built capture (process UsrClioBpInitParams1) of a "Read data" element filtered by Account=Perform task.Account AND Address=2222 AND Age=333. Asked for client+server research, a proposed solution, and a new branch in clio + the package.
Decision: emit the FilterGroup wrapper JSON ourselves from package-local DTOs (no public Terrasoft builder API; converters are internal; also keeps version-coupling minimal). Add a high-level `filter` block (object + logicalOperation + conditions[column,comparison,value|processParameter|elementParameter|expression]) to the element descriptor + a `setFilter` modify op. New server collaborator Filters/IProcessFilterService. clio forwards opaque JSON (no build/modify DTOs) — only describe-DTO `filter` field + guidance/prompt/docs/e2e. Phase 1 = engine + Signal start (EntityFilters; no Task-12 dep); Phase 2 = Read data DataSourceFilters (+ minimal target-object wiring); then Modify/Delete/Add data; then clio surface.
Discovery: (1) `DataSourceFilters` element param DataValueType 394e160f-… = MetaDataText (plain string store). Wrapper = {className:"Terrasoft.FilterGroup", serializedFilterEditData:<full, UI re-edit>, dataSourceFilters:<lean, runtime>}. (2) Runtime reads ONLY `dataSourceFilters` (Terrasoft.Nui.ServiceModel.DataContract.ProcessFilterFactory.Deserialize → JsonConvert<Filters>(wrapper["dataSourceFilters"])); serializedFilterEditData is designer-re-edit only — emit BOTH. lean = full minus className/key/leftExpressionCaption/referenceSchemaName/isAggregative/displayValue (client FilterModuleMixin.saveDataSourceFilters → deleteParameterDisplayValues). (3) Signal start uses the SAME wrapper on ProcessSchemaStartSignalEvent.EntityFilters (meta key DZ13), via the same FilterModuleMixin. (4) Lookup column equality → InFilter(filterType 4, rightExpressions[]); scalar → CompareFilter(1, rightExpression). Param ref → dataValueType 26 (Mapping), value={value:GetMetaPath() token, displayValue, Id:fresh GUID}; constant → dataValueType=column type + scalar. Enums: FilterType Compare1/IsNull2/Between3/In4/Group6; ComparisonType Equal3/NE4/Less5/LE6/Gt7/GE8/StartWith9/Contain11/EndWith13/IsNull1/IsNotNull2; Logical And0/Or1; Expr Column0/Param2; DVT Text1/Int4/Float5/Money6/DateTime7/Date8/Time9/Lookup10/Bool12/Mapping26. (5) Root-object dependency: data elements need a target object (Task 12) to resolve columns + rootSchemaName — recommend filter descriptor carries its own `object` + set minimal ReadData target so it's self-contained.
Files: spec/process-design-service/data-source-filters-design.md (new), spec/process-design-service/captures/readdata-filters-capture.md (new). Branches: clio feature/ENG-91842-data-source-filters (off feature/ENG-90883-approach1-backend-designer); package C:\Projects\workspace\ProcessBuilder feature/ENG-91842-data-source-filters (off main 52df22b). No code yet — design/proposal only, awaiting approval of scope.
Impact: locks the exact filter serialization (the single biggest risk in the whole BP-generation plan) against a real capture + client/server source, so implementation is mechanical; documents the runtime-reads-dataSourceFilters fact so we don't waste time on serializedFilterEditData fidelity for runnability.

## 2026-06-19 – ENG-91842 Phase 1 implemented (package serializer engine)
Context: after the design was approved (conditions+groups contract, lookup column-paths), implemented Phase 1 in the clioprocessbuilder package (C:\Projects\workspace\ProcessBuilder, branch feature/ENG-91842-data-source-filters, commit 91895c9).
Decision: emit the FilterGroup wrapper from package-local Dictionary<string,object> trees via System.Text.Json (no Terrasoft builder API exists; isolates version coupling). New scoped collaborator Filters/ProcessFilterService + FilterContracts (FilterDescriptor : FilterGroupDescriptor with Object; FilterConditionDescriptor; FilterElementParameterRef). ApplyFilter dispatches: ProcessSchemaStartSignalEvent -> EntityFilters (direct string prop); data task -> DataSourceFilters element param SourceValue (Source=ConstValue, mapping idiom). Build pass in ProcessDesigner after mappings; setFilter/clearFilter ops in ProcessOperationExecutor. Registered AddScoped<IProcessFilterService>.
Discovery: (1) EntitySchemaColumn has NO IsLookup — that lives on Core.DataValueType (virtual bool, overridden true for lookup types). Column has ReferenceSchemaUId. (2) numeric dataValueType = (int)column.DataValueType.ToEnum() (Terrasoft.Nui.ServiceModel.Extensions.DataValueTypeExtension) which normalizes ShortText/etc -> Text(1), Float*->Float(5), Money*->Money(6) — matches the capture. (3) Test project (clioprocessbuilder.Tests) references NO JSON lib (System.Text.Json/Newtonsoft) — assert by extracting the inner escaped JSON + Regex.Unescape + Contains. (4) MockEntitySchemaWithColumns(name, cols, lookupCols) requires the referenced lookup schema to be mocked FIRST (it resolves the ref by name at creation); the mock DOES populate lookup IsLookup+ReferenceSchemaUId so column-path traversal (Account.Code) unit-tests pass. (5) C# 7.3 in the package (no switch expressions / using declarations).
Files: package — Contracts/FilterContracts.cs, Filters/{IProcessFilterService,ProcessFilterService}.cs (new); Contracts/{ProcessDescriptorContracts,ModifyContracts}.cs, ProcessDesignConstants.cs, ProcessDesigner.cs, Operations/ProcessOperationExecutor.cs, src/ClioProcessBuilderApp.cs (mod); tests/ProcessFilterServiceTests.cs (new, 12), ProcessOperationExecutorTests.cs (ctor). clio — spec/process-design-service/{data-source-filters-design.md, captures/readdata-filters-capture.md} (new).
Impact: the cost-center serializer is done + unit-green (67 total). Remaining Phase-1: describe read-back (server decode + clio DescribedElement.filter — clio strips unknown fields), clio guidance/prompt/tool-desc/docs/e2e, Read-data minimal target-object wiring, and live verify on test-stand (deploy: rebuild package + COMPILE package in Creatio).

Context: a live clio-MCP session (share developer-3ffa66c7) asked for a record-trigger process with Name=Start; the AI built signalStart→task→end WITHOUT a filter and told the user to add it in the designer — because the (old, pre-filter) process-modeling guidance says signalStart takes no field filter and Read-data filters aren't buildable. Scoped guidance HONESTLY to the deployed reality: signalStart filter is end-to-end usable now (entity known); data-task (Read/Add/Modify/Delete) filters serialize but aren't usable until Read-data target-object config (Task 12), so guidance says use the signalStart filter only.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ProcessDesigner/ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs (new "Data source filters" section + filter on signalStart trigger example + setFilter/clearFilter in modify recipe + corrected NOT-yet-buildable scope), Tools/ProcessDesigner/{Create,Modify}BusinessProcessTool.cs ([Description]), Prompts/ProcessDesigner/{Create,Modify}BusinessProcessPrompt.cs, docs/commands/{create,modify}-business-process.md, help/en/{create,modify}-business-process.txt.
