## 2026-06-25 – PR #715 review (B2/M2/M4 + minors) addressed end-to-end
Context: a-serhiichyk-creatio CHANGES_REQUESTED on clio PR #715 (client side of the AI BP designer). Feature-owner decisions: B2 = keep CLI public (MCP stays gated); scope = everything incl. the M2 vocabulary.
Decision: B2 — close the asymmetry the *other* way (added [FeatureToggle("process-designer")] to ListUserTasksTool so all 6 MCP tools are gated) and record the deliberate CLI-public/MCP-gated split in the ADR. M2 — publish the canonical element-type mapping in docs/commands/describe-process.md ("Element type vocabulary"), link from ADR M2 caveat + create/modify docs. M4 — add service-level HTTP tests (substituted IApplicationClient) for Create/Modify/ListUserTasks/Describer asserting URL + {"request":…} body + every response branch + server-derived fields; add DUP/UNKNOWN tool-boundary tests. Minors — list-user-tasks [RequiresPackage] (Batch 1), rename KnownRoute.DescribeProcessSchema→DescribeProcess, dead Schema.cs code removed (Batch 1).
Discovery: (1) ProcessGraphValidator had a LATENT CRASH — it added a DUP finding but then BuildAdjacency did nodes.ToDictionary(n=>n.Id,…) which THROWS on duplicate keys before returning; fixed by seeding adjacency from the de-duplicated nodeById (and dropped the now-unused `nodes` param). (2) ServerProcessDescriber is the odd one out among the four services: ctor takes IApplicationClient directly (not factory+settings), calls the 1-arg Build(KnownRoute) overload and the 5-arg ExecutePostRequest(url,body,10000,3,1), and returns ErrorOr (never throws) — tests assert .IsError, not Throw. (3) clio.mcp.e2e uses NO [Category]/[Explicit] anywhere by convention — gating is Assert.Ignore when McpE2E:Sandbox:EnvironmentName is blank + project excluded from push CI; adding category attrs to only the PD files would diverge, so left intact (env name blanked to "" in Batch 1 makes the skip-gate fire). (4) describe carries NO direction field (DescribedParameter has none) → the Direction-enum minor is a non-issue for these commands; the enum is only on the raw generate-process-model path.
Files: clio/Command/ProcessModel/ProcessGraphValidator.cs; clio/Common/ServiceUrlBuilder.cs; clio/Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs; clio/docs/commands/{describe-process,create-business-process,modify-business-process}.md; spec/adr/adr-ENG-90883-backend-process-designer.md; clio.tests/Command/{CreateBusinessProcessServiceTests,ModifyBusinessProcessServiceTests,ListUserTasksServiceTests}.cs; clio.tests/Command/ProcessModel/{ServerProcessDescriberTests,ProcessGraphValidatorTests}.cs; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ValidateProcessGraphToolTests.cs. Commits 3fc8fc1e, 4981a96d (+ Batch 1 9f45e999). Full unit suite 4556 passed / 20 skipped.
Impact: PR #715 review fully answered (reply posted as issue comment). Future: the four PD client services share a service-level HTTP test recipe (substitute ISettingsRepository/IApplicationClientFactory/IServiceUrlBuilder/IApplicationClient/ILogger, assert posted URL + Wrapped(body)["request"] + each branch); the describer needs the 1-arg/5-arg/ErrorOr variant.

## 2026-06-26 – PR #715 review: AI-surface fixes + full naming alignment (describe-business-process)
Context: a-serhiichyk-creatio left 5 standalone PR comments beyond the main REQUEST CHANGES review (verb naming, B2-followup, docs hygiene, AI-surface prompt drift, naming/contract) + a server-side deprecated-EndEvent note. First reply covered only the main review; these were unanswered. User: "fix valid items" then "change everything".
Decision: Fixed in two commits. (1) 3192256f — prompt drift A/B/C (validate prompt dropped abandoned process-add-element/.djs-validate-outline → ends at create/modify+describe; describe prompt field list → real DescribedElement fields; create prompt += signalStart/signal), rule-id F (missing-node R2→R15; softened "R1–R17 pre-checked" to enforced subset), describe uid (DescribedElement.Uid added, Id doc fixed — server sends id=Name + uid=UId, clio was dropping uid), docs hygiene (removed 3 scratch handoff/state docs w/ creds+paths). (2) 2f900f15 — full naming alignment: kebab MCP args via args-records (create/modify/list: environmentName→environment-name, packageName→package-name, processName/Uid→process-name/process-uid) + verb/MCP-tool/prompt rename describe-process→describe-business-process (aliases dp, describe-process).
Discovery: (1) MCP arg casing mechanism — bare method params expose the C# param name verbatim (camel); to get kebab you MUST use an args-record with [property: JsonPropertyName] (describe/validate already did). SDK flattens the record → flat arg schema, so refactoring create/modify/list to records changes only the wire key names, not the schema shape. (2) ReadmeChecker (clio.tests/Command/ReadmeChecker.cs) gates EVERY non-hidden [Verb]: canonical name must have a Commands.md "(docs/commands/<verb>.md)" link + a help/en/<verb>.txt + docs/commands/<verb>.md file + a WikiAnchors.txt line starting "<verb>:". Renaming a verb => rename both files (git mv) + update Commands.md/WikiAnchors or the test fails. (3) Verb back-compat = add old name to [Verb] Aliases (no per-alias Hidden); CommandLineParser supports the alias. (4) e2e tests call tools over the MCP protocol with arg dicts (not method calls) so the arg-record refactor doesn't break e2e compile — but the hardcoded dict KEYS (["environmentName"]) had to be kebab-ed for the gated runs; tool NAMES use the ToolName consts so they auto-followed. (5) "describe-business-process" does NOT contain substring "describe-process" → safe to mechanical-replace describe-process→describe-business-process (only the alias spots needed preserving).
Files: ~32. Code: CreateBusinessProcessTool/ModifyBusinessProcessTool/ListUserTasksTool (+ args records), DescribeProcessCommand ([Verb]), DescribeProcessTool (ToolName), 3 prompts, ProcessModelingGuidanceResource, IProcessDescriber, ProcessGraphValidator. Tests: 4 tool tests + RemoteCommandCliogateTests + ProcessGraphValidatorTests/ValidateProcessGraphToolTests/ServerProcessDescriberTests. Docs: describe-process.{md,txt}→describe-business-process.{md,txt} (git mv), Commands.md, WikiAnchors.txt, McpCapabilityMap.md, create/modify/list docs+help, ADR, manual-qa. E2E: 4 PD e2e (arg keys). Validated full Unit 4556 passed.
Impact: PR #715 fully answered (2 PR comments posted). NOT changed (with rationale on PR): name vs schemaName (mirrors server wire), Direction enum (describe has no direction field), prompts-defer-to-guidance dedup + ai-bp-* doc consolidation (separate follow-ups). The 4 PD verbs now share the *-business-process form + uniform kebab args; describe surfaces element uid.

Decision/Work: (1) Stale knowledge docs — de-staled spec/ai-business-process-generation/ai-bp-{element-catalog,connection-rules}.md from the abandoned UI-automation framing (designer driver/morph/[data-action=setup]/.djs-validate-outline) to the shipped declarative flow; kept factual tables+R1-R17. backend-designer-manual-qa.md already backend-flow. (commit 4e13635e; delegated to a coder subagent in parallel.) (2)+(3) BP e2e — added shared ClioCliCommandRunner.IsEnvironmentReachableAsync (ping-app) + reachability skip-gate to create/modify/list/describe ArrangeAsync (was blank-only); added describe-readback to create (asserts task1+buildType) and modify (asserts signalstart replaced the start), strengthened describe assert to elements+buildType+flows. (commit 3aa9308b.) (4) SonarCloud 0% new-code coverage — diagnosed as pre-existing repo-wide infra gap (build.yml runs dotnet test with NO coverage collector + Sonar automatic-analysis only, no SonarScanner step → can't import coverage); fix = switch to CI-based Sonar analysis, maintainer-owned, out of scope; reported only.
Discovery: (a) MCP describe payload is DOUBLE-JSON-encoded (graph is a string value, quotes escaped as ") — so e2e .Contain("\"buildType\"") with literal quotes FAILS; assert the bare token .Contain("buildType"). (b) The reachability pattern: ClioCliCommandRunner.RunAsync(settings, ["ping-app","-e",env]).ExitCode==0; gate BEFORE starting the MCP session so Assert.Ignore short-circuits cleanly. (c) Reviewer's follow-up was slightly stale: prompt names already fixed in 2f900f15; committed appsettings env is "" not "pb" (their local value leaked into a skip-message). Verified bogus env => Skipped, not Failed.
Files: spec/ai-business-process-generation/ai-bp-{element-catalog,connection-rules}.md; clio.mcp.e2e/{Create,Modify,ListUserTasks,Describe}*E2ETests.cs; clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/ClioCliCommandRunner.cs. BP e2e 9/9 green on test-stand.
Impact: PR #715 residue fully addressed + replied; reviewer's items 1-3 fixed, item 4 reported as infra follow-up. PR is non-blocking/mergeable.

## 2026-06-26 – PR #715: removed the 4 process-designer CLI verbs (MCP-only), closed B2
Context: reviewer's unanswered 09:45 comment (🔴 Major) re-escalated B2 — don't gate, REMOVE the 4 CLI verbs as dead surface. Discussed with feature owner (key Q: "AI still creates processes?" — yes, MCP path is independent); owner approved removal.
Decision: feature is now MCP-only. Removed CLI exposure, kept the MCP substrate. Delegated the ~20-file mechanical removal to a coder subagent with a precise order; verified + fixed the follow-ups + ran e2e myself.
Removed: [Verb]/[Option] on the 4 options classes + CLI-only file-path props (DescriptorPath/OperationsPath) + file-read Execute branches; Program.cs CommandOption[] entries + dispatch arms; 4x docs/commands/*.md + 4x help/en/*.txt; Commands.md entries + WikiAnchors lines; converted the 4 *CommandTests off BaseCommandTests<T> (drops the ReadmeChecker [Verb] doc obligation) keeping Execute coverage.
Kept: command classes, services, DI, options CLASSES (+ [RequiresPackage]), ALL MCP tools/prompts/guidance.
Minor: modify MCP wire key process-name -> process-code (matches describe); updated modify e2e to send process-code.
Discovery: (a) AI path is fully independent of [Verb]/Program.cs — MCP tool → InternalExecute<TCommand> → command class. Proven: BP e2e 9/9 green AFTER removal. (b) The M2 "Element type vocabulary" table lived in describe-business-process.md (deleted) — had to RELOCATE it into the ADR (inline) so it survives + fix the ADR self-link. (c) ReadmeChecker iterates [Verb] types; removing [Verb] just drops them from the check (no doc obligation) — but command tests that inherited BaseCommandTests<T> had to be converted to plain fixtures or they'd fail the README assertion. (d) The coder couldn't touch clio.mcp.e2e (instructed), so the modify e2e wire key + the ADR B2/diagram/layer staleness were mine to fix.

Commit 521b9da7. Full unit 4550 passed; BP e2e 9/9.
Impact: B2 closed the clean way (MCP-only, no asymmetry). Re-add a verb only when a real human/CI consumer appears. AI process create/modify/describe/list unchanged.

## 2026-06-26 - process-designer element id->name rename across the clio MCP surface
Context: PR #715 review #2 re-open — describe `id` (a string element handle) collides with Creatio's reserved `Id` (the PK GUID). The server side (cli-process-builder #9) renamed first; clio must mirror. Platform research: an element is Name (code) + UId (GUID) + Caption, and ProcessKeyWords.UIdPropertyName = "Id" reserves "Id" for the GUID.
Decision (94ab5c2d): full sweep, no back-compat (feature is [FeatureToggle("process-designer")]). describe output element drops `id`, exposes name/uid/caption (IProcessDescriber.DescribedElement.Id removed); validate-process-graph node arg `id`->`name`, finding `node-id`->`node-name` (ProcessGraphNode.Id->Name, ProcessGraphFinding.NodeId->NodeName, ProcessGraphValidator dup/findings/messages, ValidateProcessGraphTool); element ref in mappings + removeElement `elementId`->`elementName`; flows unchanged. Realigned the full MCP surface per policy: 3 prompts, the process-modeling guidance resource, Create/Modify/Validate tool descriptions+args, docs/McpCapabilityMap.md, the sample descriptor spec, and clio.mcp.e2e (compile-only).
Discovery: create/modify-business-process forward the descriptor/operations JSON to the server as OPAQUE strings (no client-side parse of id/elementId), so the only clio-side contract enforcement is the tool [Description] / prompt / guidance text + sample JSON — all updated for AI-facing consistency. Left untouched (correctly): the data-id element-TYPE tokens + ManagerMap.ResolveDataId (type vocabulary, a separate concern), the [#...Id#] formula reference syntax, and real-GUID `id` fields on unrelated tools (OData/Application/OAuth). clio.mcp.e2e has a PRE-EXISTING standalone build issue (nullable-as-error in untouched files) unrelated to this change.
Files: clio/Command/ProcessModel/{IProcessDescriber,IProcessGraphValidator,ProcessGraphValidator}.cs, McpServer/Tools/ProcessDesigner/{Create,Modify,ValidateProcessGraph}Tool.cs, McpServer/Prompts/ProcessDesigner/*.cs, McpServer/Resources/ProcessDesigner/ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs, docs/McpCapabilityMap.md, spec/process-design-service/process-design-service-sample-descriptor.json + ~5 clio.tests files + 3 clio.mcp.e2e files
Validated: dotnet test clio.tests/clio.tests.csproj -c Debug --framework net8.0 --filter "Category=Unit&(Module=McpServer|Module=ProcessModel|Module=Command)" -> 3349 passed, 0 failed, no new CLIO* warnings.
Impact: clio's AI-facing process contract matches the server + the platform vocabulary (name/uid/caption); describe<->build<->validate all speak `name`. Branch feature/ENG-90883-approach1-backend-designer (PR #715).

## 2026-06-26 - get-process-signature: process-name -> process-code (PR #715 12:15)
Context: PR #715 12:15 residual — one process-identity vocabulary across the process tools (describe/modify use process-code; get-process-signature used process-name). User confirmed "name is the code", so align it.
Decision (b97578ac): canonical CLI option is now --process-code (the value is the process schema Name = code, or caption); --process-name kept as a HIDDEN backward-compat alias (kebab-case migration policy). MCP arg renamed process-name -> process-code (GetProcessSignatureArgs.ProcessCode + JsonPropertyName); GetProcessSignatureOptions.ProcessName -> ProcessCode; the PageUpdateTool consumer + the run-process-button guidance text + help/docs + tests + e2e updated; +backward-compat alias test (parses --process-name, asserts ProcessCode set).
Discovery: the command had NO --process-name CLI flag before — only a positional [Value(0)] + the MCP arg string. So the canonical is [Value(0)] + a new [Option("process-code")], with the hidden [Option("process-name")] alias; [Value]+[Option] on one property parses cleanly (confirmed by the alias test through the real parser).
Files: clio/Command/GetProcessSignatureCommand.cs, McpServer/Tools/ProcessDesigner/GetProcessSignatureTool.cs, McpServer/Tools/PageUpdateTool.cs, McpServer/Resources/ProcessDesigner/RunProcessButtonGuidanceResource.cs, help/en/get-process-signature.txt, docs/commands/get-process-signature.md + GetProcessSignatureToolTests + e2e
Validated: dotnet test ... --filter "Category=Unit&(Module=Command|Module=McpServer)" -> green; McpServer 1421 passed.
Impact: one consistent process-identity vocabulary (process-code) across get-process-signature / describe / modify; legacy --process-name still works as a hidden alias. Branch feature/ENG-90883-approach1-backend-designer (PR #715).

Context: PR #715 16:12 — the reviewer REVERSED their earlier process-code suggestion (the one b97578ac implemented). Correct direction: converge on `process-name`, because (1) get-process-signature is already SHIPPED on master (renaming its arg breaks a public contract), and (2) `process-name` is the platform-faithful term (a schema's identifier IS its Name) — consistent with the element id->name rename; `process-code` reintroduced the "invent a label for the platform Name" anti-pattern. ## 2026-06-24 – ENG-92146 PR #757 review fixes (cliogate HTTP readiness probe)
Context: Three Major review threads (tetiana-moshon ×3, m-dymytrova robustness note) on the cliogate HTTP readiness probe.
Decision: (1) Readiness predicate now treats only 2xx/401/403 as "serving" and retries 404/3xx/5xx (IsServingStatus helper) — a transient warm-up 5xx/3xx no longer counts as ready, which was reintroducing the ENG-92146 false-positive cascade. (2) Caller sets AllowAutoRedirect=false so a 302 is observed as a redirect, not followed to a login 200. (3) Per-request HttpClient.Timeout 30s->10s plus an optional overall deadline (CliogateHttpReadinessProbe overallTimeout ctor arg, linked CTS) wired from ClioCliCommandRunner (3 min) so an accept-then-hang host can't stack 12×30s into a ~7-8 min arrange stall. (4) Rewrote CliogateHttpReadinessProbeTests from a real HttpListener (real I/O in a [Category("Unit")] test) to a stub HttpMessageHandler — true unit test, no sockets, parallelizable; added 503-then-serving, persistent-5xx, persistent-3xx, 403-serving and overall-deadline cases.
Discovery: BuildMessage preferred lastStatusCode and dropped lastError, hiding the deadline reason when a status had been observed — now appends lastError alongside the status. clio.mcp.e2e is still not in CI (project-context), so moving the unit test into clio.tests for the unit gate would need a cross-project ProjectReference + InternalsVisibleTo (architectural change) — deferred for human decision.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/CliogateHttpReadinessProbe.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/ClioCliCommandRunner.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/CliogateHttpReadinessProbeTests.cs
Impact: probe no longer accepts transient warm-up responses as readiness, worst-case arrange time is bounded, and the contract is covered by fast true-unit tests.

## 2026-06-24 00:00 – ENG-92147 DataForge structured-error contract (investigate-first)
Context: 8 DataForge MCP E2E fixtures fail with Success=false; root is env/arrange (service not initialized / CrtDataForge maybe absent) on fresh 10.0.0.740 PostgreSQL stand. Two code-smells flagged: read-client throws InvalidOperationException on Success=false; Initialize() returns hardcoded "Scheduled" with no poll.
Decision: NO production code change. The read ops throw, but every consumer already converts the throw into a clean structured Success=false MCP response: DataForgeTool wraps each method in try/catch -> DataForgeFindTablesResponse(Success:false, Error:{code,message=ex.Message}); DataForgeContextService wraps each per-term read in try/catch -> warnings. The throw is the load-bearing contract DataForgeContextService depends on for per-term graceful degradation. Converting IDataForgeReadClient to "return structured failure" changes its return-type contract and forces a DataForgeContextService rewrite (changes dataforge-context behavior, sandbox-gated) — beyond ENG-92147 safe scope => NEEDS-DECISION for the contract change. Delivered the max safe value: regression unit tests pinning the structured-failure contract at the tool boundary + a success-path test (Module=McpServer in DataForgeToolTests.cs), plus a stand-gated spec for the env root.
Discovery: Initialize() "Scheduled" is hard-asserted by 2 E2E fixtures (DataForge{Initialize,Update}_Should_Return_Scheduled_Response) and 1 unit test (Initialize_Should_Use_Rest_Route) — DO NOT change its contract; readiness wait must live in E2E arrange polling dataforge-status. DataForgeReadClientTests.FindSimilarTables_Should_Throw_When_Proxy_Returns_Error encodes current throw behavior. clio.tests DataForge tests live at clio.tests/Common/DataForge*.cs (Module=Common) and clio.tests/Command/McpServer/DataForgeToolTests.cs (Module=McpServer). Tool surfaces ex.Message unredacted but read-client failure message is server-text/route-name only (no path/URI), so ENG-91827 redaction not applicable here.
Files: clio.tests/Command/McpServer/DataForgeToolTests.cs, spec/dataforge-e2e-readiness/dataforge-e2e-readiness-spec.md
Impact: Locks the structured-error contract for the 3 DataForge read tools against regression; documents the Initialize "Scheduled" landmine and the env/arrange remedy so the stand operator can make the 8 E2E fixtures deterministic without touching clio code.

## 2026-06-24 12:55 – ENG-92150: Tier-tag MCP e2e tests by infrastructure (NoEnvironment vs Sandbox)
Context: clio.mcp.e2e has ~60 trunk failures rooted in env-dependence (need a TeamCity-stood-up live Creatio). First structural fix: make tests separable by infra tier so the env-free subset runs fast/deterministic off-CI.
Decision: Purely-additive NUnit categories `McpE2E.NoEnvironment` / `McpE2E.Sandbox`. Class-level [Category] on uniform fixtures; per-test [Category] on mixed fixtures. No test-logic, no product, no GHA, no TeamCity-config changes (gating documented in spec only).
Discovery: Classification = arrange-gate based. Reliable Sandbox signals are the env-reach helpers PLUS two non-obvious ones that an initial signal list missed and that caused false-NOENV (tests silently `Assert.Ignore`d): (1) a bare `settings.Sandbox.EnvironmentName` read followed by `Assert.Ignore`, and (2) `ResolveReachableEnvironmentAsync`. Treating any reachable-from-test text containing `Assert.Ignore` as Sandbox closed the gap. Some Sandbox tests FAIL-FAST (not skip) locally when AllowDestructive is on but sandbox config is absent (EnsureSandboxIsConfigured before opt-in check) — documented contract in clio.mcp.e2e/AGENTS.md, reproduces on master. 346 tests total: 171 NoEnvironment, 175 Sandbox.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/*E2ETests.cs (60 files, attributes only), spec/mcp-e2e-tiering/mcp-e2e-tiering-spec.md, spec/mcp-e2e-tiering/mcp-e2e-tiering-validation.md
Impact: `dotnet test --filter "Category=McpE2E.NoEnvironment"` is a deterministic env-free gate (0 fail, 0 env-skips) anyone can run; Sandbox tier stays on the deploy-backed step. Reusable arrange-gate classifier signals listed in the spec for future e2e additions.

## 2026-06-24 16:30 – ENG-92146 probe: implemented the deferred ServiceUrlBuilder reuse + clio.tests move (PR #757)
Context: drive-pr-green on PR #757. b-horodyskyi's CHANGES_REQUESTED held the PR; Alex chose to close the three deferred Major items with code rather than leave them deferred.
Decision: Moved the probe types (ICliogateHttpReadinessProbe, CliogateHttpReadinessProbe, CliogateReadinessTimeoutException) from clio.mcp.e2e into clio/Common, kept INTERNAL — clio.csproj already grants InternalsVisibleTo to BOTH clio.tests and clio.mcp.e2e, so the unit tests live in clio.tests (CI unit gate) and the e2e runner still sees the probe, with no cross-test-project ProjectReference and no public-API growth.
Decision: ClioCliCommandRunner now composes the probe URL via `new ServiceUrlBuilder(environment).Build(CliogateProbeRoute)` (mirrors LookupRegistrationProbe) and passes an absolute URL; deleted BuildRequestUri + its bespoke /0 test, replaced by 2 cliogate-route cases in ServiceUrlBuilderCommandTests covering both IsNetCore branches.
Discovery (trap): moving the probe into clio made RegisterAssemblyInterfaceTypes (BindingsModule.cs:782) auto-register ICliogateHttpReadinessProbe→impl, whose ctor takes int/TimeSpan, so ValidateOnBuild threw and broke 143 unit tests. Fix: add the probe interface to the same DI skip-list as IReauthExecutor (runtime-only ctor args, constructed manually). Any future probe-style class moved into clio must be added to that skip-list.
Discovery: 4 IdentityServiceDeploymentServiceTests fail identically on clean origin (IIS CreateIisSite / Postgres :5432) — pre-existing macOS-infra failures from the ENG-90640 base merge, NOT from this change (CI Windows runner passes them).
Discovery: the probe is now Sonar-analyzed (clio is; clio.mcp.e2e was not) — extracted the thrice-repeated overall-deadline message into OverallDeadlineExceededMessage() to avoid an S1192 duplicate-literal smell that the e2e location had hidden.

## 2026-06-24 22:30 – ENG-90640: kill clio self-update in e2e + surface full install-gate error
Context: TeamCity run 15635409 on feature/ENG-90640_mcp-e2e-stabilization (pinned 10.0.0.740 PostgreSQL, deploy OK) — dominant red is cliogate install-gate failing at CreatioClient.Login (HttpWebRequest.GetResponse) cascading to ~30 fixtures; install stdout also showed "Updating clio 8.1.0.64 -> 8.1.0.65 in background" (autoupdate on at CI).
Decision: (1) add CLIO_NO_UPDATE_CHECK env-var skip in Program.ShouldSkipUpdateCheck and set it centrally in TestConfiguration.Load() so EVERY e2e-spawned clio (ClioCliCommandRunner.RunAsync + McpServerSession) suppresses the background self-update from one seam (no per-test JSON fan-out; the two existing `"Autoupdate": false` tests are the fragile pattern this replaces). (2) EnsureCliogateInstalledAsync now writes the FULL stdout+stderr to TestContext.Out before the (truncatable) FluentAssertions assertion so the next run reveals the real Login cause (status/401/connect) instead of a cut-off stack.
Discovery: self-update is a red herring for the Login failure itself (detached `dotnet tool update clio -g` updates the GLOBAL tool, not the e2e build-output process) — but it is real e2e noise/latency worth removing. TASK 3 (ApplicationSectionCreate_WithNonLatinCaption regex "drift") was NOT a clean fix: CaptionCultureResolver.Resolve makes a live HTTP profile-culture call, so which error wins (en-US CaptionCultureScriptGuard "contains non-Latin" vs the `--code` guidance) depends on the stand's profile culture — stand-gated/state-dependent, left unchanged.
Files: clio/Program.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/TestConfiguration.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/ClioCliCommandRunner.cs
Impact: next TeamCity run on this branch will print the complete cliogate install-gate Login error (timing vs auth) and run without the self-update confound; informs whether the cliogate root is ENG-92148 timing or ENG-91829 auth/--ep.

## 2026-06-24 – ENG-90640: login-readiness gate + WebException status in readable error
Context: cliogate install-gate fails at CreatioClient.Login on a freshly-deployed e2e stand (~30 fixtures cascade); clio logged only a bare stacktrace so 401-auth vs connect/warm-up was indistinguishable.
Decision (TASK 1, harness-only): added WaitForLoginReadinessAsync(ping-app -e env, 12×5s) BEFORE the install-gate loop in ClioCliCommandRunner.EnsureCliogateInstalledAsync. Chose ping-app because on .NET-Core stands clio routes it through CreatioClient.ExecuteGetRequest, which forces CreatioClient.Login via the lazy AuthCookie (InitAuthCookie → Login) — the SAME login codepath install-gate's UploadFile uses (verified by ilspycmd-decompiling Creatio.Client 1.0.38). So a 401 fails the gate (isolates ENG-91829 creds), a warm-up connect/timeout is retried away. On exhaustion: full stdout/stderr to TestContext.Out + descriptive "did not become loginable within Ns" assertion.
Decision (TASK 2, production): enhanced ExceptionReadableMessageExtension.GetReadableMessageException so any WebException (direct, ConnectFailure-friendly arm, or nested in a wrapper) appends "(WebException: <Status>[ (HTTP <code> <reason>)])". Non-debug only-path benefits (CI runs non-debug). ConnectFailure keeps its friendly text + hint; nested via TryGetWebException inner-chain walk.
Discovery: on .NET 10 HttpWebResponse.StatusCode delegates to a private _httpResponseMessage (HttpResponseMessage) — there is NO m_StatusCode/_statusCode int field. To fabricate one in a unit test: RuntimeHelpers.GetUninitializedObject(typeof(HttpWebResponse)) then set _httpResponseMessage = new HttpResponseMessage(statusCode). The public ctor is [Obsolete(error:true)] and the serialization ctor throws PlatformNotSupportedException.
Discovery: GetPkgListCommandTests + RestartCommand.Tests assert the connect-failure message via StartsWith(...)+Contains("Make sure the site is running") — both survive the appended status (no exact .Be match), verified green.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/ClioCliCommandRunner.cs, clio/ExceptionReadableMessageExtension.cs, clio.tests/ExceptionReadableMessageExtension.Tests.cs
Impact: next TeamCity run on this branch reveals 401-vs-connect at the login boundary and absorbs the stand warm-up window before install-gate; the WebException-status helper benefits every clio HTTP failure path, not just install-gate.

## 2026-06-25 12:00 – ENG-90640 fix JS-syntax shadowing of specific update-page validations
Context: e2e run 15636666 had 7 contract failures; most were ENG-92049 residual where a generic "JavaScript syntax error at line X, column Y" SHADOWS a more specific, offline-detectable validation error in update-page DryRun.
Decision: extended PageUpdateTool's syntax-failure path (new ResolveSyntaxFailure) to surface, in priority order, OFFLINE errors over the generic syntax message — (1) malformed resources/optional-properties argument payloads (new public PageUpdateCommand.ValidateArgumentPayloads, wording shared with ValidateCommonInput), (2) run-process STRUCTURAL processName check (runs regardless of marker integrity), (3) content chain when marker integrity holds (prior ENG-92049 behavior), (4) unknown-environment via ResolveCommand catching EnvironmentResolutionException (offline up to the guard, command discarded). Honors ENG-92049: NO HTTP signature validation on the syntax path. Genuine JS-only typos still surface the ENG-89796 wording.
Discovery: the e2e MinimalMarkerPageBody / RunProcessBody fixtures are the legacy marker-without-key object-literal shape `{ /**MARKER*/[]/**MARKER*/, ... }` — Acornima rejects at col 139, but the legacy brace-counter ValidateJsSyntax returns valid. RunProcessBody FAILS marker integrity (missing SCHEMA_ARGS pair), which is why the prior content-chain-only fallback never reached the processName check. e2e tests using a FAKE env name (Should_Reject_RunProcess..., Should_Report_Invalid_Environment) run locally (no live stand); tests calling ResolveReachableEnvironmentAsync are sandbox-gated.
Discovery (stand-gated, NOT fixed): UnAwaitedContextWarning_In_DryRun — body is valid JS, await-read is correctly a WARNING; failure is because TryUpdatePage resolves the schema context (HTTP) before the dry-run short-circuit (PageUpdateOptions.cs ~line 176) so a missing seeded schema fails. ModifyEntitySchemaColumn LookupConstDefault — ResolveConst calls _designerClient.CheckRecordExists over HTTP; inherently online. Both need a live stand/seed.
Discovery (test-drift): SchemaSync wrong-type args now yields the SDK's "Failed to deserialize argument 'args' for MCP tool 'sync-schemas': ..." (specific) vs the generic "An error occurred invoking ..." (missing wrapper). Both are IsError binding failures; aligned the invalid-type test to the deserialize contract via an AssertInvocationFailure(expectedDiagnostic) overload (missing-wrapper test keeps the generic default).
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/PageUpdateTool.cs, clio/Command/PageUpdateOptions.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/PageToolsTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/SchemaSyncToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: future shadowing fixes should follow the same "offline-validators-win, no-HTTP-on-syntax-path" precedence; net10.0-only locally-runnable e2e cases are the fake-env ones; dry-run still does schema-context HTTP so valid-body dry-run tests are stand-gated.

## 2026-06-25 – RedisDatabaseSelector fail-fast connect (bounded, no hang)
Context: clear-redis/RedisDatabaseSelector connect with AbortOnConnectFail=false + SyncTimeout=500000 + no ConnectTimeout hung indefinitely against an unreachable Redis (~10 min freeze of the MCP e2e suite on a CI agent that can't reach the deployed stand's Redis). Robustness fix for real users + unblocks the McpE2E__Sandbox__EnvironmentPath path (does NOT by itself green the ClearRedis e2e — those need stand Redis reachable, infra ENG-91829).
Decision: Extracted RedisDatabaseSelector.BuildConfigurationOptions(host,port,user,pwd) (internal static, testable) with fail-fast wiring: AbortOnConnectFail=true + ConnectTimeout/SyncTimeout/AsyncTimeout=12s (internal ConnectTimeout const) + ConnectRetry=1. Now Connect() throws RedisConnectionException within ~12s and the existing catch turns it into the descriptive "[Redis Connection Error] Could not connect to Redis at host:port" result instead of hanging. Mirrors the harness fix (RedisSandboxClient, commit f96ab03f on this branch).
Discovery: RedisDatabaseSelector was the ONLY outlier — sibling StackExchange.Redis connects already bound their wait (LocalRedisAssertion 10s, RedisAuthenticationValidator 5s, DeployInfrastructureCommand config-driven), so scope is exactly this one class. clear-redis MCP tool (ClearRedisTool) + CLI verb (RedisCommand) are a RemoteCommand over HTTP (IApplicationClient, TimeOut=30000) returning a structured CommandExecutionResult — they never touch StackExchange.Redis and do not hang: MCP reviewed, no update required. Docs reviewed, no update required (internal service; user-facing error string unchanged; no CLI option/verb/contract change).
Files: clio/Common/Database/RedisDatabaseSelector.cs, clio.tests/Common/Database/RedisDatabaseSelectorTests.cs (new)
Impact: future Redis-touching code has a tested fail-fast template; unreachable Redis now fails in ~12s with a descriptive error. Validated: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&Module=Common" green; new fixture 5/5.

## 2026-06-25 15:10 – ENG-90640: fix SandboxEnvironmentResolver full-tree scan hang
Context: Validation run 15638846 (EnvironmentUrl+EnvironmentPath+DataForge) HUNG ~15min after ClearBrowserSession, stage text frozen while elapsed climbed (genuine hang, not counter lag). Cancelled.
Discovery: NOT a Redis connect (RedisSandboxClient + RedisDatabaseSelector + clear-redis HTTP all bounded). Root = SandboxEnvironmentResolver.Resolve did `Directory.GetFiles(environmentPath, "ConnectionStrings.config", SearchOption.AllDirectories)` over the entire deployed Creatio root (C:\WebAppRoot\studioenu_<id> — tens of thousands of files). With EnvironmentPath empty (run 15636666) Resolve fast-failed before the scan; populating it (my env-var) made the unbounded recursive enumeration run → 15min+ hang. Introduced in 2a058fd4 (#ENG-91829), so fix belongs there.
Decision: probe known ConnectionStrings.config locations directly — `<root>/ConnectionStrings.config` (Core) and `<root>/Terrasoft.WebApp/ConnectionStrings.config` (Framework) — and throw a descriptive error if neither exists, instead of a recursive full-tree scan. Makes the EnvironmentPath toggle (ClearRedis ×4 + the job-config handoff recommendation) actually usable instead of a permanent hang.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/SandboxEnvironmentResolver.cs
Impact: EnvironmentPath-populated runs no longer hang; ClearRedis can exercise the real Redis path; handoff recommendation is now safe.

## 2026-06-25 15:55 – ENG-90640: make ClearRedis e2e hang-proof (WaitAsync wall-clock guard)
Context: Run 15638941 (EnvironmentPath set, from fixed HEAD d764b8a9 with the resolver fix) STILL hung >15min at the same spot. Determined the hanging test deterministically from completed run 15636666's execution order: index 50 ClearBrowserSession_Should_Succeed -> index 51 ClearRedis_Should_Remove_Seeded_Key (the first ClearRedis test). So the resolver full-tree scan was NOT the observed root cause; the hang is inside the ClearRedis test body and only appears when EnvironmentPath is populated (the only fixture whose behavior depends on it).
Discovery: every code path is nominally bounded (harness ConnectAsync internal 12s Task.Delay; RedisCommand is RemoteCommand over HTTP 30s; McpServerSession.StartAsync/CallToolAsync take the 2-min CTS), yet it froze >15min. The 2-min token on StartAsync did NOT unstick it -> the stuck call ignores cancellation (StackExchange.Redis can accept a TCP connection from a CI agent yet never service commands and not honor its own AsyncTimeout). The floor config (EnvironmentUrl-only, run 15638935) does NOT hang because ClearRedis fast-fails on the empty EnvironmentPath before any Redis op.
Decision: guard the Redis arrange/teardown steps with Task.WaitAsync(token) — it cancels the AWAIT on the wall-clock deadline regardless of whether the underlying client observes cancellation, so a stuck step fails the test fast instead of freezing the build. Connect/seed/exists use the existing 2-min arrange CTS; teardown gets a dedicated 30s budget and swallows OCE (best-effort; seeded key is test-prefixed). The durable suite-wide guard is still job-side: dotnet test --blame-hang-timeout 5m process-kills any hung test — elevated to REQUIRED before EnvironmentPath is enabled in the job.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/ClearRedisToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: EnvironmentPath toggle (ClearRedis x4) no longer risks a multi-minute suite freeze; handoff recommendation to add EnvironmentPath is now paired with the mandatory --blame-hang guard.

## 2026-06-24 01:30 – create-app-section -32001 root cause + ADR (ENG-92144 / ENG-91316)
Context: ENG-91837 brainstorm item 6 "kill or fix never-green UC tests". Investigated never-green `create-tasks-section` (0/7) in TeamCity Team_UserCustomization_Custom_AdacAgentsTestsUc.
Discovery: NOT a test bug, NOT a clio capability gap. Playwright spec lives in a SEPARATE repo (creatio-playwright-tests, default branch `trunk`, nested+gitignored under CAADT playwright-tests/); trunk spec is correct (singular "TASK LIST PAGE" matches real page titles). Drove `clio create-app-section` directly on a live stand (reg env adac-test, app UsrTasksRepro) → works in one shot, registers UsrTask_FormPage/ListPage. Real cause: agent's `create-app-section` returns `MCP error -32001: Request timed out` on cold CI stands (confirmed in 2 builds, both DBs, both models) — server-side section generation exceeds Copilot CLI's hard ~180s ceiling, which heartbeat (ENG-91274) does NOT reset. ENG-91540's 90s insert budget present in those builds and did not help. Residual ENG-91316: success-path readback Timeout.Infinite + 15×2s poll with no cumulative deadline.
Decision: ADR `spec/adr/adr-create-app-section-response-deadline.md` (Proposed) — add wall-clock RESPONSE deadline (~150s, below ceiling) to the MCP create family + background continuation (long-lived clio mcp-server lets the section finish), return the existing ENG-91540 `creatio-timeout` envelope with a new `section-created: in-progress` state + poll guidance, instead of riding to -32001. Reuses existing envelope → minimal contract change. Alternatives (heartbeat-only, longer budget, CAADT warmup, platform speedup, switch client, operation-handle) recorded + rejected.
Files: spec/adr/adr-create-app-section-response-deadline.md; ENG-92144 (comment+description); memory adac-agents-tests-uc-plan.md.
Impact: unblocks create-tasks-section without "kill"; fix generalises create-app-section for any hard-ceiling client. Code NOT started (ADR-first per BMAD). Open Q1: does Creatio ApplicationSection InsertQuery block until page generation (affects insert-budget detail) — needs one instrumented cold-stand run.

## 2026-06-24 02:30 – Implement create-app-section response-deadline + background continuation
Context: ADR adr-create-app-section-response-deadline.md (ENG-91316). Fix for create-tasks-section never-green = create-app-section -32001 on Copilot CLI hard ceiling.
Decision/impl:
- McpProgressHeartbeat.RunWithProgressAndDeadlineAsync<T>: races work (on Task.Run, NOT cancelled by deadline) vs wall-clock deadline; on deadline throws McpResponseDeadlineExceededException and leaves work running (ObserveInBackground swallows late exception). New env CLIO_MCP_RESPONSE_DEADLINE_SECONDS (default 150s, 0<n<=600). Delay shares heartbeatCts so no lingering timer on fast completion.
- McpResponseDeadlineExceededException (new file).
- IApplicationSectionCreateService.CreateSection gained optional insertTimeoutMsOverride; ResolveInsertTimeoutMilliseconds honours it (override>env>90s default). CLI path unchanged (90s); MCP path passes ApplicationSectionCreateTool.BackgroundInsertTimeoutMs=600_000 so the insert is not aborted and the section commits in background.
- ApplicationTool.ApplicationSectionCreate now uses the deadline variant; catches McpResponseDeadlineExceededException -> ApplicationToolHelper.CreateSectionInProgressResponse (error-class=creatio-timeout, section-created=in-progress, poll guidance, "do NOT retry / do NOT fall back to create-page").
- Tool [Description], ApplicationToolResponses doc, AppModelingGuidanceResource:92, ApplicationPrompt:143 all updated with the in-progress/poll contract. Docs: help/en/create-app-section.txt + docs/commands/create-app-section.md (new MCP-response-deadline subsection). Commands.md: no change (one-liner).
Tests: clio.tests McpProgressHeartbeatTests +3 (within-deadline returns result; exceeds-deadline throws + work keeps running; throws-before-deadline propagates). ApplicationToolTests + CreateSectionInProgressResponse envelope test. Updated 5 substitute setups (+Arg.Any<int?>()) + 2 Received(1) verify BackgroundInsertTimeoutMs + CaptionCulture capture. clio.mcp.e2e ApplicationSectionToolE2ETests: stalling TcpListener + CLIO_MCP_RESPONSE_DEADLINE_SECONDS=1 -> asserts in-progress envelope (deterministic; refused-port would fail fast as transport, so accept-and-stall).
Verified: clio + clio.tests + clio.mcp.e2e build clean; Module=McpServer 1383 pass / 0 fail; Module=Command only 4 pre-existing macOS Deploy_* PlatformNotSupportedException fail (unrelated). No new CLIO warnings in edited files.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/{McpProgressHeartbeat,McpResponseDeadlineExceededException,ApplicationTool,ApplicationToolSupport,ApplicationToolResponses}.cs; clio/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateCommand.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/AppModelingGuidanceResource.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/ApplicationPrompt.cs; docs (.txt/.md); tests (3 files).
Impact: create-app-section returns actionable in-progress envelope before any client ceiling instead of -32001; unblocks create-tasks-section without "kill". Not committed (await Alex). create-app (ApplicationCreate) same-treatment + platform server-latency ticket = follow-ups per ADR.

## 2026-06-24 13:00 – PR #759 review fixes (m-dymytrova CHANGES_REQUESTED)
Context: Address human reviewer findings on create-app-section MCP response deadline PR.
Decision: Applied clearly-warranted low-risk fixes; deferred architectural/governance items (SemaphoreSlim fan-out cap, ADR-Proposed/Open-Q1 scope) to the author for confirmation.
Changes:
- ToolContractGetTool.cs:935 — section-created contract now documents the 'in-progress' value (the one machine-readable surface the PR had missed).
- McpProgressHeartbeat.RunWithProgressAndDeadlineAsync — added optional Action<Exception> onBackgroundFault sink (ObserveInBackground now surfaces the unwrapped base exception), and branched on cancellationToken.ThrowIfCancellationRequested() so genuine cancellation/shutdown is no longer mislabeled as a 150s deadline.
- ApplicationTool — threads LogBackgroundSectionCreateFault (stderr, stdio-safe) into the deadline call so a post-deadline background insert failure leaves a diagnostic trail.
- Tests: ApplicationToolTests +1 CI test driving the real catch(McpResponseDeadlineExceededException)->in-progress chain (service substitute throws the deadline exception, server:null). ApplicationSectionCreateServiceTests +4 asserting the 600_000 override reaches the 3-arg ExecutePostRequest, override precedence over env var, and the is>0 fallthrough for 0/-1. McpProgressHeartbeatTests +2 (cancellation-distinct-from-deadline; background-fault sink fires with unwrapped exception) and +2 missing `because` on bare ThrowAsync assertions.
Deferred (judgment-call, left for author): unbounded background-continuation fan-out (SemaphoreSlim or documented bound) — context-gated by single-session model, reviewer offered "document it" as an alternative; ADR still Proposed + Open Q1 unresolved + ENG-91316 AC re-scope — governance, not code; minor e2e robustness (heldConnections race, temp dir cleanup, 1s deadline tightness) and duplication/magic-string cleanup — not in CI and risk-bearing.
Verified: clio + clio.tests build clean (0 errors); net10.0 Module=McpServer 1386 pass / 1 skip; ApplicationSectionCreateServiceTests 59 pass. net8.0 runtime not installed locally (CI covers it). No conflict markers, no new CLIO warnings in edited files.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/{McpProgressHeartbeat,ApplicationTool,ToolContractGetTool}.cs; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{McpProgressHeartbeatTests,ApplicationToolTests}.cs; clio.tests/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateServiceTests.cs
Impact: closes the reviewer's contract-drift, diagnosability, cancellation-semantics and CI-coverage Major items; architectural/governance items explicitly returned to the author.

## 2026-06-24 – PR #759 follow-up (concurrency doc, Sonar S3871, e2e cleanup)
Context: Automated drive-to-green pass; reviewer's fresh re-review at c64f9a89 left only the background-fan-out thread open and 1 Sonar new issue.
Changes:
- McpResponseDeadlineExceededException now public (Sonar S3871) — widening visibility is harmless; the type is thrown across the heartbeat surface and InternalsVisibleTo still covers internal callers.
- RunWithProgressAndDeadlineAsync remarks: added the explicit unbounded-fan-out concurrency assumption the reviewer offered as a thread-closing alternative — single-session stdio + McpToolExecutionLock serialise foreground execution, so detached work accumulates only across sequentially timed-out calls, never parallel bursts; a multiplexed transport would require a bounded SemaphoreSlim/queue.
- ApplicationSectionToolE2ETests: joined the accept loop before iterating heldConnections (removes the latent List<T> race), and added TryDeleteDirectory cleanup for both temp HOME dirs (deadline + transport tests).
- McpProgressHeartbeatTests: added `because` to the previously-bare deadline ThrowAsync assertion.
Deferred (unchanged): duplication/magic-string helper extraction (reviewer "Consider"), ADR-Proposed + Open Q1 + ENG-91316 AC re-scope (project/governance decision).
Verified: clio + clio.tests + clio.mcp.e2e build clean (0 errors); net10.0 Module=McpServer 1386 pass / 1 skip / 0 fail.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/{McpProgressHeartbeat,McpResponseDeadlineExceededException}.cs; clio.mcp.e2e/ApplicationSectionToolE2ETests.cs; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpProgressHeartbeatTests.cs

## 2026-06-24 – PR #759 bound the background success readback (tetiana review)
Context: drive-pr-green pass. CI green + Sonar 0 + MERGEABLE; the only blocker was tetiana-moshon's CHANGES_REQUESTED with two Major threads. Thread 4 (McpToolExecutionLock "false premise" in the fan-out rationale) was already fixed by 69f5935f — the doc now rests solely on the single-session stdio invariant, exactly as asked — so it only needed a reply + the 9-min-stale timing noted. Thread 5 (the real one): the detached `Task.Run(work, None)` continuation runs the full CreateSection chain, whose success-path readback used `Timeout.Infinite` across the 15-attempt poll; a readback Creatio accepts but never answers would park a thread-pool worker + HTTP connection for the life of the long-lived server.
Decision: bound the readback per-request on the MCP/background path only. `IApplicationSectionCreateService.CreateSection` gained an optional `readbackTimeoutMsOverride` (mirrors the existing `insertTimeoutMsOverride` pattern); `LoadCreatedSection` success-path call now passes `ResolveReadbackTimeout(override)` = `override is > 0 ? override : Timeout.Infinite`. `ApplicationSectionCreateTool.BackgroundReadbackTimeoutMs = 30_000` (mirrors the 30 s recovery-readback budget). CLI path leaves the override null → keeps patient Timeout.Infinite. No cumulative cap added — each call is now individually bounded and the agent polls list-app-sections regardless (tetiana: "low-harm").
Discovery: `IApplicationClient.ExecutePostRequest(..., int requestTimeout = Timeout.Infinite, ...)` is ONE method with an optional arg, so a 3-arg NSubstitute stub silently binds the 4th param to its default — adding a 4th real param (readback override = 30_000) made every `CreateSection(..., Arg.Any<int?>())` stub stop matching the MCP call. Had to widen the stubs/Received to 4 args (ApplicationToolTests heartbeat-contract + CaptionCultureArgMappingToolTests were the two that broke). CLI tests use a 2-arg form → both overrides default to null → unaffected. The 4 Deploy_Should_* failures in the targeted run are pre-existing (fail identically on a stashed baseline; need a real PostgreSQL/identity stand).
Files: clio/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateCommand.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ApplicationTool.cs; clio.tests/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateServiceTests.cs (+3 tests: bound/infinite/non-positive); clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{ApplicationToolTests,CaptionCultureArgMappingToolTests}.cs; spec/adr/adr-create-app-section-response-deadline.md
Impact: closes the last residual ENG-91316 hang risk on the detached continuation; the success path contract and CLI behavior are byte-for-byte unchanged.

## 2026-06-25 00:00 – Stop teaching scoped UserConnection (lifetime defect)
Context: Code-review blocker B1 — package template registered the platform-owned per-request UserConnection as a container-managed disposable (`AddScoped<UserConnection>(sp => UserConnection)`); MS.DI disposes any IDisposable resolved from a scoped/transient factory at scope close, tearing down the platform connection's DB executors + clearing UserConnection.Current mid-request. Verified end-to-end against core (C:\bare\Forest\core\trunk): ThreadContextInitializer.cs:61 sets Current; ClassFactory.Bind(CoreApiContainer.Resolve<UserConnection>) (AppConnection.cs:396) → GetService<UserConnection> → ApplicationModule.cs:238 GetUserConnection → FindOrCreateUserConnection(false) returns the SAME cached request connection; UserConnection.cs:1533 Dispose → DisposeExecutors()+ResetCurrent(); platform's own dispose at request end (HttpOperationUserConnectionFactory.HandleHttpRequestCompleted) becomes a guarded no-op.
Decision: Fix = `Func<UserConnection>` accessor (advisor-endorsed). MS.DI has NO ExternallyOwned concept; the only non-disposing overload takes a fixed instance (can't vary per request). Registering `AddTransient<Func<UserConnection>>(sp => () => UserConnection)` keeps the connection out of container tracking AND avoids the latent root-caching bug. Transient (not singleton/scoped) on the Func is deliberate — lets a singleton consumer depend on it without a captive-dependency error.
Discovery: The "skills" that teach this are MCP guidance resources surfaced via get-guidance and copied into workspace .codex/skills/creatio-config-webservice. Two carried the defect: ConfigurationWebServiceGuidanceResource.cs (CompositionRootPattern) and ComposableAppSkillGuidanceResources.cs ("Register UserConnection as scoped"). Guidance tests assert only marker substrings, so C# inside the string literals is unverified by tests — must read rendered guidance to catch contradictions (initial edit accidentally double-defined CalculatorEngine; reconciled to a separate ContactRepository example).
Files: clio/tpl/package/Files/src/cs/MainApp.cs (registration + CreateScope doc-comment); clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ConfigurationWebServiceGuidanceResource.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ComposableAppSkillGuidanceResources.cs
Impact: New generated packages + AI-authored WebServices no longer dispose the platform connection. Stale examples still carrying the old pattern (regenerate or leave): packages/ManualPkg, packages/Pkg1426397c7ffe44b, spec/data-binding/DataBindingPkg. Validated: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&Module=McpServer" (net8.0) → 1420 passed.

## 2026-06-25 14:30 – ENG-92462 per-fixture shared MCP server (McpContractFixtureBase)
Context: ENG-92462 — cut per-test startup overhead. Each NoEnvironment test was spawning a fresh clio MCP server process via McpServerSession.StartAsync (~10 s each). Profiled: 2-test ExperimentalTool fixture ran in 20 s (2 × 10 s startup); with shared server: ~11 s total.
Decision: Added McpContractFixtureBase (clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpContractFixtureBase.cs) — [OneTimeSetUp] starts one server per fixture, [OneTimeTearDown] stops it. ArrangeContext (internal sealed record, nested) provides per-test CancellationTokenSource without stopping the server on dispose. Arrange(timeout?) is private protected (allows internal ArrangeContext return type without C# accessibility violation). Test bodies use `await using var context = Arrange(...)` to avoid naming issues with the nested type.
Discovery: C# accessibility rules prevent a protected method in a public class from returning an internal type (accessibility domain mismatch). Fix: make Arrange() private protected (restricted to same-assembly derived classes) + ArrangeContext internal (same-assembly). The two domains match, no compiler error.
Fixtures converted: 15 NoEnvironment fixtures with standard ArrangeContext (GuidanceGet/18, ToolContractGet/17, PageValidate/14, ComponentInfo/13+2special, McpGuidanceResource/7, EntityBusinessRule/~11, PageBusinessRule, PageCreate, ODataRead, ODataWrite, SchemaCreate, GetUserCulture, ExecuteEsq, ExperimentalTool/2, FindEmptyIisPort). ComponentInfo has 2 special tests (composite-wire) that set CLIO_COMPONENT_REGISTRY_LOCAL_FILE env var — they call McpServerSession.StartAsync directly and are correctly NOT sharing the fixture server.
Expected saving: ~160+ NoEnvironment test-level server startups → fixture-level startups (~15 total). Savings ~1,450 s (24 min) of pure startup overhead on the full NoEnvironment run.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpContractFixtureBase.cs (new), 15 fixture .cs files (modified)
Impact: ENG-92462 AC2 fulfilled — shared-server/env bootstrap for contract test groups; mutating tests remain isolated. Follow-up candidates: ApplicationSectionToolE2ETests (12 tests, direct McpServerSession.StartAsync pattern, mixed-category fixture), OAuthConfigurationToolsE2ETests (3 tests, direct pattern).

## 2026-06-26 00:00 – min-Creatio-version gate increment 3 (provider + CLI enforcement)
Context: Final increment wiring the [RequiresCreatioVersion] gate end-to-end.
Decision: CreatioVersionProvider probes cliogate GetSysInfo first (SysInfo.CoreVersion), falls back to ApplicationInfoService (applicationInfo.sysValues.coreVersion) — reverse of PlatformVersionResolver's order, per task. Returns 4-part System.Version verbatim (no SemVer clamp — a gate must keep build/revision). Swallows all I/O so the gate fails closed. CLI gate order: feature-toggle -> creatio-version -> package -> execute; skipped via IsDefinedOn pre-check. Distinct exit code 78; only CreatioVersionRequirementException caught (InvalidOperationException propagates).
Discovery: Auto-scan in BindingsModule registers both provider+checker once the concrete provider exists; no explicit registration needed. ValidateOnBuild green (full build + full suite run).
Files: clio/Common/CreatioVersionProvider.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs (removed exclusion ~796), clio/Program.cs (gate + TryGetCreatioVersionRequirementError + CreatioVersionRequirementExitCode=78), clio.tests/Common/CreatioVersionProviderTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/ProgramCreatioVersionGateTests.cs
Impact: Commands can now gate on minimum Creatio platform version with a machine-readable failure class + distinct exit code.

## 2026-06-26 — MCP parity for [RequiresCreatioVersion] gate (increment 4)
Context: CLI version gate (exit 78) was done; MCP env-sensitive path needed parity with the package gate.
Decision: Added EnforceCreatioVersionRequirements in BaseTool.InternalExecute<TCommand>, ordered version→package (mirrors CLI). Two-catch structure: CreatioVersionRequirementException→CommandExecutionResult.FromCreatioVersionRequirementError (new factory, exit 78 = Program.CreatioVersionRequirementExitCode, embeds " [{ErrorCode}]"); catch-all→FromException (-1) so malformed attribute (InvalidOperationException) is never collapsed into 78.
Discovery: NO shipping command carries [RequiresCreatioVersion] (only test fixtures), so the real MCP process never instantiates the gate. A version-gated MCP tool must live in the production clio assembly — clio.mcp.e2e (harness) cannot inject one. Mirrors the package gate's own e2e refusal-branch gap (WorkspaceSyncToolE2ETests:119). e2e deferred to architect (option B: document gap, rely on unit tests).
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/BaseTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/CommandExecutionResult.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/BaseToolTests.cs
Impact: When any real command adopts [RequiresCreatioVersion], its MCP tool is gated automatically with zero per-tool code; e2e option A (feature-toggled test tool + RuntimeDetectionStubServer→undeterminable) becomes available then.

## 2026-06-26 — min-Creatio-version gate: third error code (version-check-failed)
Context: Team ratified splitting the gate failure space into THREE machine-readable codes (all exit 78). Distinction is "response received but no usable version" vs "no response at all" — NOT auth-vs-transport (untellable from the exception).
Decision: Introduced CreatioVersionResolution record (Version, CreatioVersionResolutionStatus {Resolved, ReachableWithoutVersion, ProbeFailed}) with static factories. ICreatioVersionProvider.GetCoreVersion()->Resolve(). Provider tracks per-source ProbeOutcome (Responded bool + RawVersion) — a returned HTTP call (even empty/garbage) = Responded; soft-degradable throw = Failed. Aggregation: first parseable version=>Resolved (secondary short-circuits); else any Responded=>ReachableWithoutVersion; else=>ProbeFailed. Checker: ProbeFailed=>version-check-failed (connectivity/access message, NOT "update Creatio"); ReachableWithoutVersion=>version-undeterminable (existing msg). IsCompatible: non-Resolved=>false. Memoize the resolution (null _resolution = not probed).
Discovery: ProgramCreatioVersionGateTests + BaseToolTests build CreatioVersionRequirementException directly and mock the checker — code-agnostic plumbing, NO real provider, so no edits needed there. CLI/MCP gates surface "{Message} [{ErrorCode}]" exit 78 for ANY code, so the new code flows through with zero Program.cs/BaseTool.cs change.
Caveat: ProbeFailed message appends strictest.Hint as usual — a command whose Hint literally says "Update Creatio…" would reintroduce that wording into a connectivity failure. Spec-consistent; flagged to architect.
Files: clio/Common/CreatioVersionResolution.cs (new), CreatioVersionRequirementException.cs, ICreatioVersionProvider.cs, CreatioVersionProvider.cs, ICreatioVersionChecker.cs, CreatioVersionChecker.cs, clio.tests/Common/CreatioVersion{Provider,Checker}Tests.cs
Impact: Automation can now distinguish unreachable/access-denied from reachable-but-no-version; commit 01d1b95.

## 2026-06-26 12:00 – ENG-91830 infra-tool MCP InternalError: real root cause = FakeKubernetes.Dispose throws
Context: ENG-90640 sub-task ENG-91830 (Reopened). assert-infrastructure / show-passing-infrastructure / deploy-creatio / restore-db e2e fixtures failed with `McpProtocolException: InternalError (-32603)` and were [Ignore]d. Prior notes blamed (a) MCP SDK typed-POCO structured-content conversion and (b) "requires reachable Kubernetes" — BOTH WRONG.
Decision: verify-root-cause before fixing. Reproduced locally (net10.0, no k8s, no Creatio) — both Assert/ShowPassing fail. String-return change did NOT fix; a try/catch around the call-tool filter's next() did NOT catch it. Added a temporary stderr ILoggerProvider to the MCP server (BindingsModule) → captured the real server exception: `System.NotImplementedException at Clio.FakeKubernetes.Dispose() (FakeKubernetes.cs:17)` thrown by `ServiceProviderEngineScope.DisposeAsync()` inside `McpServerImpl.InvokeScopedAsync`.
Discovery: MCP SDK 1.4.0 runs every tool call in a per-request DI scope and disposes it AFTER the handler returns. `IKubernetes` is a transient that falls back to `FakeKubernetes` when no kubeconfig (BindingsModule.cs:165-178); `FakeKubernetes : IKubernetes` is IDisposable and its Dispose() threw NotImplementedException → scope teardown threw → opaque -32603 OUTSIDE the tool method and OUTSIDE the call-tool filter (so string-return and filter-catch were both blind to it). FindEmptyIisPort works because it has no k8s dependency. The throw masked everything — once fixed, the original typed-POCO returns work fine (the SDK serialization was never the problem).
Fix: `FakeKubernetes.Dispose()` → no-op (clio/FakeKubernetes.cs). All 4 fixtures now pass env-free; un-ignored them and re-tiered Sandbox→NoEnvironment (the "requires reachable k8s" reasons were misdiagnoses; the failure-path Deploy/Restore tests use invalid inputs and never reach real infra). Reverted all diagnostic scaffolding (string-return, filter try/catch, DiagLogger, AddLogging). Added FakeKubernetesTests (Dispose no-throw + idempotent).
Files: clio/FakeKubernetes.cs, clio.tests/Common/Kubernetes/FakeKubernetesTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/{AssertInfrastructure,ShowPassingInfrastructure,DeployCreatio,RestoreDb}ToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: closes ENG-91830 code-side (4 fixtures green env-free). Verified: 6 infra e2e pass net10.0; clio.tests Category=Unit 2098 passed 0 failed; analyzer build clean (2 pre-existing CLIO005 in BindingsModule, not mine). Lesson reinforced ([[verify-root-cause-before-asserting]]): capture the actual server-side exception via a logger before theorising about SDK serialization — both prior write-ups were wrong because the -32603 hid the real Dispose throw.

TeamCity job is server-side (no .teamcity/ in repo); blame-hang-timeout would go in Step 3 args.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/ClioCliCommandRunner.cs (IsCliogateAlreadyServingAsync + probe-first guard).
Impact: cuts per-test arrange to a sub-second probe once cliogate is up; pairs with a CI site-prep step (reg-web-app + install-gate once, between Deploy and Run MCP e2e). ENG-91829 ClearRedis remains job-config (EnvironmentPath + --blame-hang-timeout + reachable Redis); seed-content remains stand-gated.

## 2026-06-26 — ENG-92459: share push/restore across SchemaSync + WorkspaceSync e2e fixtures
Context: ENG-90640 perf sub-task. Two sync fixtures cost ~681s on CI; the dominant cost is the push-workspace COMPILE round-trip re-run per test, not the behavior under test (run 15639765: SchemaSync 97/92/88s, WorkspaceSync 81/80/77s).
Decision: apply the proven ENG-92458 shared-package pattern. SchemaSync — `sync-schemas` is the SUT, push is pure arrange → one lazy shared workspace+package+push per fixture (`EnsureSharedSandboxPackageAsync`), each env-bound test still creates its own unique entity/lookup schemas inside it. WorkspaceSync — push-workspace IS the SUT in the dedicated push test, so KEPT per-test (shared-server is risky for heavy push, cf. ApplicationTool revert); only the two restore tests now share one CLI-pushed package (`EnsureSharedRestoreWorkspaceAsync`) and drop their redundant in-body MCP push (push assertions live in the dedicated push test).
Discovery: a live stand WAS reachable locally → real e2e validation, not Assert.Ignore. All 15 tests across both fixtures pass. First env-bound test carries the single push (slower), later ones skip it — confirms one push per fixture. Per-test context now carries `OwnsRootDirectory` so the shared root is deleted once in [OneTimeTearDown], not by per-test dispose.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/SchemaSyncToolE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/WorkspaceSyncToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: removes 2 redundant push-compiles (SchemaSync 3→1) + 1 (WorkspaceSync restore 2→1) per fixture. Test-only change; no command/MCP/docs surface touched.
