## 2026-06-26 — ENG-92460: parallelize add-item-model runtime-schema fetch under MCP
Context: ENG-90640 perf sub-task. Two slow outliers (run 15639765): GetPkgList 64s, AddItemModel 126s. Local re-measure post-#775 (probe-first arrange in umbrella): GetPkgList 11s (already in budget — arrange was the cost, #775 fixed it; no change needed), AddItemModel still 84s.
Decision: root cause of AddItemModel is in ModelBuilder.PopulateRuntimeSchemas — `add-item-model` fetches one runtime schema POST per entity; the CLI path runs Parallel.ForEach(MaxDegreeOfParallelism=4) but an `if (Program.IsMcpServerMode)` branch forced the MCP path SEQUENTIAL. That branch was added in the MCP-tool commit (36ca6f21) when the logger was not yet thread-safe; the queue+lock logger landed later (#549). Verified safe to parallelize MCP mode now: ConsoleLogger writes via ConcurrentQueue + locked buffer flush; CreatioClientAdapter is logger-free; each iteration mutates only its own Schema.Value; `_schemas` is read-only during the loop. Removed the sequential branch so both paths share the bounded MDOP=4 parallelism.
Discovery: CLI already runs this at MDOP=4 against real stands, so MCP matching it adds no new stand load. Output/contract/exit-codes unchanged — pure perf.
Files: clio/ModelBuilder/ModelBuilder.cs
Impact: AddItemModel_Should_Generate_Model_Files 84s -> 24s (-71%) on a live stand, still green. ModelBuilder unit Module=ModelBuilder 3 passed. Build clean. docs/MCP reviewed — no contract change, no update required. Base = umbrella feature/ENG-90640_mcp-e2e-stabilization-2.

## 2026-06-26 — MCP tool for describe (describe-environment) + cliogate-reduction research
Context: Kirill wants an MCP tool that describes an environment, with resources/guidance explaining the response, and to reduce cliogate reliance using the Creatio core at C:\bare\Forest\core\trunk.
Decision: Added GetCreatioInfoTool (MCP name "describe-environment"), a thin BaseTool<GetCreatioInfoCommandOptions> wrapper that maps env-name/uri/login/password/timeout and calls InternalExecute<GetCreatioInfoCommand> (env-sensitive path → returns CommandExecutionResult, gates run automatically). Added DescribeEnvironmentGuidanceResource (full field catalogue: which source supplies each field + auth/cliogate prereqs), registered in GuidanceCatalog + a routing row. Registered tool in BindingsModule (resources auto-register via [McpServerResourceType] scan — no DI line). Unit tests resolve the REAL sealed command via a substituted IApplicationClient (cannot subclass sealed GetCreatioInfoCommand — InstallGate's fake-subclass pattern does NOT apply here). E2E mirrors GetUserCultureTool (NoEnvironment fixture): advertised-read-only + invalid-env→exit 1, both pass against the real mcp-server.
Discovery (core research, C:\bare\Forest\core\trunk): license/product WITHOUT cliogate is only PARTIAL. ApplicationInfoService.GetApplicationInfo (session-only) gives coreVersion+locale/user/workspace/maintainer. LicenseService.GetCustomerId() (POST, gate CanManageLicUsers) gives CustomerId without cliogate. BUT IsDemoMode is internal-only (LicManager.IsDemoMode, no web service) and ProductName has NO core web service (SysSettings "ProductName"; cliogate derives it via product_info.txt package-match). So productName + licenseInfo.IsDemoMode still need cliogate; only db/framework + CustomerId are cliogate-free. NOTE: the trunk checkout did NOT contain GetSystemEnvironmentInfo (ENG-92465) yet, but env `bk` returns it live → checkout is behind the deployed core; endpoint is real.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/GetCreatioInfoTool.cs (new), clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/DescribeEnvironmentGuidanceResource.cs (new), GuidanceCatalog.cs, RoutingGuidanceResource.cs, BindingsModule.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/GetCreatioInfoToolTests.cs (new), clio.mcp.e2e/GetCreatioInfoToolE2ETests.cs (new), docs/McpCapabilityMap.md
Impact: describe is now reachable over MCP with a discoverable field contract. Future cliogate-reduction: wiring LicenseService.GetCustomerId into describe would make CustomerId cliogate-free (extra CanManageLicUsers round-trip); ProductName/IsDemoMode would still need cliogate unless core adds a web service.

## 2026-06-29 — ENG-90640: un-ignore DataForgeStatus poisoned-proxy e2e (self-skip bug)
Context: Finishing ENG-90640. Green run 15655114 (trunk, vcs-triggered): 362 passed / 0 failed / 8 ignored. Audited the 8 ignored: 5 hard [Ignore] (ClearRedis ×2 ENG-91829 Redis-unreachable; DataForge find/lookups/relations ×3 ENG-92147 service-gated) + 3 conditional Assert.Ignore self-skips (DataForgeStatus poisoned-proxy; InstallApplication report-file; PageBusinessRule Contact-page).
Decision: DataForgeStatus_Should_Ignore_Poisoned_Proxy_Environment_Variables was the only code-fixable one. It set HTTP(S)/ALL_PROXY=127.0.0.1:9 BEFORE ArrangeAsync(requireReachableEnvironment:true); the ping-app readiness probe spawns a normal clio child that honours those proxy vars → routes through the dead proxy → ExitCode!=0 → self-skips "not reachable" on EVERY CI run. Fix = resolve+probe reachability with a clean environment FIRST, then poison the vars, then ArrangeAsync(requireReachableEnvironment:false) so the MCP server (the SUT, whose Data Forge proxy-safe wrapper is under test) still inherits the poison but the probe is not poisoned. Act now uses the cleanly-resolved environmentName.
Discovery: sibling DataForgeStatus_Should_Return_Structured_Status_Response (non-poisoned) PASSED in 15655114 → the stand's DataForge status endpoint works, so once the probe stops self-poisoning this test should flip ignored→passed (not ignored→failed). The other 7 ignores are env/infra/service/config/seed gates: ClearRedis (job must provide runner-reachable Redis = ENG-91829), DataForge find/relations (service-gated = ENG-92147), InstallApplication (CI must set McpE2E:Sandbox:ApplicationPackagePath + a package zip), PageBusinessRule Contact (needs Contacts_FormPage readable via cliogate on the stand).
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/DataForgeToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: should reduce the ignored count 8→7 with a real test-correctness fix. Test-only change; no command/MCP/docs surface touched (MCP reviewed, no update required). Validation is via a TeamCity branch run of Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests (DataForge needs a live stand; not runnable in CI PR checks or locally without DataForge). Base = master @741c16c1.

## 2026-06-29 — ENG-90640: PR #784 drove e2e ignored 8→5 (verified on CI), + review fixes
Context: PR #784 bundles 4 commits that un-ignore three e2e tests; consolidated TeamCity run 15655828 = SUCCESS 365 passed / 0 failed / 5 ignored (was 8). Verified each on a live seeded stand (d2 = ts1-core-dev04 studioenu_15626790, registered with FQDN to dodge my-machine getaddrinfo flakiness).
Decision/fixes:
 - #1 poisoned-proxy: the reorder un-ignored it but it then FAILED (Connection refused 127.0.0.1:9) — clio routes Creatio/DataForge via the ambient HTTP_PROXY (CreatioClient is an external nuget, no per-call knob). Fix = set HttpClient.DefaultProxy = new WebProxy() in MCP-server mode (Program.cs Main), with a fail-safe-bypass-default opt-out env var CLIO_MCP_RESPECT_AMBIENT_PROXY=true|1 (review finding 1). Verified: default bypass → pass; opt-out=1 → respects proxy → fails (so the guard is not a no-op).
 - #7 install-application: bundle a minimal package fixture (Assets/ClioMcpE2EFixture.gz via new-pkg+compress) + fall back to it; installs "Application installed successfully" on d2. Added Assets/README.md with provenance + sha256 (review finding 2).
 - #8 page-business-rule Contact: hardcoded Contacts_FormPage (CRM-only) → discover any seeded *_FormPage via list-pages (Contact-preferred, any datasource-bound attr fallback); the d2/CI seed's UsrE2EContact*_FormPage is bound to base Contact (PDS) so discovery + existing resolver work. Passes on CI.
 - Review hygiene: moved the proxy-poison SetEnvironmentVariable calls inside the try so finally always restores (finding 3); aligned the test wording from "Data Forge proxy-safe wrapper" to clio's MCP-mode proxy neutralization (finding 4).
Discovery (root causes for the remaining 5, NOT clio-fixable): DataForge find/relations ×3 — the DataForge maintenance/index backend is not operational on a Studio+PostgreSQL e2e deploy (dataforge-status liveness:false, "Empty maintenance status response"); 0 SUCCESS ever in plan history, FAILURE on plan run 15644777 too — so it is not a manual-vs-plan-deploy difference. Created by Tetiana Moshon 2026-04-10 (ENG-87092), aspirational. ClearRedis ×2 — CI agent cannot reach the stand's Redis (infra); SandboxEnvironmentResolver needs a local EnvironmentPath+ConnectionStrings.config which a remote stand doesn't expose. Both are infra/deploy-gated outside clio.
Files: clio/Program.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/DataForgeToolE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/PageBusinessRuleToolE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/InstallApplicationToolE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/clio.mcp.e2e.csproj, clio.mcp.e2e/Assets/ClioMcpE2EFixture.gz, clio.mcp.e2e/Assets/README.md
Impact: e2e ignored 8→5 on CI, 0 failed; the 5 residual are documented infra/service gates (DataForge backend, Redis reachability) for separate infra tickets. Validated: Category=Unit 4497 passed/0 failed; 4 e2e tests pass on a live stand; CI run 15655828 SUCCESS.

## ## 2026-06-29 — ENG-92557: un-ignore DataForge find/lookups/relations e2e via service-state skip-guard
Context: Narrow follow-up to closed ENG-92147. The 3 DataForge similarity-search e2e fixtures (find-tables/find-lookups/get-relations) carried a bare [Ignore("ENG-92147…")] referencing a closed ticket. Task: reproduce against a stand, then un-ignore so they pass on a wired stand OR skip deterministically — never a bare [Ignore].
Reproduced (d2 = ts1-core-dev04 studioenu_15626790_0703, CrtDataForge 7.8.0, via real clio MCP server): dataforge-status = success:true but maintenance status=Unavailable ("Empty maintenance status response", all health flags false; GetFullStatus maps Unavailable from payload==null). find-tables/find-lookups/get-relations all success:false with error "Value cannot be null. Parameter name: baseUri" — the DataForge microservice URL is not configured. dataforge-initialize + full 6-min readiness poll never reaches Ready. Confirms diary's prior PR #784 note + Confluence: DataForge is an external OAuth-gated microservice needing DataForgeServiceUrl + IdentityServer* settings + use_enrichment scope + seeded index; a CI/dev stand is not wired to a tier so the index can never be Ready.
Decision: deterministic service-state skip-guard, not unconditional pass. Confluence "Toolkit and Data Forge testing" + ENG-87092 (still To Do): table similarity search returns a service-side 404 even on a fully wired Stage stand, so a blanket find-tables Success=true is unreachable by design. Therefore (1) DataForgeReadinessGate.EnsureIndexReadyAsync is now best-effort (returns bool, never Assert.Fail — was two Assert.Fail; pure IsIndexReady/OverallDeadlineReached unchanged, still 10 unit tests green); (2) each read runs SkipUnlessServiceServedRead — protocol error still fails (callResult.IsError), Success=true asserts the happy path, structured Success=false → Assert.Ignore with the service's own error (deterministic skip keyed on observed state, mirrors ResolveReachableEnvironmentAsync guard). Removed all 3 [Ignore("ENG-92147…")].
Discovery: "Unavailable" status == payload==null mapping (HTML/empty proxy response), distinct from Offline/NotReady. find-tables 404 is service-side (ENG-87092), not clio — so the guard, not a pass, is correct even on a wired stand. Stage/QA tier URLs + worked example (find-lookups/relations green on Stage) documented in Confluence pages 3743219719 / 4783112220.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/DataForgeToolE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/DataForgeReadinessGate.cs, spec/dataforge-e2e-readiness/dataforge-e2e-readiness-spec.md
Impact: 3 fixtures no longer reference a closed ticket; on a non-wired stand (d2/CI) they Skip deterministically (verified on d2: 3 Skipped with descriptive reason), on a wired stand find-lookups/relations can pass. Test-only change; no command/MCP/docs-command surface touched (MCP reviewed, no update required; command docs reviewed, no update required). Happy-path Success=true branch not provable locally (no Stage-tier secret/OAuth client); documented limitation. Branch claude/epic-sutherland-f048b5 off master @9935e533.

## ## 2026-06-30 — ENG-92127: element→process parameter mapping direction & type-compat (analysis, no code)
Context: Verifying the mental model for "Implement element's parameters mapping to the process parameters" before implementing. Reference = a real UsrConcatProcess ProcessSchema (ConcatTask UserTask + ShowTask + process-level params).
Discovery (mapping direction / storage model — confirmed against the real schema):
 - The binding ALWAYS lives on the *consumer/target* parameter, pointing at the source. Two distinct storage shapes:
   * Element INPUT ← process input / another element output: stored both as a `ProcessSchemaMapping` (TargetMetaPath=`[Element:{el}].[Parameter:{p}]`, Source=`[#...[Parameter:{procParam}]#]`) AND mirrored on the element param's own `SourceValue`. Flow = process→element (IN).
   * Process OUTPUT ← element output: stored on the *process* parameter's `SourceValue` (Source:3 formula referencing `[#...[Element:{el}].[Parameter:{outParam}]#]`). Flow = element→process (OUT). The element's output param keeps `SourceValue:{}` (it is produced by the element, a source, never a sink).
 - So "map user-task OUTPUT → process OUTPUT, not the reverse" is CORRECT: a user-task output is a source (IsResult=true, Direction=1), a process output is a sink populated at process end; you configure it from the process-param side selecting the element output as source. SourceValue `Source` enum seen: 1=constant, 3=formula/reference (incl. `[#SysVariable.X#]`).
 - `Direction` ints: 0=Input, 1=Output, 2=Bidirectional/Variable, 3=Internal (see ProcessModel/Schema.cs ProcessParameterDirection).
Discovery (AC#3 "types must be the same" is KIND-level, NOT exact UId): the reference schema maps across DIFFERENT DataValueType UIds — process params are ShortText `{325A73B8-…}` (Text50) while ConcatTask params are Text `{8B3F29BB-…}`, and the platform accepts it. Academy: source list "only displays parameters whose type **corresponds to** the target". So type-compat must be matched on `CreatioDataValueKind` (Text↔Text, Numeric↔Numeric, …), which clio already models in `clio/Common/CreatioDataValueType.cs` (GetKind/IsText/IsNumeric/…). Implementing AC#3 as strict UId equality would wrongly reject this exact working process.
Files: clio/Command/ProcessModel/Schema.cs (ProcessParameterDirection, *DataValueTypeUId consts ~526-566), clio/Common/CreatioDataValueType.cs (kind table + helpers), clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ProcessDesigner/CreateBusinessProcessTool.cs (mappings[] contract: elementName/elementParameter + processParameter|expression|value).
Impact: when wiring element→process param mapping, store the binding on the target (process-output SourceValue for OUT, element-param mapping for IN) and gate validity by CreatioDataValueKind equality, not DataValueType UId equality.

## 2026-06-29 23:30 – De-flake ApplicationGetInfo canonical-main-entity readback after sync-schemas
Context: TeamCity Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests run 15656198 (trunk/master @ 9935e533) showed 1 NEW failure on an otherwise green suite (364 passed/5 ignored): ApplicationToolE2ETests.ApplicationGetInfo_Should_Keep_Canonical_Main_Entity_Caption_After_SchemaSync failed on `canonicalMainEntity.Should().NotBeNull()` (line 464). Test history on trunk interleaves SUCCESS/FAILURE → flaky, not a clio regression (diff fbb485e8..9935e533 = docs + DataForge test + an unrelated Program.cs proxy opt-out; sync-schemas tests in the same build passed).
Decision: Make the get-app-info readback deterministic. After sync-schemas the test now polls get-app-info via new `WaitForCanonicalMainEntityAsync` (up to 39×3s ≈ 2min) until the canonical main entity (Name == app code) appears, instead of a single immediate read. Tolerates transient InvalidOperationException during the window (schema still recompiling); final attempt is unguarded so a permanent failure still surfaces. Test-only change; regression-guard intent preserved (a genuine never-returns regression still exhausts the window and fails NotBeNull).
Discovery: Root cause = eventual consistency — sync-schemas triggers an async server-side schema recompile and the ApplicationEntity readback (ApplicationInfoService.GetApplicationEntities select) races it. Validated on live stand sae_m_seeenu_15655951_0702 (registered as d2, auto-detected .NET Framework): the readback assertion passed 6/6 times it was reached. SEPARATE pre-existing flake observed under rapid repeated runs: create-app itself returned Success=false once (run failed at line 458, not the readback) — transient stand compile-contention from creating 4 apps in ~8min, passed again after settle; not addressed here (CI runs the test once). Note: when create-app fails, the new poll wastes ~2min before the create.Success assert catches it (failing path only; correctness unaffected).
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/ApplicationToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Removes the last intermittent failure on the now-green MCP e2e suite. MCP reviewed, no update required (e2e test-only, no command/tool/prompt/resource/options change); docs reviewed, no update required. Cleaned up 4 residual UsrCodex* test apps from the stand via uninstall-app-remote.
## 2026-06-30 — ENG-92127 MCP surface updated to the new mapping contract (impl lives in ProcessBuilder repo)
Context: The element→element / element→process param mapping for ENG-92127 was implemented in the SEPARATE ProcessBuilder repo (C:\Projects\workspace\ProcessBuilder, package clioprocessbuilder) — see that repo's .codex/workspace-diary.md. clio only carries the agent-facing contract for the create/modify-business-process MCP tools (feature-toggle `process-designer`, not GA), which pass the descriptor JSON through to the clioprocessbuilder web service; clio does NOT validate mappings.
Decision: extended the documented `mappings[]` contract on all four MCP surfaces — TARGET is `{elementName, elementParameter}` (an element input) OR `targetProcessParameter` (a process param, e.g. expose an element OUTPUT as a process output); SOURCE is exactly one of `{sourceElement, sourceElementParameter}` (another element's output), `processParameter`, `value`, or `expression`; parameter↔parameter mappings require compatible types (kind/group, platform parity).
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ProcessDesigner/CreateBusinessProcessTool.cs + ModifyBusinessProcessTool.cs ([Description]), Prompts/ProcessDesigner/CreateBusinessProcessPrompt.cs + ModifyBusinessProcessPrompt.cs, Resources/ProcessDesigner/ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs (authoritative mapping section).
Validation: `dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&Module=McpServer"` 1500/1500 green (net8+net10); clio.csproj builds clean. Remaining: clio.mcp.e2e CreateBusinessProcessToolE2ETests case for the new shapes (needs a live stand with the rebuilt clioprocessbuilder.gz + process-designer enabled); committed command docs are omitted while the verbs are feature-gated.

## 2026-06-29 — ENG-92563 Lever 1: gate MCP-host registration behind an explicit bool
Context: Parent ENG-90640 perf. `BindingsModule.Register(...)` registered the MCP stdio host (AddMcpServer + WithStdioServerTransport + WithRequestFilters + McpFeatureToggleFilter.RegisterEnabledPrimitives — 128 primitives with eager per-tool JSON-schema gen) UNCONDITIONALLY, so every CLI command and every per-env DI build in a live MCP session paid ~11–15ms (Debug) for a host they never use. The genuine payoff is the per-env agent-session containers (ToolCommandResolver), not the CLI.
Decision: Added an explicit `bool registerMcpHost = false` parameter to Register and wrapped the host block in `if (registerMcpHost)`. The flag is threaded EXPLICITLY (never read the static Program.IsMcpServerMode inside BindingsModule — in a live MCP session it is process-wide true, so per-env builds would still register the host). Only Program.cs Resolve<T>'s Container build passes `registerMcpHost: IsMcpServerMode`; the bootstrap bm build, ToolCommandResolver per-env builds, and all other call-sites use the default false.
Discovery (the ValidateOnBuild trap): Register ends with BuildServiceProvider(ValidateOnBuild:true). McpServerCommand's ctor depends on the McpServer singleton, so it MUST move inside the gate too — otherwise non-mcp builds fail validation (McpServerCommand→McpServer unresolvable). The ticket text said "leave AddTransient<McpServerCommand>() untouched"; that premise is WRONG (ValidateOnBuild eagerly validates every registered descriptor), so I gated McpServerCommand with the host. Crucially, the MCP tool TYPE registrations (AddTransient<XTool> at ~349-427) stay UNCONDITIONAL and that is SAFE: only StartTool/StopTool take McpServer in their PRIMARY ctor and they are NOT DI-registered (SDK-only via ActivatorUtilities, never validated); PageUpdateTool/PageSyncTool take McpServer as a METHOD parameter (SDK injects per-call), which DI validation never inspects. So among DI-registered services only McpServerCommand needed gating. Changed Program.IsMcpServerMode setter private→internal so the Program test could reproduce mcp mode.
Files: clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/Program.cs, clio.tests/Command/Program.Tests.cs, clio.tests/Command/BindingsModuleMcpHostGateTests.cs (new)
Impact: every non-mcp CLI command and every per-env agent-session container skip AddMcpServer + 128-primitive eager schema gen. No command/output/exit-code change. ValidateOnBuild + ValidateScopes kept true (acceptance criterion). Validated: Debug build 0 errors / 0 new CLIO warnings (the 2 CLIO005 on business-rule commands pre-exist on master); plain CLI --version + help OK; mcp-server stdio probe initialize + tools/list = 128 (unchanged); full Category=Unit 4499 passed / 0 failed / 34 skipped; 2 new gate tests + the McpServerCommand-resolve test green. MCP reviewed, no update required (DI-internal perf change, tool set unchanged); docs reviewed, no update required. Base = master.

## 2026-06-29 — ENG-91829: finish ClearRedis — cover clio side by unit, drop the unreachable-Redis e2e
Context: ENG-91829's other clusters are already done (cliogate readiness #775, seed pages / PageBusinessRule Contact #784). The only residual was the 2 ClearRedis happy-path e2e tests, hard-[Ignore]'d because the CI agent can't reach the stand's Redis.
Decision/key fact: clear-redis does NOT touch Redis from clio — `RedisCommand` is a `RemoteCommand` that POSTs the Creatio `ClearRedisDb` route (ServiceUrlBuilder.KnownRoute.ClearRedisDb) via IApplicationClient; Creatio flushes its OWN Redis. The e2e's `RedisSandboxClient` connected to the stand's Redis only for arrange (seed key) + assert (verify gone). clio's entire contribution (POST the right route, {} body, FW `/0/` vs Core path) is ALREADY unit-covered by clio.tests/Command/RedisCommand.Tests.cs (`ClearRedisDb_FormsCorrectApplicationRequest_When*`, ×2) + MCP mapping by clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ClearRedisToolTests.cs (×3). So the happy-path e2e added nothing for clio coverage — its only unique value was observing Creatio's backend flush, which needs a runner-reachable Redis the CI agents lack. Removed both happy-path tests + their now-dead arrange/asserts/teardown (RedisSandboxClient seed, SandboxEnvironmentResolver.Resolve, _seedKey/_sandboxContext fields). Kept the 2 env-free negative tests (invalid env name / invalid URL → fail before any Redis), which already run everywhere via ArrangeWithoutRedisAsync. No new unit test needed (coverage already existed).
Discovery: RedisDatabaseSelector (FindEmptyDatabase) is a DIFFERENT concern (install picks an empty db) — not the clear path. clear = pure HTTP POST.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/ClearRedisToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: CI e2e ignored 5→3 (the 2 ClearRedis [Ignore]s gone), 0 lost coverage (clio side unit-covered). ENG-91829's clio scope complete; residual = infra-only (reachable Redis) which is deliberately not pursued. Validated: ClearRedis unit 6 passed; the 2 negative e2e pass on a live stand (net10.0); e2e build clean. Branch off master @9935e533 (post-#784).

## 2026-06-30 – ENG-90636 native theme build (`build-theme`) + theming feature-gate (uncommitted slice)
Native `build-theme` = C# OKLCH colour math ported from the retired @creatio/theming (bit-exact vs frozen TS goldens on net8.0 AND net10.0 — `Math.Pow`/`Cbrt` did not diverge from V8, so R-08's fallback was unneeded; operator-for-operator: `RoundHalfUp` round-then-clamp, `byte.Parse(HexNumber, InvariantCulture)`, count-1 regex + `\z` end-anchor + post-fill guard) plus a bundled version-pinned template shipped in clio under `clio/tpl/themes/{version}/` (`theme.css.tpl`+`theme.json.tpl`), read via `IThemeTemplateProvider` (`LastOrDefault(v<=target)`, below-min -> ArgumentException). Surface: local `BuildThemeCommand : Command<BuildThemeOptions>` + a flat `ComponentInfoTool`-style `BuildThemeTool`, returning `BuildThemeResult { success, css, descriptor, warnings?, error? }` — `warnings` is a plural `IReadOnlyList<string>` (mirrors `ApplicationDataForgeResult.Warnings`); font-weights given without a custom `--heading-font`/`--body-font` are warn+ignore. Version source `--version` (renamed from `--creatio-version` for ComponentInfo parity) XOR `--environment-name`: explicit wins; env -> `IPlatformVersionResolverFactory` (unresolvable/"latest" -> highest bundled); both -> mutually-exclusive error; neither -> highest bundled. Per the "all theming under one feature" decision, every theme command (build/create/update/delete/list/clear-themes-cache) + its MCP tool + `ThemingGuidanceResource` carries ONE `[FeatureToggle("theming")]` key, dark until go-live — reversing the earlier server-flow/devflow "ship enabled, no toggle" decisions (their ADRs/PRD marked SUPERSEDED -> native-build ADR OQ-02). All 10 `Command/*Theme*.cs` moved to `Command/Theming/` (`namespace Clio.Command.Theming`); guidance swapped npm -> `build-theme`; the `@creatio/theming` scaffold dep (`tpl/ui-project/package.json:41`) is deliberately NOT removed — deferred to story-7 (R-01: gated on the token-catalog rehoming + a cross-repo devkit-guide repoint; removing early leaves an author-time doc gap). All native-build + server-flow + clio-devflow specs realigned to the code (feature key, `--version`, result shape, class names, OQ-02 resolved). Merge of origin/master (+246) during this slice: `ServiceUrlBuilder.KnownRoute` enum-append is a recurring conflict hotspot (renumber the later-merged side above master's max — theme routes now 53-57), and `dotnet test --no-build` on net10.0 after a net8.0-only build runs a stale dll (build each TFM before testing it). Status: Stories 1-6 done, full unit suite green on both TFMs; EVERYTHING uncommitted pending consent; story-7 (dep removal) + story-8 (go-live: flip the toggle + add the build-theme tool row to McpCapabilityMap) deferred. Files: clio/Theming/*, clio/Command/Theming/*, clio/tpl/themes/10.0/*, clio/{Program,BindingsModule}.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/{Tools/*Theme*,Resources/{ThemingGuidanceResource,GuidanceCatalog}}.cs, clio.tests/{Theming/*,Command/**/*Theme*Tests.cs}, clio.mcp.e2e/BuildThemeToolE2ETests.cs, spec/{adr,stories,test-plans,sprint-status}.

## 2026-07-01 – ENG-90636 build-theme MCP workspace-write mode (D2-A, uncommitted)
Context: analyzing the theming MCP surface surfaced that the `build-theme` MCP tool was pure-compute (returns css) while the guide's workspace/dev flow told MCP agents to use CLI `build-theme --output` — which the MCP tool cannot do (Finding F2). D2-A decision: give the MCP tool a workspace-write mode. Chosen over keeping it pure because in compute mode the full theme.css round-trips through the model twice (tool response + follow-up file-write arg = expensive output tokens); writing inside the tool and returning only the path keeps the large CSS out of the agent context.
Decision: input shape is `workspaceDirectory` (absolute) + `packageName` (NOT a raw output path), mirroring `new-ui-project` — the agent supplies workspace+package identity, clio resolves `<ws>/packages/<pkg>/Files/themes/<cssClassName>/`. Revised ADR D1 from "pure read-only compute" (flags now ReadOnly=false/Destructive=false/Idempotent=true/OpenWorld=false); OQ-03/consequences/checklist + story-4 + tp + sprint-status realigned in place.
Discovery: setting `IWorkspacePathBuilder.RootPath` then using `IsWorkspace`/`BuildPackagePath` is the canonical layout-resolve pattern (~7 commands do it: CreateUserTask/DeleteSchema/CreateWorkspace/Workspace.cs/...), so do NOT hand-roll the .clio/packages/Files constants (first pass did; refactored to reuse). FS checks + resolution live in `BuildThemeCommand` (owns IFileSystem) so the flat tool stays thin and the mode is unit-testable with substitutes — no reliance on the singleton path builder's CWD walk-up. Build/write logic is shared: private `WriteArtifacts` + `TryBuildTheme(options,outputDir,...)` overload serve CLI `--output`, and `TryBuildTheme(options,workspaceDirectory,packageName,...)` layers workspace/package validation on top. MCP tool stays flat (R-02): inline params + `[Description]`, no args-record; two new params at BuildThemeTool.cs:58-59. `BuildThemeResult` gained `path` (output-only; css/descriptor omitted in write mode). BuildThemeCommand gained an `IWorkspacePathBuilder` ctor dep — resolves via existing AddTransient<BuildThemeCommand> (no BindingsModule change, no full-suite trigger).
Files: clio/Command/Theming/BuildThemeCommand.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/BuildThemeTool.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/BuildThemeToolTests.cs (+4 cases), clio.mcp.e2e/BuildThemeToolE2ETests.cs (ReadOnly=false + workspace-write E2E), spec/{adr/adr-theming-native-build,stories/story-theming-native-build-4,test-plans/tp-theming-native-build,sprint-status}.
Impact: workspace/dev flow is now MCP-native (was CLI-only). Validated: clio + clio.tests + clio.mcp.e2e build clean net10.0, no CLIO warnings on theming files; targeted unit run Category=Unit&(Module=McpServer|Command|Theming) = 3604 passed/0 failed/11 skipped. STILL OPEN: uncommitted (needs consent); manual MCP E2E not run; F1 = rewrite ThemingGuidanceResource workspace step to route MCP agents to workspaceDirectory+packageName (still shows CLI `--output`) — until then agents aren't guided to the new capability.


## 2026-07-01 – check-theming-access MCP tool + reusable Creatio rights/license clients (uncommitted)
Context: precheck whether a caller may manage themes on an environment — operation right `CanManageThemes` + license `CanCustomizeBranding` — grounded in creatio-ui `ThemePermissionsService` (RightsService.getCanExecuteOperation + LicenseService.getLicenseOperationStatuses). clio had NO client-side rights/license check before this (only cliogate SERVER-side `DBSecurityEngine.GetCanExecuteOperation`).
Decision: two reusable Common-layer clients over a shared abstract base, plus one MCP-only tool (no CLI verb) that composes them.
- `Clio.Common`: `ICreatioRightsClient`/`CreatioRightsClient` -> `RightsService.svc/GetCanExecuteOperation` ({"operation":name} -> {"GetCanExecuteOperationResult":bool}); `ICreatioLicenseClient`/`CreatioLicenseClient` -> `LicenseService.svc/GetLicOperationStatuses` ({"licOperationCodes":[...]} -> {"GetLicOperationStatusesResult":{success,licOperationStatuses:[{Key,Value}]}}). Both derive `abstract CreatioServiceClient` (shared JsonOptions + build-url→serialize→POST→empty/non-JSON→InvalidOperationException). Naming follows the `CreatioVersionChecker`/`ICreatioVersionChecker` convention.
- Method shape mirrors the Angular reference: plain return (bool / IReadOnlyDictionary<string,bool>), NOT Try+out; transport/parse failure THROWS, caught at the edge (MCP tool -> failure result). A license success=false payload -> empty map -> not granted.
- Request settings are a Common-level `CreatioRequestOptions` (record; TimeOut/MaxAttempts/RetryDelay, defaults 100000/3/1) so the Common clients never depend on the Command layer; the tool builds a bare `new CreatioRequestOptions()` and does NOT set a timeout — it relies on the 100s default (the underlying `ExecutePostRequest` default is `Timeout.Infinite`, so a bounded default is what matters). In the same pass the whole theme family (list/create/update/delete/clear-themes-cache tools) had its hardcoded MCP `TimeOut = 30_000` removed for the same reason — a named timeout const is used elsewhere only when there is a caller-overridable range (execute-esq) or multiple distinct timeouts (ApplicationTool), neither of which applies here.
- MCP: `CheckThemingAccessTool` — a plain `[McpServerToolType]` + `[FeatureToggle("theming")]` tool injecting ONLY `IToolCommandResolver` (NOT `BaseTool`, since it wraps no command — same shape as `ExecuteEsqTool`/`ODataReadTool`/`ClearBrowserSessionTool`, which resolve services + compose). ReadOnly; `check-theming-access-by-environment` / `-by-credentials`; builds a bare `EnvironmentOptions` (the exact type `IToolCommandResolver.Resolve<T>(EnvironmentOptions)` consumes) to resolve BOTH clients per-call from the env-scoped container, then composes -> `{success,canManageThemes,canCustomizeBranding,hasThemingAccess}`.
- ServiceUrlBuilder KnownRoute ids 58/59 (RightsGetCanExecuteOperation/LicenseGetLicOperationStatuses). Guidance: ThemingGuidanceResource no-code prereq points to check-theming-access; McpCapabilityMap updated.
Discovery: clio has no base "Creatio service client" — inline `IApplicationClient.ExecutePostRequest` + per-class parse is the norm; the generic `ExecutePostRequest<T> where T:BaseResponse` is bound to the `{success,errorInfo}` envelope and does NOT fit RightsService's `{GetCanExecuteOperationResult}`. `ServiceUrlBuilder.Build(KnownRoute)` returns a FULL absolute URL (FW `/0/` baked in). `IToolCommandResolver.Resolve<T>(EnvironmentOptions)` is UNCONSTRAINED, so an MCP tool can resolve non-command env-scoped services (clients) from the per-call container — no wrapping command needed. `RemoteEntitySchemaDesignerClient` is the client prior-art but takes RemoteCommandOptions because it is Command-layer; Common-layer clients must not.
Files: clio/Common/{CreatioRequestOptions,CreatioServiceClient,CreatioRightsClient,CreatioLicenseClient}.cs, clio/Common/ServiceUrlBuilder.cs (58/59), clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/CheckThemingAccessTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ThemingGuidanceResource.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, docs/McpCapabilityMap.md, clio.tests/Common/{CreatioRightsClient,CreatioLicenseClient}Tests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/CheckThemingAccessToolTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/CheckThemingAccessToolE2ETests.cs.
Impact: reusable rights/license plumbing for any future precheck; theming server-flow has a one-call guided precheck (MCP-only). Validated: full Category=Unit 4962 passed/0 failed on net8.0 AND net10.0; clio + clio.tests + clio.mcp.e2e build clean (0 errors, no new CLIO warnings). STILL OPEN: uncommitted (needs consent); no CLI verb by design; hermetic e2e only asserts the two tool names are advertised (gated on `theming`); manual MCP E2E not run.
## ## 2026-06-30 — ENG-91314 reopened: HTML-error diagnostic + remove-package-dependency + guidance
Context: ENG-91314 (add-package-dependency, PR #698) was REOPENED after two ADAC sessions (Kateryna). Evidence (session exports): the Codex "Client status reference book" rollout hit `GetSchemaDesignItem returned an HTML error page` ~14× while adding a Lookup column to Opportunity in package Custom and NEVER reached add-package-dependency — it followed the error message's misdirection ("stale table → find-entity-schema"), tried dangerous workarounds (writing the column directly into the managed CrtLeadOppMgmtApp pkg, raw odata on SysSchema, duplicate lookups). The Claude session DID reach add-package-dependency (succeeded) but on rollback found there was no way to REMOVE a dependency.
Decision (scope confirmed with Alex, remove included now):
1. The error message is the lever (pushed into context at failure; pull-only guidance is ignored per [[eng-91134-component-discovery-decomposition]]). Enriched RemoteEntitySchemaDesignerClient.cs:289 InvalidOperationException to name BOTH causes in order — (1) MISSING A DEPENDENCY on the owner of the extended object's upper layer → add-package-dependency; (2) stale table → find-entity-schema — plus "do NOT write into the managed pkg / no raw SQL/OData/DataService" and "MCP agents: get-guidance name=package-dependencies". No clio context at line 289 to auto-detect which cause, so both listed honestly.
2. NEW remove-package-dependency CLI verb + MCP tool, symmetric mirror of add. IPackageDependencyManager.RemoveDependencies(pkg, names): match by name (case-insensitive), RemoveAll, SAVE ONLY when removedCount>0 (no-op stays side-effect-free), return resulting names. MCP tool Destructive=true (it drops an entry, unlike add) Idempotent=true. Wired BindingsModule (tool+command) + Program.cs (verb list + dispatch).
3. NEW PackageDependenciesGuidanceResource (docs://mcp/guides/package-dependencies) registered in GuidanceCatalog + RoutingGuidanceResource (row under both Entities&schemas and the HTML-error symptom). Keyed to the exact symptom string.
Discovery: ReadmeChecker (BaseCommandTests) enforces docs for EVERY command — a new verb fails Command_ShouldHave_DescriptionBlock_InReadmeFile until it has all 4: Commands.md `(docs/commands/X.md)` link, help/en/X.txt, docs/commands/X.md, and a `X:` line in Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt. GetPackages() goes through DataService SelectQuery (SysPackage), NOT cliogate. Many registered SAE/older stands 404 that endpoint (degraded/torn-down); core 10.1.19.0 studio stands `dev`/`adac-test` are healthy.
Files: clio/Command/EntitySchemaDesigner/RemoteEntitySchemaDesignerClient.cs, clio/Command/RemovePackageDependencyCommand.cs(new), clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/RemovePackageDependencyTool.cs(new), clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/PackageDependenciesGuidanceResource.cs(new)+GuidanceCatalog.cs+RoutingGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Package/{IPackageDependencyManager,PackageDependencyManager}.cs, clio/{BindingsModule,Program}.cs, docs (Commands.md, help/en, docs/commands, Wiki/WikiAnchors, docs/McpCapabilityMap.md), tests (PackageDependencyManager.Tests, RemovePackageDependencyCommand.Tests, McpServer/RemovePackageDependencyToolTests, RemoteEntitySchemaDesignerClientTests, McpGuidanceResourceTests) + clio.mcp.e2e/RemovePackageDependencyToolE2ETests.
Impact: agent that hits the HTML error now sees the dependency root cause + the one-call fix in context (the thing that misdirected Codex), and can roll back a dependency it added. Verified: full Unit 4722 passed/0 failed (net10.0); Release build clean (no new CLIO/warnings). LIVE on env `dev` (core 10.1.19.0): add CrtBaseConsts→{CrtCore,CrtBaseConsts}, re-add idempotent (no dup), remove→{CrtCore} net-zero; no-op remove of absent dep doesn't save; add of non-installed dep → clear "not found". HTML-message runtime repro not performed (needs a layered-extend failure on a stand) — covered deterministically by the unit test.
## ## 2026-06-30 — ENG-92127 E2E VERIFIED on a live stand + clio describe DTO gap fixed
Context: Verifying the element-parameter mapping on the test-stand stand (local IIS "Creatio" :1026, physical path C:\Projects\Creatio\TSBpm\..., clioprocessbuilder Pkg folder is a JUNCTION to C:\Projects\workspace\ProcessBuilder\packages\clioprocessbuilder — FS shared, FSM on).
Discovery (deploy saga root cause): the runtime kept serving OLD clioprocessbuilder behavior through ~8 deploy attempts (build+restart, pkg-to-db, push-pkg×N, clear-redis, compile-creatio). TWO independent causes, both finally understood:
 1. REAL blocker: the package UID was mismatched — workspace descriptor.json UId f100e6d2-... vs the stand DB SysPackage.UId 8219c8e1-... So Creatio saw same-name-different-UID and never replaced the runtime assembly ("The package already exists with a different UID" on FS-update). FIXED by the user unlocking the package + me aligning the workspace descriptor UId to 8219c8e1 (working-tree only, NOT committed — canonical git UId stays f100e6d2) + the user loading from file system. push-pkg also kept failing its post-install cliogate Unlock step (UnlockPackages returns non-JSON → broken on the stand) — the FS-load path bypasses it.
 2. FALSE NEGATIVE that masked success: clio's DescribeProcessCommand DESERIALIZES the server response into Clio.Command.ProcessModel.DescribedParameter and re-serializes it — and that DTO had no `direction`/`isResult`, so the new server fields were STRIPPED from describe output. My chosen deploy indicator (direction/isResult in describe) was therefore invisible regardless of stand state. source/value are NOT stripped — used those to verify instead.
VERIFICATION (live, test-stand, process UsrEng92127Test): modify-business-process addParameter(ProcResult, Guid, Out) + addMapping{targetProcessParameter:ProcResult, sourceElement:DoTask, sourceElementParameter:ActivityResult} → describe shows ProcResult source=Script value="[#...[Element:{DoTask}].[Parameter:{ActivityResult}]#]" (AC#2 ✓, element-qualified meta-path auto-built). A type-mismatch addMapping (Integer Duration → Guid ProcResult) was rejected with "Cannot map 'Duration' to 'ProcResult': ... incompatible data value types ..." (AC#3 ✓). The new mapping fields being honored at all proves the stand runs my code.
Fix this session: added `Direction` (string) + `IsResult` (bool?) to DescribedParameter (clio/Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs) so describe surfaces what the server returns (omitted vs older servers via WhenWritingNull). Validated: Category=Unit&(Module=ProcessModel|Module=McpServer) 1624/1624 green (net8+net10); clio.csproj clean.
Open items: (a) descriptor.json UId discrepancy stand(8219c8e1) vs git(f100e6d2) — reconcile properly (working-tree change is uncommitted/local); (b) the stand's cliogate UnlockPackages endpoint returns non-JSON — worth a separate look; (c) clio.mcp.e2e automated coverage still pending.
Files: clio/Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs.
Impact: ENG-92127 is implemented, reviewed, unit-verified (176 ProcessBuilder + 1624 clio), AND now E2E-verified live (AC#2 + AC#3). The describe surface now reports direction/isResult.

## 2026-06-30 19:32 — ENG-92127 test gaps filled (ProcessBuilder unit + clio unit + clio E2E)
Context: After live E2E verification, the coverage assessment flagged three gaps. "добавь тестов которые нужны" — closed them across both repos.
Decision: split coverage by ownership. Server-side type/projection logic → ProcessBuilder unit tests; clio's only contributions (DTO round-trip + MCP forwarding/readback) → clio unit + clio E2E.
Files (ProcessBuilder repo, committed 1aa9dfe): tests/clioprocessbuilder/ParameterTypeCompatibilityTests.cs (type-group matrix 14 cases, exotic exact-match fallback, lookup reference-object rule), tests/clioprocessbuilder/ProcessDescriberTests.cs (element-parameter projection filter: surfaces outputs/IsResult + bound params, omits unbound inputs). Suite 195/195 green (dev-nf, net472).
Files (clio repo): clio.tests/Command/DescribeProcessCommandTests.cs — Execute_ShouldWriteParameterDirectionAndIsResult_WhenPresent (the re-serialization regression: element param direction/isResult must survive the DescribedParameter DTO round-trip); clio.tests/Command/ProcessModel/ServerProcessDescriberTests.cs — Describe_ShouldReadParameterDirectionAndIsResult_WhenServerReportsThem + _WhenServerOmitsThem (wire deserialize + older-server null-safety). Committed f5c86ae9. clio.mcp.e2e/CreateBusinessProcessToolE2ETests.cs — CreateBusinessProcess_Should_MirrorElementParameterType_AndSurfaceDirectionOnReadback (typeFromElement mirror of task1/Recommendation + describe surfaces "direction"). Committed 0b67725c.
Discovery: clio E2E for the mapping SOURCE shapes (element->element, element->process-param) can't be made self-contained — "Recommendation" is a mapping TARGET (an element input), so it's not a valid source, and ActivityUserTask's real output param name isn't known here. Scoped clio E2E to what clio owns (forward typeFromElement; read back direction); the source/target combos + type-compat are covered by ProcessBuilder unit tests + the live manual TC-01..TC-13. typeFromElement copies the type direction-agnostically, so mirroring an input param is valid.
Validation: clio Category=Unit&(Module=Command|Module=ProcessModel) 1969/1969 green on net8.0 (net10 build skipped — running local MCP server holds bin/Debug/net10.0/clio.dll); clio.mcp.e2e net8.0 build 0 errors. E2E not in CI — run manually against a stand with ProcessDesignService + the ActivityUserTask "Recommendation" param.
Impact: ENG-92127 coverage now complete at all three tiers. Note for whoever runs the new E2E: it needs the rebuilt clioprocessbuilder on the stand and process-designer feature enabled; if a stand's "Perform task" lacks a "Recommendation" param, adjust the handle in BuildTypeMirrorDescriptor.

## 2026-06-30 - ENG-91843 clio side: describe `direction` field + AI surface + e2e (parameter ops)
Context: ENG-91843 "Add and modify process parameters" — the clioprocessbuilder server gained default value / setParameter / removeParameter+dependency-validation / supported-type allow-list / describe direction; this is the clio MCP layer that surfaces them. Branch feature/ENG-91843-process-parameters.
Decision: operations stay OPAQUE pass-through (no typed op model in clio), so the clio change is surface-only: (1) DescribedParameter gained a `direction` field (IProcessDescriber.cs) — auto-maps from the server's new describe `direction` via [JsonPropertyName] + case-insensitive System.Text.Json and re-serializes into describe output; (2) Modify/Create tool [Description]s + both prompts + ProcessModelingGuidanceResource now teach setParameter/removeParameter/addParameter-`value` + the supported-type set + direction read-back; (3) ADR adr-ENG-90883 incremented (Key design choices #8-#11) AND scrubbed of retrospective/PR framing to read as current source-of-truth.
Discovery: type AND direction are editable in the visual designer (an earlier "immutable" assumption was wrong) — type-change is deferred by SCOPE (value/reference re-validation), direction is editable; ADR #10 corrected. The describe identity arg `process-code` returned "provide exactly one…" while `process-uid` worked in the running MCP — used uid for live checks; pre-existing describe-arg quirk, orthogonal to this ticket, worth a follow-up.
Tests: clio.mcp.e2e ModifyBusinessProcessToolE2ETests (+4: setParameter+direction read-back, blocked remove, type-change rejected, unsupported-type rejected) + CreateBusinessProcessToolE2ETests (+1: create-with-default → ConstValue). All 5 PASS against pb over the real MCP path (2m44s + 50s background runs, 0 skipped). clio unit suite (Module=McpServer|ProcessModel) NOT run in this checkout — building net10.0 locks the running MCP apphost; defer to CI or a stop-MCP run.
Live verify (pb): describe UsrClioParamDefaultS2 → Retries direction "Out" (C1); setParameter value 3->5, Note->NoteText UId-preserved; setParameter type-change and addParameter Binary both rejected with clear messages ("…data type … is not supported"; "Parameter type 'Binary' is not supported. Supported types: Text, Long text, Integer, Float, Money, Boolean, Date, Date-time, Time, Guid, and Lookup…").
Files: clio/Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ProcessDesigner/{Modify,Create}BusinessProcessTool.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/ProcessDesigner/{Modify,Create}BusinessProcessPrompt.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ProcessDesigner/ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs; spec/adr/adr-ENG-90883-backend-process-designer.md; clio.mcp.e2e/{Modify,Create}BusinessProcessToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: clio surfaces the full parameter add/update/remove/default capability to AI agents with direction read-back; all server behavior lives in cli-process-builder (separate diary). ENG-91843 AC-complete + e2e-green on pb.

## ## 2026-07-01 — ENG-92127: e2e for element→process mapping + incompatible-type (PR #800 review follow-up)
Context: PR #800 reviewer (a-serhiichyk-creatio) flagged deferred clio.mcp.e2e for the new mapping shapes. User asked to write them proactively even though e2e isn't on CI yet.
Discovery: needed a user task with a scalar OUTPUT (mapping source). Scanned all 162 ProcessUserTask schemas in C:\Projects\PackageStore (node scan; discriminator = Resources/<Name>.ProcessUserTask; params in metadata.json FJ1; **output marker = obfuscated key `L13:true` (=IsResult)**, cracked via ActivityUserTask.ActivityResult which the earlier live verify used). 30 tasks have a scalar output. Picked **CrtBase :: CheckCanExecuteOperationUserTask → CanExecuteOperation:Boolean** — CrtBase is always installed, and the builder resolves a task by name via ProcessUserTaskSchemaManager.FindInstanceByName (the FULL superset, not the SysProcessUserTask palette subset — confirmed in clioprocessbuilder UserTaskElementHandler.cs:67 / UserTaskCatalog.cs:17-19), so a non-palette internal task still builds by name (list-user-tasks would NOT list it, but the descriptor hardcodes userTaskName so that's irrelevant).
Added (clio.mcp.e2e/CreateBusinessProcessToolE2ETests.cs, committed 5a5528e8): MapElementOutputIntoProcessParameter (AC#2: targetProcessParameter ← sourceElement/sourceElementParameter; describe readback of the process param + element output) and RejectMapping_WhenTypesAreIncompatible (AC#3: Boolean output → Integer process param → asserts "incompatible" surfaces; the equivalent modify addMapping rejection was verified live 2026-06-30). userTaskName + output-param name are fixture constants (SourceUserTaskName/SourceOutputParameter) for a one-line swap to the palette ActivityUserTask/ActivityResult:Guid.
Validation: clio.mcp.e2e net8 build 0 errors. NOT run (e2e not in CI; needs a live stand with process-designer enabled). First live run may need assertion tweaks (how clio surfaces the server error / whether the generic-element task exposes CanExecuteOperation in describe).
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/CreateBusinessProcessToolE2ETests.cs; scan script in session scratchpad.
Impact: the PR #800 residual (mapping-shape e2e) is now written and CI-safe-gated; remaining un-added: element→element (needs a 2nd element with a compatible input; deferred).
## 2026-07-01 16:00 – LoadSchema: gate enriched re-issue on schema availability (ENG-91314)
Context: PR #802 review (b-horodyskyi, Major/correctness) — enriched-error re-issue was gated on `response == null`, but `TryGetSchemaDesignItem` returns a non-null envelope whenever `Success == true`, so a `Success == true` response with a null `Schema` skipped the `GetSchemaDesignItem` re-issue and emitted the generic "returned no schema" message, losing the missing-dependency diagnostic.
Decision: Recompute `schemaUnavailable` after the auto-resolve retry and gate the re-issue on `if (schemaUnavailable)` instead of `if (response == null)`.
Discovery: Existing `SetupUnavailableSchema` mock already returns a non-null response with `Schema == null`, so the old tests only asserted the exception type, not that the enriched `GetSchemaDesignItem` path actually fired — strengthened them with message + `Received(1)` assertions and added a `Success=true, Schema=null` regression test.
Files: clio/Command/EntitySchemaDesigner/RemoteEntitySchemaColumnManager.cs, clio.tests/Command/RemoteEntitySchemaColumnManagerTests.cs
Impact: Both LoadSchema call sites (write + verification reload) now surface the enriched diagnostic on any unavailable schema, not just the HTML-500 path.

## 2026-07-01 17:30 - ENG-92558 PR #804 review fix: pre-migrate shared clio home once (Major #2)
Context: PR #804 review (m-dymytrova) Major — vetted parallel NoEnvironment fixtures that do not override ConfigureMcpServerSettings share the runner's global CLIO_HOME; under 2 workers two [OneTimeSetUp]s start two clio mcp-server processes whose SettingsBootstrapService.Load(applyRepairs=true) WRITES appsettings.json on a missing/legacy home, a cross-process race with only an in-process lock.
Decision: Added assembly-wide [SetUpFixture] McpSharedHomeSetUpFixture (namespace Clio.Mcp.E2E) whose [OneTimeSetUp] starts one shared MCP server with default (non-overridden) settings and disposes it, so the create/migrate write happens once, serially, before any parallel fixture; later loads on that home are read-only and the race window never opens. Isolated-home fixtures (OAuth/canary) are unaffected.
Discovery: In-process priming would be wrong — with CLIO_HOME unset, AppSettingsFolderPath keys off the entry assembly's Company/Product, so the testhost process resolves a different home than the clio.dll child. Starting a real child server is the only way to migrate the same home the parallel children read. Verified: e2e builds clean; McpFixturePolicyTests + FindApp/AddItemModel contract fixtures pass (4/4) with ~11s one-time server start proving the SetUpFixture runs.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/McpSharedHomeSetUpFixture.cs, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: The shared-global-home write race for the vetted parallel NoEnvironment cohort is closed; full cohort timing/behaviour proof remains a CI/TeamCity concern (needs a stand).

## 2026-07-01 17:30 - ENG-92558 PR #804 review fix: Sandbox guard CI coverage confirmed (Major #1)
Context: PR #804 review (m-dymytrova) Major — McpFixturePolicyTests ([Category("Unit")]) lives in clio.mcp.e2e; the GitHub Actions unit lane only runs clio.tests.csproj, so the reviewer feared AC-05's Sandbox-seriality guard never executes automatically.
Decision: No code change (variant C). Confirmed via TeamCity that the guard does execute automatically: build config Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests runs `dotnet test clio.mcp.e2e/clio.mcp.e2e.csproj` with NO --filter (runsettings sets only NumberOfTestWorkers=2), so the whole project incl. Category=Unit runs; build 15666087 executed and passed both McpFixturePolicyTests cases (395 tests total).
Discovery: The VCS trigger branchFilter is `+:<default> +:8.* +:10.*`, so this build auto-runs on master + 8.x/10.x release branches (and manual triggers), not as a per-PR required GitHub check. Protection is real but at integration level (post-merge/master), not a pre-merge PR gate.
Files: (none - investigation only)
Impact: Reviewer's "guard never runs" premise is disproven; open follow-up if a per-PR required gate is wanted (add a GitHub Actions step running clio.mcp.e2e --filter "Category=Unit").

## 2026-07-02 12:05 – Fix process-builder build warnings (CS9113 + E2E nullability)
Context: Clean up compiler warnings from the cli-process-builder PR #11 build output (process-designer surface only).
Decision: Removed the unused `ILogger logger` primary-ctor param from `ServerProcessDescriber` (CS9113) — the command layer (`DescribeProcessCommand`) already surfaces every error, so the describer's logger was dead. For the four process-designer E2E fixtures, adopted the repo's `string? environmentName = settings.Sandbox.EnvironmentName;` idiom (settings props are `string?`), used `!` at the `IsEnvironmentReachableAsync` call (matches `GenerateProcessModelToolE2ETests`), and made each `ArrangeContext` record's `EnvironmentName`/`ProcessCode` nullable — honest because the `requireReachableEnvironment:false` path skips the null-guard and all consumers only feed `Dictionary<string,object?>`.
Discovery: The net10.0 `clio.dll`/`clio.exe` in bin are locked by two running clio MCP-server processes (this session's), so a normal build/test can't copy obj→bin; verified via `-p:BuildProjectReferences=false` (E2E), redirected `--output` (clio), and ran ProcessModel units on `-f net8.0` (unlocked) → 88 passed. Left intentionally: CLIO005 x2 (business-rule commands) and GetBrowserSession CS8600 / EntitySchema CS8625 — unrelated features. MCP + docs reviewed, no update required (no command contract/option/behavior change).
Files: clio/Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs, clio.tests/Command/ProcessModel/ServerProcessDescriberTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/DescribeProcessToolE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/CreateBusinessProcessToolE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/ModifyBusinessProcessToolE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/ListUserTasksToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Process-designer projects compile warning-free; future edits to these E2E Arrange helpers have a clean nullable baseline.

## 2026-07-02 – Fix RightsService route in theming precheck
Root cause: `RightsGetCanExecuteOperation` used the WCF path `ServiceModel/RightsService.svc/...`, which returns an empty body; the working endpoint is `/rest/RightsService/GetCanExecuteOperation`. Fixed the route in `ServiceUrlBuilder`; response shape (`GetCanExecuteOperationResult`) unchanged.
Also removed the derived `HasThemingAccess` field from `ThemingAccessResult` (now returns only `canManageThemes` + `canCustomizeBranding`) and made the precheck advisory in the theming guidance — `create-theme` is the authoritative access test.
Files: clio/Common/ServiceUrlBuilder.cs, clio/Common/CreatioRightsClient.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/CheckThemingAccessTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ThemingGuidanceResource.cs (+ tests).

## 2026-07-02 – Theme preview: base -500 only, presentation to skill
Remark: preview showed full palette ramps (stops 50,100,300,500,800) as raw hex, which users don't understand. Fix (variant B): `theme-color-advisor operation=preview` default now emits only the base -500 per role (`PreviewStops = { 500 }`); `fullStops=true` still returns all 12. Moved colour-presentation format out of clio guidance (dropped "inline-code swatches") into the CAADT skill, which now renders a visual swatch of the base -500 and hides stops unless asked. clio owns value+verdict, skill owns presentation.
Files: clio/Command/Theming/ThemeColorAdvisor.cs, ThemeColorAdvisorResult.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ThemeColorAdvisorTool.cs, Resources/ThemingGuidanceResource.cs, CAADT skills/creatio-theme-orchestrator/SKILL.md (+ unit & e2e tests).

Remark: only persona/noun-first name in the ~150-tool MCP surface (siblings verb-first: build-theme/create-theme/list-themes). Renamed wire name + the wire-adapter class ThemeColorAdvisorTool → AdviseThemePaletteTool (+ its test classes/files + DI reg). Kept service cluster ThemeColorAdvisor/IThemeColorAdvisor/ThemeColorAdvisorResult — Result is the service's return type (paired with the service), unlike tool-owned BuildThemeResult. MCP-only, so no CLI help/docs/alias; e2e invokes via ToolName const. Spec/ADR left as history.

## 2026-07-03 14:30 – Theming MCP: single canonical tools + standard args wrapper
Context: agent session analysis showed opaque MCP binding failures (flat/wrapped + camelCase/kebab mix); decision — drop -by-credentials twins, rename theming tools to clean CLI-verb names, migrate all 8 to the standard args wrapper.
Decision: one tool per operation (create-theme, list-themes, update-theme, delete-theme, clear-themes-cache, check-theming-access, build-theme, advise-theme-palette); kebab args records with soft binding, in-body structured validation (FromValidationError), ExtensionData + LegacyAliases rename hints; credentials mode covered by reg-web-app → env tools.
Discovery: wrapped+hard-wrapper = 101/150 methods, flat+hard = 40 (incl. old theming), wrapped+soft = 0; describe-environment's uri fallback lacks is-net-core (silent NET472 assumption) — backlog with group-101 opacity fix.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/*Theme*.cs+CheckThemingAccessTool.cs+AdviseThemePaletteTool.cs, Resources/ThemingGuidanceResource.cs, docs/McpCapabilityMap.md, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{8 tool tests,GuidanceGetToolTests}, clio.mcp.e2e/{8 fixtures,GuidanceGetToolE2ETests}.
Impact: wrong args → structured kebab-named error instead of generic invocation failure; per-tool round-trip tests (real MCP serializer options, ComponentInfoToolTests pattern) pin the JSON->record binding, hermetic e2e wire tests pin the args wrapper + validation errors through the real server. Validated: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&Module=McpServer"; dotnet test clio.mcp.e2e --filter FullyQualifiedName~Theme (15 passed against the real MCP server). Follow-up: sync CAADT theme-orchestrator skill arg/tool names after merge.

## 2026-07-03 – Theming css-class-name rule: single source + path-boundary guard
Root cause: valid-css-class-name rule (`^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_-]*` + max 100) was copied in 3 places (ThemeCssBuilder `\z`, ThemeRequestBuilder `$`, ThemeCssClassName max-len) — already drifted (`$` accepted trailing `\n`); and ThemeCssClassName.TryResolve returned an explicit css-class-name unvalidated, so a workspace-write css-class-name like `../evil` was only stopped downstream by ThemeCssBuilder's regex (ordering-fragile traversal risk).
Fix: new canonical Clio.Theming/ThemeCssClassNameRules (MaxLength + Matches + IsValid, `\z`); ThemeCssBuilder/ThemeRequestBuilder/ThemeCssClassName all reference it (each keeps its own message); TryResolve now rejects a malformed explicit value at the path boundary. Standardized on `\z` (fixed ThemeRequestBuilder's trailing-newline gap).
Files: clio/Theming/ThemeCssClassNameRules.cs (new), clio/Theming/ThemeCssBuilder.cs, clio/Command/Theming/{ThemeRequestBuilder,ThemeCssClassName}.cs, clio.tests/Command/{ThemeCssClassNameTests, McpServer/BuildThemeToolTests}.cs. Validated: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&(Module=Theming|Module=Command|Module=McpServer)" (3737 passed).

## 2026-07-03 – Consolidate theme parameter validation into ThemeParameterValidator
Context: after unifying the css-class-name rule, id/caption/css-content validation still lived as scattered privates in ThemeRequestBuilder + css-class resolve in a separate ThemeCssClassName — wanted one home for all theme-parameter checks.
Decision: new Clio.Command.Theming/ThemeParameterValidator (command layer, since id/caption/css-content limits are the ThemeService contract, not the CSS engine's) holds Slugify/TryResolve (moved from ThemeCssClassName, deleted) + TryValidateId (moved from ThemeRequestBuilder) + new TryValidateCssClassName/TryValidateCaption/TryValidateCssContent + MaxCssContentBytes. css-class-name char rule still delegated to engine ThemeCssClassNameRules (engine enforces it too, can't move up). ThemeRequestBuilder.TryValidateRequest stays but delegates each field. Name: "Parameter" not "Field" — "field" is taken for MCP/JSON wire fields.
Files: clio/Command/Theming/ThemeParameterValidator.cs (new), ThemeRequestBuilder.cs, {Create,Build,Delete}ThemeCommand.cs (callers), deleted ThemeCssClassName.cs; clio.tests/Command/ThemeParameterValidatorTests.cs (new, replaces ThemeCssClassNameTests.cs), ThemeRequestBuilderTests.cs. Validated: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&(Module=Theming|Module=Command|Module=McpServer)" (3743 passed).

## 2026-07-03 – Fold ThemeCssClassNameRules into ThemeParameterValidator (one place)
Decision: user wanted a single validation type. ThemeParameterValidator has no upward deps (pure string logic), so moved it down to Clio.Theming and folded ThemeCssClassNameRules into it (css-class-name pattern/MaxCssClassNameLength/IsValidCssClassName now live inside it). ThemeCssBuilder (engine) delegates to ThemeParameterValidator.IsValidCssClassName — no more separate engine rule type, still one source, engine keeps its own enforcement point. The layering-purity worry (server limits in the theme engine) yielded to "one place"; no cycle since the validator needs nothing from the builder.
Files: clio/Theming/ThemeParameterValidator.cs (moved+merged), deleted clio/Theming/ThemeCssClassNameRules.cs + clio/Command/Theming/ThemeParameterValidator.cs; ThemeCssBuilder.cs, ThemeRequestBuilder.cs/{Create,Delete}ThemeCommand.cs (+using Clio.Theming); test moved to clio.tests/Theming/ThemeParameterValidatorTests.cs (Module=Theming), ThemeRequestBuilderTests +using. Validated: 3743 passed.
