## 2026-07-02 16:30 – Process-designer MCP surface: modify-safety guidance + validate/build fork + readData caveat
Context: Audit of the AI-exposed process-designer API (tools/guidance) vs the cli-process-builder server implementation and the Confluence capability matrix (page 4769087495) showed the "breaks an existing process" risks were documented in Confluence but absent from the MCP surface. User asked to close three gaps.
Decision: (1) Added a "Modifying an existing process — safety rules" section to ProcessModelingGuidanceResource (no structural validation on modify path, removeElement cascade without gap re-join, validate the post-op graph, hands-off non-buildable constructs, whole-diagram relayout warning); (2) amended validate-process-graph tool [Description] + ValidateProcessGraphPrompt with "a passing graph is NOT necessarily buildable" (R1-R17 cover the full catalog; builder does start/signalStart/end/userTask + sequence flows only); (3) annotated the readData alias (guidance buildable list, element catalog entry, create tool [Description]) as landing UNCONFIGURED (source/filters/columns not settable). Also: modify tool [Description] got a pointer to the new guidance section, and fixed GuidanceCatalog drift — its process-modeling summary wrongly claimed Timer start in the supported slice (Timer start is Not impl., ENG-91849).
Discovery: Server evidence for the guidance claims — ProcessGraphBuilder.cs:237-238 (ValidateStructure is create-path only), :151-166 (removeElement cascades flows+mappings, no re-join); server contracts are [DataContract] so unknown JSON fields (e.g. an invented signal filter) drop silently — NOT yet covered in guidance (deferred with the closed-contract warning + server-side lint ideas). Routing map (RoutingGuidanceResource.cs) has no process row at all — process guides are reachable only via tool descriptions/prompts; worth a follow-up.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ProcessDesigner/ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ProcessDesigner/{ValidateProcessGraphTool,CreateBusinessProcessTool,ModifyBusinessProcessTool}.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/ProcessDesigner/ValidateProcessGraphPrompt.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/GuidanceCatalog.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ProcessModelingGuidanceResourceTests.cs
Impact: An AI client now sees the modify-path hazards (broken-graph saves, cascade deletes, relayout) and the validate-vs-build fork before touching a real process; readData can no longer be presented as a working data operation. Validated: dotnet test clio.tests -f net8.0 --filter "Category=Unit&Module=McpServer" (1548 passed). MCP e2e reviewed, no update required (tool contracts/behavior unchanged — description metadata + resource text only; no e2e asserts on these strings). Docs reviewed, no update required (no CLI docs reference these MCP-only surfaces).

## 2026-07-07 – ENG-91842 data-source filters (Option B): live-verified dates + macros + date-parts
Context: continuation of the 2026-07-06 entry. With a live stand (test-stand) the deferred vocabulary was verified against real saved schemas and finished: date/time constants, relative-date/system macros, and left-hand date-parts. User deploys the package + restarts the stand; I create+describe via MCP and inspect the raw metadata.json on disk.
Decision: (1) Date/DateTime/Time CONSTANT is stored as a quote-wrapped LOCAL ISO string literal ("\"yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fff\"") in BOTH reps, plus a UTC `dateValue` (...Z) in the edit rep only; TZ from `_userConnection.CurrentUser.TimeZone` (try/catch → UTC). A bare string renders as `<?>` in the designer — the quote-wrapping is mandatory. (2) A MACRO is a right-hand `FunctionExpression{functionType:1, macrosType, functionArgument?(Integer)}`, always a Compare (never lookup-In); a date macro on a date column sets `trimDateTimeParameterToDate=true`. Argument macros (NextNDays) require `macroArgument` or throw. (3) A DATE-PART is a LEFT-hand `FunctionExpression{functionType:3, datePartType, functionArgument:ColumnExpression{columnPath}}` compared to an Integer value; column recovered from functionArgument on read-back. New `DatePartCatalog` (Day/Week/Month/Year/Weekday/Hour; HourMinute/Time deferred — needs the time carrier).
Discovery: THE recurring trap — every filter field needs a property on BOTH the package `FilterConditionDescriptor` AND clio's `DescribedFilterCondition`. Macro read-back silently DROPPED live even with green unit tests because clio's DTO lacked `macro`/`macroArgument` and System.Text.Json discards unknown JSON on re-serialize; same fix applied pre-emptively for `datePart`. clio must be rebuilt+restarted to pick up a DTO change (a stale MCP-server process serves the old DTO). Confirmed we CANNOT reuse core `EntitySchemaQueryFilter`/DataContract wholesale: the platform serializes PascalCase, lacks the client-only edit fields (className/dateValue/displayValue), and there is no server-side wrapper producer — the metadata is exactly what the visual designer used to build only on the client. Hand-rolled encoder kept; core read as SPEC only.
Files: package — Filters/DatePartCatalog.cs (new), Filters/ProcessFilterService.cs (BuildLeftExpression + datePart/Integer typing in BuildCondition), Filters/FilterDescriptorReader.cs (ReadColumnPath digs into functionArgument + TryReadDatePart), Contracts/FilterContracts.cs (DatePart), ProcessDesignConstants.cs (datePartType/functionType=3/Integer=4 + date value types), tests/FilterDescriptorReaderTests.cs (+3 date-part tests). clio — Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs (DescribedFilterCondition.DatePart), Resources/ProcessDesigner/ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs (datePart bullet), Tools/ProcessDesigner/{CreateBusinessProcessTool,ModifyBusinessProcessTool}.cs (macro+datePart in [Description]), clio.tests/Command/ProcessModel/ServerProcessDescriberTests.cs (+datePart read test).
Verify: package FilterDescriptorReaderTests 16 pass (dev-nf); clio ProcessModel 92 + McpServer 1549 pass (net8). Dates + macros live-verified on test-stand; date-parts pending redeploy for live-verify (Year(CreatedOn)=2026). CLI docs reviewed, no update required (process-designer commands are feature-toggled → omitted from committed Commands.md/help/docs); MCP guidance + tool descriptions updated.

## 2026-07-07 – ENG-91842 filters: signal-start param-ref guard + serializer/applier split
Context: user observed in the designer that a signalStart filter cannot map a column's right side to a parameter, while Read data can — asked to confirm in the client code and then enforce it. Then asked to do it "architecturally correct".
Decision: (1) CONFIRMED in the platform client — `FilterModuleMixin.getFilterModuleConfig` defaults `filterEditConfig` to `ProcessDesignerFilterEditFilter`, whose `applyMenuItems` adds the `SELECT_PARAMETER` menu item (maps the right side to a process/element param, dataValueType=MAPPING). `BaseSignalEventPropertiesPage.getFilterModuleConfig` OVERRIDES it to `filterEditConfig:null`, so a signal filter falls back to the base `Terrasoft.FilterEdit.Filter` (`applyMenuItems: Terrasoft.emptyFn`) — no parameter option. Read/Add/Modify/Delete data pages do NOT override, so they keep it. Semantic reason: a signal is evaluated to decide which records START the process, before any instance exists → no parameter has a value yet. (2) Enforced it server-side: reject a `processParameter`/`elementParameter`/`expression` right-hand source anywhere in a signal-start filter tree (constants/macros/date-parts/isNull only). (3) REFACTOR (architecturally-correct placement): split the old `ProcessFilterService` — it now knew about element kinds via ApplyFilter/ClearFilter — into a PURE serializer `ProcessFilterService` (`BuildFilterValue` + descriptor→JSON only, zero element knowledge) and a new element-aware `ProcessFilterApplier` (`ApplyFilter`/`ClearFilter`: EntityFilters-vs-DataSourceFilters storage routing, the signal param-ref guard, root-schema resolution). Applier depends on `IProcessFilterService`. Rationale: keep all "knows about signal/data element" logic cohesive in one class; the serializer stays reusable and testable in isolation.
Discovery: `ApplyFilter` was ALREADY element-type-aware before this task (it must be — signal stores the filter in `EntityFilters`+`HasEntityFilters`, a data node in a `DataSourceFilters` parameter), so the guard rode on existing knowledge; the refactor just moved that whole seam out of the serializer. Blast radius of the split: `ProcessDesigner` + `ProcessOperationExecutor` (ctor dep `IProcessFilterService`→`IProcessFilterApplier`), DI in `ClioProcessBuilderApp`, and 6 test fixtures (ApplyFilter/ClearFilter tests migrated `ProcessFilterServiceTests`→new `ProcessFilterApplierTests`; 4 ctor call sites now pass an applier).
Files: package — Filters/IProcessFilterApplier.cs (new), Filters/ProcessFilterApplier.cs (new), Filters/ProcessFilterService.cs (slimmed to serializer), Filters/IProcessFilterService.cs, ClioProcessBuilderApp.cs (DI), cs/ProcessDesigner.cs, Operations/ProcessOperationExecutor.cs; tests — ProcessFilterApplierTests.cs (new, 8 tests incl. 3 guard tests), ProcessFilterServiceTests.cs (apply/clear tests removed), ProcessDesigner{Orchestrator,RoundTrip,Security}Tests.cs + ProcessOperationExecutor{,GraphOps}Tests.cs (ctor arg). clio — Resources/ProcessDesigner/ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs (SIGNAL-START RESTRICTION bullet), Tools/ProcessDesigner/{CreateBusinessProcessTool,ModifyBusinessProcessTool}.cs ([Description]).
Verify: package 273 pass / 1 known-unrelated pre-existing fail (ProcessMappingServiceTests DataValueTypeManager harness gap); clio McpServer 1549 + ProcessModel 92 pass (net8). CLI docs reviewed, no update required (process-designer feature-toggled → omitted from committed docs). Live-verify of date-parts + this guard still pending a redeploy.

## 2026-07-07 – ENG-91842 filters: code-review pass (4 dimensions) + fixes + coverage
Context: /creatio-code-review over all commits on both feature/ENG-91842-data-source-filters branches (clio 18 commits, ProcessBuilder 10). Ran 4 parallel dimension reviewers (correctness, security, clarity/conventions, tests).
Decision: fixed the one real defect + the cheap correctness/clarity wins; added coverage for the top gaps.
Discovery + fixes:
- **[Major, real latent bug] Nested filter group broke describe.** FilterDescriptorReader returned a `FilterDescriptor` for EVERY group incl. nested ones; nested groups live in the declared `List<FilterGroupDescriptor>`, and the WCF describe endpoint serializes with the default `DataContractJsonSerializer` (WebMessageFormat.Json, BodyStyle=Wrapped, no custom formatter) → a derived instance in a base-typed list throws without `[KnownType]`. So `describe-business-process` would throw for any process with an AND/(B OR C) filter. Flat filters were fine (root `Filter` is declared `FilterDescriptor`), which is why live tests passed — no test/e2e described a nested-group filter through WCF. Fix: `ReadRootGroup` (FilterDescriptor, root only) + `ReadNestedGroup` (base FilterGroupDescriptor) + shared `PopulateGroupMembers`. Guard test asserts `Groups[0]` is exactly `FilterGroupDescriptor`.
- **[Minor] Get vs Find.** ResolveColumn (`GetInstanceByUId` ×2) and ProcessFilterApplier.ResolveRootSchema (`GetInstanceByName`) used the throwing Get*, making the friendly "not found" messages dead code (raw ItemNotFoundException surfaced). Switched to FindInstanceByUId/FindInstanceByName (see [[creatio-manager-get-vs-find-semantics]]).
- **[Major, clarity] Magic value-type literals** 4/5/6/12 in ConvertConstant → added Float/Money/Boolean constants and switched on named constants; removed a stale date comment.
- **[Minor, defensive] New guards:** reject datePart+macro on one condition; reject isNull/isNotNull carrying a right-hand source (was silently dropped).
- Security review: CLEAN (auth gate reused = the stronger CheckCanManageProcessDesign; no string-built JSON — all via dictionaries + JsonSerializer; fail-closed JsonDocument parse; no secret leakage). Not fixed / deferred: bare `catch(Exception)` in ResolveUserTimeZone (kept — intentional UTC fallback; narrowing risks an unknown platform exception type in prod); read-back of an un-catalogued macro/datePart code yields a partial condition (narrow, tied to deferred HourMinute).
- Coverage added (+18 package cases, +1 clio): nested-group type guard, date UTC/offset branch, bare-group input, full comparison vocabulary, valid-JSON-not-filter→null, column-not-found, expression encoder input, unknown process-parameter, datePart+macro & isNull+value rejection, ClearFilter(data element), signal object-derivation, signal expression rejection; clio DTO expression/elementParameter deserialization.
Files: package src FilterDescriptorReader.cs, ProcessFilterService.cs, ProcessFilterApplier.cs, ProcessDesignConstants.cs; tests FilterDescriptorReaderTests.cs, ProcessFilterServiceTests.cs, ProcessFilterApplierTests.cs; clio ServerProcessDescriberTests.cs.
Verify: package 291 pass / 1 known-unrelated pre-existing fail; clio ProcessModel 93 pass (net8). Live-verify of date-parts + signal guard still pending a redeploy.

## 2026-07-07 – ENG-91842 filters: date offset-detection via DateTime.Kind (drop HasExplicitOffset)
Context: reuse question — does HasExplicitOffset (our manual Z/+/- scan deciding instant-vs-naive for a caller ISO string) duplicate client or core logic?
Discovery: NO reusable analog. Client (`core/…/data/filters/parameter.js` setValue/getSerializableObject) works from a JS `Date` object — stores `dateValue` = Date, emits `dateValue`(UTC …Z) + `value` = `Terrasoft.encodeDate(dateValue)`; it never parses an arbitrary string, so the "has offset?" question can't arise. Core (`Terrasoft.Core/Entities/EntitySchemaColumnDef.cs:527`) parses a date const via `DateTime.TryParseExact(value, DateTimeColumnConstFormat, Invariant, DateTimeStyles.None)` — one fixed format, naive, no offset sniffing. Our helper is a legit artifact of the string-based MCP contract (accepts 2026-05-01 / …T12:00:00 / …Z / …+02:00).
Decision: replaced the manual scan with framework-native detection — `DateTime.TryParse(raw, Invariant, DateTimeStyles.RoundtripKind, out probe)`; `probe.Kind == Unspecified` ⇒ naive (interpret in user zone), `Utc`/`Local` ⇒ absolute instant (re-read via `DateTimeOffset` for a host-TZ-independent UtcDateTime). Deleted `HasExplicitOffset`. More robust on edges (fractional seconds, +00:00, space vs T) than the `Z`/`+`/`-` scan; behavior on well-formed ISO unchanged.
Files: packages/clioprocessbuilder/Files/src/cs/Filters/ProcessFilterService.cs (BuildDateConstant rewrite, HasExplicitOffset removed); tests/clioprocessbuilder/FilterDescriptorReaderTests.cs (+numeric-offset +02:00, +date-only midnight).
Verify: package 293 pass / 1 known-unrelated pre-existing fail. +02:00→10:00Z conversion confirms Kind-detection live.

## 2026-07-07 – ENG-91842: split ProcessDesigner (10-dep facade) into per-use-case handlers + guard
Context: ProcessDesigner's ctor had grown to 10 params (UserConnection + 9 services) and kept accreting one per new capability. Question: architecturally OK?
Discovery: real cohesion smell (constructor over-injection). Dependency-usage map showed deps partition by METHOD, not shared: BuildProcess used {repository, layout, graphBuilder, parameterService, mappingService, filterApplier}; ModifyProcess {repository, layout, graphBuilder, operationExecutor}; ListUserTasks {userTaskCatalog}; DescribeProcess {describer}. Describe/List were pure pass-throughs (auth-check + delegate) to already-existing services. So the class was 4 separable jobs sharing only the CanManageProcessDesign gate.
Decision: option 1 (full split). New `IProcessDesignGuard`/`ProcessDesignGuard` (UserConnection) = the shared gate in one place. `IProcessBuildHandler`/`ProcessBuildHandler` (guard + 6 create collaborators) owns BuildProcess + its rollback. `IProcessModifyHandler`/`ProcessModifyHandler` (guard + 4) owns ModifyProcess + its session-release. `ProcessDesigner` is now a thin facade (buildHandler, modifyHandler, guard, userTaskCatalog, describer = 5): delegates build/modify, runs list/describe as guarded pass-throughs. Guard is called inside each handler's try (build/modify) and the facade's try (list/describe) so "unauthorized → error response" behavior is preserved. DI in ClioProcessBuilderApp: register guard (needs Connection) + both handlers + facade (all compose from registered services → plain AddScoped, no ActivatorUtilities/Connection). New capability now lands in a focused handler, not the shared ctor.
Tests: moved BuildProcess tests → new ProcessBuildHandlerTests (7), ModifyProcess → ProcessModifyHandlerTests (5), both substituting the guard (no user-gate setup needed). Real gate → new ProcessDesignGuardTests (authorized-passes, missing-op-throws; the non-General branch is NOT unit-testable — TestFramework's mocked SysUserInfo.ConnectionType can't be driven off General, and the old ProcessDesignerSecurityTests never covered it either). ProcessDesignerOrchestratorTests rewritten to facade-level (delegation + list/describe guard wiring). Deleted ProcessDesignerSecurityTests (coverage split into guard + facade tests).
Files: package src IProcessDesignGuard.cs, ProcessDesignGuard.cs, IProcessBuildHandler.cs, ProcessBuildHandler.cs, IProcessModifyHandler.cs, ProcessModifyHandler.cs (new); ProcessDesigner.cs, ClioProcessBuilderApp.cs (mod). Tests: ProcessBuildHandlerTests.cs, ProcessModifyHandlerTests.cs, ProcessDesignGuardTests.cs (new); ProcessDesignerOrchestratorTests.cs, ProcessDesignerRoundTripTests.cs (mod); ProcessDesignerSecurityTests.cs (deleted).
Verify: package 298 pass / 1 known-unrelated pre-existing fail. Optional further step (not done): group {parameterService, mappingService, filterApplier} into an IProcessContentApplier to drop the build handler from 7→5.

## 2026-07-07 – ENG-91842: architecture-review batch (core reuse + DRY + edit-rep guard)
Context: user asked for a critical architecture pass + "could core be reused?" Ran 3 parallel analysts (enum reuse, serialization/column reuse, architecture critique), then "делай все".
Decision + changes (all package unless noted):
- **Core enum reuse (ProcessDesignConstants.Filters):** replaced ~37 magic wire codes with (int)core-enum values, kept const (enum members are compile-time constants) and FULLY-QUALIFIED to dodge the nested `Filters` name clash. FilterType/DataValueType/FunctionType/DatePart → Terrasoft.Core.Entities.Filters.*; ComparisonType → Terrasoft.Core.Entities.FilterComparisonType; ExpressionType → EntitySchemaQueryExpressionType (member SchemaColumn=0); Logical → Terrasoft.Common.LogicalOperationStrict (NOT LogicalOperation — And=1 trap). Macros stays hand-coded (client-only primary-column values 34/35/36/41 absent from the reachable C# enum + IsDateKind/RequiresArgument metadata). Compiler now re-binds the codes on upgrade.
- **Column-path reuse:** ResolveColumn now calls EntitySchema.FindSchemaColumnByPath (splits, hops lookups via ReferenceSchema, caches) instead of hand-walking split + FindInstanceByUId; ~30 lines → ~8, drops the 2nd manager lookup. VERIFIED the test mock (TestEntitySchemaManager) supports the real method incl. Account.Code lookup-hop (59 filter tests green).
- **DRY:** ResolveReference reuses ProcessSchemaElementLocator.ResolveElement (killed the 4th inline copy); one shared FilterConditionDescriptor.HasReference predicate now used by BOTH serializer + signal guard (was two divergent definitions); deleted dead AddSingleton<ILog> + now-unused Common.Logging using + LoggerName const; aligned clio DescribedSignal.On doc (single event, not "combination").
- **Facade:** ProcessDesigner.ListUserTasks/DescribeProcess now go through one Execute<TResponse>(guard + try/catch → error-response) boundary; build/modify still own their rollback/session error handling in their handlers.
- **Edit-rep drift guard:** added BuildFilterValue_ShouldPutDesignerOnlyKeysInEditRepOnly (asserts className/leftExpressionCaption/isAggregative/referenceSchemaName/displayValue live in serializedFilterEditData ONLY, never dataSourceFilters) — closes the gap that the round-trip decoder (runtime-rep only) left; and converted the Build* methods' untyped Tuple<object,object> to a named ValueTuple (Edit, Run).
NOT done (deliberate): DataContract-based serialization (edit rep has client-only className/dateValue/displayValue absent from Terrasoft.Nui.ServiceModel.DataContract.Filters → stays hand-built; matching the lean output = test churn for half the payoff); "build-once-derive-lean" output restructuring (risky; the new guard already detects drift).
Files: package ProcessDesignConstants.cs, Filters/ProcessFilterService.cs, Filters/ProcessFilterApplier.cs, Contracts/FilterContracts.cs, ClioProcessBuilderApp.cs, Design/ProcessDesigner.cs, tests/ProcessFilterServiceTests.cs; clio Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs (doc).
Verify: package 299 pass / 1 known-unrelated pre-existing fail; clio ProcessModel 93 pass (net8).
## 2026-07-06 – PR#748 review fixes + shared response-body sanitizer
Fixes (verified vs current source): (#3) ThemeParameterValidator regex now guarded by IsMatchSafe / try-catch RegexMatchTimeoutException → fail-closed, matching the SchemaValidationService / PageInsertDowngradeDetector convention (patterns are linear + inputs length-capped, so it's a backstop, not a real ReDoS — reviewer's "reliably triggerable crash" was overstated). (#4) log-injection: extracted shared TextUtilities.SanitizeForDisplay (control chars→space, truncate 500+"...") and routed ThemeServiceResponseParser, CreatioServiceClient.PostAndDeserialize, DeletePackageCommand through it — was 3 raw-body echoes incl. a duplicate private copy. (#5) create/update-theme MCP tool [Description]: note inline-only CSS (CLI keeps --css-content-file). (#2) McpCapabilityMap counts refreshed from a live mcp-server stdio probe (137 tools / 67 prompts / 92 resources / 1 template); old 65/50/5 were stale across the whole surface, not a theme delta.
Discovery: a live stdio JSON-RPC probe of the built dll is the only reliable MCP count source (splits resources vs resource-templates; source-grep can't replicate). #1 (e2e) already covered by ThemingSandboxE2ETests.
Files: clio/Common/TextUtilities.cs (+TextUtilitiesTests), clio/Command/Theming/ThemeServiceResponse.cs (+ThemeServiceResponseParserTests), clio/Common/CreatioServiceClient.cs, clio/Command/DeletePackageCommand.cs, clio/Theming/ThemeParameterValidator.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/{Create,Update}ThemeTool.cs, docs/McpCapabilityMap.md. Validated: dotnet test --filter Category=Unit (5133 passed).
## 2026-07-02 23:20 – BL-1: unified --json output envelope (Agent-DX pilot)
Context: Backlog item BL-1 — a stable machine-readable output contract for read-only commands so agents/scripts stop parsing human text. Piloted on list-packages, list-environments, healthcheck.
Decision: New envelope {schemaVersion:"1.0", ok, command, data, error{code,message}} (CommandEnvelope) serialized via IJsonResponseFormater.FormatEnvelope (Newtonsoft, honors [DataMember]). error.code registry (CommandErrorCodes) EXTENDS the existing CreatioVersionRequirementException taxonomy (version-*) rather than redefining it, + code→exit map. list-packages --json now defaults to the envelope; --legacy-form preserves the old {value,success,errorInfo}. Kept --json per-command (base EnvironmentOptions would collide with 6 commands already declaring --json); unified the serialization layer instead. --json is plain bool (bool? required a value in CommandLineParser → bare --json errored). Global logs→stderr in json mode via Program.IsJsonOutputMode + ConsoleLogger so stdout is exactly one JSON object (fixes auto-updater [INF] polluting stdout). healthcheck failure honors data/error mutual-exclusivity (data:null, detail in error.message).
Discovery: The auto-updater's [INF] line writes to stdout at startup — any --json contract needs the global stderr routing, not just per-command discipline. bool? options are not bare flags in this CommandLineParser. MCP tools (GetPkgListTool/ShowWebAppListTool) return record projections and bypass the CLI --json path, so MCP parity + e2e are unaffected.
Files: clio/Common/Responses/CommandEnvelope.cs, clio/Common/CommandErrorCodes.cs, clio/Common/JsonResponseFormater.cs, clio/Common/ConsoleLogger.cs, clio/Program.cs, clio/Command/GetPkgListCommand.cs, clio/Command/HealthCheckCommand.cs, clio/Command/ShowAppListCommand.cs, docs (help/en + docs/commands ×3), tests (GetPkgListCommandTests, HealthCheckCommand.Tests, ShowAppListCommand.Tests, Common/CommandEnvelopeTests)
Impact: Reusable envelope + error.code registry + json-mode stderr routing are in place; extending the pilot to more read-only commands is now mechanical (add --json, route Execute output through FormatEnvelope). Full unit suite 4764 passed.

## 2026-07-03 17:30 – ENG-92865 PR #814 review fixes: mcp-http DI validation, compile log parity, HTTP security, install prereq
Context: PR #814 (m-dymytrova) four Major review comments on the new `clio mcp-http` HTTP transport. Alex confirmed decisions: runtime-only installs NOT supported (#4 = doc only); security = variant A (Host+Origin validation, no auth); enable DI validation (#1); per-tool PreserveMessages fix (#2).
Decision: (1) Replaced the no-op `builder.Services.Configure<ServiceProviderOptions>` with `builder.Host.UseDefaultServiceProvider(ValidateOnBuild+ValidateScopes)` — the WebApplicationBuilder root provider reads options from UseDefaultServiceProvider, not from the collection it builds. (2) CompileCreatioTool.Execute now saves->sets->restores `logger.PreserveMessages=true` locally (like BaseTool/SchemaSyncTool/EntitySchemaTool/AddItemModelTool) so compile output is captured over HTTP where the process-wide flag is never set. (3) Added `app.UseHostFiltering()` (AllowedHosts = bound host + loopback aliases; `*` only for wildcard bind) + an Origin-validation middleware (absent Origin -> pass for native clients; foreign Origin -> 403) between Build() and MapMcp(). (4) Documented the ASP.NET Core shared-runtime prerequisite in README install section + mcp-http.md/.txt, plus a Security section describing the new Host/Origin controls.
Discovery: Verified fail-fast empirically — launched `clio mcp-http --port 8071`; host boots (ValidateOnBuild passed -> DI graph valid over the HTTP host), and live curl probes confirmed: foreign Origin -> 403, foreign Host -> 400, loopback/no-Origin -> reach the MCP handler (500 only from empty JSON body). `Microsoft.AspNetCore.HostFiltering` + `UseDefaultServiceProvider` resolve from the already-referenced Microsoft.AspNetCore.App shared framework — no new package.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpHttpServerCommand.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/CompileCreatioTool.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpHttpServerCommandTests.cs, clio/docs/commands/mcp-http.md, clio/help/en/mcp-http.txt, README.md
Impact: mcp-http now fails fast on DI misconfig, returns real compile output over HTTP, and enforces the MCP-spec Origin/Host controls against DNS rebinding. Validated: dotnet test clio.tests -f net10.0 --filter "Category=Unit&Module=McpServer" (1555 passed, 1 pre-existing skip). MCP e2e: CompileCreatioTool behavior change not asserted by clio.mcp.e2e (compile needs a live Creatio env; e2e not in CI) — flagged in PR reply, no e2e added.

## 2026-07-06 11:10 – Release 8.1.0.71: CI smoke-test broke on packageSourceMapping (--add-source)
Context: Merged PR #811 (BL-1 --json envelope) to master and cut release 8.1.0.71. The GitHub tag+release published fine but the release-to-nuget workflow FAILED at the "Verify packaged tool version" smoke step, so "Publish to NuGet" was skipped — the package never reached NuGet.
Decision: Root-caused to CI infra, not the release content: the smoke step ran `dotnet tool install clio --add-source ./output`, and newer NuGet SDKs hard-reject `--add-source` when the repo nuget.config has <packageSourceMapping> active ("The --add-source option cannot be combined with package source mapping"). The identical step + identical nuget.config passed on 8.1.0.70 (02.07) under the older SDK — only the self-hosted runner's SDK changed. Fix (PR #819): the smoke step now writes a dedicated temp nuget.config at step time (clio -> local ./output source; * (+ creatio.client) -> nuget.org; Terrasoft.*/Creatio.* -> mirror, mirroring the repo config) and installs with `--configfile` instead of `--add-source`. Then re-cut the release: deleted tag+release 8.1.0.71 and recreated it on the fixed master HEAD (2e5890b1) so the `release: published` event re-ran the workflow with the fixed file — Test/Pack/Verify/Publish all green, clio.8.1.0.71.nupkg pushed to nuget.org.
Discovery: (1) A `release: [published]` workflow checks out the TAG's commit, so a fix committed to master after tagging does NOT apply to a re-run — the tag must be moved to a commit that already contains the fix (delete+recreate release with `gh release create <tag> --target <full-sha>`; short SHA / stale tag errors with "Release.target_commitish is invalid"). (2) PowerShell here-strings inside a YAML `run: |` block: the closing `"@` must land at column 0 AFTER YAML strips the block's base indent — align `@"`/`"@` to the run block's own indent (10 spaces here), verified by parsing the YAML and checking the delimiter columns. (3) The workflow filename is misspelled `.github/workflows/reliase-to-nuget.yml` — grep by content, not by expected name.
Files: .github/workflows/reliase-to-nuget.yml (PR #819)
Impact: Release publishing is resilient to the runner's NuGet source-mapping enforcement; future X.Y.Z.W releases no longer need `--add-source`. If a release's NuGet publish fails after the tag is cut, fix on master then delete+recreate the release targeting the fixed full SHA rather than re-running the old workflow.

## 2026-07-06 – ENG-92868 PR #816 review fixes: Helm hardening (image pull, port config, security, netpol)
Context: PR #816 CHANGES_REQUESTED (m-dymytrova Major x3, tetiana-moshon x3) on the mcp-http Dockerfile + Helm chart.
Decision: (1) image pullPolicy IfNotPresent->Always (keep latest as convenience alias; Jenkins still emits immutable tag). (2) Dockerfile: split ENTRYPOINT (fixed) from CMD (host/port/path defaults) so Helm overrides connection args via container `args`; deployment now passes --host/--port/--path from values (mcp.path was dead config before). (3) probe port templated from mcp.port (was literal 8005) so probes track an mcp.port override. (4) container securityContext drop-ins: allowPrivilegeEscalation:false, capabilities.drop:[ALL], seccompProfile:RuntimeDefault. (5) opt-in default-deny NetworkPolicy template (networkPolicy.enabled=false) with documented allowedClients selectors. (6) .dockerignore broadened to **/bin **/obj.
Discovery: kept tcpSocket probes (not httpGet) — MapMcp(path) has no plain-200 GET/HEAD health endpoint (GET on /mcp is MCP streamable-HTTP/SSE, would flap an httpGet probe); a real health route is a base-feature (ENG-92865) code change, out of scope for a deploy PR. readOnlyRootFilesystem left off (clio writes settings/profile files — needs writable-path audit). Verified with helm lint + helm template (default + mcp.port=9000 override + networkPolicy.enabled): args, containerPort and probe ports all track mcp.port; NetworkPolicy renders valid YAML with and without allowedClients.
Files: deploy/mcp-http/Dockerfile, .dockerignore, deploy/mcp-http/helm/values.yaml, deploy/mcp-http/helm/templates/deployment.yaml, deploy/mcp-http/helm/templates/networkpolicy.yaml
Impact: mcp.port/mcp.path are now honored end-to-end (process + Service + probes), rollouts are reproducible, the container runs least-privilege, and operators can fail-closed the ingress. NetworkPolicy client selector and app-level authN remain deployment/base-feature decisions.

## 2026-07-06 15:40 – ENG-92761: self-describing lazy surface — resident flag + core-rules line
Context: Follow-up sub-task of ENG-90312 (lazy MCP tool surface, PR #743, still open on spike/mcp-lazy-schema). Today nothing in the get-tool-contract compact index told an agent which tools are resident (present in tools/list, callable natively) vs. hidden long-tail (reachable only via clio-run). Branched off origin/spike/mcp-lazy-schema (NOT master — McpCoreToolProfile, ToolContractIndexEntry, and clio-run only exist on that unmerged branch); did not rebase the spike branch itself since PR #743 has active review threads.
Decision: (F2) Added McpCoreToolProfile.ResidentToolNames + IsResident(name) — a static HashSet<string> reflected once from the [McpServerTool(Name=...)] methods declared on CoreToolTypes ∪ AlwaysOnLazyToolTypes (same BindingFlags as McpToolInvokerRegistry.EnumerateToolMethods, so residency never diverges from what the SDK's WithTools(types) would register). Chose static reflection over threading a new IMcpToolInvokerRegistry.IsResident(name) method through the registry, because the AC requires a non-nullable bool even when no registry is supplied (unlike the existing nullable Destructive flag). Added ToolContractIndexEntry.Resident (bool) between ContractAvailable and Destructive, populated in BuildIndexEntry via McpCoreToolProfile.IsResident(name); updated the self-contract's "index" field description. (F4) Appended the exact canonical line to CoreRulesGuidanceResource.Guide: "Resident tools (get-tool-contract index: resident=true) are called natively; every other tool is invoked via clio-run <command>. Never wrap a resident tool in clio-run." — this is the string ENG-92762 (CAADT, separate repo) mirrors verbatim.
Discovery: get-tool-contract excludes itself from its own compact index (BuildIndexToolNames filters ToolContractGetTool.ToolName out), so "get-tool-contract → resident: true" from the AC is untestable via an index lookup — asserted at the predicate level instead (McpCoreToolProfile.IsResident(ToolContractGetTool.ToolName)). Verified all ~20 resident types' [McpServerTool] methods set Name= explicitly (no silent resident:false from a missing attribute name). "Index ordering byte-for-byte unchanged" (AC) means the array ORDER from BuildIndexToolNames stays untouched — per-entry JSON bytes necessarily grow with the new field; pinned with a BeInAscendingOrder(Ordinal) test instead of a serialization diff.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpCoreToolProfile.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ToolContractGetTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/CoreRulesGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ToolContractGetToolTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/CoreRulesGuidanceResourceTests.cs (new), clio.mcp.e2e/ToolContractGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: An agent can now self-select native vs. clio-run dispatch from the index alone (closes F1/F5 as behavioral consequences per the ticket). Validated: dotnet test clio.tests -f net10.0 --filter "Category=Unit&Module=McpServer" (1712 passed, 1 pre-existing skip unrelated). Docs reviewed, no update required beyond the in-code self-contract (docs/McpCapabilityMap.md documents high-level flows only, not the get-tool-contract wire shape). MCP e2e coverage added (ToolContractGet_Should_PopulateResidentFlag_InCompactIndex) but not run against a live server in this session — flagged as the manual e2e gate per repo policy (MCP e2e not yet in CI). Branch is stacked on spike/mcp-lazy-schema; when PR #743 merges this PR may need reopen+retarget to master (precedent: PR #709).

## 2026-07-06 18:10 – ENG-90312/PR #743: migrate clio.mcp.e2e off flat tools/list to the lazy surface
Context: PR #821 (ENG-92761) e2e evidence showed 234 identical e2e failures on both the PR branch and its base spike/mcp-lazy-schema — structural, not PR-specific: the e2e suite still asserted full flat tool advertisement (`tools/list` must contain every tool) and called long-tail tools natively, while PR #743's lazy core profile exposes only ~27 resident tools (§4 hard-bindings risk, spec/mcp-lazy-schema/mcp-lazy-schema-migration-inventory.md). User asked to fix this in PR #743's own scope rather than carry the red baseline forward.
Decision: Added a transparent routing shim in `clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpServerSession.cs` instead of rewriting every functional Act call: `CallToolAsync` (both overloads) now checks `tools/list` membership per tool name (cached HashSet, fetched once — registration is fixed at server-process start) and silently redirects a non-resident tool through `clio-run` (`{"command":name,"args":innerArgs}`), so every existing `Session.CallToolAsync(flatName, …)` call site keeps working unchanged. Added `ListReachableToolNamesAsync` (tools/list ∪ get-tool-contract compact index) as the replacement for "tool X must be advertised" gates, and `GetToolContractIndexAsync` for per-tool `destructive`/`contract-available` flag assertions on hidden tools. Fanned out 4 parallel coder agents (one per file range) to migrate every advertisement/arrange-gate/annotation-read/binding-error assertion across ~60 e2e files per 6 patterns (P1 advertisement→reachability, P2 arrange-gate, P3 destructive-flag via index, P4 description/schema via full get-tool-contract, P5 binding-error-contract rewrite, P6 feature-toggle canaries), briefed via a shared migration doc so patterns stayed consistent across agents.
Discovery: The BuildClioRunArguments "omit empty args" heuristic (meant to preserve the native "missing args wrapper" binding failure for P5 tests) initially broke 2 legitimate no-op calls (`install-skills`/`delete-skill` with `{"args":{}}`, a deliberate empty-but-present wrapper) — fixed by keying the omission on the caller's TOP-LEVEL dict being empty (Count==0, truly no args key sent) rather than on the inner args value being empty; a present `{"args":{}}` now forwards verbatim. Two coder agents hit the session token/rate limit mid-task and returned near-empty final reports (2.5k/4.5k tokens) after ~21 min and 105-125 tool calls each — verified their assigned files were still fully migrated (git diff) before trusting the (missing) self-report. A manual full-repo sweep after the fan-out found 4 genuine misses the local NoEnvironment-tier run couldn't catch because they live in `[Category("McpE2E.Sandbox")]` fixtures (GenerateProcessModelToolE2ETests, GetProcessSignatureToolE2ETests, InstallApplicationToolE2ETests, FsmModeToolE2ETests — all P2 arrange-gates for non-resident tools) — fixed directly. One compile error (CS1744, a `NotContainAny("a","b", because:…)` positional/named-arg clash in EntityBusinessRuleToolE2ETests.cs) and one CS0136-shaped self-shadow (`ContainSingle(entry => entry.Name==…)` assigned to a local also named `entry`) surfaced only at build time — neither caught by any agent's own report.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpServerSession.cs (shim), ~60 clio.mcp.e2e/*ToolE2ETests.cs files (migrated asserts), GenerateProcessModelToolE2ETests.cs/GetProcessSignatureToolE2ETests.cs/InstallApplicationToolE2ETests.cs/FsmModeToolE2ETests.cs (manual post-sweep fix), EntityBusinessRuleToolE2ETests.cs (compile fix), GetUserCultureToolE2ETests.cs (shadowing fix)
Impact: `dotnet test clio.mcp.e2e -f net10.0 --filter "TestCategory=McpE2E.NoEnvironment"` went from 61 failed/133 passed (after the shim alone) to 194/194 passed (after the full migration + fixes) — the entire NoEnvironment tier is green against the lazy surface without touching the surface itself. `[Category("McpE2E.Sandbox")]` fixtures (env-gated) could not be run locally; validated via TeamCity Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests instead. Anyone extending PR #743's tool profile should route new e2e coverage through `Session.CallToolAsync`/`ListReachableToolNamesAsync`/`GetToolContractIndexAsync` rather than raw `ListToolsAsync`, and re-run the full suite (not just NoEnvironment) before trusting a local pass — Sandbox-tier misses are invisible locally by construction.

## 2026-07-08 12:45 – ENG-91842: merge origin/master into the data-source-filters branch (semantic guidance conflict)
Context: User asked to merge master into both feature branches and fix conflicts. ProcessBuilder side (origin/main) done earlier (db3105f). clio was 122 commits behind master; `git merge origin/master` produced 4 textual conflicts (CreateBusinessProcessTool / ModifyBusinessProcessTool [Description], ProcessModelingGuidanceResource, workspace-diary) — all resolved by union (my filter contract + master's referenceSchema/guidance-first pointer/package requirement).
Decision: Beyond the 4 textual conflicts, reconciled one SEMANTIC conflict the merge surfaced only at test time. Master had added `GetGuide_ShouldDiscloseSignalTriggerLimits_WhenRead` pinning the pre-feature guidance claim "NO record filter" on a signalStart. Our feature makes a signalStart record filter buildable, so that claim is now false (correctly dropped when I took my side of the guidance conflict) — BUT master's paragraph had conflated it with an orthogonal, still-true limit: a "modified" trigger fires on ANY field change and you cannot restrict WHICH columns count (tracked-change columns are not buildable). That true half had been dropped wholesale. Restored it as its own guidance bullet (records CAN be filtered; column-level change cannot) and retargeted the test at the surviving limit ("column-level restriction cannot be built yet" + "ANY field change").
Discovery: A large squash-behind merge can be textually clean on a file yet semantically wrong because a NEW test on the incoming side pins wording the outgoing feature invalidated — the build stays green and only the unit run catches it (1 failed / 1841). Also: guidance-resource assertions must target a substring that lives wholly on ONE source line — the raw-string bullet wraps "…are not\n  buildable yet", so a cross-line phrase never matches as a contiguous substring (first fix attempt failed on exactly this). appsettings.json process-designer:true stayed unstaged (` M`), so the merge commit excluded the local toggle as required.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ProcessDesigner/ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ProcessModelingGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ProcessDesigner/{Create,Modify}BusinessProcessTool.cs
Impact: clio branch is now current with master (merge 00855f4d, pushed) and its unit suite for the touched modules is green (Module=McpServer|ProcessModel, 1841 passed). Future merges of this branch family should re-run the unit suite after a clean auto-merge — textual cleanliness does not imply the guidance still matches the feature; and any new guidance test must assert on a single-line substring.
## 2026-07-08 – PR #748 theming review fixes: golden-gate checkout-independence + go-live
Context: Both open Blockers point at ThemeCssBuilder.Build (its golden bit-exact parity gate); reviewer misread a red CI as a dropped ~350-byte builder block. Real root cause: CommentStripRegex `\*/\n?` is EOL-sensitive (consumes the trailing newline for an LF template but not CRLF), so build-theme output depended on git core.autocrlf — the ~350 bytes were the 352 CRLF-vs-LF line endings, not lost content.
Decision: Fixed at source — ThemeCssBuilder.Build normalizes templateCss to LF at entry → deterministic LF output regardless of checkout; regenerated golden to LF (only the css values changed); pinned clio/tpl/themes/**/*.tpl + Theming/Fixtures/*.json eol=lf. Other review findings verified already-fixed in later commits (DTO mutation → new normalizedOptions; DeriveSecondary magic numbers → named consts; BuildThemeTool css-class-name validated at MCP boundary; log-injection sanitised on both branches). ColorMetrics FP loop left as-is (operator-for-operator TS port, ADR C-D3; IEEE-754 basic ops deterministic → no real drift). A ThemeTemplateProvider memoisation cache was prototyped then reverted at the user's request. Resolved the toggle-vs-docs contradiction by go-live (docs-match-code, user-chosen): [FeatureToggle("theming")] stays removed (author's commit 3debbfd) and the docs were reconciled — ADR Status/C-D6 + Consequences/Open-items and sprint-status story-8 now read live/done; public docs already ship in the generated baseline; the release note belongs in the GitHub Release, not RELEASE.md (process-only). The ThemePaletteAdvisor rename (review comment #3, naming drift, authored in parallel) is committed alongside. story-7 (decommission @creatio/theming) stays deferred (external gate).
Discovery: fixture theme.css.tpl is csproj-Link'd from bundled clio/tpl/themes/10.0/theme.css.tpl (no separate copy). Working tree is sparse-checkout (cone, only `prompts`) — theming source isn't on disk; `git sparse-checkout disable` to build, `git sparse-checkout set prompts` to restore. Validated by byte-level LF and CRLF template flips (both pass) + full Command|Theming unit tier (2173 passed). NB: PowerShell Get-Content -Raw + WriteAllText corrupts UTF-8 em-dash; flip EOL at byte level.
Files: clio/Theming/ThemeCssBuilder.cs, clio.tests/Theming/Fixtures/theme-css-golden.json, .gitattributes, spec/adr/adr-theming.md, spec/sprint-status.yaml; + ThemePaletteAdvisor rename (clio/Command/Theming/ThemePaletteAdvisor{,Result}.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/AdviseThemePaletteTool.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio.tests/Command/{McpServer/AdviseThemePaletteToolTests,ThemePaletteAdvisorTests}.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/AdviseThemePaletteToolE2ETests.cs)
Impact: build-theme output deterministic on any runner; parity gate green; theming officially go-live (toggle removed, docs reconciled). Validated: full unit suite 5390 passed (net8.0); clio.tests + clio.mcp.e2e build clean.

## 2026-07-07 – Pre-release MCP live regression against a fresh stand (mcp-regress)
Context: Release regression of `clio mcp-server`. master already at 674e7c1c (up-to-date; PR #743 lazy surface + e2e migration merged). Unit + e2e (NoEnvironment tier) were already green per prior runs; user asked specifically for REAL testing against a live stand: http://ts1-core-dev04:88/studioenu_15688975_0709.
Decision: Registered the stand as clio env `mcp-regress` (Supervisor/Supervisor, IsNetCore=false — same shape as every other studioenu:88 stand; ping OK via the `0/` prefix). Drove the real `clio mcp-server` over stdio with a Python JSON-RPC client (scratchpad/mcp_driver.py + mcp_sys.py) instead of the e2e harness, to exercise the actual release binary end-to-end. Did NOT run the McpE2E.Sandbox tier locally: SandboxEnvironmentResolver requires a LOCAL deployed web-app root (ConnectionStrings.config on disk) to verify Redis/DB side effects — the stand is remote, so that tier stays a TeamCity job (Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests). Kept all live calls read-only (stand has no cliogate; destructive opt-in not needed for a smoke).
Discovery: (1) Contract confirmed on the live binary — resident tools take a single `args` record so tools/call arguments must be `{"args":{...}}`; a top-level-fields call is rejected with an actionable wrapper hint. clio-run takes TWO top-level params `{"command":"<tool>","args":{...}}`. (2) 27 resident tools advertised (lazy surface intact); `describe-environment` is NOT resident — reached via clio-run to the hidden long tail, worked. (3) Stand coreVersion 10.1.185.0, en-US, workspace Default; cliogate 2.0.0.32+ absent so ProductName/LicenseInfo unavailable but describe-environment still exits 0 with a warning (graceful degrade). (4) Negative path (invalid env name) returns a clean structured isError=true with the available-envs list, no crash.
Files: scratchpad/mcp_driver.py, scratchpad/mcp_sys.py (throwaway MCP stdio clients); no repo changes.
Impact: Live release gate for MCP is green — server boot + initialize handshake, tools/list, get-tool-contract compact index, get-guidance routing, list-environments, and LIVE env-sensitive tools (describe-environment via clio-run, list-packages, list-apps, get-sys-setting x2, list-sys-settings) all succeed against a fresh stand; negative path clean. Reusable recipe for future release smokes: reg-web-app + ping + the two scratchpad drivers (remember the `{"args":{...}}` vs clio-run `{"command","args"}` shapes).

## 2026-07-07 – MCP tool-DISCOVERY regression: 4 sandboxed subagents vs live stand (lazy surface)
Context: Follow-up to the same-day live MCP smoke. Goal per user: verify the lazy-surface tool-DISCOVERY regression — give an agent a natural-language task (NO tool names), deny it the clio CLI, force it through the MCP tool surface only, then analyze the session. Related existing asset: skill `caadt-metarepo/.claude/skills/clio-mcp-regression` (Method A = deterministic static MCP-surface diff via scripts/mcp_dump.py+mcp_call.py; Method B = heavy functional sweep via the `creatio-adaclio-testing` harness + Playwright + fresh TeamCity stands). This run is a lightweight Method B.
Decision: Built a faithful MCP stdio client bridge (scratchpad/mcp_bridge.py + per-task wrappers mcp_t1..t4.py) that exposes ONLY what a real MCP client sees — server `instructions` from initialize + resident `tools/list` + `tools/call` by name — and logs every request/response to t{N}.log.jsonl (ground truth for session analysis, independent of the agent's self-report). Global `clio` is NOT on PATH and not a global dotnet tool, so subagents physically cannot shortcut via CLI; the bridge points at the freshly-built clio/bin/Debug/net10.0/clio.dll. Spawned 4 general-purpose subagents, each sandboxed (only the bridge; no dotnet/clio, no other mcp__ servers, no reading clio source), each one natural-language task: t1 env profile, t2 package/app inventory, t3 sys-settings read, t4 ensure-a-Text-sys-setting (destructive-discovery).
Discovery: ALL 4 completed correctly with genuine discovery — verdict PASS. (1) Lazy surface healthy: 27 resident tools advertised; hidden long-tail (describe-environment, create-sys-setting, odata-read) all found via the get-tool-contract compact index and invoked via clio-run. (2) Server instructions + get-guidance routing/core-rules work: t1/t4 read them and hit the right tool with zero wrong guesses. (3) clio-run tolerance verified live in BOTH shapes — canonical {"command","args"} (t3 odata-read) AND wrapped-flat {"args":{"command",...flat}} recovered by RecoverWrappedCall/StripCommandKey (t1 describe-environment, t4 create-sys-setting incl. a DESTRUCTIVE tool). (4) SYSTEMIC FRICTION (4/4 agents): every agent's FIRST call failed because the mandatory {"args":{...}} envelope is not discoverable from tools/list — only from the McpToolErrorFilter hint text; all recovered in exactly 1 retry, so it's a repeatable +1 round-trip tax, not a blocker. (5) Minor: dataforge-* tools return functional failures via structured content with isError=false (long-tail POCO envelope), and DataForge is uninitialized on a bare product stand ("baseUri null"); t3 correctly routed around it via odata-read. Independently re-read confirmed t4's write really landed: get-sys-setting ClioMcpDiscoveryCheck -> "passed" on the stand (left in place; stand is disposable, auto-expires ~24h).
Files: scratchpad/mcp_bridge.py, mcp_t1..t4.py, t1..t4.log.jsonl (throwaway); no repo changes.
Impact: Reusable, CLI-free discovery-regression recipe: faithful MCP bridge + per-task JSONL logging + sandboxed subagents whose ground-truth calls are auditable independent of their self-report. The lazy-surface + clio-run dispatch path (read AND destructive) is proven end-to-end against a live stand. Actionable follow-up for the team: the undiscoverable {"args":{...}} wrapper is the single biggest first-call discoverability tax (every fresh client pays it once) — consider advertising the envelope in tools/list inputSchema or server instructions. Side note: the plugin MCP server `plugin:creatio-ai-app-development-toolkit:clio` shows "Failed to connect" because it runs bare `clio mcp-server` and global clio is absent on this box.

## 2026-07-07 – CORRECTION to the discovery-regression p.4 "wrapper friction" (it was a harness artifact)
Context: User pushed back on the prior entry's finding #4 (every fresh agent's FIRST MCP call fails on the {"args":{...}} envelope), noting ENG-92653 / ENG-92761 / ENG-92462 were supposed to address this. Investigated the three tickets + the live inputSchemas.
Decision/finding: My p.4 was a FALSE POSITIVE caused by MY bridge, not a clio regression. Root cause: mcp_bridge.py `list` printed only name+description and HID each tool's inputSchema, so the sandboxed subagents hand-built JSON blind and guessed top-level fields → hit the McpToolErrorFilter wrapper hint → self-corrected. A real MCP client (Claude Code) injects the full inputSchema, which correctly advertises the envelope. Verified against the live net10 build:
  - ENG-92653 fix IS present: clio-run.args inputSchema = {"type":["object","null"]} (the untyped-args bug that made Claude Code drop the object is fixed).
  - Resident tools (get-guidance/list-packages/get-tool-contract) expose args typed as object with inner props and required:["args"] — the envelope is fully self-describing.
  - ENG-92761 (resident flag + core-rules line) present; ENG-92462 is an e2e-runtime perf task, unrelated to wrapping.
Proof: added inputSchema to the bridge `list` output (faithful-client parity) and re-ran the same two tasks. Ground-truth logs: env-profile retest = 3 calls, 0 wrapper failures (get-tool-contract{}, get-tool-contract{tool-names}, clio-run{command,args}); inventory retest = list-packages/list-apps, 0 wrapper failures. First-attempt correct wrapping in BOTH the resident {"args":{...}} shape and the clio-run {"command","args"} envelope. Prior run (schema hidden) had a 1st-call wrapper failure on 4/4 agents; with schema shown it is 0/N.
Files: scratchpad/mcp_bridge.py (list now emits inputSchema), t1/t2 retest logs; no repo changes.
Impact: The three tickets hold — do NOT reopen on the strength of the earlier p.4. Lesson for this discovery-regression recipe: the bridge MUST surface each tool's inputSchema in `list`, else you measure the harness's blind-spot, not the server. The real (schema-typed) client wraps correctly first try. Any future "wrapper friction" claim must be reproduced with schema exposed before it counts.

## 2026-07-09 – ENG-93208 Story 1 spike: per-request context flow (RISK #1) → GO
Context: BMAD blocking spike for mcp-http-credential-passthrough; must prove singleton ICredentialContextAccessor-over-IHttpContextAccessor seam + FR-06 async-flow independence before any downstream code.
Decision: GO on the seam; FR-06 verdict = AsyncLocal-on-singleton OK (scope MUST open inside BaseTool.InternalExecute try/finally, not middleware). No fallback to per-request DI scope needed.
Discovery: MCP AspNetCore/Core 1.4.0 — .WithHttpTransport() defaults EnableLegacySse=false & PerSessionExecutionContext=false; AspNetCore sets FlowExecutionContextFromRequests=!PerSessionExecutionContext (decompiled line 1898/1947) → Core captures request ExecutionContext (33709) and runs each handler under its own ExecutionContext.Run (22591), firing without await (22593) = genuine concurrency. XML doc explicitly: PerSessionExecutionContext=true "prevents you from using IHttpContextAccessor in handlers" ⇒ false enables it. ilspycmd on macOS needs DOTNET_ROOT=~/.dotnet to launch. Runtime probe (throwaway, scratchpad) booted real SDK host on loopback, 2 concurrent tools/call with distinct X-Marker headers + Barrier(2) forced overlap → all pass (readback correct, no ValidateOnBuild throw, independent flows).
Files: spec/mcp-http-credential-passthrough/context-flow-spike-findings.md, spec/stories/story-mcp-http-credential-passthrough-1.md, spec/sprint-status.yaml
Impact: unblocks stories 3-15. step-15e assertion target = IOptions<HttpServerTransportOptions> EnableLegacySse/PerSessionExecutionContext/Stateless all false; recommend also pinning them explicitly in WithHttpTransport(o=>...). ADR edit deferred to orchestrator (recommended text supplied). No repo scaffold kept.

## 2026-07-09 — Story 7 (keystone): ephemeral passthrough resolution + FR-12 (ENG-93208)
Context: wire per-request credential context → ephemeral EnvironmentSettings → ToolCommandResolver → Story-3 bearer factory, SSRF validator first; fix FR-12 misleading error.
Decision: Cross-host DI resolved with null-object pattern. Shared `BindingsModule.RegisterInto` registers `NullCredentialContextAccessor`/`NullTargetUrlValidator` as defaults (next to `IToolCommandResolver`); `McpHttpServerCommand.Run` registers the REAL `CredentialContextAccessor`/`TargetUrlValidator` AFTER the shared build → last-registration-wins gives real-in-HTTP, null-in-stdio + ephemeral containers. Both interfaces stay in the RegisterAssemblyInterfaceTypes skip-list (skip keyed on interface → suppresses both real + null auto-registration). `ToolCommandResolver.Resolve` reads `Current` FIRST (before bootstrap/registry); non-null → passthrough branch: EnsureAllowed(url) → cookie/missing-auth guard as EnvironmentResolutionException (exit 1) → BuildEphemeralSettings(context) (internal static, IVT-tested directly) → dedicated cache key hashing kind+token+cookie+login+password → GetRequiredService.
Discovery: passthrough must NOT gate on PassthroughModeEnabled (that flag is Story-10/FR-19). Cookie must be intercepted in resolver (exit 1) or the deep ApplicationClientFactory NotSupportedException surfaces as exit -1 "wiring bug". Cache secret material is SHA-256'd; `BuildCacheKey` (existing) omits token → cross-tenant collision, hence the dedicated passthrough key. FR-07 unification + FR-08 TTL/eviction remain Story 8. The "before-client-build" ordering is structural (linear method), not observable via a mock (client is Lazy, container not mockable) — tests assert Received(1) on happy path + full abort on throwing validator.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/NullCredentialContextAccessor.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/NullTargetUrlValidator.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ToolCommandResolver.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/McpHttpServerCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ToolCommandResolverTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ToolCommandResolverNoWriteTests.cs
Impact: unblocks Story 8 (cache unification/TTL) and Story 10 (passthrough-mode enforcement). Tests: McpServer filter 1805/0, full Unit 5074/0 (both DI graphs validate, no ValidateOnBuild regression).

## 2026-07-10 12:00 – ENG-93208 Story 15 (FR-16) consolidated test suite + FromException redaction fix
Context: Final story of mcp-http-credential-passthrough — unit coverage audit (15a), exhaustive secret-leak matrix (15b), transport-default pin (15e), and authored-but-manual e2e (15c/15d/AC-07).
Decision: 15a = coverage MAP is the deliverable; no unit gaps → added no duplicates. 15b = the genuine gap: CommandExecutionResult.FromException (-1 catch-all) was NOT redacted and McpToolErrorFilter never sees it because BaseTool.ExecuteLocked RETURNS the envelope (doesn't throw). Fixed at source: SensitiveErrorTextRedactor.Redact around FormatExceptionChain INSIDE FromException (scoped to -1; exit-1 FromResolverError/FromValidationError left secret-free). Proved leak test-first (RED with fix reverted, GREEN after). 15e = shared internal static ConfigureHttpTransport lambda used by BOTH .WithHttpTransport(...) and McpHttpTransportDefaultsTests [Integration] so pin & assertion cannot diverge (EnableLegacySse/PerSessionExecutionContext/Stateless all =false).
Discovery: (1) EnableLegacySse is [Obsolete]→MCP9004; setting it to the SAFE value still warns → scoped #pragma with justification at prod + test sites. (2) IOptions<HttpServerTransportOptions> resolves fine from a bare AddMcpServer().WithHttpTransport() provider — no HttpServerTransportOptionsSetup DI-dependency fallback needed. (3) McpClient.CallToolAsync returns ValueTask (needs .AsTask() for WhenAll/WhenAny). (4) e2e session harness is stdio-only (StdioClientTransport); HTTP passthrough needs HttpClientTransport + HttpClientTransportOptions{Endpoint,AdditionalHeaders,TransportMode=StreamableHttp} — new McpHttpServerSession helper spawns `clio mcp-http` + connects, McpHttpPassthroughStand gates via Assert.Ignore on CLIO_MCP_HTTP_E2E_* env vars. (5) EnvironmentSettings.Password is NOT [JsonIgnore] (deliberately persisted) — only AccessToken/Cookie are; CLI-stdout matrix sink must seed only the transient passthrough secrets.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/CommandExecutionResult.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/McpHttpServerCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/CredentialPassthroughSecretHygieneTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpHttpTransportDefaultsTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpHttpServerSession.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpHttpPassthroughStand.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpHttpConcurrencyIsolationE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpHttpMultiTenantE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpHttpNoRegressionE2ETests.cs
Impact: closes the FR-11 -1-envelope leak for ALL mcp tools (not just passthrough); certifies multi-tenant safety/secret-hygiene/no-regression before the edge faces the gateway. Tests: full Unit net10.0 5178/0/35; new Integration 2/2; clio.mcp.e2e compiles. e2e NOT in CI — manual live-stand.

Impact: clio compiles clean (net10.0); ProcessModel 94 unit tests pass; e2e compiles (env-gated — needs the rebuilt package + a stand). MCP + docs reviewed and updated. No CLI docs/help to touch (create/modify-business-process are feature-toggled -> omitted from the committed baseline). Neither repo committed — awaiting user push.

## 2026-07-09 – Enumerate the full macro vocabulary in process-modeling guidance
Context: During manual testing a second agent doubted whether `CurrentHalfYear` is supported and nearly rolled it back. Root cause: the process-modeling guidance listed macros as `Today | Yesterday | CurrentUser | CurrentYear | NextNDays | …` — a truncated example list that never named the HalfYear (or Hour, or recurring "every year") families, so an agent reading the guide couldn't confirm them and (wrongly) assumed the designer/platform lacked them.
Decision: Replaced the truncated example with a dedicated bullet listing the COMPLETE macro set grouped by family — relative periods (Yesterday/Today/Tomorrow + Previous/Current/Next × Week/Month/Quarter/HalfYear/Year/Hour), argument macros (NextNDays/PreviousNDays/NextNHours/PreviousNHours + macroArgument), recurring "every year" (DayOfYearToday/DayOfYearTodayPlusDaysOffset/NextNDaysOfYear/PreviousNDaysOfYear), and system/lookup (CurrentUser/CurrentUserContact). Also stated explicitly that an unknown macro is REJECTED at build (validated against the platform catalog via MacrosCatalog.Resolve), NOT silently accepted — closing the "the API just took the string" misconception (that caution is true for the `expression`/formula path, not for `macro`).
Discovery: `CurrentHalfYear` IS fully supported — MacrosCatalog binds it to `(int)Terrasoft.Core.Entities.EntitySchemaQueryMacrosType.CurrentHalfYear` (server enum; the package compiles against it, so the member exists and resolves at runtime). The designer has the Half-Year submenu (user screenshots this session). Only the 4 primary-column macros are genuinely client-only.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ProcessDesigner/ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs
Impact: clio compiles clean (net10.0); no test asserts the process-modeling macro text (safe). Future agents can confirm HalfYear/Hour/every-year macros from the guide instead of guessing.

## 2026-07-08 – ENG-90636 theming: sanitize list-themes success-path output
Context: PR #748 review — the error paths sanitized server strings but the list-themes success path printed server-controlled descriptor fields raw.
Fix: route theme Id/Caption/CssClassName/CssFilePath through TextUtilities.SanitizeForDisplay (reusing ThemeParameterValidator.MaxIdLength/MaxCaptionLength/MaxCssClassNameLength, promoted private→internal; CssFilePath uses the default cap) so a hostile caption can't forge output lines or inject terminal escapes; the per-field cap also bounds column width.
Files: clio/Command/Theming/ListThemesCommand.cs, clio/Theming/ThemeParameterValidator.cs, clio.tests/Command/ListThemesCommandTests.cs
Impact: closes the only merge-worthy finding from the PR review; regression test verifies collapse-to-spaces via the public Execute path.

## 2026-07-08 – PR #748: validate latest request-changes review, fix confirmed findings
Context: Latest CHANGES_REQUESTED review (id 4654343682) listed 9 findings; validated each against HEAD d7cb9dd0 before fixing.
Discovery: Finding "delete-package always exits 0 (IApplicationPackageListProvider unregistered)" is false — BindingsModule.RegisterAssemblyInterfaceTypes auto-registers all Clio.* interface impls, so the optional ctor param IS injected. Private JsonSerializerOptions{PropertyNameCaseInsensitive} is a codebase-wide idiom (6+ sites), not a two-file drift.
Fix: sanitize list-themes MCP result fields via SanitizeForDisplay (mirrors CLI printer); add 6 missing theming-tool AddTransient registrations; cache parameterized regexes in ThemeCssBuilder (ConcurrentDictionary); merge duplicate IOException/UnauthorizedAccessException catch pairs via `when` filter (ThemeRequestBuilder, BuildThemeCommand).
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ListThemesTool.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/Theming/ThemeCssBuilder.cs, clio/Command/Theming/ThemeRequestBuilder.cs, clio/Command/Theming/BuildThemeCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ListThemesToolTests.cs

## 2026-07-09 00:00 – Port `register-sso-provider` command from creatio-identity-platform-cli
Context: User asked to replicate the identity-platform-cli `sso register-provider` command in clio.
Decision: Added `register-sso-provider` (alias `sso-register-provider`) as a `RemoteCommand<RegisterSsoProviderOptions>` extending `IdentityCommandOptions` (gives `--format text|json`). Posts a camelCase payload to `api/SsoProvider/Register`; secret resolution order file > flag > `CLIO_OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET` env var; create-only (server rejects duplicate `--code`).
Discovery: The SsoProvider controller route is the INVERSE of the usual Creatio convention — net8 (.NET Core) serves it under the `0/` workspace prefix, net472 (.NET Framework) without one. So I did NOT reuse `RemoteCommand.RootPath` (which prefixes `/0` for framework, not core); the route is built explicitly from `EnvironmentSettings.IsNetCore`. The source CLI trial-and-errors both routes because it only has a URL; clio knows IsNetCore, so a deterministic route is cleaner. `BaseCommandTests<T>` enforces 4 doc artifacts via ReadmeChecker: Commands.md index entry, help/en/<verb>.txt, docs/commands/<verb>.md, and a Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt line.
Files: clio/Command/RegisterSsoProviderCommand.cs, clio/Program.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/Commands.md, clio/Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt, clio/help/en/register-sso-provider.txt, clio/docs/commands/register-sso-provider.md, clio.tests/Command/RegisterSsoProviderCommandTests.cs
Impact: New command builds clean and 9 unit tests pass on net8.0+net10.0. No MCP tool surface added (MCP exposure is opt-in per command). If a stand is misconfigured on IsNetCore, the command will hit the wrong route (404) — unlike the source CLI it does not fall back.

## 2026-07-09 00:30 – register-sso-provider: two runtime fixes found via live run
Context: User's F5 run exited 0 with NO output (VS swallowed the parser exception). Ran the built clio.exe directly to surface the real error.
Discovery: (1) Option-name collision — `EnvironmentOptions` (CommandLineOptions.cs:13) already declares a hidden `--url` alias for `--uri`, so my `--url` made CommandLineParser throw "Sequence contains more than one matching element" during verb matching → renamed the option to `--issuer-url` (C# property stays `Url`). Lesson: check the base option long-names in clio/Command/CommandLineOptions.cs before naming a new [Option]; hidden aliases count. (2) `--IsNetCore` is `bool?` not `bool`, so CommandLineParser requires an explicit value — `--IsNetCore true`, a bare `--IsNetCore` errors "has no value". Also hardened ProceedResponse to fail on non-JSON responses (was reporting false success on HTML/404 bodies). Verified end-to-end against a live net8 stand: server returned {Id,Code,Name} (PascalCase) and exit 0.
Files: clio/Command/RegisterSsoProviderCommand.cs, clio/help/en/register-sso-provider.txt, clio/docs/commands/register-sso-provider.md, clio.tests/Command/RegisterSsoProviderCommandTests.cs
Impact: register-sso-provider confirmed working. Two reusable gotchas recorded: base hidden `--url` alias, and `bool?` options needing explicit values.

## 2026-07-09 01:15 – register-sso-provider: routing corrected to standard convention
Context: My initial route logic was inverted (netcore→/0, framework→no /0), copied from the source identity-platform-cli comment. User confirmed the target stand is .NET Framework and it needs /0, i.e. the standard clio convention: NetCore→no /0, Framework→/0.
Decision: Dropped the custom absolute-URL ServicePath and its IsNetCore branch; ServicePath is now the relative "/api/SsoProvider/Register" and RemoteCommand.RootPath (IsNetCore ? Uri : Uri+"/0") supplies the /0 prefix — same rule as every other clio RemoteCommand. Flipped the two routing unit tests accordingly. Also improved ProceedResponse: a bare JSON-string body (e.g. "SsoProvider with Code 'x' already exists.") is now surfaced verbatim as the failure message instead of the generic "non-JSON response" text (added TryGetJsonStringMessage).
Discovery: Verified live against the Framework stand — calling WITHOUT --IsNetCore routes to /0/api/SsoProvider/Register and succeeds (Test3code created). On this stand a POST to /api/SsoProvider/Register (no /0) returns an empty body (route not matched). Empirical platform check: POST /ServiceModel/AuthService.svc/Login → 200 at root. Note: earlier "success" runs used --IsNetCore true against a framework stand, which under the OLD inverted code produced /0 by accident — masking the bug. Lesson: don't trust a cross-repo routing comment over clio's own RootPath convention.
Files: clio/Command/RegisterSsoProviderCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/RegisterSsoProviderCommandTests.cs, clio/docs/commands/register-sso-provider.md
Impact: Routing now matches clio convention and the user's rule. 11/11 unit tests green on net8.0+net10.0; live create confirmed. OAuth (clientId/clientSecret) still returns Unauthorized on this stand — a stand-side OAuth-client config issue, not the command (proven with clio ping).

Discovery: origin/master merges silently reverted master's `string?` fix (71157d66) in 4 process-designer e2e fixtures — branch commits 5a843e4f/ecb457b7 touched the adjacent gate line, so conflict resolution took the branch hunk; PR diff showed it as our change.
Files: clio/Theming/ThemeCssBuilder.cs, clio/Theming/ColorMetrics.cs, clio/Theming/ThemeTemplateDefaults.cs, clio/Command/Theming/BuildThemeCommand.cs, clio/Command/Theming/ThemePaletteAdvisor.cs, clio/Command/Theming/ThemeTemplateProvider.cs, docs/McpCapabilityMap.md

## 2026-07-10 00:00 – ENG-93090 update-page append/full-config up-front guard
Context: Sub-task of ENG-90634 session analysis (rollout 2026-05-27). update-page had two contract-friction failures: `body` passed as an object ("must be a string, not dict") and append merge failing only after a server round-trip when the body used the full-config form.
Decision: Auto-conversion of full-config→diff was rejected as infeasible (a diff cannot be losslessly derived from a resolved config). Instead added an OFFLINE up-front guard: PageBodyMerger.UsesUnsupportedFullConfigForm(body, out message) (shared web/mobile message constants; the existing MergeWeb/MergeMobile throws now reuse them). PageUpdateTool.TryValidateAppendBodyForm runs in the pre-execution phase, only when mode=append, and short-circuits before ResolveCommand so no HTTP occurs. Clarified the `body` [Description] (raw string, not object) and `mode` [Description]; aligned page-modification-overview guidance and update-page docs.
Discovery: PageSchemaSectionReader's marker regex requires `*/` immediately after the marker name, so a scan for SCHEMA_VIEW_MODEL_CONFIG does NOT match SCHEMA_VIEW_MODEL_CONFIG_DIFF — detection is safe. `--mode` and `--body-file` were real but undocumented CLI options; now documented. No tool-level append test previously existed (only command-level at PageToolsTests ~3859), so the guard introduces no regressions.
Files: clio/Command/PageBodyMerger.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/PageUpdateTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/PageModificationOverviewGuidanceResource.cs, clio/help/en/update-page.txt, clio/docs/commands/update-page.md, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/PageToolsTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/PageUpdateToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: append+full-config now fails fast with a corrective hint (use replace / convert to diff) before any network call; body-as-object is steered away via the description; guidance/docs/MCP tool contract aligned. Targeted tests: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&(Module=McpServer|Module=Command)" green (3963 passed); 6 new unit tests pass.
## 2026-07-11 – Test plan for mcp-passthrough-tool-parity (ENG-93347, BMAD Phase 4)
Context: PRD/ADR/17 stories for the mcp-http passthrough tool-parity fix were already adversarially reviewed; QA phase needed the consolidated plan.
Decision: 87 unit TCs (per dependency-path, incl. separate tests per nested c1 call site), 0 integration (all I/O mocked at IApplicationClient/service boundary — deliberate), 35 E2E cases owned entirely by Story 15 (manual live-stand, NOT in CI); Story 16 registry makes the (tool, path, scenario)→test mapping self-enforcing in CI.

Module trait pattern for filters: [Category("Unit")] + [Property("Module","McpServer")] (GetUserCultureToolTests.cs:14-15). Stories 1 and 10 touch BindingsModule.cs → full unit suite trigger.
