## 2026-07-11 – Story 1 mcp-passthrough-tool-parity: credential-passthrough guard + link-from-repository fail-fast (ENG-93347)
Context: First implementation slice of the ENG-93347 ADR — class c3 (link-from-repository-*) fail-fast under credential passthrough, on branch claude/bmad-eng-93347-aeb7bd (stacked on claude/clio-mcp-multi-tenant-73a807).
Decision: New ICredentialPassthroughToolGuard (Tools/CredentialPassthroughToolGuard.cs) reads ICredentialContextAccessor.Current; BaseTool<T> gained optional ctor param `passthroughGuard` + `RejectIfPassthroughUnsupported(toolName, guidance)` helper. Rejections use CommandExecutionResult.FromValidationError (exit 1, EXPECTED refusal per ENG-91825 exit-code contract) — NOT FromError(-1) as the ADR snippet literally showed; AC-ERR says no exit-code requirement. Registered AddSingleton in BindingsModule next to the ICredentialContextAccessor null-object block; consumer lands in the same slice so CLIO005 stays clean.
Discovery: Relaxing MCP schema requiredness for a method-parameter tool needs BOTH dropping [Required] AND giving the param a default value — which forces reordering (optional params last). Existing positional unit-test call sites break semantically (silently shift args), so they must be updated to named args; MCP protocol/e2e callers are unaffected (JSON named args). `private protected` BaseTool helpers ARE testable from clio.tests via IVT + a derived probe tool.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/CredentialPassthroughToolGuard.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/BaseTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/LinkFromRepositoryTool.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/LinkFromRepositoryToolPassthroughTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/CredentialPassthroughToolGuardTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/LinkFromRepositoryToolTests.cs
Impact: Stories 2-17 can reuse the guard + BaseTool helper for every other guard-only path; the FromValidationError-vs-FromError decision and the reorder-for-optional pattern apply to every [Required] relaxation in FR-05a (Application args, PageSyncArgs).

## 2026-07-11 12:00 – Story 2 (ENG-93347): settings-based overloads for shared c1 deps
Context: mcp-passthrough-tool-parity Story 2 — shared-dependency slice that stories 3-9 build on.
Decision: Extracted settings-typed core helpers so name-based and settings-based overloads converge (CaptionCultureResolver: NormalizeOverrideCulture/ResolveProfileCulture/WarnAndFallBack; ApplicationInfoService: GetApplicationInfoCore/FindApplicationIdCore + EnsureApplicationIdentifier/EnsureApplicationCode). Settings-based paths never touch ISettingsRepository (ADR invariant); ArgumentNullException guard precedes any factory call.
Discovery: Adding an overload whose first param differs only by reference type breaks existing NSubstitute checks that pass an untyped `default!` literal — CS0121 at 10 call sites (`DidNotReceiveWithAnyArgs().GetApplicationInfo(default!, ...)`). Fix: `default(string)!` preserves the original overload binding. Stories 3-9 adding overloads to IApplicationListService/IApplicationCreateService/section services will hit the same pattern — pre-grep `default!` call sites.
Files: clio/Command/EntitySchemaDesigner/CaptionCultureResolver.cs, clio/Command/ApplicationInfoService.cs, clio.tests/Command/CaptionCultureResolverTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/ApplicationInfoServiceTests.cs, spec/stories/story-mcp-passthrough-tool-parity-2.md
Impact: Nested culture/app-info lookups in c1 tools can now be routed per-request; the `default(string)!` gotcha is documented for the remaining slices.

## 2026-07-11 13:40 – Story 3 (ENG-93347): list-apps routed through the resolver (c1)
Context: mcp-passthrough-tool-parity Story 3 — first of the serialized c1 tool chain (3→9); flagship PRD tool.
Decision: IApplicationListService gained the settings-based GetApplications overload (name-based + settings-based converge on private GetApplicationsCore, never touching ISettingsRepository). ApplicationGetListTool reworked onto BaseTool<EnvironmentOptions>(null, logger, commandResolver) + options-aware ExecuteWithCleanLog(options, ...) (FR-05 tenant lock/in-flight guard) with commandResolver.Resolve<EnvironmentSettings>(options) inside the body; [Required] dropped from ApplicationGetListArgs.EnvironmentName (FR-05a — no reordering needed, single already-defaulted param). Curated get-tool-contract entry for list-apps aligned (required list emptied + conditional-requiredness field description).
Discovery: The tools/list serialized-size ratchet (McpProfileGatingTests.RegisterEnabledPrimitives_ShouldKeepToolsSerializedSizeWithinBudget_WhenCalled, 34 KiB) had only ~120 bytes headroom — verbose passthrough notes in [Description] attributes broke it (34939 vs 34816). Keep FR-05a description additions TERSE ("Optional under credential passthrough."); stories 4-9 add 6 more and will need the same discipline or a conscious ratchet raise. Also: derived tools cannot reference BaseTool's primary-ctor param — store a private readonly copy (CheckThemingAccessTool pattern).
Files: clio/Command/ApplicationListService.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ApplicationTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ApplicationToolArgs.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ToolContractGetTool.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ApplicationGetListToolPassthroughTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ApplicationToolTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/ApplicationListServiceTests.cs, spec/stories/story-mcp-passthrough-tool-parity-3.md
Impact: Reference implementation for stories 4-9 (same file, same pattern); size-budget constraint and the _commandResolver-copy idiom documented before the remaining six tools repeat them.

## 2026-07-11 14:55 – Story 4 (ENG-93347): get-app-info routed through the resolver (c1)
Context: mcp-passthrough-tool-parity Story 4 — second of the serialized c1 chain (3→9); consumes Story 2's settings-based GetApplicationInfo overload, no service changes.
Decision: ApplicationGetInfoTool reworked onto BaseTool<EnvironmentOptions>(null, logger, commandResolver) with the private readonly _commandResolver copy; the options-aware ExecuteWithCleanLog(options, ...) runs INSIDE the McpProgressHeartbeat.RunWithProgressAsync work delegate (Func<TResult> is synchronous) so the FR-05 tenant lock is held on the worker thread for the whole backend call and never across an await — heartbeat + exactly-one-of id/code validation unchanged. [Required] dropped from ApplicationGetInfoArgs.EnvironmentName (string? = null; all following params already defaulted, all call sites named — no reordering). Curated get-tool-contract entry aligned (required list emptied + conditional-requiredness description).
Discovery: The 34-KiB tools/list ratchet is now EXHAUSTED. Story 3's exact method-param wording ("required unless credential passthrough supplies the tenant") tripped it by 25 bytes on this second tool; fixed by the terser "required unless passthrough" (args-record suffix "Optional under credential passthrough." kept verbatim). Remaining headroom ≈7 bytes — stories 5-9 CANNOT add any tools/list-visible text without an explicit ratchet-raise decision.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ApplicationTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ApplicationToolArgs.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ToolContractGetTool.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ApplicationGetInfoToolPassthroughTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ApplicationToolTests.cs, spec/stories/story-mcp-passthrough-tool-parity-4.md
Impact: Documents the async-tool variant of the c1 pattern (lock inside the heartbeat delegate) for create-app/create-app-section/update-app-section (stories 6-8, all async), and escalates the size-budget decision before story 5.

## 2026-07-11 16:20 – Story 5 (ENG-93347): create-app routed through the resolver incl. nested culture/polling (c1)
Context: mcp-passthrough-tool-parity Story 5 — third of the serialized c1 chain (3→9); first story whose service has a NESTED name-based call graph (culture at ApplicationCreateService.cs:88, polling/readback at :471/:484).
Decision: IApplicationCreateService gained the settings-based CreateApplication overload. Both public overloads converge on a private CreateApplicationCore parameterized with TWO delegates (Func<string> resolveProfileCulture, Func<string?, string, ApplicationInfoResult> loadApplicationInfo) — the name-based path binds the pre-change name-based nested calls byte-for-byte (AC-07), the settings-based path binds Story 2's settings-based overloads (AC-03/AC-04), so the "no settings-based overload calls a name-based overload" invariant holds without duplicating the ~70-line create flow. Tool follows Story 4's async shape (ExecuteWithCleanLog(options, ...) inside the heartbeat delegate); the tenant is resolved FIRST inside the locked body so mixed input dies before the class-(b) enrichment probe (AC-06; enrichment left untouched per AC-05). ApplicationCreateArgs.EnvironmentName relaxed to schema-optional and MOVED after name/code (C# optional-params-after-required; all call sites named). Architect-approved ratchet raise applied: MaxLazyToolsSerializedBytes 34816→35328; measured payload after this story = 34810 bytes (~518 headroom for stories 6-9).
Discovery: Removing [Required] from a record param that PRECEDES required params forces reordering, not just a default — safe only because every construction site uses named arguments (grep first). The delegate-core pattern is the right template for stories 6-8 (section create/update repeat the same nested culture + GetApplicationInfo calls, some twice). Nested-path proof works best by driving a REAL service from the tool fixture with substituted leaves (repository/culture-resolver/info-service) and asserting DidNotReceiveWithAnyArgs on the name-based overloads + repository.
Files: clio/Command/ApplicationCreateService.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ApplicationTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ApplicationToolArgs.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ToolContractGetTool.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ApplicationCreateToolPassthroughTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ApplicationToolTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpProfileGatingTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/ApplicationCreateServiceTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/CreateAppCommandTests.cs, spec/stories/story-mcp-passthrough-tool-parity-5.md
Impact: Establishes the delegate-parameterized-core pattern for every remaining c1 service with nested name-based calls (stories 6-9) and resets the tools/list budget headroom they will consume.

## 2026-07-11 – Story 15 live-stand run (ENG-93347): clio-run gap for long-tail tools in MCP E2E harness
Context: user supplied two real, distinct forms-auth Creatio tenants on ts1-core-dev04 to actually execute Story 15's deferred "manual live-stand run" (originally left as a follow-up since the implementing agent had no stand access).
Decision: added `McpHttpServerSession.EncodeLoginPasswordCredentials` + per-tenant `CLIO_MCP_HTTP_E2E_TENANT{1,2}_LOGIN`/`_PASSWORD` env vars to `McpHttpPassthroughStand` (bearer `_TOKEN` path untouched) — both real tenants were `IsNetCore=false`, no OAuth token available, only Supervisor/Supervisor forms-auth.
Discovery: the live run immediately surfaced that Story 15's own E2E fixtures called long-tail MCP tools (11 of the 15 touched tools — everything except list-apps/get-app-info/list-app-sections/get-component-info, the 4 resident in lazy-mode tools/list per ENG-90312/92761) DIRECTLY by name via tools/call, which only resolves resident tools and returns "Unknown tool: '<name>'" for the rest. A real client MUST reach the long tail via `clio-run` (`{"command":"<tool>","args":{...}}`) — `clio-run` forwards its own "args" field verbatim to the target's SDK-native binding, so wrapping is purely mechanical and preserves each tool's existing wrapped-record vs. flat-parameter shape. The AC-03 registered-env no-regression leg's identical direct-by-name bug was MASKED by its own deliberately narrow assertion (checks only for the ABSENCE of the passthrough-rejection text, which "Unknown tool" also satisfies) — a false green. Separately, the two pre-existing ENG-93208 describe-environment two-tenant tests assumed the response echoes the target host; it doesn't (coreVersion + seed-data GUIDs only) — both live tenants shared identical seed-data GUIDs (same base image) but differed on coreVersion, so the fix uses a response-level "not byte-identical + both carry real metadata" proof instead of a specific field value.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/McpHttpMultiTenantE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpHttpConcurrencyIsolationE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpHttpNoRegressionE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpHttpPassthroughStand.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpHttpServerSession.cs, spec/stories/story-mcp-passthrough-tool-parity-15.md
Impact: Final live result 55/55 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped, across two real tenants. Any FUTURE MCP E2E fixture that calls a tool NOT in the lazy-mode resident set (get-tool-contract / tools/list) must route through clio-run — direct tools/call by name silently produces "Unknown tool", and assertions that only check for a specific rejection substring can mask that failure as a false pass. Worth a general callout for anyone authoring new MCP E2E coverage, not just this feature.

## 2026-07-10 23:55 – ENG-93386 BMAD: standard MCP authorization (OAuth 2.1 RS)
Context: ENG-93208 testing showed the `--platform-api-key` front door is opaque + partial; mcp-http goes public → Alex wants standard MCP auth on ASP.NET.
Decision: front-door OAuth 2.1 Resource Server (AddJwtBearer + SDK McpAuthenticationHandler PRM/401), whole-endpoint RequireAuthorization; tenant Creatio creds kept as a SEPARATE upstream plane (MCP JWT never forwarded). AS = AI-Platform identity-platform; M2M via client_credentials (pre-registered confidential client, private_key_jwt). Target MCP spec 2025-11-25 + CC extension.
Discovery: C# SDK has native auth (AddMcp/ResourceMetadata/AddAuthorizationFilters), evolving → pin version. DCR now MAY; pre-registration first-priority (our case). codex research succeeded on retry (workspace credits).

Branch feature/ENG-93386-mcp-http-standard-authorization off umbrella claude/clio-mcp-multi-tenant-73a807 (commits 4a978cc3 ADR, 5ed4d9cb PRD/stories/tp). NOT pushed.
Impact: BLOCKING prereq = Story 1 spike (OQ-B: does identity-platform support client_credentials + RFC8707 resource + private_key_jwt). No code before that verdict.

## 2026-07-11 01:30 – ENG-93386 Story 2/3 implemented (OAuth RS auth for mcp-http)
Context: after Story-1 spike resolved OQ-B, implement config + JWT bearer validation.
Decision: AuthConfiguration/AuthEnvironment (5 --auth-* flags + CLIO_MCP_HTTP_AUTH_* env, enabled iff --auth-authority). McpHttpAuthentication.ConfigureServices = AddJwtBearer(issuer/audience/RS256+PS256/lifetime, MapInboundClaims=false, iss trailing-slash norm) + AddAuthorization 'mcp' policy via RequireAssertion lambda (scope/scp) — deliberately NOT a handler class (assembly-scan/ValidateOnBuild trap, per advisor + ENG-92146 lesson). Wired in Run() behind Enabled gate + UseAuthentication/UseAuthorization (hermetic fail-safe off; UseAuthentication w/o scheme throws).
Discovery: clio multi-targets net8.0+net10.0 and mcp-http compiles on both; JwtBearer package is net-major-bound (10.0.9 net10-only) -> pinned per-TFM in Directory.Packages.props (10.0.9 net10 / 8.0.11 net8). Bare-ServiceCollection ValidateOnBuild trips on AuthorizationPolicyCache->EndpointDataSource (routing-only) -> test resolves scheme+policy instead.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/AuthConfiguration.cs, McpHttpAuthentication.cs, McpHttpServerCommand.cs; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{AuthConfiguration,McpHttpAuthentication}Tests.cs (24 tests); Directory.Packages.props, clio/clio.csproj; help/en+docs/commands stubs; stories 2/3 -> done. Commit d8400836 on feature/ENG-93386-mcp-http-standard-authorization. NOT pushed.
Impact: endpoint enforcement (RequireAuthorization) + HTTP 401/403 matrix + fail-safe/public-bind guard = Story 5 next. Build clean both TFMs; McpServer unit 2040 passed/0 failed.

## 2026-07-11 15:20 – ENG-93386 Story 6: back-door hardening (FR-07/09/13 done, FR-08 scoped down)
Context: continuing sequential Story 2->8 implementation of the standard-OAuth redesign; Story 6 addresses the credential-passthrough back door now that whole-endpoint enforcement exists (Story 5).
Decision: consulted advisor before implementing FR-08 (gateway->tenant claim authorization) since Story-1's spike evidence contradicts the story's premise -- advisor confirmed: don't invent a fictional claim contract, implement only what's real (FR-07/09/13), scope FR-08 down explicitly with an ADR OQ + Jira comment rather than a silent gap or a speculative `ITenantAuthorizationPolicy` seam (would violate no-speculative-abstraction + risk CLIO005/ValidateOnBuild traps). FR-07: CaptureCredentialContext gained `requireAuthenticatedPrincipal` (= authConfiguration.Enabled) -- when standard OAuth is configured, header ignored unless `context.User.Identity.IsAuthenticated`; additive, OAuth-off mode unaffected (old platform-api-key gate keeps working alone).
Discovery: FR-09 (no-token-passthrough invariant) already held structurally before this story -- `ToolCommandResolver.BuildEphemeralSettings(CredentialContext)` never had access to `HttpContext.Request.Headers.Authorization`, confirmed by grepping every outbound Creatio client construction site (incl. the child-container site where B1 hid). FR-13: the existing generic `SensitiveErrorTextRedactor` (JWT/Bearer/credential-pair regexes) already scrubs both credential planes in one message -- no new redaction pipeline needed, just a combined-secrets test proving it.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpHttpServerCommand.cs (CaptureCredentialContext signature + Run() call site); clio.tests/Command/McpServer/CredentialPassthroughAuthHardeningTests.cs (new, 4 tests) + CredentialPassthroughMiddlewareTests.cs (3 call-site updates) + SensitiveErrorTextRedactorTests.cs (+1 combined-planes test); clio/help/en/mcp-http.txt + docs/commands/mcp-http.md (header-strip + FR-08 scope-down documented); spec/adr/adr-mcp-http-standard-authorization.md (new OQ-G); spec/stories/story-mcp-http-standard-authorization-6.md + spec/sprint-status.yaml (status: done, FR-08 caveat noted); Jira ENG-93386 comment filed for the platform team (per-tenant claim ask).
Impact: Story 7 (retire/gate --platform-api-key + unified error taxonomy) is next; it inherits a header-capture path that already understands "authorization configured" as a first-class parameter, so gating/removing the old gate should compose cleanly. Build clean both TFMs; McpServer unit 2064 passed/0 failed/1 skipped.

## 2026-07-11 16:10 – ENG-93386 Story 7: retire --platform-api-key as default front door (OQ-D/D-6)
Context: continuing sequential Story 2->8 implementation; Story 7 reconciles the ENG-93208 legacy platform-api-key gate with the new OAuth front door now that Stories 5/6 protect the whole endpoint and gate credential-capture on the authenticated principal.
Decision: ADR D-6 already resolved OQ-D (retire as default, keep as opt-in dev/offline fallback) -- implemented literally: EnforcePlatformApiKeyGate takes a new `authorizationEnabled` param; when true it bypasses the legacy key check entirely and derives PassthroughEnabledItemKey from `context.User.Identity.IsAuthenticated` alone (RequireAuthorization from Story 5 already guaranteed it); when false, byte-for-byte unchanged pre-ENG-93386 behavior. Key realization that made this non-arbitrary: once OAuth is on, `Authorization: Bearer <token>` is claimed by JwtBearerHandler -- there's no room left on that header for the old key, so "combining" the two schemes isn't just undesirable, it's structurally impossible. Added `ShouldWarnPlatformApiKeyIgnored` pure predicate (mirrors EvaluatePublicBindGuard's style) for a startup warning when both are configured (inert, not insecure).
Discovery: the "unified auth error taxonomy" (FR-11) the story asked for was already substantially closed by Stories 5/6 (RequireAuthorization + SDK PRM/401-challenge resolve auth outcomes before the tool layer) -- Story 7's real remaining piece was just removing the two-Bearer-schemes ambiguity, not building new taxonomy machinery.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpHttpServerCommand.cs (EnforcePlatformApiKeyGate signature/logic, ShouldWarnPlatformApiKeyIgnored, Run() wiring, --platform-api-key HelpText); clio.tests/Command/McpServer/PlatformApiKeyDispositionTests.cs (new, 7 tests) + CredentialPassthroughMiddlewareTests.cs (5 call-site updates); clio/help/en/mcp-http.txt + docs/commands/mcp-http.md (disposition documented in 3 places); spec/stories/story-mcp-http-standard-authorization-7.md + spec/sprint-status.yaml (status: done).
Impact: Story 8 (per-tool AddAuthorizationFilters + docs + e2e) is the last story; it inherits a clean single-front-door model with no legacy-key interaction to reason about when OAuth is on. Build clean both TFMs; McpServer unit 2071 passed/0 failed/1 skipped.

## 2026-07-11 18:30 – ENG-93386 Story 8 + final adversarial review: ALL 8 STORIES DONE
Context: final story of the sequential Story 1->8 implementation ("роби все по порядку") -- per-tool authorization filters decision, full doc sweep, e2e fixtures, MCP-surface check, and the mandatory final adversarial review gate before ready-to-merge (AGENTS.md code-review policy).
Decision: consulted advisor again before FR-10 (per-tool AddAuthorizationFilters) since it's the same shape as Story 6's FR-08 scope-down -- confirmed: identity-platform issues one undifferentiated scope for the gateway client, so a per-tool filter can only be a no-op (baseline scope, already enforced) or reject-all (an un-issued elevated scope); documented as ADR "Out of scope", one line, no speculative infra. Ran the FINAL adversarial review (3 parallel agents: code-quality-reviewer, performance-reviewer, security-reviewer, all opus) over the full branch diff vs claude/clio-mcp-multi-tenant-73a807 -- all 3 independently converged on the SAME two real gaps (high-confidence triangulation): (1) HIGH -- enabling OAuth via --auth-authority alone, with neither --auth-audience nor --auth-required-scopes set, silently disabled audience validation -> accepts ANY token from the (often-shared) issuer for ANY resource; (2) MEDIUM -- the Story 5 public-bind guard's IsWildcardHost only matched 4 literal spellings (0.0.0.0/*/:://[::]), missing a bind to a concrete LAN/public IP or DNS hostname. Both fixed with the SAME Ok/Warn/Refuse pattern already established (EvaluateAudienceScopeGuard + --auth-allow-any-audience; IsPublicBind broadening). Also fixed lower-severity findings: unified a duplicated/inconsistent truthy-env-var parser, stale e2e doc wording, ADR status flip to Accepted.
Discovery: running 3 independent review lenses in parallel and getting the SAME finding from all 3 (not just 1-of-3) is a strong signal to fix immediately rather than triage -- worth the pattern for future final-gate reviews on security-sensitive features.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpHttpServerCommand.cs (new EvaluateAudienceScopeGuard + IsPublicBind + --auth-allow-any-audience option), clio/Command/McpServer/AuthConfiguration.cs (IsTruthy made internal/shared), clio.tests/Command/McpServer/AudienceScopeGuardTests.cs (new) + PublicBindGuardTests.cs (extended), clio.mcp.e2e/McpHttpOAuthAuthorizationE2ETests.cs + Support/Mcp/{McpHttpOAuthStand,OAuthClientCredentialsTokenFetcher}.cs (new) + McpHttpServerSession.cs (extraArguments param), clio/help/en/mcp-http.txt + docs/commands/mcp-http.md + clio/Commands.md + docs/McpCapabilityMap.md (doc sweep), spec/adr/adr-mcp-http-standard-authorization.md (Status: Accepted + D-8 final-review-fixes section), spec/stories/story-mcp-http-standard-authorization-8.md + spec/sprint-status.yaml (status: done).
Impact: ENG-93386 (all 8 stories) is feature-complete on branch feature/ENG-93386-mcp-http-standard-authorization, unpushed per standing instruction. Full McpServer unit suite: 2087 passed/0 failed/1 skipped, both net8.0/net10.0. Next actions (not done in this session, per standing "don't push/open PR unless asked"): push the branch and open a PR when the user asks; run the OAuth e2e fixtures against a real identity-platform stand once one is available.

## 2026-07-11 19:15 – ENG-93386 PR #846 opened + codex review fixes
Context: Alex asked to open the PR, then to run a codex-CLI review on it.
Decision: pushed feature/ENG-93386-mcp-http-standard-authorization and opened PR #846, stacked on the still-open PR #830 (base=claude/clio-mcp-multi-tenant-73a807, not master) -- matches the ENG-93208 stacking precedent. Ran `codex review --base claude/clio-mcp-multi-tenant-73a807` (non-interactive, no MCP server for codex was available in-session, so used the CLI's dedicated `review` subcommand instead of `exec`). Codex found 2 real P2 issues in the Story-8 final-review fixes themselves (a second-pass catch on code written just hours earlier):
1. `IsPublicBind` reused the narrow `IsLoopbackAlias` (4-literal set) instead of a full loopback-range parser, so `127.0.0.2` would have been misclassified as public and refused a harmless local bind -- fixed by promoting `TargetUrlValidator.IsLoopbackIpOrLocalhost` (private->internal) and reusing it.
2. The e2e `OAuthClientCredentialsTokenFetcher` sent the documented comma-separated scope list verbatim as the OAuth `scope` form field; RFC 6749 requires space-delimited -- fixed with a `ToSpaceDelimitedScope` normalizer.
Discovery: even a feature that just passed a 3-reviewer adversarial gate can have a follow-up review (different tool, different angle) catch something real -- codex caught a regression I introduced IN the fix for the adversarial review's own finding (IsPublicBind). Worth running an independent second-opinion review after fixing first-pass findings, not just after the original implementation.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpHttpServerCommand.cs (IsPublicBind), clio/Command/McpServer/TargetUrlValidator.cs (IsLoopbackIpOrLocalhost visibility), clio.tests/Command/McpServer/PublicBindGuardTests.cs (+2 tests), clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/OAuthClientCredentialsTokenFetcher.cs (ToSpaceDelimitedScope).
Impact: PR #846 pushed with both rounds of fixes (commits a9177b27 + d45ecbca). McpServer unit suite: 2089 passed/0 failed/1 skipped, both net8.0/net10.0.

## 2026-07-09 13:40 – ENG-92669 facts-first: TeamCity PR-checks blocker = no github.com credential
Context: Alex wants Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests + Team_Atf_ClioUnitTests to auto-run on github.com clio PRs and show as commit-status checks (TeamCity-native: Pull Requests + Commit Status Publisher). Chose "investigate facts first". TeamCity is company-internal infra.
Discovery (teamcity CLI, TeamCity 2025.11): (1) BLOCKER — the only GitHub OAuth connection reachable from Team_Atf→_Root is a GitHub App for creatio.ghe.com (GHE) [_Root/PROJECT_EXT_114, appId 11307]. clio lives on public github.com — different host — so NO github.com credential exists; TeamCity cannot read PRs or post statuses to github.com today. Connection hierarchy sweep (_Root/Team/Team_Atf) was exhaustive; only _Root has OAuth connections (GHE App, GitLab, Vault, Docker). (2) Both VCS roots authMethod=ANONYMOUS; e2e uses _Root-scoped `Team_Atf_ClioGitHub` (branch refs/heads/%BranchNameClio%), unit uses Team_Atf-scoped root pinned #refs/heads/master; neither has PR branch spec, Pull Requests feature, or Commit Status Publisher. (3) My rights = PROJECT_ADMIN on p:Team_Atf → I can add build features/trigger/OAuth connection at Team_Atf myself once a credential exists; I CANNOT edit the shared _Root VCS root (largely unneeded — Pull Requests feature fetches refs/pull/* at runtime).
Decision: Did NOT mutate any TeamCity config (Alex picked facts-first; credential choice is his). Single decision fork surfaced: PAT (repo:status write + PR read, Alex mints — low friction) vs org GitHub App for github.com (admin-heavy). Runbook for the moment a credential lands written to spec.
Files: spec/teamcity-pr-checks/teamcity-pr-checks-findings.md (findings + runbook); no repo code changes.
Impact: Turns the ticket's vague "auth prerequisite" into a concrete, verified fork + a ready-to-apply runbook. Unblocks the rest the instant Alex provides a github.com credential.

(3) Wrote .github/workflows/teamcity-mcp-e2e.yml (pull_request, self-hosted Windows/PowerShell, paths clio/** clio.mcp.e2e/** cliogate/** Directory.Packages.props, concurrency per-PR, POSTs buildQueue with BranchNameClio=head.ref + Deploy/Destructive/ProductName). DeployCreatioBuild=false makes a fast build that still exercises the publisher (cheap status-chain test). ## 2026-07-10 – ENG-93296 theming surface gated to Creatio 10.x via [RequiresCreatioVersion]
Context: theming commands call the native ThemeService (10.x-only); pre-10 environments got raw HTTP/HTML errors. First production consumer of the existing version-gate infra.
Decision: [RequiresCreatioVersion("10.0.0")] (shared const ThemeServiceRequirement.MinVersion, no custom Hint) on create/update/delete/list-themes + clear-themes-cache + check-theming-access options; build-theme/advise-theme-palette stay ungated (pure compute). MCP gate moved from InternalExecute<TCommand> into BaseTool.ResolveCommand (same chokepoint as the package gate) so typed-response tools are covered; InternalExecute maps CreatioVersionRequirementException → exit 78. In ResolveCommand the version gate runs BEFORE the package gate, mirroring the CLI dispatch order (feature-toggle → creatio-version → package); a dual-attribute BaseToolTests harness pins the precedence (version refusal wins, package checker never resolved). McpCapabilityMap "Version gate (exit 78)" refreshed (typed-response refusal shape, gate order, coverage posture kept consumer-agnostic) and the theming section states the 10.0.0 floor.
Discovery: typed-response tools (CreateThemeTool/ListThemesTool) bypassed the old post-resolve version gate entirely. CheckThemingAccessTool was the only theming tool without a command — extracted CheckThemingAccessCommand (rights+license composition) so the tool routes through ResolveCommand and the gate needs no protected API (EnforceCreatioVersionRequirements stayed private).
Files: clio/Command/Theming/ThemeServiceRequirement.cs, clio/Command/Theming/CheckThemingAccessCommand.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/BaseTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/CheckThemingAccessTool.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/BaseToolTests.cs, docs/McpCapabilityMap.md
Impact: pre-10.x refusal is uniform on CLI (exit 78) and MCP (structured failure) with test-pinned cross-surface gate precedence; tool contracts/help/docs/guidance advertise the floor; per-tool refusal tests (all 6 tools) pin gating behaviorally; BuildThemeCommandTests pins that build-theme is not gated. E2E scope: enforcement itself is not e2e-testable without a pre-10 stand; the advertised floor IS covered — ThemingVersionFloorContractE2ETests asserts all six gated tool contracts (get-tool-contract) state the floor, and a ThemingGuidanceResourceTests drift guard pins the theming guide text to ThemeServiceRequirement.MinVersion (the guide is a raw string with JSON braces, so const interpolation was rejected in favor of the test).

## 2026-07-10 – Fix get-syssetting alias writing empty value (data-loss footgun)
Context: Ticket — `clio get-syssetting <code>` (alias of set-syssetting) read mode was gated solely by --get, so a value-less get fell through to the write path and POSTed {"<code>":""} via PostSysSettingsValues, silently clearing the setting. Value-less `set-syssetting <code>` did the same.
Decision: Two-layer fix. (1) Program.NormalizeGetSysSettingArgs — when args[0]=="get-syssetting" (case-insensitive) and no --get present, append --get so the get alias resolves to the read path; wired into NormalizeCommandLineArgs (runs for dispatch/help/version, harmless for the latter two). (2) SysSettingsCommand.Execute — write path now returns 1 with a user-facing error when opts.Value is null (defense in depth; also covers the scenario/YAML runner which builds options directly via Activator and bypasses arg normalization). Also added a WriteWarning when a value is supplied on the get path (value ignored, read-only) — from review Finding 1.
Discovery: CommandLineParser does NOT surface which alias invoked a verb, so alias→read intent must be handled at the arg-normalization layer (same pattern as create-data-binding --environment→-e and the --json bare-flag injection). Cut line is `opts.Value is null` (omitted positional) NOT IsNullOrEmpty — an explicit `set-syssetting code ""` stays a legitimate "set empty" write, consistent between CLI and YAML. Scenario runner (YAML/Step.cs) constructs SysSettingsOptions directly, which is exactly why the Execute-level guard (not just normalization) is load-bearing. Base BaseClioModuleTests.TearDown is [TearDown] virtual — override it (call base) to avoid CS0114; several sibling fixtures still hide it (pre-existing warnings).
Files: clio/Program.cs, clio/Command/SysSettingsCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/SysSettings.cs (refactored fixture to DI-container SUT resolution per convention), clio.tests/Command/SysSettingArgsNormalizationTests.cs (new), clio/help/en/set-syssetting.txt, clio/docs/commands/set-syssetting.md. MCP reviewed, no update required (get/set/list/create-sys-setting tools are separate and already read/write-correct; TryUpdateSysSetting already rejects null value).
Impact: get-syssetting now reads; a value-less write can never clear a setting. Validated: dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit" (full suite, 5418 passed / 0 failed on net8.0; Program.cs change = full-suite trigger).

## 2026-07-10 – ENG-91842 elementId→elementName rename + full e2e verification
Context: reviewer follow-up (PR #745 / #6) flagged the filter element-parameter-ref key `elementId` — it holds an element Name, not a GUID — as inconsistent with the `elementName` handle used by mappings/ops.
Decision: the surface is unpublished (feature-toggled off) and `elementId` is never persisted (the server resolves the ref to a meta-path token), so did a clean RENAME rather than a back-compat alias — nothing external can have pinned it, and an alias would perpetuate the misnomer. Supersedes the "deferred" note in the delta-review entry above.
Fixes: package FilterElementParameterRef.elementName + ResolveReference/HasReference + error messages (75635a9); clio describe DTO DescribedFilterElementRef.elementName + guidance/spec examples + forward-compat test (b378c303); fixed a stale reader trim comment — the whole-day trim is ANY comparison, not just `equal` (b3a50d6); swept the serializer/reader pair, no other equal-scoped trim wording remained.
Discovery: WCF (WebMessageFormat.Json) deserializes the request via DataContractJsonSerializer; clio forwards the descriptor with JsonNode.Parse (keys verbatim), so the rename belongs on the package DTO only. No e2e sends a filter elementParameter ref (they use mappings' elementName or constant/macro/datePart/processParameter), so the rename can't affect the e2e suite.
Verification: full ProcessDesigner MCP e2e against a live test stand — 33/33 passed, 0 failed/skipped (incl. the new signalStart param-ref rejection, date-only trim round-trip, setFilter/clearFilter); package unit dev-nf 397 passed; clio unit McpServer+ProcessModel 1957 passed. Reviewer notes posted on #745 + #6.
Files: (package) Contracts/FilterContracts.cs, Filters/ProcessFilterService.cs, Filters/FilterDescriptorReader.cs, tests; (clio) Command/ProcessModel/IProcessDescriber.cs, McpServer/Resources/ProcessDesigner/ProcessModelingGuidanceResource.cs, tests, spec/process-design-service/*.
Impact: the filter element-parameter ref is now consistently `elementName` across both repos; full stack green live. The elementName item is CLOSED (no longer deferred).

Decision: `new-integration-test-project` (`integration-test`) is environment-independent and generates only a .NET test project, Allure config, CI-friendly settings, and an ATF base fixture. Runtime configuration comes from NUnit parameters or environment variables using password or access-token authentication.

## 2026-07-10 - Durable MCP invocation (forgiving tool surface) — BMAD artifacts
Context: release-testing PR #743 surfaced that `clio createw` ships `clio/tpl/workspace/AGENTS.md` naming long-tail MCP tools (get-fsm-mode, compile-creatio, push-workspace, restart-by-environment-name, pkg-to-file-system/db) that #743's lazy surface removed from tools/list → direct tools/call dead-ends. Root cause: clio has CLI-flag backward-compat (CLIO001) but NO equivalent at the MCP-tool boundary; listing was reduced for context economy but invocation was silently reduced with it.
Decision: make the stdio MCP server forgiving. New feature branch `feature/mcp-durable-invocation` off origin/master. BMAD ADR-first artifacts written: PRD/ADR/5 stories/test-plan (spec/*). Design: (D1) register `McpDurableCallToolHandler` via SDK `WithCallToolHandler` (runs on ToolCollection miss, before "Unknown tool"); (D2) resolve via new `IMcpToolCompatibilityCatalog`; (D3) non-destructive → execute through EXISTING `IClioRunExecutor` (ClioRunTool.cs:22/48, already DI) + advisory `_meta` note; destructive → `confirmation-required` retry shape, never silent (IsDestructive fail-closed); (D5) structured error codes; (D6) drift-guard test scanning tpl/guidance vs surface + add clio/tpl/** to maintenance targets; (D7) rewrite tpl AGENTS.md to delegate to get-guidance/get-tool-contract/clio-run + strip BOM. B4 progressive disclosure REJECTED (cache churn, stateless-HTTP unreleased, cross-session leak). Jarvis/CLI durability OUT (its SKILL.md call shell `clio <verb>`, not MCP — separate CLI-contract task).
Discovery: verified via 4 explore agents + independent codex pass (task-mrezpvfr-gnnwa9) + live JSON-RPC repro. Codex corrections folded: `generate-source-code`->`restore-workspace` is NOT a clio rename (both verbs exist; drift is inside Jarvis's own map). Seam is `WithCallToolHandler` (cleaner than reusing the single AddCallToolFilter/McpToolErrorFilter). Registry silently keeps first duplicate name (McpToolInvokerRegistry.cs:112) -> make fail-fast. tools/list confirmed = 27 resident.
Files: spec/prd/prd-mcp-durable-invocation.md, spec/adr/adr-mcp-durable-invocation.md, spec/stories/story-mcp-durable-invocation-{1..5}.md, spec/test-plans/tp-mcp-durable-invocation.md, spec/sprint-status.yaml. No code yet (BMAD gate). Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/adaptive-leaping-bee.md.
Impact: implementation-ready; stories 1->2->3->4->5. Needs Jira key before PR. Next: implement Story 1 (catalog) then Story 2 (handler).

## 2026-07-10 - ENG-93370 ADR adversarial review (Codex) -> NO-GO -> ADR revised
Context: ran BMAD adversarial review of adr-mcp-durable-invocation.md via Codex (task-mrf28k5r-naq2pv), cross-checked against real code file:line. Verdict NO-GO on first draft.
Decision: accepted findings; revised ADR (round 1) + stories 1/2/3 + test-plan + PRD. Key design changes: (B1) authz = reproduce pre-#743 per-tool Destructive gate over the pre-#743 standalone set (NOT "all non-destructive") + audit Destructive annotations of high-impact write tools (install-gate/reg-web-app/experimental/get-browser-session/toolkit/hotfix) + completeness test - per Alex "execute for all tools that were standalone before #743"; (B2) native-call dispatch InvokeResolvedAsync (no {"args":{"args"}} double-wrap); (B3) register handler at STDIO call-site only (BindingsModule.cs:129), NOT transport-neutral RegisterMcpServer (:789) which mcp-http also calls (:McpHttpServerCommand.cs:64); (B4) drift oracle rewritten resident-or-bridged - "registry name resolves" proves existence not valid invocation, would leave regression green; (H5) preserve+restore RequestContext (Params/MatchedPrimitive/_meta/progress-token) in finally; (H6) advisory in Content not _meta (executor comment ClioRunTool.cs:149 says _meta not model-read); (H7) catalog-alias precedence + remove restart-by-environmentName duplicate method atomically; (H8) discriminated resolution result Resolved|Disabled|Unknown|Foreign|Ambiguous; (H9) eager startup collision validation (registry is transient DI, ctor not eagerly run); (M10) return-not-throw so McpToolErrorFilter doesn't flatten codes; (M12) correlation-id on all outcomes + test through RegisterMcpServer/filter.
Discovery (confirmed by Codex): WithCallToolHandler exists in MCP SDK 1.4.0, fires on ToolCollection miss, coexists with AddCallToolFilter, no resident shadowing. Zero duplicate tool-name keys today (restart pair are distinct names) so registry fail-fast won't break current catalog - use synthetic fixture. generate-source-code->restore-workspace is NOT a clio rename (both exist) - drift is in downstream consumer's own map.
Files: spec/adr/adr-mcp-durable-invocation.md (rewritten + Review-round-1 appendix), spec/stories/story-mcp-durable-invocation-{1,2,3}.md, spec/test-plans/tp-mcp-durable-invocation.md, spec/prd/prd-mcp-durable-invocation.md, spec/sprint-status.yaml.
Impact: ADR now GO-with-changes-applied; ready for implementation (or a re-review). Still no code.

## 2026-07-10 - ENG-93370 Story 1 implemented (compatibility catalog + registry fail-fast)
Context: started implementation. Story 1 = the durable core (IMcpToolCompatibilityCatalog) + registry collision fail-fast.
Decision/Discovery: (1) CLIO005 only tracks EXPLICIT Add*<T>()/typeof registrations - clio's reflection-loop RegisterAssemblyInterfaceTypes (AddTransient(ifaceVar, typeVar)) is INVISIBLE to it, so a new I*-interface auto-registers as transient and is NOT flagged even while unconsumed (Story 2 consumes it). So the catalog needs no clio-run wiring in Story 1. (2) ValidateOnBuild=true constructs every transient at container build across the whole suite -> the catalog's ctor validation runs eagerly for free (satisfies H9 "eager"). (3) restart-by-environmentName legacy [McpServerTool] method removal MOVED to Story 2 (atomic with the handler that reads the catalog) to avoid a regression window where nothing serves the alias. (4) Registry silent-first-duplicate (McpToolInvokerRegistry.cs:112) -> throw InvalidOperationException; zero dup names today so it only fires on authoring mistakes, surfaced at ValidateOnBuild startup. nameof(IMcpToolCompatibilityCatalog) resolves cross-namespace (Tools nested under McpServer). (5) Nullable disabled project-wide (no <Nullable>), LangVersion 12; internal ctor for tests (IVT clio.tests) - DI uses only public ctors so no ambiguity.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpToolCompatibilityCatalog.cs (new: interface + record + 2 enums + validated impl + seed), clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/McpToolInvokerRegistry.cs (fail-fast), clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpToolCompatibilityCatalogTests.cs (9 tests).
Validated: dotnet build clio.csproj net8.0 clean (0 err, 0 new CLIO warnings); dotnet test --filter "Category=Unit&Module=McpServer" net8.0 = 1868 pass / 0 fail / 1 skip.
Impact: catalog ready for Story 2 handler to consume; alias map + fail-fast validation proven.

## 2026-07-10 - ENG-93370 Story 2 implemented (durable call-tool handler) + LIVE protocol proof
Context: the core of the feature - the forgiving unmatched-name handler.
Decision: McpDurableCallToolHandler (new, interface IMcpDurableCallToolHandler auto-DI) registered via SDK WithCallToolHandler CHAINED AT THE STDIO CALL-SITE in BindingsModule.Register (after WithStdioServerTransport) - NOT in transport-neutral RegisterMcpServer, so mcp-http never gets it (review B3). Executor gains InvokeResolvedAsync (native args verbatim - no BuildChildParams re-wrap, review B2) + shared DispatchAsync with Params/MatchedPrimitive restore in finally (review H5) + RedactFailureContent extracted (shared redaction backstop). RunAsync also resolves catalog aliases now. RestartTool legacy method REMOVED (alias served by catalog). ToolContractIndexEntry gains optional aliases field projected from McpToolCompatibilityCatalog.SeedAliasesByCanonical (static seed view, same SeedEntries as DI instance).
Discovery: (1) RequestParams.ProgressToken is READ-ONLY in SDK 1.4 - derived from Meta; copying Meta preserves progress. (2) CallToolRequestParams.Task is [Experimental] MCPEXP001 -> pragma with justification. (3) CallToolRequestParams.Arguments is IDictionary (not IReadOnlyDictionary). (4) Test tools MUST be built with BindingsModule.CreateMcpSerializerOptions() (McpJsonUtilities camelCase) - JsonSerializerOptions.Default binds PascalCase and record fields silently arrive default. (5) LIVE stdio proof: direct get-fsm-mode EXECUTES (reached the tool, failed only on DNS) + advisory in Content + durable-invocation meta; destructive restart -> confirmation-required with clio-run-destructive retry shape; legacy camelCase alias -> canonical; typo -> unknown-tool + candidates [get-fsm-mode,...]; tools/list still 27.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpDurableCallToolHandler.cs (new), Tools/ClioRunTool.cs (InvokeResolvedAsync/DispatchAsync/RedactFailureContent/static BuildSuggestions/ExpectsSingleComplexArgsParameter/alias resolve), Tools/RestartTool.cs (legacy method removed), Tools/ToolContractGetTool.cs (aliases field), McpToolCompatibilityCatalog.cs (SeedAliasesByCanonical), BindingsModule.cs (WithCallToolHandler), tests: McpDurableCallToolHandlerTests.cs (7), ClioRunNativeDispatchTests.cs (4), ClioRunDispatchTests.cs (ctor).
Validated: full unit suite 5371 pass / 0 fail (BindingsModule touched -> full run); Module=McpServer 1879/0; live JSON-RPC repro above.
Impact: pre-#743 invocation contract restored on stdio; Stories 3-5 remain.

## 2026-07-10 - ENG-93370 Stories 3+4+5 implemented (drift guard, template fix, e2e, docs) - feature COMPLETE
Context: closing out the remaining stories in one pass per Alex "continue to the end".
Decision: (S3) WorkspaceTemplateGuidanceDriftTests - resident-or-bridged oracle over shipped tpl AGENTS.md files + McpServerInstructions.Text; tokenization: inline-backticked kebab tokens only, fenced blocks skipped, negations skipped (regex tolerates markdown **NOT**), MCP-name classification takes precedence over same-named CLI verb (otherwise the #743 set slips through as "CLI verbs"), bridge = same PHYSICAL line contains clio-run/get-tool-contract/get-guidance. Guidance-article bodies deliberately OUT of scope (live channel, McpGuidanceForcingTests owns them). + BOM guard test. (S4) tpl/workspace/AGENTS.md deploy/FSM section rewritten: live-channel-authoritative banner (get-guidance -> get-tool-contract -> clio-run), every long-tail imperative bridged via clio-run ON THE SAME LINE (per-line oracle!), all hard-won FSM facts preserved; BOM stripped from 11 tpl text files. RED->GREEN transition proven (old template flagged push-workspace et al.). (S5) DurableInvocationToolE2ETests (5 cases, env-free): experimental executes+advisory (exit-code 0 feature table over the wire!), destructive restart -> confirmation-required, legacy camelCase alias -> canonical, typo -> unknown-tool+did-you-mean, tools/list unchanged. (2) SerializeResult JSON-escapes apostrophes (') - assertion markers must avoid quoted substrings. (3) Test-built SDK tools MUST use BindingsModule.CreateMcpSerializerOptions() (camelCase) or record args silently bind defaults.

Validated: Module=McpServer 1885/0; e2e DurableInvocation 5/5 vs real stdio server; drift red->green transition.

## 2026-07-11 - ENG-93370 pre-PR gate (Codex comprehensive review round 2) - NO-GO fixed -> ready for PR
Context: repo policy comprehensive pre-PR review. My 4 parallel reviewer subagents died on session limit; Alex redirected the review to Codex MCP (single 4-lens pass: correctness, security, quality, tests). Verdict NO-GO: 0 Blockers, 3 Highs.
Decision (all Highs + cheap M/L fixed): (H1) confirmation-required NO LONGER echoes argument values (restart-by-credentials would reflect password into transcript; worse than clio-run which never re-echoes) -> retry shape = canonical command + argument-names only; BuildRetryArgs + ExpectsSingleComplexArgsParameter deleted as dead code. (H2) KEY CORRECTION: ValidateOnBuild does NOT instantiate transients - my round-1 "eager via ValidateOnBuild" was wrong; MCP host build now explicitly GetRequiredService's catalog+registry after container build (BindingsModule.Register registerMcpHost path) -> malformed surface aborts startup; McpHostStartupValidationTests pins it (host container builds fine in a unit test, ~440ms). (H3) registry was TRANSIENT via assembly-scan -> full reflection+SDK-tool-build per resolve, twice per unmatched call; now explicit AddSingleton for IMcpToolInvokerRegistry + IMcpToolCompatibilityCatalog on the MCP host (last registration wins over the scan). (M) catalog rejects executor names as canonical/alias + RunAsync canonicalizes aliases BEFORE the self-dispatch guard (latent alias->clio-run recursion). (M docs) capability map "stdio only" false (mcp-http exists unreleased); Resident XML doc now lists all reachability paths. (L) SanitizeName strips control chars + caps 64 for prose reflection.
Discovery: reordering alias-resolution before the executor guard broke 3 ClioRunDispatchTests that pinned "guard fires BEFORE TryGetTool" via DidNotReceive - the read-only lookup now legitimately precedes the guard; assertions relaxed to the actual invariant (refusal message = no dispatch). Test-assembly registry scan hits pre-existing duplicate fixture names (two echo-tool EchoToolType fixtures) - fine for the TC-U-05 throw test.
Files: McpDurableCallToolHandler.cs (no-echo retry, SanitizeName), McpToolCompatibilityCatalog.cs (executor rejection, doc), ClioRunTool.cs (guard reorder, dead code removed), McpToolInvokerRegistry.cs (comment), BindingsModule.cs (singletons + eager resolve), ToolContractGetTool.cs (Resident doc), docs/McpCapabilityMap.md, tests: +McpHostStartupValidationTests (2), +duplicate-name registry test, +cancellation/exception restore tests, +executor-rejection catalog tests, strengthened no-echo assert, 3 guard asserts relaxed, dead helper test removed.
Validated: full unit 5428/0; e2e DurableInvocation 5/5. Advisory leftovers for final ready-to-merge gate: TC-I-04 filter-pipeline composition, TC-U-11 foreign-command, exact 27-count e2e pin, typed e2e assertions.
Impact: all High findings closed; branch ready for PR.

## 2026-07-11 – `clio config` deploy-creatio defaults for Explorer right-click
Context: The Windows Explorer "clio: deploy Creatio" action runs `clio deploy-creatio --zip-file "%1"` with no other args, so it falls through to Kubernetes/Rancher. Users had no way to make it deploy to a local DB + Redis.
Decision: New `config` verb (General group) persists a `deploy-creatio-defaults` block in appsettings.json (db-server-name, redis-server-name, site-name, site-port, deployment). A new `IDeployCreatioDefaultsResolver` fills options omitted on the CLI from those defaults and derives a site name from the zip file name when none is set; it runs at the very start of `InstallerCommand.Execute` BEFORE the `_kubernetes is FakeKubernetes && DbServerName empty` guard, so a configured default db-server-name routes the plain Explorer deploy to local. Explicit CLI flags always win.
Discovery: The K8s-vs-local branch keys solely off `options.DbServerName`, so injecting defaults pre-guard is the single lever needed. The interactive STEP-1 site-name loop in CreatioInstallerService only runs `while (SiteName is empty)`, so any explicit --site-name (and now a derived one) already bypasses the existing-site collision prompt — derivation is consistent with existing --site-name behavior, not a new regression. `SanitizeSiteName` restricts to ASCII [A-Za-z0-9_] and prefixes a leading digit with `_` because real Creatio build zips start with a version (10.1.257_...), which is otherwise an invalid unquoted DB identifier.
Files: clio/Command/ConfigCommand.cs, clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/DeployCreatioDefaultsResolver.cs, clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/InstallerCommand.cs (ctor + ApplyDefaults call + shared AllowedDeploymentMethods/AutoDeploymentMethod consts), clio/Environment/ConfigurationOptions.cs + ISettingsRepository.cs (DeployCreatioDefaults + get/set), clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/Program.cs, clio/HelpSystem/CommandHelpCatalog.cs, docs (help/en/config.txt, docs/commands/config.md, Commands.md, Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt), tests (ConfigCommandTests, DeployCreatioDefaultsResolverTests, SettingsRepositoryDeployDefaultsTests + 2 updated InstallerCommand ctor call sites).
MCP: reviewed — not applicable. `config` is a local-workstation config command with no agent value (writes appsettings.json). deploy-creatio's existing MCP tool contract is unchanged: defaults are applied inside InstallerCommand, the tool's arguments/descriptions/destructive flags are untouched.
Impact: `clio config --deploy-db-server-name my-local-postgres --deploy-site-port 40018 --deploy-deployment iis` makes the right-click deploy target local DB+Redis with zero further args. Validated: full Unit tier 5475 passed (both net8.0/net10.0); Command+Core 2416 passed after 3-lens pre-PR review fixes (leading-digit/ASCII sanitization, explicit --show, site-port range validation, shared deployment-method constants).

## 2026-07-11 20:45 – clio-ring: deploy auto-defaults + uninstall-creatio on main ring (SHIPPED)
Context: Kirill's 4 asks — explain slow AOT, source deploy db/redis from clio config or Rancher, pre-select port, add uninstall-creatio per env on the ring; keep C:\Tools clean (one final at C:\Tools\clio-ring).
Decision: Deploy wizard auto-runs discovery on Window.Opened (InitializeAsync) — db/redis already come from clio config (show-passing-infrastructure→ISettingsRepository), so pre-filling on open satisfies "from config" + "port pre-selected"; both overridable. uninstall-creatio = default actions.json ClioCommand on the MAIN radial ring (shells `clio uninstall-creatio -e {env}` via IClioAdapter), hardened per codex review: RequireTypedConfirm (exact env name, no bypass) + resolved endpoint + ConsequenceText red line + destructive-only listed-env guard.
Discovery: main ring executes actions via child-process shelling (IClioAdapter), NOT the experimental IPC window; show-passing-infrastructure reads clio's own infra config; uninstall-creatio has no interactive CLI prompt (safe to shell). AOT publish must be invoked as `cmd /c <abs path>` from PowerShell — `cmd //c relative.cmd` under Git-Bash silently no-ops. C:\Tools cleaned to a single clio-ring; staging moved OUTSIDE to C:\Projects\clio-ring-run\aot-stage.
Files (clio-ring repo C:\Projects\clio-ring-spike-claude, branch spike/ring-clio-ipc): ClioLauncher/ViewModels/DeployWizardViewModel.cs, Views/DeployWizardWindow.axaml(.cs), ViewModels/RingViewModel.cs, Views/RingView.axaml, Views/RingIcons.cs, Models/ActionCatalog.cs, ClioLauncher.Desktop/actions.json+schema, new ClioLauncher.Tests/. Commits 595ebdd, aa8348e, 45e6551. Final AOT → C:\Tools\clio-ring (SHA-256 A9B3CE00…E5D263).
Impact: no clio source touched. Ring now ships a per-env destructive uninstall with strong irreversible-op guards + a pre-populated deploy wizard. Pattern for future ring builds: JIT-iterate, AOT-final via aot-final.cmd, atomic promote in place, never spawn extra C:\Tools dirs.

## 2026-07-11 21:10 – clio-ring: deploy diagnosis + structured receipt + atomic promote
Context: Kirill ran a real deploy (dry-run off) via the ring wizard and asked where the new Creatio instance was.
Decision: Diagnose inspection-only (no re-deploy, per codex). Build a structured redacted deploy receipt so future runs are diagnosable; fix promotion to be atomic.
Discovery: NO instance was created (no new IIS site, no new instance dir after the run — proven). The wizard's output was UI-only/unpersisted, so the exact blocker was unrecoverable — "preflight blocked" is a hypothesis, not fact (don't overstate). The main-ring/deploy output had bare `Output +=` outcome writes that bypassed AppendLine (missed by any line-mirror log) — routed all through AppendLine. clio uninstall/deploy shell via IClioAdapter; deploy runs deploy-creatio via clio-run.
Files (C:\Projects\clio-ring-spike-claude, branch spike/ring-clio-ipc): ClioLauncher/Diagnostics/DeployReceipt.cs (new, replaced DeployLog.cs), Startup.cs (DI), ViewModels/DeployWizardViewModel.cs (IDeployReceipt inject + Complete-in-finally + OpenDeployLog), Views/DeployWizardWindow.axaml (Open log button), ClioLauncher.Tests/DeployReceiptTests.cs + DeployWizardViewModelTests.cs (4 outcome receipts). Commits 2ce458a (interim), c30db39 (structured). Final AOT SHA FB610394…3B40.
Also: promote-ring.cmd (C:\Projects\clio-ring-run) — atomic rename-into-place promotion (verified sibling tree outside C:\Tools → same-volume rename swap + rollback). AOT publish invoked via PowerShell `cmd /c <abs path>` (Git-Bash `cmd //c relative.cmd` silently no-ops).
Impact: deploy runs now leave a redacted receipt (%LOCALAPPDATA%\clio-ring\deploy-<ts>.log) with clio-invoked flag + outcome + derived URL; releases can't leave a partial install; C:\Tools stays one clio-ring. Standing rule: agents never initiate deploys — Kirill triggers, I read the receipt.
## 2026-07-12 10:25 – clio-ring out-of-order manifest fix + fresh JIT
Context: Live MCP probing proved progress callbacks can enter the Ring concurrently and out of sequence (seq=2 before seq=1/0), causing max-sequence de-dup to discard the seq=0 manifest and leave the pipeline empty.
Decision: Made ClioStageEventAdapter.Consume thread-safe and replaced monotonic max-sequence filtering with exact (runId, sequence) de-dup; distinct late events, especially the manifest, are forwarded. Kept the fix scoped to the Ring and did not initiate a deployment.
Discovery: The prior contract assumption that a single MCP session dispatches notifications sequentially was false. Transport callback arrival order cannot be used as event identity or as proof that lower sequences are stale.
Files: C:/Projects/clio-ring-spike-claude/ClioLauncher.Ipc/ClioStageEventAdapter.cs, C:/Projects/clio-ring-spike-claude/ClioLauncher.Ipc/ClioStageEvent.cs, C:/Projects/clio-ring-spike-claude/ClioLauncher.Tests/ClioStageEventAdapterTests.cs, C:/Tools/clio-ring-jit
Impact: Commit 19b21c9; 82/82 Ring tests pass. Fresh framework-dependent JIT targets C:/Projects/clio/clio/bin/Debug/net10.0/clio.dll and includes the honest-failure envelope plus manifest preservation.

## 2026-07-12 11:03 – Ring install happy-path: discovery redaction + callback ordering fixed
Context: The Ring selected a real 10.1.268 build but clio reported the ZIP missing; the UI pipeline also remained empty/running despite the command finishing.
Decision: Built reusable `--install-probe` and `--pipeline-order-probe` harnesses. Fixed clio-run failure classification so a successful payload's ordinary `message`/`detail` does not trigger redaction. Replaced Ring immediate progress forwarding with contiguous sequence buffering from seq=0.
Discovery: The path was not accidentally trimmed or JSON-mangled. Successful `list-creatio-builds` returned `message="Found 36 builds"`; clio-run treated any `message` as failure and redacted the real full-path to the literal `[redacted-path]` (15 chars), which the Ring then faithfully sent. After the classifier fix the same record was 109 chars, existed, and fingerprinted B33020CC0A49EF8D. The real harness install created DB/site/app pool `semse`; concurrent callbacks still required ordered buffering for terminal UI state.

Context: Clio and ClioRing release independently, but Ring depends on clio MCP tools, nested clio-run commands, typed progress, receipts, and error envelopes.

## 2026-07-12 18:00 – TeamCity typed-progress race fixed
Context: PR 851's external TeamCity MCP E2E build failed only `DeployCreatio_Should_Stream_Typed_Stage_Events_Via_Progress_Meta_When_Archive_Is_Invalid` on net8.0 while focused local runs passed.
Decision: Made the E2E session wait up to five seconds for the terminal typed progress event instead of snapshotting the raw notification queue immediately after the tool response.

## 2026-07-12 20:45 – TeamCity progress E2E preflight made deterministic
Context: PR #851 TeamCity build 15718038 still captured zero typed deploy progress events after a 30-second wait while focused local runs passed.
Decision: Traced the real net8 MCP child with the dnSpy debugger and made the corrupt-archive request supply an explicit unused database-server name so it always crosses the environment-dependent installer preflight before failing at unzip.
Discovery: The progress token reached `clio-run`, the retargeted `deploy-creatio` request, and `StageEventProgressForwarder.Subscribe` locally. ## 2026-07-12 21:05 – TeamCity deploy-progress fixture isolated from IIS root
Context: TeamCity build 15718099 still emitted zero events after the explicit database-server argument crossed the Kubernetes preflight.
Decision: Gave `DeployUninstallProgressTests` a fixture-scoped `CLIO_HOME` with a writable temporary `iis-clio-root-path`, leaving the production MCP contract unchanged.
Discovery: Windows `auto` deployment selects IIS and creates the configured IIS root before `StageEventEmitter.Begin`; a clean CI identity can fail at the default `C:\inetpub\wwwroot\clio` before any progress event exists. The exact TeamCity SDK 9.0.315 plus net8.0 runtime 8.0.28 harness passes with the isolated root and two NUnit workers.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/DeployUninstallProgressTests.cs
Impact: The corrupt-archive E2E now controls both pre-manifest environmental dependencies (database-server selection and IIS-root writability), so it deterministically reaches unzip without touching a real deployment target.

## 2026-07-12 21:20 – PR #851 Blocker/High review via Visualizer room (Codex pairing)
Context: Codex requested a pre-merge review of PR #851 (ring-typed-deploy-events, head 600e4870 + c0dc35ae) in Visualizer room codex-e2e-test-debugging-clio-mcp-e2e; also debugged the TC zero-events E2E failure live.

(3) 5 High findings: unwrapped post-Begin throws kill terminal-event guarantee (CreatioInstallerService.cs:1471/1475/1563/1566); name-only IIS match became a destructive uninstall target (CreatioUninstaller.cs:276-287); Ring drawer displays unmasked creds via show-web-app-list default action; ClioIpcClient resolves the child by dotnet.exe name-diff and can tree-kill unrelated processes; 750ms teardown force-kill has no in-flight-run guard.

## 2026-07-13 10:40 – Concurrent settings mutation safety
Context: Concurrent deploy-creatio commands, deploy/uninstall overlap, and manual edits could be lost when a long-running command saved its startup settings snapshot.
Decision: Serialize settings mutations with a sibling cross-process lock, reload immediately before each mutation, and replace a securely prepared file only after exact-content validation under a deny-write handle.
Discovery: SettingsRepository loaded settings in its constructor and every mutation recreated appsettings.json from that stale model; deploy registration and uninstall removal both reached this path.
Files: clio/Environment/ConfigurationOptions.cs, clio/Environment/SettingsBootstrapService.cs, clio.tests/Command/SettingsRepositoryConcurrencyTests.cs
Impact: Cooperating clio processes no longer overwrite one another, completed manual edits are retained, malformed settings fail closed, and real multi-process coverage protects the behavior.

Decision (findings): (1) negative token-validation now enforced in CI, not just configured -- 3 real-TestServer reject tests (foreign-key RS256 sig, HS256 alg-confusion forged from RSA public modulus, wrong iss) each isolating one control -> 401. (2) IsTruthy fail-closed [TestCase] matrix (yes/2/0/false/blank/null->false; true/1 case+ws-tolerant->true) -- the single parser behind 3 security-downgrade flags. (3) CVE-2025-55315: no version bump (pin is a build-time floor under FrameworkReference); documented patched-runtime control (.NET8>=8.0.21 / .NET10>=10.0.9) in Directory.Packages.props + docs/commands/mcp-http.md. Coarse-authz Major already documented in ADR OQ-G + Out-of-scope (per-tenant claim filed as platform follow-up) -> replied, no code change. Sonar S125 (comment false-positive) cleared by rewording.
Files: clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{McpHttpAuthenticationPipelineTests,AuthConfigurationTests,CredentialPassthroughMiddlewareTests}.cs; clio/Command/McpServer/McpHttpServerCommand.cs; Directory.Packages.props; clio/docs/commands/mcp-http.md; spec/sprint-status.yaml + .codex/workspace-diary.md (merge unions). Commits 7d231528 (merge+compile-fix) + 7452042a (review fixes).
Impact: PR #846 MERGEABLE/CLEAN, Sonar 0 issues + gate passed, CI 4/4 green, all 3 review threads resolved, re-requested tetiana-moshon. McpServer unit 2153 passed/0 failed both TFMs. reviewDecision stays CHANGES_REQUESTED until her re-review.

## 2026-07-13 11:42 – Contributor issue-first PR policy
Context: Contributor guidance needed a mandatory, searchable GitHub issue trail for every pull request.
Decision: Require an issue before each PR, classify it with an enabled issue type and relevant labels, link it with a closing keyword when appropriate, and self-assign the PR; contributors without metadata permissions must request maintainer help before review.
Discovery: The organization enables `Task`, `Bug`, and `Feature` issue types; the repository has domain and change-kind labels available for filtering.
Files: CONTRIBUTING.md
Impact: Contributors now have a concise issue-to-PR workflow with clear ownership and searchable classification.

## 2026-07-13 12:06 – Draft-first pull request workflow
Context: Repository auto-merge can complete a ready pull request as soon as protection checks pass, while project review may happen outside GitHub.
Decision: Require every pull request to start as a draft and prohibit enabling auto-merge until implementation, validation, documentation, and external review are complete and the pull request is marked ready.
Discovery: Draft status provides an explicit author-controlled readiness boundary without adding a GitHub approval requirement or privileged pull-request automation.
Files: CONTRIBUTING.md
Impact: Human and agent contributions now have a clear handoff from work-in-progress to protected auto-merge eligibility.

## 2026-07-13 11:26 – Explorer deploy site-name inference regression traced
Context: Right-clicking a Creatio ZIP in Windows Explorer derives the deployment site name from the archive, producing oversized or incompatible database and IIS identifiers for long build filenames.
Decision: Proposed removing only the unconditional ZIP-name derivation from DeployCreatioDefaultsResolver; preserve explicit --site-name and configured deploy-site-name precedence, then let the existing interactive installer prompt when neither is present.
Discovery: PR #845 introduced DeriveSiteNameFromZipWhenUnset after the Explorer registry verb had already passed only --zip-file; this fills SiteName before CreatioInstallerService can execute its existing prompt.
Files: clio/reg/clio_context_menu_win.reg, clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/DeployCreatioDefaultsResolver.cs, clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/CreatioInstallerService.cs, clio.tests/Command/DeployCreatioDefaultsResolverTests.cs, clio/docs/commands/config.md, clio/help/en/config.txt
Impact: The future fix can restore per-deployment site-name entry for Explorer without changing the registry command or MCP wire contract.

## 2026-07-13 12:06 – Explorer deploy prompts for site name
Context: GitHub issue #855 requested an explicit site-name prompt when deploying a ZIP from the Windows Explorer context menu.
Decision: Removed ZIP-filename site-name inference, retained explicit and configured defaults, and made silent deployments without a site name fail fast instead of waiting for input.
Discovery: The existing Explorer registry verb and interactive installer prompt already provide the desired flow; only the defaults resolver prevented the prompt. The MCP tool and ClioRing always provide a site name, so their contracts remain unchanged.
Files: clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/DeployCreatioDefaultsResolver.cs, clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/InstallerCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/DeployCreatioDefaultsResolverTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/InstallerCommandSilentSiteNameTests.cs, clio/docs/commands/deploy-creatio.md, clio/help/en/deploy-creatio.txt
Impact: Explorer deployments now request a safe explicit site name, while automation receives a clear configuration error and existing explicit/configured precedence remains compatible.

## 2026-07-14 02:25 – ENG-93348 BMAD Phase 1 runtime-routing PRD
Context: Credential passthrough from PR #830 always inherited `IsNetCore=false`, so AI Platform requests could not address .NET Core/NET8 Creatio routes.
Decision: Defined required JSON boolean `isNetCore` on the passthrough header and fail-closed HTTP 400 behavior when missing/invalid; required the runtime to participate in the canonical container/lock identity. Auto-detection is out of scope.
Discovery: `CredentialHeaderParser`, `CredentialContext`, `BuildEphemeralSettings`, and `BuildPassthroughCacheKey` all omit runtime today. The existing registration detector is not bearer-aware and is unnecessary when the unreleased gateway contract can supply the authoritative value.
Files: spec/prd/prd-mcp-http-passthrough-runtime-routing.md, clio/Command/McpServer/CredentialHeaderParser.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/CredentialContext.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ToolCommandResolver.cs
Impact: Phase 1 is ready for facilitator approval before ADR design; no production code changed.

## 2026-07-14 03:05 – ENG-93348 BMAD Phase 2 runtime-routing ADR
Context: Phase 1 approved a required `isNetCore` boolean for credential passthrough, with HTTP 400 on missing/invalid values and no detection/default.
Decision: Preserve the raw JSON token until validation, carry a compile-required boolean through parse/context records, set ephemeral `EnvironmentSettings.IsNetCore`, and add runtime to the one canonical passthrough key already shared by cache, lock, and in-flight guards.
Discovery: `ServiceUrlBuilder` and all environment-bound client constructors already honor `IsNetCore`; `SessionContainerCache`, `TenantExecutionLockProvider`, and `McpToolExecutionLock` need no API or production changes. ClioRing consumes stdio `mcp-server`, not the HTTP credential header.
Files: spec/adr/adr-mcp-http-passthrough-runtime-routing.md, spec/prd/prd-mcp-http-passthrough-runtime-routing.md, clio/Command/McpServer/CredentialHeaderParser.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/CredentialContext.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/McpHttpServerCommand.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ToolCommandResolver.cs
Impact: Phase 2 is ready for facilitator approval before story decomposition; no production code changed.

## 2026-07-14 03:35 – ENG-93348 BMAD Phase 3 implementation stories
Context: The approved runtime-routing PRD and ADR needed buildable, test-carrying work items before QA planning.
Decision: Split implementation into four serialized stories: fail-closed header propagation, runtime-bound settings/canonical identity, dual-runtime process/live E2E proof, and documentation/compatibility closure. Each story owns relevant validation; there is no production-only slice.
Discovery: The live .NET Core bearer stand remains an operational prerequisite, while ClioRing, MCP tool arguments, cache APIs, and ServiceUrlBuilder require review but no expected contract redesign.
Files: spec/stories/story-mcp-http-passthrough-runtime-routing-{1,2,3,4}.md, spec/sprint-status.yaml, spec/prd/prd-mcp-http-passthrough-runtime-routing.md, spec/adr/adr-mcp-http-passthrough-runtime-routing.md

## 2026-07-14 04:05 – ENG-93348 BMAD Phase 4 QA test plan
Context: Runtime-routing stories required an executable quality gate spanning parser validation, canonical identity, real HTTP routing, and two live Creatio runtime families.
Decision: Defined unit, real-process, and manual MCP E2E layers with full FR/AC traceability. A sanitized route capture proves root versus exactly one `/0/`, while distinct live Core bearer and Framework runs remain non-waivable exit criteria.
Discovery: Existing MCP HTTP E2E is manual/not in CI and its encoders/stand configuration omit runtime today; strict per-tenant runtime variables and compile-required encoder booleans close that gap. A successful tool response alone is not route-layout evidence.

## 2026-07-14 05:10 – ENG-93348 runtime-routing implementation
Context: Implemented all four serialized stories for explicit runtime routing in MCP HTTP credential passthrough.

## 2026-07-13 12:30 – ENG-93320 Binary/blob (logo) sys-setting upload via clio
Context: bpms-tools had added sys-setting CRU to clio but ENG-88957 (PR #615, Artem Kulykov) deliberately dropped Binary, assuming PostSysSettingsValues is scalar-only. A working browser fetch proved the platform accepts a Binary value as a Base64 string, so the block was wrong for the write path.
Decision: Support Binary as WRITE-ONLY. Added a Binary branch in SysSettingsManager.UpdateSysSetting that validates Base64 and sends it verbatim in sysSettingsValues[code]. clio reads a file and Base64-encodes it locally (CLI: pass a file path when Type=Binary; MCP: new value-file-path arg on update-sys-setting), keeping the blob out of the tool-call arguments. Re-added Binary to SupportedValueTypeNames. Read-back left unsupported (no scalar BinaryValue column — media-link) so list/get still omit Binary.
Discovery: TryUpdateSysSetting(UpdateSysSettingArgs) never consulted SupportedValueTypeNames, so update was blocked only by the manager else-branch. ClioRing does not consume the sys-settings tools (grep clio-ring clean) — MCP contract change is not Ring-consumed.
Files: clio/Common/ISysSettingsManager.cs, clio/Command/SysSettingsCommand.cs, clio/Command/SysSettingsModels.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/SysSettingsTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ToolContractGetTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/SysSettingPrompt.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/SysSettingsGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/GuidanceCatalog.cs, clio/help/en/set-syssetting.txt, clio/docs/commands/set-syssetting.md, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/SysSettingsToolTests.cs, clio.tests/Common/SysSettingsManagerNewBehaviorTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ToolContractGetToolTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/SysSettingsToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: net8 unit tests (Module=McpServer|Module=Common) 2596 passed / 0 failed / 3 skipped. Verified live: `clio set-syssetting LogoImage <file> Binary` set the logo on 3 envs (localhost dev, ts1-studio-mssql/MSSQL, ts1-sems-pg/PostgreSQL) — DB-backend agnostic. ClioRing compatibility reviewed, no Ring-consumed contract changed.

## 2026-07-13 13:30 – ENG-93320 review follow-ups (discovery + guards)
Context: code review raised two items beyond the AC — (1) `value-file-path` read any local file unbounded; (2) Binary settings were invisible to `list-sys-settings`, blocking the downstream Branding agent (ENG-92981) from discovering logo/background codes.
Decision: (F1) Add a 10 MB sanity cap + audit log in SysSettingsCommand.EncodeFileToBase64 (checked via IFileSystem.GetFileSize before read); NO path-locking and NO config — clio is a personal local tool and its other file-path tools (`body-file`) are unrestricted, so a hard root would be inconsistent and break "logo lives anywhere". (F2 = Option B) list-sys-settings now INCLUDES Binary with value shown as `<binary>`; added `includeBinary` flag (default false) to GetAllSysSettingsWithValues so the manifest/download path stays Binary-free and its tests are untouched. Minor: CLI now reports a clear "file not found" for a path-like Binary value with no file (Base64 never contains '.', '\\' or ':'); added CLI unit tests.
Files: clio/Command/SysSettingsCommand.cs, clio/Common/ISysSettingsManager.cs, plus wording realign in SysSettingsTool.cs / ToolContractGetTool.cs / SysSettingPrompt.cs / SysSettingsGuidanceResource.cs; tests in SysSettingsToolTests.cs, SysSettingsManagerNewBehaviorTests.cs.
Impact: net8 unit tests (Module=McpServer|Module=Common) 2598 passed / 0 failed / 3 skipped.

## 2026-07-13 13:14 – Ring deploy password-reset warning hidden inside restore stage
Context: A Ring deployment completed successfully but Creatio still required the Supervisor password change.
Decision: Traced the deployed run receipt to its per-operation database log and compared the Ring/MCP plan with the installer helper.
Discovery: Ring has no password-reset input or stage; MCP hard-codes `DisableResetPassword = true`, and the helper runs inside `restore-db`. Run `10ab095c-431c-41b4-add1-82c3b93a0c0f` failed to update `SysAdminUnit` with PostgreSQL error 42501 (permission denied), but the helper intentionally logged a warning and continued, so restore and the overall deployment were reported successful.

## 2026-07-13 13:52 – Preserve password-change state for Ring and Explorer deploys
Context: Issue #861 requested `DisableResetPassword = false` for ClioRing and Windows Explorer ZIP deployments after a Ring run silently failed to clear `SysAdminUnit.ForceChangePassword`.
Decision: Made false the deploy-creatio CLI default and the explicit MCP/Ring adapter value; Explorer's existing `--ZipFile` command inherits the CLI default. Updated MCP tool, prompt, lazy contract, deploy guidance, CLI help, and detailed docs to state that clio preserves the restored database value rather than promising to enable it.
Discovery: `DisableResetPassword = false` skips the SQL that writes false; it does not write true. The pre-PR quality/security/correctness review caught this distinction and also found the legacy compatibility fixture's `UnitTests` category excluded it from the mandatory `Category=Unit` gate, so the fixture now uses `Unit`.

Impact: Ring and Explorer no longer clear the restored build's password-change state by default; Command/MCP suites pass 4196/4211 with 15 skips on net8 and net10, MCP E2E passes 2/2 per framework, Ring passes 95/95, and Windows x64 NativeAOT publish succeeds.

## 2026-07-13 18:30 – PR #866 file policy and Binary readback review
Context: User proposed enforcing Creatio file-extension policy for Binary sys-setting uploads and adding symmetric Binary readback to the sys-settings surface.
Decision: Treat environment file-policy enforcement and operation-specific errors as an actionable correctness/security review finding; request an explicit product decision on Binary download because ENG-93320 only requires setting a logo, but the established sys-settings surface is otherwise bidirectional.
Discovery: Creatio documents FileSecurityMode, FileExtensionsAllowList, FileExtensionsDenyList, and AllowFilesWithUnknownType as applying to integrations and web services. PR #866 sends Base64 through PostSysSettingsValues without checking them. Binary settings retain bytes but no source filename, and saving readback locally would make the MCP operation filesystem-mutating rather than read-only.
Files: clio/Command/SysSettingsCommand.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/SysSettingsTool.cs, clio/Command/SysSettingsModels.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/SysSettingsGuidanceResource.cs
Impact: The upload path should apply the target environment's active policy and return a specific extension error; Binary readback is best exposed through an explicit destination path or a dedicated non-destructive download tool with byte-for-byte E2E coverage.
