## 2026-07-15 07:49 – uninstall-creatio leaves IIS AppPool profiles by design
Context: Investigated why removed environments `calc`, `d1`, and `lcap-local` left directories under `C:\Users`.
Decision: Diagnosis only; no code or system state changed.
Discovery: `CreatioUninstaller` detects `C:\Users\<site-name>` before IIS deletion but intentionally emits `delete-apppool-profile` as skipped/not-supported and never deletes it. All three folders are registered unloaded `Win32_UserProfile` entries with IIS virtual-account SIDs (`S-1-5-82-*`), so correct cleanup must remove the Windows profile registration as well as files; plain recursive directory deletion would be incomplete. The Ring guided-deploy PRD explicitly scoped real profile deletion out.
Files: clio/Common/CreatioUninstaller.cs, clio/docs/commands/uninstall-creatio.md, spec/ring-guided-deploy/prd/prd-ring-guided-deploy.md, clio.tests/Common/CreatioUninstallerTestFixture.cs
Impact: The residual folders are confirmed expected current behavior, not an intermittent uninstall failure; a future fix should use profile-aware Windows cleanup after stopping/deleting IIS and before unregistering the environment.
## 2026-07-15 21:59 – IIS HTTPS deployment refinement
Context: Refined support for deploying local Creatio instances to IIS over HTTPS without implementing code.
Decision: Reuse `--use-https` as the exclusive protocol selector, resolve an installed LocalMachine certificate by the derived FQDN with an optional thumbprint override, create exactly one IIS binding, and apply idempotent .NET Framework-only Web.config transformations before site creation.
Discovery: IIS deployment currently logs an HTTPS URL but the scenario handler, environment registration, and browser launch hardcode HTTP; MCP and ClioRing cannot request HTTPS. The host has two matching valid certificates (2026-11-05 and 2030-12-22 expiry), and the working bank binding uses the latter. The live `deloitte-nf` site has both HTTP and HTTPS bindings; its root Web.config uses HTTPS ServiceModel sources while `Terrasoft.WebApp/Web.config` still has `wsService encrypted="false"`.
Files: clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/InstallerCommand.cs, clio/Common/DeploymentStrategies/IISDeploymentStrategy.cs, clio/Common/ScenarioHandlers/CreateIISSite.cs, clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/CreatioInstallerService.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/InstallerCommandTool.cs, clio-ring/ClioRing.Ipc/DeployPlan.cs
Impact: Implementation must cover certificate preflight/selection, exclusive IIS binding creation, NetFramework config mutation, correct registered/launched URI, and additive MCP/Ring contract updates; no source code was changed during refinement.

## 2026-07-15 22:01 – IIS HTTPS refinement correction
Context: User clarified why the live `deloitte-nf` site currently exposes both protocols.
Decision: Treat the dual binding as temporary manual setup, not existing product behavior; retain the requirement that clio creates exactly one HTTP or HTTPS binding.
Discovery: The HTTP binding remains only because the user has not removed it after manually adding HTTPS.
Files: .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Acceptance criteria should validate the binding created by clio, without inferring a migration or cleanup requirement from the current `deloitte-nf` site.

## 2026-07-15 22:14 – IIS HTTPS fallback and certificate pinning refinement
Context: User required HTTPS deployment to remain non-failing without an installed certificate and proposed a persistent `pin-certificate` selection command.
Decision: Treat HTTPS as opportunistic: use a usable matching certificate when available, otherwise warn and deploy consistently over HTTP. Store an optional normalized thumbprint in root clio settings; `pin-certificate --thumbprint` validates and persists it, while interactive invocation without a thumbprint lists usable LocalMachine certificates and prompts for selection. A stale/invalid pin never blocks deployment; it falls back to deterministic usable-certificate selection or HTTP.
Discovery: The generated appsettings schema comes from `clio/tpl/jsonschema/schema.json.tpl`, but `SaveSchema` currently skips an existing `%LOCALAPPDATA%/Creatio/clio/schema.json`; template-only changes would leave current users stale. The implementation should refresh the generated schema atomically when template content changes. No new TeamCity E2E may require a Creatio ZIP or mutate a local IIS deployment.
Files: clio/Environment/ConfigurationOptions.cs, clio/Environment/ISettingsRepository.cs, clio/Environment/SettingsBootstrapService.cs, clio/tpl/jsonschema/schema.json.tpl, clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/InstallerCommand.cs, clio/Common/DeploymentStrategies/IISDeploymentStrategy.cs, clio/Common/ScenarioHandlers/CreateIISSite.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/InstallerCommandTool.cs, clio/Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt
Impact: The feature should be covered by unit/contract tests with certificate-store, IIS, filesystem, and settings abstractions; real ZIP/IIS proof is a manual local Windows validation, not a TeamCity E2E gate.

## 2026-07-13 18:10 – ENG-93320 PR #866 review round (Krylov CHANGES_REQUESTED)
Context: Kyryl Krylov requested changes on PR #866: (1) validate target type before uploading a file; (2) honor Creatio file-security policy; (3) size cap bypassable via inline Base64 + read race; (4) MCP update prompt not aligned with the value-file-path contract; plus a round-trip test gap and a readback-design preference (file-based download tool).
Decision: Fixed 1, 3, 4 in this PR; deferred 2 (file-security policy) and the download-sys-setting-file readback tool to a linked follow-up (ENG-93320 only requires SET). (1) EnsureExistingSettingIsBinary resolves the existing setting via GetAllUsersDefaultWithType and rejects a file upload to a non-Binary/missing setting, on both MCP and CLI paths, before the file is read. (3) Moved the cap to SysSettingsManager.MaxBinaryValueBytes and enforce the DECODED byte length in the manager Binary branch (covers inline Base64 too); EncodeFileToBase64 keeps a pre-read GetFileSize fast-fail and adds a post-read length recheck to close the grow-after-sizing race. (4) update-sys-setting prompt: value now optional, added value-file-path param, body says "exactly one of value/value-file-path" and notes the target must be Binary.
Discovery: byte fidelity file->wire is already unit-proven (captured Base64 == Convert.ToBase64String(fileBytes)); a full server round-trip cannot go through MCP because Binary readback is empty by design — deferred to the download-tool follow-up. Sonar quality gate passed (0 new issues); TeamCity Binary E2E passed; the 2 failing E2E are unrelated flat-tool-discovery tests.
Files: clio/Common/ISysSettingsManager.cs, clio/Command/SysSettingsCommand.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/SysSettingPrompt.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/SysSettingsToolTests.cs, clio.tests/Common/SysSettingsManagerNewBehaviorTests.cs
Impact: net8 unit (Module=McpServer|Module=Common) 2601 passed / 0 failed / 3 skipped (+3 tests: non-Binary reject, missing-setting reject, oversized-inline reject).

## 2026-07-14 12:57 – ENG-93302 requirements: apply theme to current user's profile
Context: ENG-93302 ("Branding agent. New theme is saved to user's profile") had almost no requirements; reconstructed actors and contract before implementation.
Decision: Recommend a standalone `set-user-theme` CLI command + paired MCP tool; the "automatic after create-theme" behavior lives in ThemingGuidanceResource, not inside create-theme. Spec written to spec/prd/spec-set-user-theme.md.
Discovery: Per-user theme is written via DataService UpdateQuery on the VIRTUAL entity `SysUserProfile`, column `Theme` = the theme's cssClassName (not GUID), filter Id = current user's SysAdminUnit Id (a SelectQuery on SysUserProfile returns exactly one row — the current user — regardless of filters). Server persists it as SysProfileData["Theme"]={"ThemeId":"<cssClassName>"} via SysUserProfileEntityEventListener; Shell fallback order is profile Theme → DefaultTheme SysSetting → built-in. Write gates: feature `ChangeTheme` (silent no-op when off!), license op `CanCustomizeBranding`, system op `CanChangeOwnTheme` (granted to All employees by default). Verified in Creatio 10 source at C:\Projects\Autodeploy\creatio10 (Pkg CrtBase/CrtNUI/CrtUIv2).
Files: spec/prd/spec-set-user-theme.md, spec/adr/adr-theming.md (D-D6 to amend), clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ThemingGuidanceResource.cs
Impact: Full server contract documented; implementation can start without re-deriving the SysUserProfile mechanics; open questions (AI Toolkit scope, Krylov confirmation) captured in the spec.

## 2026-07-14 12:20 – ENG-93320 PR #866 review round 2 (Krylov, all 5 addressed)
Context: Second CHANGES_REQUESTED with 5 inline comments (3 High, 2 Medium): (1) size check not an allocation boundary (grow/replace race); (2) enforce Creatio file-security policy on upload (deferring download-readback to #873 is fine, upload-policy is not); (3) allocates from attacker-controlled encoded input before the decoded cap; (4) update prompt interpolates neither value nor value-file-path; (5) prove byte fidelity now via legacy read + add `because` on two E2E asserts.
Decision: Implemented all five in-PR.
- (1) EncodeFileToBase64 now reads from a single IFileSystem.OpenReadStream handle in 80KB chunks into a MemoryStream, aborting the moment content exceeds MaxBinaryValueBytes — no reliance on GetFileSize, bounded allocation. Added OpenReadStream(string)->Stream to IFileSystem for a mock-friendly seam.
- (2) Verified in core: file-security is sys-settings-driven (FileSecurityMode lookup + FileExtensionsAllow/DenyList + AllowFilesWithUnknownType) and FileUploadSecurityFilter only runs on multipart uploads with a filename — the sys-setting write path is NOT gated. Implemented an ADVISORY client-side guard: SysSettingsManager.GetFileSecurityPolicy() reads the 4 settings (mode GUIDs are platform constants), and SysSettingsCommand.EnforceFileSecurityPolicy applies extension allow/deny + unknown-type to the value-file-path; inline Binary is refused while a policy is active (no extension to validate).
- (3) ValidateBinaryBase64 rejects on the encoded-length upper bound BEFORE allocating, then validates format + exact decoded size; distinguishes Malformed vs TooLarge.
- (4) update-sys-setting prompt rebuilt as a block body: echoes exactly the supplied value/value-file-path, validates exactly-one, and softens the get-sys-setting verify note for Binary (returns empty).
- (5) E2E now reads the stored value back via legacy get-syssetting (cliogate), decodes, and asserts equality with the source bytes; added the missing `because` on the two asserts.
Discovery: after merging master the resident tools/list payload exceeded the 34,816-byte budget by 40 bytes due to my expanded descriptions — trimmed the three sys-settings tool descriptions (kept value-file-path/<binary>/logo).
Files: clio/Common/ISysSettingsManager.cs, clio/Common/IFileSystem.cs, clio/Common/FileSystem.cs, clio/Command/SysSettingsCommand.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/SysSettingPrompt.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/SysSettingsGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/SysSettingsTool.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/SysSettingsToolTests.cs, clio.tests/Common/SysSettingsManagerNewBehaviorTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/SysSettingsToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: net8 unit (Module=McpServer|Module=Common) 2660 passed / 0 failed / 3 skipped. Follow-up #873 now scoped to the download-sys-setting-file readback tool only (file-security upload enforcement moved into this PR).

## 2026-07-11 – cliogate UnlockPackages/LockPackages null-payload crash under Developer Mode (#848)
Context: With Developer Mode on, the post-install "unlock maintainer packages" step crashed. clio sends `{"unlockPackages": null}` (null = "unlock all by maintainer", the gate's else branch), but the gate logged `string.Join(", ", unlockPackages)` BEFORE the null guard → ArgumentNullException → HTTP 500 HTML page → clio failed with the misleading `System.Text.Json.JsonException: '<' is an invalid start of a value`. Blocked install-application, push-workspace --use-application-installer, set-dev-mode.
Decision: null-coalesce both `string.Join` log lines (UnlockPackages + LockPackages) with `?? Array.Empty<string>()`, and route the nullable `SysPackage.Description` handling through two pure internal helpers `BuildUnlockDescription` / `SplitLockDescription` (each null-guarded) so the latent NRE on null Description is fixed AND unit-testable. clio-side `PackageUnlocker.CallGate` now guards a null/empty gate response and wraps a non-JSON body in a clear InvalidOperationException (echo sanitized via `TextUtilities.SanitizeForDisplay`, matching the CreatioServiceClient precedent) instead of the raw JsonException. Versions NOT touched (AssemblyInfo 1.1.1.2 vs descriptor 2.0.0.44 desync is a separate item).
Discovery: cliogate.tests CAN build/run here (CreatioSDK/CreatioMock restored). BUT UnlockPackages/LockPackages read the HttpContext-backed `BaseService.UserConnection` PROPERTY (NRE in the harness) while testable code uses the `_userConnection` FIELD from `ClassFactory.Get<UserConnection>()` — so full-method Description tests can't run in-harness; hence the pure-helper extraction + `[assembly: InternalsVisibleTo("cliogate.tests")]`. The null-input full-method tests still guard the ArgumentNullException (thrown at the join BEFORE the UserConnection access). Pre-PR 3-lens review found one High (null gate response bypassed `catch (JsonException)` as a raw ArgumentNullException) — fixed with an IsNullOrEmpty guard + null/empty regression tests.
Files: cliogate/Files/cs/CreatioApiGateway.cs, cliogate/Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs (InternalsVisibleTo only), cliogate.tests/CreatioApiGatewayTestFixture.cs, clio/Package/PackageUnlocker.cs, clio.tests/Package/PackageLockManagerTests.cs (new), regenerated clio/cliogate/cliogate.gz + cliogate_netcore.gz via build.ps1.
MCP/docs: reviewed, no update required — no command options/verb/handler-contract/help/MCP-argument change; observable behavior is only "no longer crashes".
Impact: unlock/lock steps null-safe and Description-null-safe; confusing JSON `'<'` error replaced by an actionable message. Validated: cliogate.tests 25 passed (net472); clio.tests Category=Unit&Module=Package 85 passed (net8.0+net10.0); clio Release 0 errors. Branch fix/848 merged with origin/master (only the diary conflicted, resolved as union). NOTE: the gate fix ships once the .gz are rebuilt (done) and redeployed (push-pkg).

## 2026-07-14 12:34 – Explorer deploy honors sole local infrastructure
Context: Issue #874 reproduced an Explorer ZIP deployment selecting unavailable Rancher Desktop Kubernetes while one enabled local PostgreSQL server and a default local Redis server were configured; the error terminal then closed immediately.
Decision: Preserve explicit and deploy-specific precedence, select the sole enabled local database only when unambiguous, retain Kubernetes fallback for zero or multiple local choices, and conditionally pause the Explorer deploy terminal after non-zero exit.
Discovery: The live deployment log stopped at the Kubernetes restore branch because an empty DbServerName was treated as Kubernetes; defaultRedis was already resolved correctly after local database mode is selected.
Files: clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/DeployCreatioDefaultsResolver.cs, clio/reg/clio_context_menu_win.reg, clio.tests/Command/DeployCreatioDefaultsResolverTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/ExplorerContextMenuRegistrationTests.cs, spec/explorer-deploy-local-defaults/
Impact: Explorer deployment uses the user's sole enabled local PostgreSQL and configured Redis preference without changing explicit MCP or ClioRing infrastructure selection, and failures remain readable.

## 2026-07-14 13:15 – Scope Explorer defaults and harden context-menu execution
Context: The first comprehensive review of issue #874 found that a general sole-local fallback could redirect non-Explorer Kubernetes callers and that quoting the entire cmd.exe payload was unsafe for metacharacter-bearing paths.
Decision: Gate sole-local inference behind a hidden --explorer-launch marker, preserve ordinary CLI/MCP/ClioRing Kubernetes semantics, invoke clio directly from Explorer, and let the command prompt only after a failed Explorer deployment.
Discovery: Quoting a filename is insufficient protection when cmd.exe expands percent variables; removing the shell prevents legal metacharacter-bearing ZIP filenames from becoming executable syntax.
Files: clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/InstallerCommand.cs, clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/DeployCreatioDefaultsResolver.cs, clio/reg/clio_context_menu_win.reg, clio.tests/Command/ExplorerContextMenuRegistrationTests.cs
Impact: Rancher Desktop can remain off for Explorer deployment when one local server is configured, while automation callers retain their established Kubernetes contract and Explorer failures remain visible exactly once.

## 2026-07-14 13:38 – Keep every Explorer deployment failure visible
Context: Final review found that moving failure acknowledgement into clio still left early validation returns and exceptions able to close the Explorer terminal.
Decision: Track Explorer success across the whole command, render operation-log and exception details first, and acknowledge every unsuccessful non-silent Explorer exit from the outer finally path.
Discovery: Failure visibility must span defaults resolution, infrastructure validation, installer return codes, and thrown exceptions; handling only the installer result is incomplete.
Files: clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/InstallerCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/InstallerCommandSilentSiteNameTests.cs
Impact: Explorer success closes immediately, while all tested failure paths display the diagnostic and log location before waiting for Enter.

## 2026-07-16 – ENG-93089 second-round PR-review fixes on create-app-section contention guard
Context: 4 PR-review findings on the section-create serialization guard (FIX A/B/C major, FIX D low).
Decision: (A) Guard now wraps ONLY each destructive TryCommitAttempt insert; the read-only settle-verify, the 2s backoff, and the retry orchestration run OUTSIDE the guard, so the held span no longer inflates past the 120s wait cap and starves concurrent same-app callers. (B) On the RETRY insert only, a terminal ServerError is re-verified by generated id (TryVerifySectionExistsWithSettle) before surfacing — if visible, swallow and continue to readback (insert#1 committed but answered detail-less); rethrow otherwise. Only ServerError is gated, never transport/timeout. (C) Retry policy is now an explicit `bool enableContentionRetry = false` param on IApplicationSectionCreateService.CreateSection + impl (after the two timeout overrides), decoupled from insertTimeoutMsOverride.HasValue; MCP tool passes true, CLI leaves default false. (D) Guard XML remarks reworded: cross-process CLI-vs-CLI gets reclassify+verify but not the retry (MCP-only).
Discovery: EndSpinner nulls its CTS, so a second EndSpinner is a no-op — FIX B's EndSpinner(true) after TryCommitAttempt's EndSpinner(false) does not double-render. NSubstitute optional-param stubs bind the omitted 5th arg to literal false, so setup/Received calls that flow through the MCP true-path needed Arg.Any<bool>()/true added.
Files: clio/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateCommand.cs, clio/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateSerializationGuard.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ApplicationTool.cs, clio.tests/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateServiceTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/ApplicationToolTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/CaptionCultureArgMappingToolTests.cs
Impact: enableContentionRetry is an internal service arg (not an MCP tool argument), so the MCP/Ring contract is unchanged; observable MCP retry-once behavior preserved. Targeted tests: 144 + 7 pass on net10.0.
## 2026-07-14 14:40 – ENG-93320 PR #866 review round 3 (Krylov: 2 blockers + 2 corrections)
Context: Round-2 re-review confirmed 4 items resolved; two policy blockers + two corrections remained.
Decision:
- (Blocker 1) Enforced the file-security gate at EVERY Binary write entry point, not just MCP update: added the inline-Binary-under-active-policy refusal to the CLI inline path and to create-sys-setting's initial value (checked before insert). Overload RejectInlineBinaryUnderActivePolicy(code, targetIsBinary) reused across paths.
- (Blocker 2) GetFileSecurityPolicy now fails CLOSED: reads the authoritative All-Users value, accepts Disabled ONLY for the known Disabled GUID, and resolves missing/malformed/unknown ids to a new FileSecurityMode.Unknown -> UnknownPolicy; Enforce/Reject helpers refuse the upload ("Cannot determine the environment file-security mode..."). Malformed AllowFilesWithUnknownType is treated conservatively (false); missing uses the platform default (true).
- (Correction 3) Propagate the specific Binary validation cause to the MCP result: new ISysSettingsManager.TryValidateBinaryValue(value, out error); TryUpdateSysSetting resolves the target type once and, for an inline Binary value, returns the specific malformed/too-large error instead of the generic update-failure message.
- (Correction 4) E2E temp file renamed .bin -> .png (bin is in Creatio's default DenyList; the bytes are already a PNG signature) so the happy path passes on a default environment.
Tests: added manager GetFileSecurityPolicy resolution tests (3 known ids + missing + malformed + unknown), command tests (CLI inline reject, create-with-value reject, Unknown-mode file refusal, specific-cause propagation). net8 unit (Module=McpServer|Module=Common) 2670 passed / 0 failed / 3 skipped.
Live: CLI happy/inline-reject/deny-ext verified on localhost (dev) + ts1-web03 (both DenyList). MCP resident tools verified over mcp-http: list-sys-settings shows LogoImage as <binary>, get-sys-setting returns empty for Binary.

## 2026-07-14 14:08 – Make MCP progress waits timeout-safe
Context: TeamCity build 15725495 failed unrelated PR #866 because the corrupt-archive deploy progress test received a stale partial snapshot after its terminal-event wait timed out.
Decision: Replace polling with notification-driven wakeups, recheck one final snapshot at the timeout boundary, and throw a secret-safe diagnostic timeout rather than returning unsatisfied data.
Discovery: The old cancellation path broke out of its delay without refreshing the queue, so a boundary-arriving terminal event could be present while the caller still received the previous stage-only snapshot.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpServerSession.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/DeployUninstallProgressTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/AGENTS.md, spec/mcp-progress-wait-timeout/
Impact: Shared MCP E2E runs no longer convert a timeout race into misleading event-order failures, while genuine missing terminal events fail explicitly with safe diagnostics.

## 2026-07-14 15:02 – Isolate and order MCP progress assertions
Context: The first comprehensive review of issue #876 found that the fixture-wide capture queue could leak terminal events across invocations, accumulate wakeups, and format an unbounded diagnostic history; repeated validation then reproduced concurrent callback ordering as a separate flake.
Decision: Scope every wait to an explicit progress token, coalesce notification wakeups, cap diagnostics to the latest 20 matching notifications, and replay typed events by their protocol sequence before asserting order.
Discovery: MCP notification handlers can complete out of arrival order even within one request, so queue insertion order is not the protocol order; the versioned sequence field is authoritative and matches ClioRing's ordered-buffering contract.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpServerSession.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/DeployUninstallProgressTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/AGENTS.md, spec/mcp-progress-wait-timeout/
Impact: Ten fresh-process fixture runs passed on each of net8.0 and net10.0 without installing or uninstalling Creatio, while unrelated streams can no longer satisfy a wait.

## 2026-07-14 15:18 – Keep opaque progress tokens out of diagnostics
Context: Final security review found that timeout text echoed the caller-supplied progress token even though opaque correlation values may contain sensitive identifiers and failures are written to CI logs.
Decision: Use the token only for in-memory stream filtering, omit it from exception text, and cover the rule with a secret-shaped unrelated token.
Discovery: Secret-safe typed-event summaries do not require exposing the correlation key used to select the stream.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpServerSession.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/DeployUninstallProgressTests.cs, spec/mcp-progress-wait-timeout/mcp-progress-wait-timeout-qa.md
Impact: Timeout diagnostics retain event, sequence, stage, status, and outcome evidence without leaking opaque caller input.

## 2026-07-14 15:36 – Require complete progress streams and broadcast wakeups
Context: Final quality and testing reviews found that a terminal callback could arrive before lower sequences and that one shared consuming semaphore could wake the wrong token waiter.
Decision: Broadcast each notification through a versioned task-completion signal, compare typed progress-token values, and require a distinct contiguous sequence from zero through terminal before completing a wait.
Discovery: Sorting a partial snapshot cannot recover callbacks that have not arrived; terminal presence proves completeness only when every preceding sequence is already captured. This supersedes the earlier coalescing-semaphore decision.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpServerSession.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/DeployUninstallProgressTests.cs, spec/mcp-progress-wait-timeout/
Impact: Deterministic terminal-first and typed-token regressions pass, and the five-test fixture passed ten fresh-process runs on each of net8.0 and net10.0 without real Creatio lifecycle operations.

## 2026-07-14 13:40 – ENG-93302 story 1: set-user-theme CLI command implemented
Context: Implemented the first (clio-hosted) part of ENG-93302 on branch feature/ENG-93302-set-user-theme (off updated master, 95 commits ahead of the old blob branch).
Decision: Followed the theming RemoteCommand family style (RemoteCommand<TOptions> + public virtual TrySetUserTheme) rather than a separate service interface, so the story-2 MCP tool reuses it exactly like CreateThemeTool reuses CreateThemeCommand. Reused SelectQueryHelper for Select/read-back envelopes and ListThemesCommand.TryGetAvailableThemes for theme resolution (id→cssClassName→caption + built-in 'default'/'dark' aliases).
Discovery: BaseCommandTests<TOptions> enforces a README gate (ReadmeChecker) requiring 4 doc artifacts per command (Commands.md index entry `(docs/commands/<name>.md)`, help/en/<name>.txt, docs/commands/<name>.md, WikiAnchors line `<name>:`). A command unit-test fixture fails until all 4 exist — so docs cannot be fully deferred to a later docs story. Created all 4 for set-user-theme now.
Discovery: DataService UpdateQuery to clear the theme uses empty-string Theme value; Id filter uses TextDataValueType(1) matching the captured profile-page request. SelectQuery on virtual SysUserProfile with empty filters returns the current user's single row.
Files: clio/Command/Theming/SetUserThemeCommand.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/Program.cs, clio/help/en/set-user-theme.txt, clio/docs/commands/set-user-theme.md, clio/Commands.md, clio/Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt, clio.tests/Command/SetUserThemeCommandTests.cs

## 2026-07-14 14:30 – ENG-93302 story 1: live-tested, found + fixed Theme-Id bug
Context: User asked to test the CLI against a real localhost Creatio (Supervisor/Supervisor) and whether the theme applies immediately. Tested via CLI + browser MCP.
Discovery (BUG, now fixed): SysUserProfile.Theme stores the theme **Id** (e.g. 'custom-theme-1', 'abc'), NOT the cssClassName. The Freedom UI Shell calls themeRegistryService.getThemeById(<profile value>) and maps id->cssClassName for the body class. My command originally wrote the cssClassName ('crt-custom-theme-1'/'my-theme') — the server stored it faithfully (so read-back "passed") but getThemeById returned nothing and the Shell silently fell back to freedom-theme. Proven live: writing id 'custom-theme-1' -> body class 'crt-custom-theme-1' (applied); writing cssClassName 'my-theme' or 'dark-mode' -> 'freedom-theme' (fallback). This corrects the earlier source-only note in the explore report.
Fix: SetUserThemeCommand now writes match.Id (resolution still accepts id/cssClassName/caption). Removed the built-in 'default'/'dark' alias map — built-ins use opaque client GUIDs not available via list-themes, and writing their cssClassNames does NOT work; --reset (empty value) covers "back to default". AppliedUserTheme record gained an Id field; output/message/docs updated.
Discovery (immediacy): theme is NOT applied to an already-open page — the Shell reads it only at load. Confirmed: profile change left an open tab unchanged until reload. "Refresh the page to see it" message is correct. The two extra profile-page calls (RefreshCurrentUser, ResetWorkplaceCache) remain irrelevant to theme (FR-7).
Files: clio/Command/Theming/SetUserThemeCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/SetUserThemeCommandTests.cs (12 tests), clio/help/en/set-user-theme.txt, clio/docs/commands/set-user-theme.md, spec/prd/spec-set-user-theme.md (§3 corrected)
Impact: Command verified working end-to-end (custom-theme-1 renders live after refresh). Full unit suite green both frameworks (5889/0). Story 2 (MCP tool) will wrap the corrected TrySetUserTheme.

## 2026-07-16 – ENG-93302 story 2/3/4: MCP tool + guidance + review fixes
Context: Continuing ENG-93302 after story 1. Ran a Creatio code review of the working tree (informed by PR #866 reviewer insights: validate-before-side-effect, step-specific errors, contract/guidance alignment, test fidelity), then implemented the MCP surface and closed the maintenance-policy items.

Full `Category=Unit` must run in CI where network tests behave (BindingsModule.cs touched -> full-suite trigger).

## 2026-07-16 – ENG-93302: pre-PR adversarial review + fixes + live verification
Context: Before opening the PR, ran 3 independent review agents (correctness, clarity/conventions, requirement-alignment) over the diff against the PR #866 flaw taxonomy (validate-before-side-effect, step-specific errors, contract/guidance alignment, test-proves-persistence, bounded-IO). (read-back verification passed live) -> --reset -> "Cleared" -> delete-theme by id. Test gates: unit Category=Unit net8.0 5496/0/23 and net10.0 5496/0/23 (each triggers a blame hang-dump on one unrelated connection-retry test that idles >timeout; 0 real failures). McpServer module 1982/0. Touched fixtures 42/0. clio.mcp.e2e set-user-theme+create-theme 6/0 on both frameworks. Build 0 errors.

## 2026-07-14 - ENG-93208 PR #830: systemic fix for the WorkspaceSyncTool CwdLock Major (tetiana-moshon)
Context: PR #830's last remaining blocker was a `CHANGES_REQUESTED` Major from tetiana-moshon on `WorkspaceCommandToolBase.ExecuteInWorkspace`: it pinned process-wide cwd (`SetCurrentDirectory`) around the ENTIRE network-bound `push-workspace`/`restore-workspace` install/restore under the single global `McpToolExecutionLock.CwdLock` — the same lock `PageSyncTool.WriteVerifiedBodyFile` takes to anchor page output to cwd, so any tenant's push/restore head-of-line-blocked every OTHER tenant's page-sync for the whole network call. A narrow lock-scoping fix was rejected up front: the workspace/package machinery reads process cwd transitively in multiple places (`WorkingDirectoriesProvider.CurrentDirectory`, `PackageArchiver`/`PackageDownloader`/`BasePackageInstaller`/`CreatioPackage` cwd fallbacks), so shrinking the lock without removing the cwd dependency would reintroduce the exact cross-tenant race the lock existed to prevent.
Decision: Two Explore-agent audits (dispatched before touching code) found (1) the ENTIRE push/restore call graph (`Workspace.Install`/`Restore` → `WorkspaceInstaller`/`WorkspaceRestorer` → `PackageArchiver.Pack`/`ZipPackages`, `BasePackageInstaller`, `PackageBuilder`, `ComposableApplicationManager`) resolves every path through `IWorkspacePathBuilder.PackagesFolderPath`/etc — EVERY raw `Environment.CurrentDirectory`/`GetCurrentDirectoryIfEmpty` site in that graph is a dead branch already guarded by a non-empty explicit argument; the only real seam is `IWorkspacePathBuilder.RootPath` (already settable — `Workspace.PublishToFile`/`PublishToFolder` already use it); (2) `IWorkspacePathBuilder` is registered `AddSingleton` in `BindingsModule.cs`, but `BindingsModule.Register` builds a BRAND-NEW `ServiceProvider` per call, cached PER-TENANT by `SessionContainerCache` (keyed off credentials) — so "Singleton" means per-tenant-container, not process-global; two different tenants already get two different `IWorkspacePathBuilder` instances. This made a PLAIN mutable field (no `AsyncLocal`) provably safe — advisor flagged this exact fork before implementation ("storage and lock structure are coupled": AsyncLocal needs no lock, a plain field on a per-tenant singleton needs the existing per-tenant lock to stay wrapped, NOT dropped). Implementation: `WorkspaceCommandToolBase.ExecuteInWorkspace` now resolves `IWorkspacePathBuilder` via a newly-`private protected` `BaseTool.ResolveFromCallContainer<TService>` (was `private`) — the SAME per-tenant container `execute()`'s `InternalExecute<TCommand>` resolves the command from — sets `.RootPath = workspacePath` for the duration of `execute()` inside the KEPT `ExecuteUnderTenantLock` wrapper (guards the SAME tenant's own concurrent calls), and resets it to `null` in `finally`. The `SetCurrentDirectory`/`CwdLock` pin is gone entirely for this tool; zero changes to `Workspace.cs`/`WorkspaceInstaller.cs`/`WorkspaceRestorer.cs`/`PushWorkspaceCommand.cs`/`RestoreWorkspaceCommand.cs`/`BindingsModule.cs` — the fix is confined to `Command/McpServer/Tools/{BaseTool,WorkspaceSyncTool,McpToolExecutionLock}.cs`.
Discovery: the extra `ResolveFromCallContainer<IWorkspacePathBuilder>(options)` call happens BEFORE `execute()`'s own `ResolveCommand<TCommand>` gate, so an unknown/unreachable environment now throws `EnvironmentResolutionException` a step earlier than before — without a matching catch this would have regressed `WorkspaceSyncContractToolE2ETests.PushWorkspace_Should_Report_Invalid_Environment`/`RestoreWorkspace_Should_Report_Invalid_Environment` (expect graceful exit code 1, not an unhandled exception). Added a `catch (EnvironmentResolutionException) → FromResolverError` / `catch (Exception) → FromException` mirroring `InternalExecute<TCommand>`'s own resolve-catch exactly, plus a new unit test reproducing it (`PushWorkspace_Should_Report_Unresolvable_Environment_Gracefully`). `ResolveFromCallContainer<TService>(options)` (single-arg, no gates) does NOT invoke `EnforcePackageRequirements`/`EnforceCreatioVersionRequirements` — those live only in `ResolveCommand<TCommand>` — so no double-gate risk.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/{BaseTool,WorkspaceSyncTool,McpToolExecutionLock}.cs; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/WorkspaceSyncToolTests.cs (rewrote the two "executes from requested workspace" tests to assert `IWorkspacePathBuilder.RootPath` instead of `Directory.GetCurrentDirectory()`, since asserting cwd literally tested the OLD pin mechanism; added a deterministic CwdLock-non-blocking test using a background-thread lock holder + Stopwatch, and the unresolvable-environment regression test).
Verify: full Unit suite green both net8.0 and net10.0 (6213/0/39 each) after the exception-handling fix; McpServer module alone 2340/0/1 on net8.0. Existing `WorkspaceSyncToolE2ETests`/`WorkspaceSyncContractToolE2ETests` (push/restore against a real sandbox + invalid-environment contract) already exercise the changed root-resolution path end-to-end — no new e2e test added; TeamCity MCP e2e run recommended before merge. ClioRing reviewed: grepped `clio-ring/ClioRing.Ipc`, `clio-ring/ClioRing`, `clio-ring/ClioRing.Desktop/actions.json` for `push-workspace`/`restore-workspace`/`PushWorkspace`/`RestoreWorkspace`/`WorkspaceSyncTool` — zero matches, Ring does not consume this tool surface. MCP contract (tool names, args, destructive flags, error message text) unchanged — prompts/resources/docs need no update.
Impact: push-workspace/restore-workspace no longer contend for `McpToolExecutionLock.CwdLock` at all, closing the cross-tenant head-of-line-blocking Major from tetiana-moshon without weakening isolation — resolves the last CHANGES_REQUESTED item on PR #830.

## 2026-07-14 - ENG-93208 PR #830: b-horodyskyi agent-assisted review (FR-11 redaction gap + stale precedence docs)
Context: A follow-up agent-assisted review (b-horodyskyi) on PR #830 flagged one Major security finding, one Major (explicitly non-blocking) performance finding, and one Minor doc-drift finding.
Decision: (1) `BaseTool.ExecuteResolved`'s two catch blocks handed `ex.Message`/`$"{ex.Message} [{ex.ErrorCode}]"` straight to the tool's `onFailure` factory unredacted — every other MCP error path (`CommandExecutionResult.FromException`, `McpToolErrorFilter`, the per-tool `SensitiveErrorTextRedactor.Redact(ex.Message)` pattern used by ~40 other tools) redacts before crossing the MCP boundary. Wrapped both catches in `SensitiveErrorTextRedactor.Redact(...)`, unconditionally (matching the majority per-tool pattern, not gated on passthrough — `Redact` is documented safe to call on already-clean text). The three consumers of `ExecuteResolved` (CheckThemingAccessTool, CreateThemeTool, ListThemesTool) inherit the fix automatically; additionally, CreateThemeTool/ListThemesTool each had a SEPARATE unredacted leak — `TryCreateTheme`/`TryGetAvailableThemes`'s own `errorMessage` out-param (server-supplied `ThemeServiceResponse.ErrorInfo?.Message`) bypasses `ExecuteResolved`'s catch entirely since it's a normal `bool` return, not a thrown exception — redacted both at the call site. (2) The performance finding (per-request container build running two full-assembly reflection scans on the passthrough hot path, `ToolCommandResolver.cs`/`BindingsModule.cs`) is explicitly non-blocking per the reviewer's own words ("not a merge blocker for incubation... worth fixing before graduation") — deferred as a tracked follow-up rather than a hasty architecture change to the DI composition root; left the thread open (not resolved) pending the reviewer's own call, matching the established CwdLock-deferral precedent on this PR. (3) Fixed the stale auth-precedence doc/label drift the reviewer called out: `CredentialHeaderParser`'s XML docs (interface + class) said `accessToken → cookie → login+password`, contradicting the actual resolution order `accessToken → login+password → cookie` (confirmed correct at the parser's own inline comment, line 119, and by `CredentialHeaderParserTests`); `CredentialContext.cs`'s `CredentialKind` enum doc and the `FromCookie`/`FromLoginPassword` factory doc comments had Cookie labeled implicitly-middle and LoginPassword labeled "(lowest precedence)" — swapped to Cookie "(lowest precedence)" / LoginPassword "(middle precedence)". Doc-only, no behavior change; no dedicated inline review comment existed for this one (mentioned only in the review summary text), so no thread to reply/resolve for it — folded into the same commit as the two inline findings.
Discovery: `ExecuteResolved<TCommand,TResponse>`'s single-arg `ResolveFromCallContainer<TService>` overload (used by the earlier CwdLock fix in this same PR) does NOT run `EnforcePackageRequirements`/`EnforceCreatioVersionRequirements` — those live only in the two-arg `ResolveCommand<TCommand>` gate — so the redaction fix here needed no equivalent double-gate consideration.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/{BaseTool,CreateThemeTool,ListThemesTool}.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/{CredentialHeaderParser,CredentialContext}.cs; clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{CheckThemingAccessToolTests,CreateThemeToolTests,ListThemesToolTests}.cs (new redaction regression tests: URI + credential-shaped text in both the exception path and the command errorMessage out-param path, asserting the sensitive substrings are stripped).
Verify: targeted McpServer module 2343/0/1 (net8.0) before the doc fix; full Unit suite rerun both net8.0/net10.0 after all three fixes landed.
Impact: closes the FR-11-style redaction gap on the one typed-response path (`ExecuteResolved`) that had bypassed the PR's own secret-hygiene sweep, and corrects security-relevant precedence documentation that could mislead a future gateway integrator. The reflection-scan performance finding remains open as an accepted, reviewer-acknowledged non-blocking follow-up.

## 2026-07-15 10:30 – Clean IIS application-pool profiles during uninstall
Context: Issue #881 required uninstall-creatio to remove the actual IIS virtual-account profile when possible and complete successfully with one warning when Windows keeps it locked.
Decision: Capture and validate the app-pool SID/ProfileList registration before IIS deletion, call DeleteProfileW after IIS/files cleanup with bounded retries, and carry warning plus success-with-warnings through CLI, MCP, and ClioRing.
Discovery: DeleteProfileW may remove ProfileList registration while leaving locked files behind, so native success and residual-directory state both matter; destructive fallback cleanup must reject reparse roots at every recursion boundary.
Files: clio/Common/AppPoolProfileCleaner.cs, clio/Common/CreatioUninstaller.cs, clio/Requests/IISScannerRequest.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Progress/, clio-ring/ClioRing/, clio.mcp.e2e/UninstallCreatioWarningE2ETests.cs, spec/apppool-profile-cleanup/
Impact: Uninstall now removes registered IIS profiles best-effort, preserves exit 0 and IsError=false on cleanup warnings, renders a yellow completed-with-warnings Ring state, refreshes environments, and has live locked-profile proof against Creatio 10.0.0.802.

## 2026-07-15 09:16 – Classify get-info target failures safely
Context: Issue #390 required get-info and its aliases to distinguish invalid URLs, unreachable targets, authentication failures, non-Creatio responses, and malformed Creatio responses without leaking response bodies or parser details.
Decision: Make ApplicationInfoService the mandatory base probe, map failures to stable user-facing classifications, and run system-info and ClioGate enrichment only after a valid base report and as non-fatal best-effort steps.
Discovery: Creatio.Client can surface transport failures through nested exceptions and returns non-success response bodies directly, so classification must inspect exception chains while keeping debug output limited to safe metadata.
Files: clio/Command/GetCreatioInfoCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/GetInfoCommand.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/, clio.mcp.e2e/GetCreatioInfoToolE2ETests.cs, clio/docs/commands/get-info.md, spec/get-info-target-classification/
Impact: CLI aliases and MCP now share stable secret-safe errors, valid Creatio instances still report base information without ClioGate, and Ring compatibility remains covered by its consumer tests and NativeAOT publish gate.

## 2026-07-15 09:31 – Close get-info review edge cases
Context: The comprehensive pre-PR review tested response classification against ambiguous plain text and minimally shaped JSON.
Decision: Treat only object/array-prefixed parse failures as malformed JSON, require a non-empty coreVersion in the base report, and filter remote-failure catches to known transport, authentication, timeout, and JSON exception families.
Discovery: JSON permits scalar top-level values, but ApplicationInfoService does not; using generic JSON scalar prefixes caused text such as `404 Not Found` to be misclassified.
Files: clio/Command/GetCreatioInfoCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/GetInfoCommand.cs, clio/help/en/get-info.txt, spec/sprint-status.yaml
Impact: Digit-prefixed non-Creatio responses and empty sysValues envelopes now fail with the correct stable classification, while programming defects are no longer swallowed by remote-operation boundaries.

## 2026-07-15 09:44 – Make secret-safe boundaries recoverable by policy
Context: Final re-review traced real ClioGate compatibility failures through package-list parsing and found recoverable exception types beyond the transport whitelist.
Decision: Catch every recoverable exception at base and optional remote-operation boundaries while explicitly propagating fatal runtime conditions and clear programming defects; validate optional success tokens by type before conversion.
Discovery: ClioGate compatibility can throw InvalidOperationException or System.Text.Json parsing errors before GetSysInfo, so a narrow HTTP/Newtonsoft exception list cannot guarantee nonfatal enrichment or secret-safe MCP output.
Files: clio/Command/GetCreatioInfoCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/GetInfoCommand.cs
Impact: Recoverable client/library failures now remain classified and redacted, while fatal or programming-defect exceptions still propagate for diagnosis.

## 2026-07-15 11:01 – Preserve shared IIS pools during uninstall
Context: Codex Review found that issue #881 could delete an application pool and Windows profile still used by another IIS application.
Decision: Validate target topology before deletion, remove only the target site/application, requery assignments, and delete the pool/profile only after verified pool removal.
Discovery: Root-site deletion can also remove nested sibling applications, and incomplete appcmd XML must be treated as unsafe rather than as proof of exclusive ownership.
Files: clio/Requests/IISScannerRequest.cs, clio/Common/CreatioUninstaller.cs, clio.tests/Requests/IisScannerHandlerValidationTests.cs, clio.tests/Common/CreatioUninstallerTestFixture.cs, spec/apppool-profile-cleanup/
Impact: Shared pools and profiles survive uninstall, destructive IIS decisions fail closed, and profile cleanup cannot run while a pool remains.

## 2026-07-15 11:25 – Bind IIS cleanup to the resolved target identity
Context: Final review of the shared-pool fix exposed malformed-output, same-name replacement, silent-delete, and concurrent reassignment edges around AppCmd.
Decision: Require strict AppCmd XML, bind stop/delete to name plus normalized path plus pool, verify target absence, and bracket pool removal with fresh assignment and absence snapshots.
Discovery: AppCmd cannot stop a nested application, and its process wrapper does not expose exit status; safe orchestration therefore uses a nested no-op and proves every destructive result from fresh IIS state.
Files: clio/Requests/IISScannerRequest.cs, clio/Common/CreatioUninstaller.cs, clio.tests/Requests/IisScannerHandlerValidationTests.cs, clio.tests/Common/CreatioUninstallerTestFixture.cs
Impact: Same-name replacements and sibling applications are not mutated, error-shaped output fails closed, and profile cleanup remains gated on verified pool absence.

## 2026-07-15 11:58 – Integrate dbHub with local Creatio lifecycle
Context: Issue #882 requested Windows-first dbHub installation plus safe automatic source synchronization for local deploy and uninstall.
Decision: Pin dbHub 0.23.0, bind its unauthenticated HTTP server to loopback, reconcile only marked clio-owned TOML blocks through locked atomic writes, retain a lazy in-memory control source for dbHub's non-empty-source requirement, and treat lifecycle verification failures as typed non-fatal warnings.
Discovery: dbHub 0.23.0 rejects PostgreSQL `prefer`/`allow` TLS tokens and zero-source configurations; deploy previously printed database and Redis connection strings, so lifecycle validation also required credential-free connection output.
Files: clio/Common/DbHub/, clio/Command/InstallDbHubCommand.cs, clio/Command/SyncDbHubCommand.cs, clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/CreatioInstallerService.cs, clio/Common/CreatioUninstaller.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/, clio-ring/, spec/dbhub-integration/
Impact: Local environments hot-add/remove dbHub sources after successful lifecycle stages, manual TOML content and shared IIS resources remain protected, offline dbHub does not fail the primary operation, and both net8.0/net10.0 destructive local E2E paths prove complete cleanup without secret output.

## 2026-07-15 13:23 – Harden dbHub adoption and lifecycle verification
Context: Comprehensive review of issue #882 found collision, no-op restart, TOML parsing, local disclosure, permission, and process-ownership gaps before PR delivery.
Decision: Require explicit read-only SQL tools and safe ACLs for adopted TOML, detect dbHub-normalized source collisions, preserve healthy compatible installs as true no-ops, lex TOML strings before marker recognition, and prove scheduled-task identity plus listener ownership around repair.
Discovery: dbHub normalizes every non-alphanumeric source character to underscore and exposes an unsuffixed `execute_sql` tool for a single source; verification must derive the live tool name from the complete inventory rather than assume a suffix.
Files: clio/Common/DbHub/, clio.tests/Common/DbHub/, clio.mcp.e2e/DbHubLifecycleWarningE2ETests.cs, clio/docs/commands/install-dbhub.md, spec/dbhub-integration/
Impact: Manual configuration is adopted only under an explicit least-privilege contract, lifecycle sync cannot steal a normalized ID, healthy installs remain uninterrupted, and real net8.0/net10.0 warning-path deployment proves bounded cleanup.

## 2026-07-15 14:12 – Keep archive lifecycle proof developer-local
Context: The Creatio archive-backed dbHub lifecycle proof cannot run in the TeamCity environment and opened the disposable site in the workstation browser.
Decision: Mark the fixture NUnit Explicit and LocalOnly, require destructive sandbox opt-in, and hard-skip whenever TEAMCITY_VERSION is present; CI retains only non-destructive MCP contract coverage.
Discovery: MCP deploy-creatio intentionally auto-runs the deployed application, so archive lifecycle validation is disruptive even when all IIS, database, settings, and TOML state is isolated.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/DbHubLifecycleWarningE2ETests.cs, spec/dbhub-integration/dbhub-integration-test-plan.md
Impact: TeamCity cannot install Creatio through this fixture, while a developer can still run the verified net8.0/net10.0 proof manually when explicitly requested.

## 2026-07-15 14:21 – Resolve issue 882 automated review findings
Context: The PR Codex review identified generic PostgreSQL keys, omitted SQL TLS settings, and custom dbHub tool names as compatibility gaps.
Decision: Fall back to Npgsql when the generic connection string is not valid SQL Server syntax, map omitted SQL TLS options to required encryption, and refuse adopted TOML whose custom tool already owns the generated MCP name.
Discovery: Microsoft.Data.SqlClient defaults cannot distinguish explicit options through ContainsKey; ShouldSerialize identifies whether TLS fields were actually supplied.
Files: clio/Common/DbHub/DbHubConnectionSourceFactory.cs, clio/Common/DbHub/DbHubTomlStore.cs, clio.tests/Common/DbHub/
Impact: clio-generated local connections synchronize without weakening transport security, and live verification cannot succeed against a pre-existing colliding tool.

## 2026-07-15 23:23 – Support opportunistic HTTPS for local IIS deployment
Context: Issue #887 requested corporate machine-certificate HTTPS for local Creatio while preserving non-failing deployment on machines without a usable certificate.
Decision: Resolve eligible LocalMachine/My certificates by FQDN with optional thumbprint pinning and deterministic expiration ordering, create one actual HTTP or HTTPS binding, transform the three required .NET Framework settings only for actual HTTPS, and propagate the actual scheme through registration, receipts, browser launch, MCP, and ClioRing.
Discovery: Updating CertificateHash on an already-created Microsoft.Web.Administration binding did not populate HTTP.sys; replacing the provisional HTTPS binding through BindingCollection.Add(bindingInformation, hash, store, sslFlags) produced the working SSL registration. A SAN extension with no DNS entries must not fall back to CN, and schema refresh must remain best-effort because schema.json is derived editor metadata.
Files: clio/Common/IIS/, clio/Common/DeploymentStrategies/IISDeploymentStrategy.cs, clio/Common/ScenarioHandlers/CreateIISSite.cs, clio/Command/PinCertificateCommand.cs, clio/Environment/ConfigurationOptions.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/, clio-ring/, spec/iis-https-deployment/
Impact: .NET Framework and .NET 8 local IIS deployments can use the corporate certificate end-to-end, missing candidates fall back to HTTP with an accurate URL, settings/schema/contracts stay aligned, and developer-local ZIP validation remains outside TeamCity.

## 2026-07-15 23:56 – Close IIS HTTPS delivery review findings
Context: Sonar and GitHub review of issue #887 found three maintainability findings plus two edge-case regressions before merge.
Decision: Extract IIS HTTPS DI registration, evaluate both XML transformations explicitly, keep dotnet-hosted deployments on their actual HTTP Kestrel URL, and reject malformed explicit thumbprints before certificate-store lookup.
Discovery: `--use-https` is an IIS binding option; allowing it to affect DotNetDeploymentStrategy registration points readiness at an endpoint that the strategy deliberately removes from appsettings. Normalization can turn an all-nonhex explicit thumbprint into an empty value, which must not share interactive-cancellation semantics.
Files: clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/Common/IIS/NetFrameworkHttpsConfigurator.cs, clio/Common/DeploymentStrategies/DotNetDeploymentStrategy.cs, clio/Command/PinCertificateCommand.cs, clio.tests/Common/DotNetDeploymentStrategyTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/PinCertificateCommandTests.cs
Impact: All reported review findings are covered by focused net8.0/net10.0 tests without broadening HTTPS beyond IIS.

## 2026-07-16 06:20 – Add a ClioRing app-settings schema and dev-clio guidance
Context: Issue #890 requested editor validation for Ring settings and an explanation of how a release-labeled Ring can launch a development clio IPC build.
Decision: Ship a Draft 2020-12 schema beside the desktop executable, reference it from the sample settings, document launch precedence and trust boundaries, and validate schema/model parity with JsonSchema.Net integration tests.
Discovery: `Channel` is only a Ring display label; clio selection is valid `DevClioPath`, then complete `ClioIpc`, then the machine default. Existing local settings can be preserved while refreshing the NativeAOT program files.
Files: clio-ring/ClioRing.Desktop/app-settings.schema.json, clio-ring/ClioRing.Tests/AppSettingsSchemaTests.cs, clio-ring/README.md, spec/ring-app-settings-schema/
Impact: Editors now catch unknown, incomplete, and whitespace-only settings, developers can intentionally target a trusted local clio build, and `C:\Tools\clio-ring` has been refreshed without losing its IPC configuration.

## 2026-07-16 06:55 – Stabilize unit tests on the dev self-hosted runner
Context: PR #891 passed on TS1-CORE-DEV24 but two consecutive runs on TS1-MRKT-WEB01 failed in different tests and target frameworks while the Ring-only tests remained green.
Decision: Join deliberately detached heartbeat test work before disposing its synchronization handles, and use a locked StringWriter for process-wide console capture in CommonProgramTest.
Discovery: TS1-MRKT-WEB01 is current on .NET SDK 10.0.301/runtime 10.0.9 but its GitHub runner 2.333.0 trails 2.335.1. The first failure was concurrent StringBuilder access; the retry captured a late cancelled-operation fault in the next test, confirming background-work isolation rather than product behavior.
Files: clio.tests/CommonProgramTest.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpProgressHeartbeatTests.cs
Impact: Core and MCP Server tests no longer dispose gates under detached work or read console buffers during background writes, improving deterministic execution across self-hosted runner speeds.

## 2026-07-16 00:00 – Section-create guard: canonical env key + waiter bound (ENG-93089)
Context: Two PR-review fixes on feature/ENG-93089 for the create-app-section serialization guard.
Decision: (1) Added SectionCreateSerializationGuard.BuildEnvironmentKey(EnvironmentSettings) (trim/lower/strip one trailing '/', empty on null) and used it in BOTH CreateSection overloads so registered-name and settings/MCP-passthrough calls to one server share a gate. (2) Layered a per-key in-flight counter (ConcurrentDictionary<string,int>) with MaxConcurrentWaiters=8 over KeyedSemaphore in the guard: an excess caller degrades to best-effort immediately instead of parking a thread-pool worker on the synchronous Wait.
Discovery: The guard's BuildKey lowercases both parts, so BuildEnvironmentKey normalization is idempotent with it. KeyedSemaphore is shared with ComponentRegistryClient, so the waiter bound had to live in the guard, not KeyedSemaphore. Holder counts toward the in-flight bound, so bound-1 same-key callers park and the rest run best-effort.
Files: clio/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateSerializationGuard.cs, clio/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateCommand.cs, clio.tests/Command/SectionCreateSerializationGuardTests.cs, clio.tests/Command/ApplicationSectionCreateServiceTests.cs
Impact: create-app-section now serializes same-server creates across both overloads and cannot starve the MCP thread pool under a deep same-app fan-out; ComponentRegistryClient unaffected.

## 2026-07-16 16:37 – Clarify MCP resident versus lazy tool routing
Context: The resident versus clio-run classification needed to be explained from the current MCP implementation.
Decision: Treat the compact `get-tool-contract` index `resident` flag as the caller-facing oracle and `McpCoreToolProfile` as the source-code oracle.
Discovery: Residency is selected per tool type from `CoreToolTypes` plus `AlwaysOnLazyToolTypes`; non-resident destructive tools use `clio-run-destructive`, while non-destructive tools use `clio-run`.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpCoreToolProfile.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/McpFeatureToggleFilter.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ToolContractGetTool.cs, docs/McpCapabilityMap.md
Impact: Future MCP callers and maintainers can classify tools without guessing from names or implementation attributes.

## 2026-07-16 16:45 – Correct lazy MCP executor routing detail
Context: Deeper tracing of `ClioRunExecutor` corrected the earlier assumption that destructive metadata selects a separate executor.
Decision: Document `clio-run` as the single preferred long-tail executor for both read-only and destructive targets.
Discovery: `clio-run-destructive` is a deprecated compatibility alias with identical behavior; host confirmation is applied because both executor tools are themselves annotated destructive.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/ClioRunTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/McpToolInvokerRegistry.cs
Impact: Guidance now matches the current executor implementation and avoids routing based on target destructive metadata.

## 2026-07-16 13:55 – Resolve uninstall warning E2E target without EnvironmentPath
Context: TeamCity master build 15735619 failed the issue #881 locked-profile MCP E2E before uninstall because the registered `dev` environment had an empty `EnvironmentPath`.
Decision: Resolve the registered environment URI through the fresh clio process, then fail-closed match its scheme, port, host binding, and application path to exactly one IIS application pool; keep file-reading sandbox helpers path-based.
Discovery: TeamCity exposes `dev` at `http://<agent>:88/<application>` with a wildcard IIS binding and no path metadata. The disposable 10.0.0.802 Studio NET8 PostgreSQL run proved URI resolution, warning propagation, successful uninstall, and complete cleanup.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/ClioEnvironmentCommandResolver.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/IisApplicationPoolResolver.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/UninstallCreatioWarningE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/UninstallWarningIisApplicationPoolResolverE2ETests.cs, spec/uninstall-warning-e2e-uri-resolution/
Impact: The warning E2E no longer depends on unrelated `EnvironmentPath` metadata and aborts safely on unmatched, ambiguous, or pool-less IIS targets.

## 2026-07-16 14:10 – Bind uninstall warning E2E to the local machine
Context: Final security review found that a wildcard IIS binding could accept a stale URI hostname for another machine and still select a local pool.
Decision: Require the registered URI host to identify the current machine by machine name, FQDN, loopback, or DNS/local-interface intersection before IIS matching; reject URI user information and sanitize query/fragment data from diagnostics.
Discovery: The final worktree code passed 8 resolver/security regressions on net8.0 and net10.0, then the repeated 10.0.0.802 locked-profile E2E passed against the local FQDN and cleaned all recorded resources.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/IisApplicationPoolResolver.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/UninstallWarningIisApplicationPoolResolverE2ETests.cs, spec/uninstall-warning-e2e-uri-resolution/
Impact: Wildcard bindings no longer authorize foreign-host targets, and secret-bearing URI components cannot leak through failure messages.
## 2026-07-16 15:20 – Resolve routed TeamCity uninstall-warning sandbox pool
Context: TeamCity build 15736567 showed that the issue #893 URI resolver still assumed the public `DeployedUrl` mirrored local IIS bindings and application paths.
Decision: Read TeamCity's existing `ApplicationPoolName` through its configuration-properties file, then require the explicit pool to match the routed URI target or direct IIS topology and have exactly one live application assignment.
Discovery: TeamCity exposes the sandbox as `http://<agent>:88/<pool>` while the agent-local IIS application can be a root app behind different bindings; public URL inference alone cannot identify the pool safely.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/TeamCityBuildParameterResolver.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/IisApplicationPoolResolver.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/UninstallCreatioWarningE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/UninstallWarningIisApplicationPoolResolverE2ETests.cs, spec/uninstall-warning-e2e-uri-resolution/
Impact: The destructive warning E2E supports TeamCity routing without trusting an unrelated/shared/missing-site pool; 17 resolver tests pass on net8/net10 and the exact locked-profile test passed against a disposable 10.0.0.802 deployment with complete cleanup.

## 2026-07-16 15:08 – Treat shared TeamCity pool as a non-applicable warning scenario
Context: TeamCity build 15736978 resolved the configured sandbox pool but found two live IIS application assignments.
Decision: Keep pool resolution fail-closed and report the locked-profile test ignored for a shared pool because production uninstall intentionally preserves shared pools and skips profile deletion.
Discovery: A disposable 10.0.0.802 site with a synthetic same-site `/0` application reproduced the two-assignment skip; removing only that application let the exact warning E2E pass and uninstall cleanly.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Configuration/IisApplicationPoolResolver.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/UninstallCreatioWarningE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/UninstallWarningIisApplicationPoolResolverE2ETests.cs, spec/uninstall-warning-e2e-uri-resolution/
Impact: TeamCity no longer fails a warning-path test on a topology where the production behavior correctly cannot delete the pool profile, while exclusive disposable pools retain full warning-contract coverage.

## 2026-07-17 – ClioRing compatibility gate for ENG-93187: PASSED
Context: Item 1 of the consolidated review (spec/requests-registry/requests-registry-code-review.md) required the mandatory ClioRing gate for the ToolContractGetTool discovery changes on feature/ENG-93187-requests-registry (tip 1ff55a95).
Decision: Gate passed and attested in the review file. The get-tool-contract {} index change on the default surface (157 -> 147: +get-request-info, -11 leaked names from disabled process-designer and deploy-identity surfaces) is accepted as deliberate Ring-tolerated leak-closure, not an additive-only change; no protocol version bump needed because Ring asserts nothing about index membership (ClioIpcClient.ParseCatalog) and dispatch paths bypass the index.
Discovery: (1) Ring tests 111/111 green; NativeAOT publish succeeds but requires vswhere.exe on PATH (C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer) - a raw shell lacks it and the link step fails with a mangled MSB3073, so prepend that dir when publishing outside a VS developer prompt. (2) Parity method that works: build both commits Release/net8.0 (base via git worktree), drive dotnet clio.dll mcp-server over newline-delimited JSON-RPC stdio with a PowerShell driver, isolate settings with a fresh CLIO_HOME per side; PowerShell-written response files escape quotes as " so plain grep misses strings. (3) A follow-up finding was recorded as review item 20 (index safety-flag mislabeling for a curated-but-unregistered tool); superseded by the later decision to make the request surface unconditional (see the 13:40 entry).
Files: spec/requests-registry/requests-registry-code-review.md, clio-ring/ (validation only), scratchpad parity evidence (head2.resp2.json et al.)
Impact: PR body attestation text is ready in the review file item 1; the vswhere PATH note saves the next AOT publish attempt on this machine.

## 2026-07-17 – Remove the requests-registry feature toggle entirely
Context: Requirements clarification on feature/ENG-93187-requests-registry: the request-registry surface was never meant to be feature-toggled and must ship enabled for everyone; everything the branch gated behind [FeatureToggle("requests-registry")] becomes unconditional, matching the former feature-ON behavior exactly.
Decision: Delete the toggle wiring rather than flip a default: removed the attribute from RequestInfoTool / ListPrintablesTool / ListPrintablesOptions / WhenToUseRequestsGuidanceResource; made Routing/PageModification/MobilePage/PageSchemaHandlers guidance resources static again with the feature-ON article text baked in (no ctor-injected IFeatureToggleService, no BuildGuide(bool)); removed GuidanceCatalog.ArticleBuilder and the when-to-use-requests featureGateType; deleted the GuidanceArticleText splicing helper and its tests; ComponentRegistryRefreshCommand always refreshes the requests flavor and --all always enumerates the requests cache subdir. Feature-off tests (unit + RequestRegistryGatingE2ETests) deleted; feature-on tests converted to unconditional pins. Recorded as Resolved decision 2 in spec/requests-registry/requests-registry-code-review.md (supersedes items 6, 7, 20 and the gating aspects of 3/3a/11/13/14/15).
Discovery: The FeatureGateType mechanism on GuidanceCatalogEntry predates this branch and still serves process-designer gating — only the ArticleBuilder content-splicing layer was requests-registry-specific and could be removed wholesale. Builds against clio/bin/Debug/net8.0 fail with file locks while the session's clio-dev MCP server runs from that directory; Release builds work.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/{RoutingGuidanceResource,PageModificationGuidanceResource,MobilePageGuidanceResource,PageSchemaHandlersGuidanceResource,GuidanceCatalog,WhenToUseRequestsGuidanceResource}.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/{GuidanceGetTool,RequestInfoTool,ListPrintablesTool}.cs, clio/Command/{ComponentRegistryRefreshCommand,ListPrintablesCommand}.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/AGENTS.md, clio/docs/commands/component-registry-refresh.md, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/*, clio.mcp.e2e/*

## 2026-07-17 – ENG-93302 PR #895 review fixes (Krylov CHANGES_REQUESTED)
Context: Kirill Krylov requested changes on PR #895 (set-user-theme). Addressed 6 of 7 findings + the Allure convention; #3 (Destructive flag) deferred pending a decision (user asked to characterise the gate model first).
Decision: (#7) Extracted apply behavior into IUserThemeApplier/UserThemeApplier; SetUserThemeCommand is now a thin adapter; introduced IThemeCatalog (implemented by ListThemesCommand) so the applier no longer depends on the concrete command (CLIO001). (#5) SelectQueryHelper.ExecuteSelectQuery gained maxAttempts/retryDelay (default 1/1 keeps other callers unchanged); the applier's two profile SELECTs now carry options.MaxAttempts/RetryDelay like list/update. (#6) Replaced broad catch(Exception) with `catch (Exception) when (IsExpectedIoOrParseFailure(e))` (WebException/HttpRequestException/TaskCanceledException/TimeoutException/InvalidOperationException/JsonException) so programming errors propagate; the local UpdateQuery parse narrowed to catch(JsonException). (#1) SetUserThemeTool redacts the command error via SensitiveErrorTextRedactor.Redact (matches CreateThemeTool/ListThemesTool). (#2) ThemingSandboxE2ETests teardown now resets the user theme BEFORE deleting the throwaway theme, each guarded so one failing still runs the other (no dangling profile ref). (#4) removed the ChangeTheme-text Assert.Ignore that masked apply mismatches — the sandbox is required to be correctly configured, fail on mismatch. Allure: AllureDescription on the new E2E tests + AllureApi.Step phases on the sandbox lifecycle.
Discovery: clio's Destructive convention IS overwrite/modify=true (UpdateThemeTool/DeleteThemeTool) vs add-only=false (CreateThemeTool). set-user-theme overwrites a profile value → by convention Destructive=true — but that routes it through the durable confirmation gate (long-tail: bounce to clio-run-destructive, host-gated), which conflicts with the ENG-93302 no-confirmation auto-apply AC. Left the flag unchanged pending the decision. Added 3 unit tests (retry carried into selects; unexpected exception propagates; expected transport failure reported).
Files: clio/Command/Theming/{IUserThemeApplier→UserThemeApplier.cs (new), IThemeCatalog.cs (new), SetUserThemeCommand.cs, ListThemesCommand.cs}, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/SetUserThemeTool.cs, clio/Package/SelectQueryHelper.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio.tests/Command/{SetUserThemeCommandTests, McpServer/SetUserThemeToolTests}.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/{ThemingSandboxE2ETests, SetUserThemeToolE2ETests}.cs
Impact: full unit suite 6712/0 (net8.0); no-env MCP E2E 3/3; build clean. #3 awaits decision.

## 2026-07-17 – ENG-93302 #3 resolved: set-user-theme marked Destructive=true
Context: Decision on Krylov finding #3 — mark the MCP tool destructive (follow the reviewer) rather than keep the no-confirmation auto-apply.
Decision: SetUserThemeTool Destructive=false→true (overwrites an existing profile value = in-place modification, not additive; matches update-theme/delete-theme). Removed set-user-theme from the durable gate's silent-write baseline (DurableInvocationGateCompletenessTests) and flipped the flag unit test to assert Destructive=true (the confirmation-gate coverage). Updated ThemingGuidanceResource + McpCapabilityMap + spec FR-3 to say the MCP apply is a confirmed write (host-gated; lazy surface re-issues via clio-run-destructive), still own-profile-only and reset-reversible.
Discovery: E2E is NOT affected — the harness routes long-tail tools through clio-run (executes directly, no host in the loop), the same path already-destructive delete-theme uses in the CRUD test. CLI is unaffected (Destructive is an MCP-only annotation). CAADT skill (PR #47) "needs no confirmation" wording now needs updating in that repo.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/{Tools/SetUserThemeTool.cs,Resources/ThemingGuidanceResource.cs}, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/{DurableInvocationGateCompletenessTests,SetUserThemeToolTests}.cs, docs/McpCapabilityMap.md, spec/prd/spec-set-user-theme.md
Impact: full unit suite 6712/0 (net8.0). PR #47 (toolkit skill) follow-up pending.
## 2026-07-17 09:29 – Prototype ClioRing runtime switch
Context: Issue #903 requests release clio as the ordinary Ring runtime and an unavoidable main-surface warning when a development/custom clio is running.
Decision: Separate persisted runtime selection from the saved development target, reserve banner space above the radial control for Development, and keep Release as a compact green identity strip; runtime changes are represented as next-launch choices until the owned IPC child can be safely retargeted.

## 2026-07-17 10:13 – Complete ClioRing runtime selection
Context: The approved issue #903 design needed production-safe runtime propagation, persistence, and final validation.
Decision: Resolve one immutable runtime for IPC workflows, environment discovery, and radial commands; trust Release only from dotnet-owned user roots; preserve next-launch selection in Ring's own settings with atomic writes and an actionable invalid-Development fallback.
Discovery: Runtime identity cannot be inferred from a command name or saved development path, and ordinary Ring actions previously bypassed the IPC runtime target entirely.
Files: clio-ring/ClioRing.Ipc/ClioIpcModels.cs, clio-ring/ClioRing/Models/ResolvedClioRuntime.cs, clio-ring/ClioRing/Services/ClioAdapter.cs, clio-ring/ClioRing/Services/ClioSettingsStore.cs, clio-ring/ClioRing/ViewModels/ClioSettingsViewModel.cs, clio-ring/ClioRing/Views/RingView.axaml, clio-ring/ClioRing.Tests/, spec/ring-clio-runtime-switch/
Impact: Release and Development choices now govern the full Ring surface consistently; 129 focused tests, final three-lens review, and Windows x64 NativeAOT publish pass.

## 2026-07-17 17:30 – Add safe ClioRing clio tool updates
Context: Issue #905 requires Ring to announce and install stable Release clio updates even when agent-hosted MCP processes lock the tool.
Decision: Check NuGet through an isolated NuGet.org source, serialize Release clio use with an app-wide process gate, inspect locks through Restart Manager, and require a separate revalidated gesture before terminating only exact trusted clio processes.
Discovery: Ring, Claude, and Codex can retain independent `clio mcp-server` processes; updater safety therefore requires PID, path, and start-time identity plus bounded child shutdown and no parent-process termination.
