Impact: Ring now provides deduplicated main/tray update attention, explicit safe update and kill-retry flows, persistent check state, 149 focused tests, clean three-lens review, and a passing Windows x64 NativeAOT publish.

## 2026-07-17 – ENG-93375 Sequence default honors mask prefix
Context: modify-entity-schema-column/create-entity-schema silently dropped the static prefix of a Sequence default (LN-{0} → 00001 instead of LN-00001).
Decision: Parse the default-value-config.value mask in EntitySchemaDesignerSupport.ResolveSequencePrefix — a single trailing {0} yields the static prefix; suffixes/repeated placeholders/value+sequence-prefix combos/value-source are rejected with actionable errors. Prefix still settable via sequence-prefix alone.
Discovery: Sequence resolver is passthrough; the prefix flows through CreateDefaultValueDto + readback CreateDefaultValueConfig (SequencePrefix). Runtime round-trip on ts1-dev04 proved a created record gets UsrCode=LN-00001; suffix mask rejected before save.
Files: clio/Command/EntitySchemaDesigner/EntitySchemaDesignerSupport.cs, EntitySchemaDefaultValueConfig.cs; McpServer/Tools/ToolContractGetTool.cs, Prompts/EntitySchemaPrompt.cs; docs/commands/{create,modify,update}-entity-schema.md; help/en/*.txt; clio.tests/Command/EntitySchemaDesignerSupportTests.cs; clio.mcp.e2e/EntitySchemaToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Autonumber masks with a static prefix now work end-to-end; unsupported masks fail loudly instead of silently. Left a scratch schema UsrEng93375Seq (1 record) on ts1-dev04.

## 2026-07-17 — ENG-93373 read-only MCP response deadline
Context: read-only MCP ops could hang indefinitely (session evidence: list-pages hung 7m33s, user killed it). Ticket asks for a response deadline + structured creatio-timeout on read tools, mirroring create-app-section's write-path deadline.
Decision: pipeline-layer, shape-agnostic mechanism (NOT per-tool). Gate = McpReadDeadlineGate.IsRetrySafe = !destructive && (readOnly || idempotent) — covers get-page (ReadOnly=false/Idempotent=true), excludes non-idempotent writes. New helper McpReadResponseDeadline (knob CLIO_MCP_READ_DEADLINE_SECONDS, default 120s, separate from write's CLIO_MCP_RESPONSE_DEADLINE_SECONDS) races work vs deadline → structured CallToolResult {error-class:creatio-timeout, read-response-timed-out:true, retry-guidance}. Wired at 3 dispatch vectors: McpToolErrorFilter (matched/advertised), McpDurableCallToolHandler (unmatched long-tail via registry.IsRetrySafe), ClioRunTool.RunAsync inner dispatch (clio-run, Ring's list-apps path).
Discovery: SDK 1.4.1 exposes tool.ProtocolTool.Annotations.{ReadOnly,Destructive,Idempotent}Hint. clio-run/clio-run-destructive are Destructive=true so the filter never double-wraps them. Ring parser (ClioWorkflowViewModel.ParseRows) tolerates the timeout envelope (IsError→Failure, no `applications`→raw JSON, no crash). AOT publish macOS→win-x64 blocked by host ("Cross-OS native compilation not supported") — managed build clean; validate on Windows CI. ClioRing.Tests 151/152 (the 1 fail = DeploymentReceipt backslash path-traversal, pre-existing macOS `\`-not-separator issue, unrelated).
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpReadDeadlineGate.cs, McpReadResponseDeadline.cs, McpToolErrorFilter.cs, McpDurableCallToolHandler.cs, Tools/ClioRunTool.cs, Tools/McpToolInvokerRegistry.cs, Resources/CoreRulesGuidanceResource.cs, AGENTS.md; tests in clio.tests/Command/McpServer/* + clio.mcp.e2e/ReadResponseDeadlineToolE2ETests.cs; spec/adr/adr-read-only-mcp-response-deadline.md.
Impact: any future read-only MCP tool inherits the deadline automatically via annotations — no per-tool wiring. e2e (list-pages vs stalling endpoint, CLIO_MCP_READ_DEADLINE_SECONDS=1) passed locally in 12s (runtime self-verified).

## 2026-07-17 — ENG-93373 review-gate fixes
Context: 3 parallel reviewers (correctness/concurrency, security/contract, test-coverage) on the working diff.
Decision: (1) SECURITY HIGH — gate `readOnly||idempotent` wrongly bounded idempotent SERVER writes (install-gate/generate-source-code/add-package-dependency) with "safe to retry" → concurrent-duplicate-write risk. Narrowed gate to `!destructive && (readOnly || toolName=="get-page")`; Idempotent hint dropped; get-page admitted by explicit one-tool allowlist (Creatio read + local-only write). (2) M2 — reordered ObserveAndDispose before ThrowIfCancellationRequested. (3) M3 — separate delayCts cancels the deadline timer on fast completion (no lingering 120s timer). (4) L5 — workCts disposed in the observe-continuation after work completes, not synchronously (avoids ObjectDisposedException on a cooperative tool). (5) Test HIGH — added clio-run e2e (execute-esq via clio-run, the primary long-tail vector) proving inner-dispatch bounding.
Discovery: execute-esq is NON-resident + ReadOnly → clio-run is the PRIMARY long-tail read vector (agents reach non-resident tools via clio-run per core-rules), durable handler is the raw-name fallback. execute-esq expects a SelectQuery OBJECT (rootSchemaName), not SQL; its internal timeout 30s > 1s read-deadline. H1 (abandoned read holds per-tenant Monitor / parks pool thread) and M4 (abandoned DispatchAsync restores shared RequestContext late) accepted+documented as the same single-session detach trade-off as the write path (benign: fresh context per request; agent-paced ~1 abandon/timeout). L6 (get-page cross-identity double-write) = narrow pre-existing PageFileWriter edge, same-tenant serialized, documented not fixed.
Files: same as prior entry + updated gate signature (toolName param) across McpToolErrorFilter/McpToolInvokerRegistry; ADR + AGENTS.md gate section corrected.
Impact: gate is now reads-only (never server writes); all 3 dispatch vectors (filter/clio-run/durable) bounded; 2 e2e (filter + clio-run) pass locally. 2420 McpServer unit tests green.

## 2026-07-17 – ENG-93374 sync-schemas transient retry + resume-plan
Context: A sync-schemas batch aborted whole (~14 min wasted) on one transient DNS flap; no retry, no machine-readable completed/failed/not-run output. clio-side fix.
Decision: Per-op transient retry (3 attempts, 1s/2s backoff) via injectable synchronous IRetryDelay (loop is inside lock(McpToolExecutionLock) → await illegal). New TransientNetworkFailureClassifier (typed exception walk + bounded message markers, incl. connection-refused + 5xx per operator decision; session-expiry stays fail-fast). Additive response fields status/operation-index/attempts + new resume-plan block (failed + not-run ops echoed re-submittable). Added seed-data as first-class input op type so a create-succeeded-seed-failed resume never recreates the schema. Registration retried in its OWN scope so a reg flap never re-runs the applied create. abort-on-first-failure preserved. results semantics unchanged (not-run only in resume-plan) for back-compat.
Discovery: RunAttempts discards failed-attempt raw messages, keeps only final attempt + info retry-notes (avoids 3x duplicate error spam). Outer catch filters McpExceptionPolicy.IsUnrecoverable so programming defects propagate (aligns with project rule). 19 full-suite failures are PRE-EXISTING env-specific (.mcp.json/TLS/Windows-path/ClioRing) — confirmed on master, not mine.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/SchemaSyncTool.cs, TransientNetworkFailureClassifier.cs, clio/Common/IRetryDelay.cs, BindingsModule.cs, ToolContractGetTool.cs, 4 guidance resources, docs/commands/sync-schemas.md, SchemaSyncToolTests.cs, TransientNetworkFailureClassifierTests.cs, SchemaSyncToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: One transient flap no longer kills a batch; agents resume from resume-plan.operations instead of hand-composing catch-up. Runtime-verified via 2 no-sandbox e2e against real mcp-server; sandbox resume-round-trip runs on TeamCity Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests.

(M2) seed-data NOT auto-retried (RunAttempts retryable:false) — non-idempotent write, fail-fast→resume-plan; +tests standalone & inline. (M3) create-lookup idempotent on same-target-package collision → continue to registration (closes reg-resume hole); +test. ## 2026-07-17 18:30 – ENG-93727 get-page-hierarchy (bulk replacing-chain read)
Context: migration discovery walked the replacing-schema chain with N sequential per-schema reads (~13-18 model turns). Ticket named non-existent tools (list-schema-hierarchy / get-classic-schema-by-uid) — reporter paraphrase; real gap was that no MCP tool surfaced every schema's OWN body in one call.
Decision: new read-only tool/CLI `get-page-hierarchy` (verb + MCP), long-tail (NOT resident — resident set is byte-budget-capped at 35_328; adding one busts it by ~2.2KB, against ENG context-reduction). Discovery via a pointer in ExistingAppMaintenance guidance + get-tool-contract. Keyed by page schema name. Chain ordered root-first (= bundle merge consume order); bodies inline; offset/limit + metadata-only as the size guardrail (AC#3).
Discovery: `IPageDesignerHierarchyClient.GetParentSchemas` (ClientUnitSchemaDesignerService.svc, useFullHierarchy=true) already returns the whole chain WITH bodies in one HTTP call — so this was a missing MCP surface, not backend work. Resolution mirrors PageGetCommand exactly (metadata -> GetDesignPackageUId -> GetParentSchemas -> root-normalize + re-fetch) to guarantee chain parity; unifying the 4 copies is ENG-93249. FR-06 guard requires a PassthroughToolClassificationRegistry row for every discovered tool (added NotApplicable).
Verification: live runtime self-verify BLOCKED — every registered env is stale/unhealthy for authenticated ESQ (shipped get-page fails too; ClioGate 404). Covered by clio.mcp.e2e (sandbox + NoEnvironment discoverability) instead — runs on TeamCity ClioMcpE2eTests. Unit: 5 new + FR-06 guard green; McpServer+Command modules 5025/0. Full suite has 19 PRE-EXISTING macOS failures (DbHub/TOML, AppPool Windows paths, IIS) — confirmed failing on origin/master, not this change.
Files: clio/Command/GetPageHierarchyCommand.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/PageHierarchyGetTool.cs, Program.cs, BindingsModule.cs, ExistingAppMaintenanceGuidanceResource.cs, help/en/get-page-hierarchy.txt, docs/commands/get-page-hierarchy.md, Commands.md, Wiki/WikiAnchors.txt, clio.tests/.../PassthroughToolClassificationRegistry.cs, clio.tests unit x2, clio.mcp.e2e x1.
Impact: one-call chain read removes N per-schema round-trips in migration/page discovery. Pattern for adding a long-tail read tool: command+tool+Program+BindingsModule+PassthroughRegistry row+docs quartet+guidance pointer.

## 2026-07-17 13:13 – Add universal clio guidance-development skill
Context: Claude and Codex needed the same repeatable framework for researching Creatio behavior and projecting it into reliable clio MCP guidance without maintaining divergent skill copies.
Decision: Made `.ai/skills/clio-guidance-development/SKILL.md` the canonical instruction source and exposed it through minimal relative-path redirect skills under both `.claude/skills` and `.codex/skills`; the redirects contain no guidance rules.
Discovery: Git symlinks materialize as one-line regular files on ordinary Windows checkouts with `core.symlinks=false`. Portable redirect skills validate on every platform while preserving one canonical guidance body. The framework assigns every concept one owner; the ESQ family uses a router plus separate frontend construction, backend construction, and runtime parsing owners.
Files: .ai/skills/clio-guidance-development/SKILL.md, .ai/skills/clio-guidance-development/agents/openai.yaml, .claude/skills/clio-guidance-development/SKILL.md, .codex/skills/clio-guidance-development/SKILL.md, .codex/skills/clio-guidance-development/agents/openai.yaml, .codex/workspace-diary.md
Impact: Both agents now invoke one evidence-driven lab-to-guidance workflow, while edits occur once under `.ai` and cannot drift across agent-specific skill directories.

## 2026-07-17 17:53 – Split ESQ filter guidance by responsibility
Context: The VirtualEntity guidance lab proved the empty group envelope, flat AND, nested OR, and both mixed AND/OR nesting shapes through native C# versus ATF.Repository/DataService runtime-tree comparison.
Decision: Kept `esq-filters` as a short stable router, moved the existing serialized JSON rules to `esq-filters-frontend`, and added independent `esq-filters-backend` construction and `esq-filter-parsing` runtime-consumption articles.
Discovery: ATF `A || B` reaches runtime as an AND root containing one OR group; preserving that envelope and all nested groups is necessary for structural equality even when result rows are semantically identical.

Context: Backend history and the lab proved that Entity-level virtual CRUD is a new 10.0 capability rather than established 8.3 behavior.

## 2026-07-17 22:15 – Complete primitive ESQ Compare guidance
Context: The backend validation plan had only partial scalar Compare coverage and lacked concrete C# recipes for the remaining operators.
Decision: Used representative Integer and MediumText examples instead of an operator/type Cartesian product; kept native construction in `esq-filters-backend` and runtime interpretation in `esq-filter-parsing`.
Discovery: ATF.Repository 2.0.3.5 emits source `A && B && C` as flat `AND(C, A, B)`. Negated string methods arrive as dedicated negative comparison types, not leaf/group negation. Case-variant results prove the handler's explicit `OrdinalIgnoreCase` policy, not PostgreSQL collation behavior.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersBackendGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs, spec/esq-primitive-comparisons/
Impact: Agents now have concrete, tested construction and fail-closed parsing guidance for the complete scalar Compare operator catalog without overclaiming untested type combinations.

## 2026-07-17 22:32 – Close primitive comparison PR gates
Context: The primitive Compare guidance needed final regression and adversarial review before publication.
Decision: Protected all 12 operators with exact-token assertions, kept provider-owned case semantics distinct from database collation, and separated full-tree validation from short-circuit evaluation.
Discovery: Substring assertions such as `Equal` can pass through `NotEqual`; exact backend and Markdown tokens are required. Full validation must precede short-circuit evaluation so invalid hidden branches still fail closed.

## 2026-07-17 22:48 – Validate complete ESQ column paths
Context: Codex review on PR #915 identified that terminal `SchemaColumnName` matching can accept a forwarded path such as `Account.Name` as root `Name`.
Decision: Changed the parsing recipe to compare the exact `LeftExpression.Path` to the provider's allowed path and explained that direct-only providers thereby reject lookup filters.
Discovery: `EntitySchemaQueryExpression.Path` retains the complete expression path, while `SchemaColumnName` exposes only its terminal schema column.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs

## 2026-07-17 14:35 – Validate disabled ESQ nodes and collection IsNot
Context: Extend the backend ESQ construction and parsing guidance using the VirtualEntityGuidance lab.
Decision: Document disabled-node transport differences separately from semantic evaluation, and apply collection `IsNot` only after combining enabled children.
Discovery: Native C# retains disabled leaves/groups while SQL compilation omits them; DataService removes disabled children before the executor boundary. Native C# and DataService preserve collection `IsNot`. ATF.Repository 2.0.3.5 cannot author group negation through LINQ, so the lab used a test-only decorator over its public `ISelectQuery` contract.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersBackendGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Agents now have lab-verified rules for creating and parsing disabled nodes and negated groups, including the native-versus-DataService shape boundary.

## 2026-07-17 – Hide never-in-CI manual e2e suites from ClioMcpE2eTests stats
Context: ClioMcpE2eTests plan showed "ignored: 51"; wanted them present in source but absent from plan statistics.
Decision: Assert.Ignore counts as 'ignored' by design → cannot hide via attribute. Use NUnit [Category] + run-step --filter exclusion (same mechanism as existing McpE2E.ProcessDesigner). Introduced category "McpE2E.Manual" on the 46 never-in-CI tests.
Discovery: dotnet test maps NUnit [Category] to VSTest TestCategory trait; multi-valued `TestCategory!=X` excludes a test if ANY of its categories == X. `dotnet test --list-tests --filter` is a reliable local proof of selection without running e2e. TeamCity Team_Atf has versioned-settings OFF, so build-step edits via REST persist. The `/parameters/{name}` value endpoint rejects the CLI's JSON content-type; GET-modify-PUT the whole /steps/{id} entity works.
Split: class-level on Concurrency/MultiTenant/OAuth/DbHubLifecycleWarning/UninstallCreatioWarning (100% manual); method-level on McpHttpNoRegression (keep 16 passing Stdio_* tests). Left runtime-conditional skips (DataForge x3, EntitySchema+SchemaSync Pg-gated x2) untouched — those can run on a properly configured stand.
Files: clio.mcp.e2e/{McpHttpConcurrencyIsolation,McpHttpMultiTenant,McpHttpOAuthAuthorization,McpHttpNoRegression,DbHubLifecycleWarning,UninstallCreatioWarning}E2ETests.cs; TeamCity Team_Atf_ClioMcpE2eTests step Run_MCP_e2e_tests args filter.
Impact: plan will report 498 passed / 5 ignored (down from 51) once next build runs; DataForge+Pg remain the only visible skips.

## 2026-07-18 00:30 – Validate text IsNull and IsNotNull
Context: Project milestone 4 of the backend ESQ filter validation plan into clio guidance.
Decision: Teach the dedicated native null-filter APIs and a separate left-only parser contract with zero right expressions.
Discovery: Native C# and ATF/DataService shapes matched exactly. For the verified MediumText columns, PostgreSQL SQL compilation used `column = ''` and `NOT column = ''`; the lab provider therefore evaluates null and empty string alike without generalizing to other data types.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersBackendGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Agents can now construct and parse text null predicates from verified runtime and SQL evidence rather than treating null as an ordinary scalar parameter.

## 2026-07-18 01:25 – Validate backend In cardinality
Context: Project milestone 5 of the backend ESQ filter validation plan into clio guidance.
Decision: Document native membership as Equal plus a right-expression collection and keep DataService filterType 4 as a transport-only discriminator.
Discovery: Zero, one, and two values matched exactly between native and DataService runtime trees. SQL emitted invalid `column = ` for zero, scalar equality for one, and IN for two; Guid arrays require conversion to object[] to avoid one array-valued parameter.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersBackendGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Agents can now construct and parse membership without confusing serialized In metadata with the runtime comparison enum or broadening empty membership.

## 2026-07-18 01:53 – Validate backend Between filters
Context: Project milestone 6 of the backend ESQ filter validation plan into clio guidance.
Decision: Document first-class Between separately from the equivalent pair of inclusive Compare leaves and correct the frontend serialized-bound ordering.
Discovery: Native and DataService first-class shapes matched as one Between leaf with ordered lower/upper Integer parameters and inclusive SQL. DataService counterintuitively requires rightLessExpression for the first/lower value and rightGreaterExpression for the second/upper value; the two-Compare alternative remains two leaves.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersBackendGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Agents can author and parse both inclusive range representations without reversing DataService bounds or assuming structural normalization.

## 2026-07-18 02:27 – Validate typed primitive and lookup filters
Context: Project milestones 7 and 8 of the backend ESQ filter validation plan into clio guidance.
Decision: Validate CLR parameter type together with ParameterValueForcedType and treat lookup Guid values as a schema-specific family distinct from plain Guid columns.
Discovery: Boolean and plain Guid retained BooleanDataValueType and GuidDataValueType. Native UsrOwner resolved to runtime UsrOwnerId, while its Guid parameters were LookupDataValueType; multi-value lookup requires object[] and compiled to IN. Low-level DataService accepted raw lookup Guid parameters, while designer-owned frontend JSON retains its display-value object contract.

Impact: Agents can distinguish Boolean, plain Guid, and lookup Guid parameters without coercion or path-based guessing.

## 2026-07-18 03:15 – Validate temporal ESQ filters
Context: Project milestone 9 of the backend ESQ filter validation plan into clio guidance.
Decision: Document temporal literals by schema path, trim-to-date as an explicit leaf flag, and macros/date parts by their expanded recursive runtime trees.
Discovery: Date, DateTime, and Time arrive as CLR DateTime. DataService preserved ticks but changed UTC Kind to Unspecified and reordered flat AND siblings. CurrentYear and PreviousNDays expanded to half-open boundary groups; Year became a date-part leaf; HourMinute equality became a one-minute half-open range.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersBackendGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Agents can author and recursively parse verified temporal filter shapes without inferring date-only semantics, timezone policy, or macro boundaries.

## 2026-07-18 03:36 – Correct HourMinute backend recipe overload
Context: The Codex connector found that the temporal guidance combined an expression argument with the four-argument DateTime overload shape.
Decision: Use the stable column-path overload for HourMinute and strengthen both resource contract tests to assert that exact compiling recipe.
Discovery: The expression-based DateTime overload has a different signature on documented Creatio API versions, so a lab-compiling overload must not be generalized when a cross-version column-path overload expresses the same intent.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersBackendGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs
Impact: Agents copying the published HourMinute example receive a valid native C# call, guarded on both in-process and real MCP resource paths.

## 2026-07-18 04:02 – Validate backward-path subquery filters
Context: Project milestone 10 of the backend ESQ filter validation plan into clio guidance.
Decision: Document Exists, NotExists, and Count subqueries together because they share the same generated mixed-path correlation but differ at the outer expression boundary.
Discovery: Exists and NotExists use a null left expression plus one right Activity subquery. Count uses the subquery on the left and retains both Id and aggregate columns. DataService preserves its serialized child filters as one extra AND envelope; only that validated wrapper is safe to normalize for semantic parity.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersBackendGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Agents can construct and recursively parse verified relationship subqueries without confusing schema-column correlations with parameters, assuming one child column, or introducing N+1 execution.

## 2026-07-18 04:12 – Bound subquery parser execution
Context: Pre-PR adversarial review of the promoted subquery guidance identified aggregate-shape and fallback-execution gaps.
Decision: Require the exact lab-proven non-distinct Count(Id) operand after applying the selected-expression budget, and require bounded batched fallback execution when provider pushdown is unavailable.
Discovery: Avoiding one query per root record is insufficient if an implementation instead materializes an unbounded child source; correlation-key, fan-out, timeout, and cancellation limits are part of the parser contract.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: The published parser fails closed on semantically different Count functions and cannot recommend an unbounded preload as an N+1 workaround.

## 2026-07-18 04:35 – Validate saved Segment filters
Context: Complete milestone 11 of the backend ESQ filter validation plan and promote the final pending frontend family.
Decision: Publish the native SegmentFilterOptions recipe and parse only the exact expanded current-membership tree, with membership tables restricted by trusted metadata or an allowlist.
Discovery: Native and DataService IN/NOT IN requests produced identical Exists/NotExists subqueries with RecordId correlation and RemovedOn IsNull. The Segment Id and filterType 7 authoring metadata do not survive into esq.Filters. ATF.Repository 2.0.3.5 can serialize numeric filter type 7 but does not name it in its enum.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFiltersBackendGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Agents can construct saved Segment membership and safely recognize its executor-visible expansion without trusting dynamic table names or claiming unverified option variants.

## 2026-07-18 04:48 – Require Segment authorization before table access
Context: Pre-PR adversarial review found that a membership-table allowlist alone could bypass caller/root Segment authorization and that malformed child roots could escape the secret-safe rejection path.
Decision: Require request-scoped, permission-checked SysDataSegment resolution for the caller and root schema on every access, then use only the exact authorized table identifier through the provider query builder.
Discovery: Dynamic SQL identifiers cannot be parameterized; authorization must derive the exact identifier from trusted metadata, while every value remains parameterized. Parser guards now null-check the child root and validate the selected RecordId schema-column expression before authorization.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: An expanded Segment-shaped subquery cannot access an allowlisted table without proving caller authorization and target-schema ownership, and malformed subqueries fail through controlled validation.

## 2026-07-18 04:55 – Validate before Segment authorization lookup
Context: Re-review found that malformed repeated Segment leaves could trigger metadata authorization work before cheap shape rejection and repeat it within one query.
Decision: Validate the complete in-memory Segment tree first, then authorize each distinct table once using a cache scoped to caller, root-schema UId, table name, and request lifetime.
Discovery: Authorization results must never be shared across callers or retained globally; query-scoped reuse avoids repeated metadata work without turning stale authorization into ambient trust.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/EsqFilterParsingGuidanceResource.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Invalid trees are rejected before external work, while valid repeated Segment leaves reuse only identity-safe request-scoped authorization.

## 2026-07-18 05:03 – Pin Segment validation presence in contracts
Context: Final comprehensive review found that the validation-order assertion could pass when the validation marker was absent because IndexOf returned -1.
Decision: Require ValidateCurrentMembershipFilters explicitly in unit and both MCP E2E resource contracts before asserting its order relative to authorization.
Discovery: Ordering assertions over string indices must independently prove that both ordered markers exist.
Files: clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpGuidanceResourceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/McpGuidanceResourceE2ETests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/GuidanceGetToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Removing the fail-closed validation step now fails every relevant published-resource contract instead of accidentally satisfying the order assertion.
## 2026-07-18 12:00 – ENG-92761: DataForgeTool moved out of resident MCP profile
Context: DataForge tools are a niche path but sat resident in tools/list (all 8 methods, per-TYPE), costing ~19% of the tools/list payload (~1.5k tokens / 28566→ measured) every session. Alex asked to move to long-tail (option 1) with backward-compat like ENG-93370.
Decision: Removed `typeof(DataForgeTool)` from `McpCoreToolProfile.CoreToolTypes`. NO McpToolCompatibilityCatalog entry — no canonical name changed; the ENG-93370 durable unmatched-name handler already provides BC (read tools execute w/ advisory, destructive returns confirmation-required → clio-run-destructive). Read-only dataforge stays discoverable via get-tool-contract curated `CanonicalToolNames` (independent of CoreToolTypes) so `DataForge_Contract_Policy` e2e stays green and dataforge-context stays in the cheap index.
Discovery: (1) get-tool-contract default `detail=full` set is gated by the hand-maintained `CanonicalToolNames` list (ToolContractGetTool.cs:588), NOT by IsResident — so residency change doesn't drop read dataforge from discovery. (2) `McpServerSession.CallToolAsync` (e2e) auto-routes unadvertised tools through the SAFE clio-run executor, which REFUSES destructive commands — so long-tail destructive e2e (dataforge-initialize/update, + DataForgeReadinessGate warm-up) must use new `Session.CallDestructiveAsync` (clio-run-destructive). Read e2e just needed the local `Contain(toolName)` assert removed. (3) tools/list 27→19; ProtocolTool payload 30.1k→28566 bytes.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpCoreToolProfile.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpProfileGatingTests.cs (ratchet 30→22 count, 35328→30000 bytes, DataForge Contain→NotContain + PageListTool positive), clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/McpServerSession.cs (+CallDestructiveAsync), clio.mcp.e2e/Support/Mcp/DataForgeReadinessGate.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/DataForgeToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Any future "move a resident tool to long-tail" follows this exact playbook: profile edit + gating-test ratchet + e2e destructive-executor routing; BC is free via the durable handler when no name changes. Verified live: dataforge resident=false in get-tool-contract, initialize direct call → confirmation-required. Ring does not consume dataforge.

## 2026-07-18 12:00 – Redis auto-detect bounded retry (#883)
Context: deploy-creatio (--redis-db -1) hard-failed on a single transient Redis connect blip (Rancher Desktop port-forward hiccup) since ENG-90640 made FindEmptyLocalDatabase fail-fast (AbortOnConnectFail=true, ConnectRetry=1, 12s).
Decision: Added a bounded retry (MaxConnectAttempts=3) with exponential backoff (500ms→1000ms) around the connect in RedisDatabaseSelector.FindEmptyDatabase, keeping the per-attempt 12s fail-fast timeout. Extracted SelectEmptyDatabase (uses `using` to dispose the multiplexer). Retry only on transient failures; a definitive error is surfaced immediately.
Discovery: StackExchange.Redis surfaces bad credentials as RedisConnectionException with FailureType=AuthenticationFailure (not a distinct type). Naive `ex is RedisConnectionException` over-matches and would retry auth misconfig 3x (~37.5s). IsTransientConnectFailure must exclude AuthenticationFailure and ProtocolFailure. Connect+probe seam made testable via internal ctor taking Func<ConfigurationOptions,IConnectionMultiplexer> + Action<TimeSpan> (public parameterless ctor wires ConnectionMultiplexer.Connect + Thread.Sleep); DI binds the public ctor. Worst-case unreachable Redis stays bounded ~37.5s (seconds, not minutes).
Files: clio/Common/Database/RedisDatabaseSelector.cs, clio.tests/Common/Database/RedisDatabaseSelectorTests.cs, clio/docs/commands/deploy-creatio.md
Impact: A momentary blip no longer aborts deploy-creatio and leaves a half-configured ConnectionStrings.config; genuinely unreachable/auth-misconfigured Redis still fails fast. Shared selector so LocalRedisAssertion/K8RedisAssertion (assert flows) also benefit.

## 2026-07-18 12:35 – ENG-91315 restart --wait-ready + async compile-status contract
Context: Compile→restart→warm-up cycle had no first-class contract (source: ENG-91312 session atomization); agents hand-rolled ~10min of healthcheck polling after restart, and compile-creatio's fully-synchronous call hard-failed clients with a ~180s ceiling (Copilot CLI) on a 3-15min full compile.
Decision: New shared `IServerReadinessWaiter` (clio/Common) drives HealthCheckCommand for both `restart-web-app --wait-ready --ready-timeout` and CreatioInstallerService's deploy wait-ready stage (timing preserved exactly: 15s initial/45s budget for installer). MCP `restart-by-environment-name` defaults `waitReady=true` (Alex's explicit call — agents almost always want to wait, false-negative debugging in ENG-91312 outweighs latency risk) wrapped in McpProgressHeartbeat.RunWithProgressAndDeadlineAsync; on deadline returns a NEW `CommandExecutionResult.FromInfo` (exit 0 + InfoMessage) in-progress envelope pointing at `clio-run healthcheck`. `compile-creatio` gets the same deadline wrap plus a new in-memory `ICompileOperationRegistry` (singleton — explicitly skip-listed out of BindingsModule's auto-transient-scan) and a new read-only `compile-status` tool to poll it.
Discovery: `CompileCreatioTool.ExecuteFullCompile/ExecutePackageCompile` call `commandResolver.Resolve<TCommand>(options)` OUTSIDE `Execute<TOptions>`'s own try/catch — an unregistered-environment `EnvironmentResolutionException` there propagated straight out of the heartbeat work delegate, meaning `registry.Finish` never ran and the tracked operation stayed "Running" forever for the single most common failure. Caught only by the NEW e2e test chaining compile-creatio(invalid env)→compile-status(same env); no unit test had a resolver that could throw. Fixed with an explicit try/catch mirroring BaseTool's own EnvironmentResolutionException→FromResolverError / Exception→FromException handling (RestartTool didn't need this fix — it already goes through BaseTool.InternalExecute<TCommand>, which already handles it). Also: NSubstitute can proxy a concrete non-sealed class like HealthCheckCommand directly (`Substitute.For<HealthCheckCommand>(ctorArgs...)`) for waiter tests. Also: ClioRing's ClioWorkflowViewModel hardcodes `Tool = "restart-by-environment-name"` with only `environmentName` — verified compatible (no client-side call timeout, CancellationTokenSource only cancels on explicit user action, progress notifications already rendered to the output log), with one accepted UX nuance: an in-progress envelope on a slow restart still shows Ring's green "Success" badge since IsError=false, even though the app is still warming up — descriptive text remains visible in the log regardless.
Files: clio/Common/ServerReadinessWaiter.cs, clio/Command/RestartCommand.cs, clio/Command/CreatioInstallCommand/CreatioInstallerService.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/RestartTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/CompileCreatioTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/CompileOperationRegistry.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/CompileStatusTool.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/CommandExecutionResult.cs, clio/BindingsModule.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/CoreRulesGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Prompts/{RestartPrompt,FsmAndCompilePrompt}.cs, clio/tpl/workspace/AGENTS.md, clio/help/en/restart-web-app.txt, clio/docs/commands/restart-web-app.md, clio/Commands.md
Impact: Targeted unit modules (Command/Common/McpServer) at 5858 passed / 19 pre-existing-unrelated failures (verified via git-stash clean-baseline diff: AppPoolProfileCleaner/NetFrameworkHttpsConfigurator/DbHubTomlStore, all Windows-path-assuming tests failing identically pre-change); e2e McpE2E.NoEnvironment 291/292 (1 pre-existing IIS-target-resolution failure, same verification); ClioRing.Tests 151/152 (1 pre-existing Windows-backslash-path-escape failure, same verification). NativeAOT win-x64 publish gate NOT run (macOS session) — flagged to Alex rather than silently skipped.

## 2026-07-18 13:30 – SysSettings (get-sys-setting/list-sys-settings) moved out of resident MCP profile
Context: same playbook as ENG-92761 (DataForge) — Alex asked to move get-sys-setting/list-sys-settings to long-tail too.
Decision: removed typeof(SysSettingGetTool)/typeof(SysSettingsListTool) from McpCoreToolProfile.CoreToolTypes. Simpler than DataForge: each is a single-method class (no destructive ride-along), both names already in get-tool-contract's CanonicalToolNames independent of residency, and no e2e test asserted residency via the stale tools/list-Contain pattern (SysSettingsToolE2ETests.cs already used the residency-agnostic ListReachableToolNamesAsync helper for BOTH these resident reads and the already-long-tail destructive create-/update-sys-setting — zero e2e changes needed). No McpToolCompatibilityCatalog entry (no name changed).
Discovery: mid-task, re-verifying the DataForge (#925) precedent surfaced that my own earlier claim ("the SAFE clio-run executor refuses destructive commands") was WRONG — commit 6fc54cc3 (2026-06-19, well before #925's base) made clio-run/clio-run-destructive dispatch ANY tool directly with no internal refusal; the "refuses destructive" behavior belongs to a DIFFERENT code path (McpDurableCallToolHandler, only triggered on a bare/raw unmatched-name tools/call, e.g. via CallToolRawAsync — NOT the auto-routed Session.CallToolAsync which wraps calls in clio-run). This means the CallDestructiveAsync helper + DataForgeReadinessGate special-case I added in #925 were unnecessary (though not broken — clio-run-destructive still works as a pure alias). Reviewer tetiana-moshon already caught the same false comment on #925 and it was already fixed on the remote branch (commit 5b18b147) by the time I re-checked — not re-touched here.
Files: clio/Command/McpServer/McpCoreToolProfile.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/McpProfileGatingTests.cs (ratchet MaxLazyToolCount 30->28, MaxLazyToolsSerializedBytes 39*1024->37*1024 measured 36852; +2 NotContain assertions)
Impact: tools/list 26 tools (was 28, -2), payload 36852 bytes (ratchet locks the win). Verified live: get-sys-setting/list-sys-settings resident=false + contract-available=true in get-tool-contract, sit alongside the already-long-tail create-/update-sys-setting. Full McpServer unit suite green (2483 passed). For any FUTURE "move resident tool to long-tail": Session.CallToolAsync auto-routes any unadvertised tool (destructive or not) through clio-run correctly on its own — do NOT add a special CallDestructiveAsync/clio-run-destructive path unless the durable RAW/bare-name path specifically needs testing (McpDurableCallToolHandler is the only place that still gates destructive on residency).

## 2026-07-21 – ENG-93886: create-data-binding --help dispatch fix (Default=0 hypothesis DISCONFIRMED)
Context: `clio create-data-binding --help` returned 0 bytes (exit 0); the ticket's own suspected root cause was `Default = 0` on `CreateDataBindingOptions.InstallType`. An earlier code-analysis pass (this session's own understand-and-plan workflow) proposed removing that Default as the fix.
Decision: Did NOT touch `InstallType`/`Default=0` — empirically disconfirmed by building clio.dll and reproducing: `create-app` (`ApplicationCreateCommand.cs`, no int Option at all) is independently broken the same way (0 bytes), so a single attribute value cannot be the trigger. Instead widened `Program.TryHandleBuiltInHelp` (clio/Program.cs) with a new branch: `<verb> --help`/`-h` (verb not literally "help", not `--WEB`/`-W` mode) now short-circuits to `renderer.TryRenderCommandHelp(verb)` — the SAME call the working `clio help <verb>` branch already uses — BEFORE CommandLineSDK's own parser ever runs. Falls through unchanged when the renderer returns null (typo verbs, feature-toggled-off commands).
Discovery: (1) Confirmed root cause shape empirically, not just by inspection: a verb breaks `--help` when its name is a case-insensitive prefix of another registered verb's name AND it has no `[Verb] Aliases` AND its options class inherits `EnvironmentOptions` (create-data-binding vs. -db, create-app vs. -section both break this way; add-package/update-cli/upload-license have Aliases and work; register/remove-data-binding-row don't inherit EnvironmentOptions and work). Tested and DISCONFIRMED that adding an `Aliases` entry alone fixes it (tried on create-data-binding, temporarily, reverted) — even invoking through the newly-added alias itself still returned 0 bytes, so the trigger is deeper than the Aliases attribute; a closed-source CommandLineSDK dispatch fragility, not a single flag. (2) Proved the fix is low-risk via direct execution, not just review: `create-data-binding-db --help` and `help create-data-binding-db` were already byte-identical BEFORE this fix — meaning a working verb's `--help` already renders through this exact same `CommandHelpRenderer.TryRenderCommandHelp` path today (via `LocalHelpViewer.CheckHelp` inside CommandLineSDK's `CustomHelpViewer` hook, see `clio/WikiHelpViewer.cs`), so the new intercept changes nothing for already-working verbs (confirmed via before/after diff on create-data-binding-db, add-package, list-pages, a typo verb, and the feature-toggled-off `ring` verb — all byte-identical). (3) The fix repairs `create-app`/`create-app-section` as a free side effect (same mechanism) — deliberately left as an incidental PR callout, not a new acceptance criterion. (4) `--WEB`/`-W` mode is unaffected (explicitly excluded by the new branch, matching the exact pre-existing `args[1]` position check) — verified `_webBrowser.Enabled` is Windows-only (`WebBrowser.cs`) so this is safe to test on macOS without hitting real network/browser. (5) `CommonProgramTest.cs` (Module=Core) already has an established pattern for driving `Program.ExecuteCommands([verb, "--help"])` in-process (`add-item`, `ping`, `set-pkg-version` cases already existed) — a later code-analysis pass had wrongly claimed no such pattern existed; extended that file rather than inventing a new one. (6) Running the hidden `__generate-help-artifacts` exporter to regenerate `create-data-binding.md` also touched ~140 unrelated files (Commands.md 417 lines, WikiAnchors.txt 99 lines, help.txt 342 lines, several docs/commands/*.md deleted for now-feature-toggled/removed commands) — the committed docs have drifted badly from what the generator currently produces, unrelated to this ticket; reverted everything except the one clean `create-data-binding.md` diff and flagged the drift separately rather than bundling a ~140-file unrelated diff into a Minor help-text fix.
Files: clio/Program.cs, clio/help/en/create-data-binding.txt, clio/HelpSystem/CommandHelpCatalog.cs, clio/docs/commands/create-data-binding.md, clio.tests/CommonProgramTest.cs
Impact: `create-data-binding --help` (and `create-app`/`create-app-section --help` as a bonus) now render real help instead of silently succeeding with empty output; zero regressions (full `Category=Unit` suite: same pre-existing 19 failures before and after, all unrelated DbHub/Windows-profile/.NET-Framework environment tests, confirmed via git-stash A/B diff). Future agents: do not re-propose the `Default=0` narrow fix, and do not assume `docs/commands/*.md`/`Commands.md`/`WikiAnchors.txt` are in sync with the live exporter — check the actual diff size before committing a regeneration.

## 2026-07-21 (same day, follow-up) – ENG-93886: adversarial self-review caught a BLOCKER in the first fix version
Context: ran an independent self-review agent against the diff above before presenting it. It found that the first version's help-token scan (`normalizedArgs.Any(IsHelpFlagToken)`, matching bare `-h`/`--help` ANYWHERE in the args) hijacked any verb that already binds `-h` to its own option — `healthcheck -h <host>` (`[Option('h',"web-host")]`) and `publish-app -h <url>` (`[Option('h',"app-hub")]`, aliases ph/publish-hub/publish-workspace/publishw) silently showed help (exit 0) instead of running, with no error. Reproduced live before trusting the finding: `dotnet clio.dll healthcheck -h 192.168.1.1` printed the help screen instead of pinging the host.
Decision: replaced the blind token scan with `Program.IsUnclaimedHelpFlagToken(token, optionsType)` (now `internal static`, clio/Program.cs) - reflects over the TARGET VERB's own `[Option]` attributes (reusing the same `OptionAttribute.ShortName`/`LongName` shape `CommandHelpRenderer.BuildOptionDisplay` already reads) and only treats `-h`/`--help` as a help request when that verb has not already claimed the name for itself. `--help` (long form) collisions are not currently possible in this codebase (grepped: only 2 verbs anywhere claim short `-h`, none claim long `--help`), but the check covers both directions symmetrically rather than assuming long-form is permanently safe.
Discovery: my first regression test for this (driving `healthcheck`/`publish-app` through the full `Program.ExecuteCommands` pipeline and asserting on output/exit code or on a thrown "no environment registered" exception) passed in isolation but FAILED when the full `Category=Unit` suite ran end-to-end - some other test in the full run leaves a registered environment behind (shared static/on-disk settings state across fixtures), so "no environment registered" no longer held. `CommonProgramTest.cs` has zero precedent for driving a non-`--help` verb through `ExecuteCommands` (every existing case short-circuits on help before touching environment resolution), which is exactly why this gap wasn't already covered. Fix: test `IsUnclaimedHelpFlagToken` directly and hermetically (`typeof(PublishWorkspaceCommandOptions)` -> false, `typeof(CreateDataBindingOptions)` -> true) instead of through the full pipeline - no DI, no environment, no shared state, deterministic in isolation AND inside the full suite (re-verified: same pre-existing 19 failures, this test not among them).
Files: clio/Program.cs, clio.tests/CommonProgramTest.cs
Impact: the shared help-dispatch chokepoint (~207 verbs) no longer risks silently replacing a real command run with a help screen for any verb that owns its own `-h`/`--help`-named option, present or future. Lesson for future agents: when a fix widens a shared dispatch chokepoint, a token-presence scan over the whole args array is not enough - check whether the specific verb has already claimed that token for itself. And when a new test needs to prove "real execution happened" for a verb that requires environment/DI setup, prefer testing the underlying decision function directly over relying on incidental full-pipeline side effects (they can depend on cross-test global state and pass in isolation while flaking in the full suite).

## 2026-07-23 09:18 – Trace missing configuration web-service skill (#951)
Context: Issue #951 reported that generated workspace instructions require `$creatio-config-webservice`, but the JM_Test_Systems Codex skill catalog does not contain it.
Decision: Treat the report as a template contract drift rather than a missing personal skill; defer implementation until choosing between restoring the workspace skill and routing the instruction to the existing MCP guides.
Discovery: Commit e034be1d added the workspace-local skill on 2026-03-10. Commit 03ddd4a9 promoted its subject into the `configuration-webservice` and `configuration-webservice-tests` MCP guides on 2026-05-12. Commit 689f2f3d then deleted the three skill files while leaving the mandatory instruction in `clio/tpl/workspace/AGENTS.md`; its message mentioned only `.serena`, obscuring the removal. Current master still registers and tests both MCP guides, while ignored `tmp` and `bin` copies are stale build artifacts rather than shipped template sources.
Files: clio/tpl/workspace/AGENTS.md, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ConfigurationWebServiceGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/ConfigurationWebServiceTestsGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/GuidanceCatalog.cs
Impact: Newly generated workspaces can mandate a skill they do not receive. The guidance content still exists through `get-guidance`, so the likely repair is to align the shipped workspace instruction with that supported surface and add a drift test.

## 2026-07-23 09:22 – Locate the canonical web-service skill pair
Context: The implementation skill needed to be surfaced for review together with its unit-test guidance before deciding how issue #951 should restore or expose it.
Decision: Use the existing curated skill pair as the review source instead of reconstructing the older March clio template snapshot.
Discovery: `creatio-config-webservice` and `creatio-config-webservice-tests` remain complete under `C:\Projects\creatio-composableapp-skills\.curated`; their descriptions link to each other, and the test companion requires NUnit-style fixtures, explicit AAA sections, `[Description]`, FluentAssertions because reasons, status/payload assertions, and application-service substitutes injected through the package composition root.

Impact: The exact paired artifacts are available for review and can serve as the source if the workspace template is changed to ship skills again.

## 2026-07-23 09:23 – Confirm guide replacement for the removed skill
Context: Revalidate whether issue #951 is truly a missing artifact or only a stale name now that equivalent clio guidance may exist.

Impact: The complete repair is instruction and routing alignment plus a drift guard; duplicating the guidance as a restored local skill is unnecessary.

## 2026-07-23 09:43 – Harden issue 951 template guidance drift guard
Context: Mandatory pre-PR agentic review found that the initial template fix covered only the historical singular WebService directory and that the existing drift test did not parse `get-guidance name=...` references.
Decision: Cover both WebService and canonical WebServices directory conventions and extend the shipped-template drift test to resolve every explicitly named guidance article through `GuidanceCatalog`.
Discovery: The previous resident-or-bridged scanner intentionally tokenized only standalone backticked kebab-case names, so both new guide pointers were invisible to it. The focused fixture now contains seven passing tests and explicitly pins the implementation/test guide pair.
Files: clio/tpl/workspace/AGENTS.md, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/WorkspaceTemplateGuidanceDriftTests.cs
Impact: Workspace templates cannot silently regress to a renamed or removed configuration web-service guide, and both directory conventions trigger the live guidance.

## 2026-07-23 09:48 – Make guidance drift checks syntax- and toggle-aware
Context: Final re-review found that the first catalog guard recognized only contiguous `get-guidance name=...` syntax and ignored feature-gated visibility.
Decision: Extract every `name=` argument from any template line that invokes `get-guidance`, resolve it through the toggle-aware catalog with default-disabled feature state, and pin core-rules, routing, and both configuration web-service guides to the workspace template specifically.
Discovery: The shipped template already uses both compact and Markdown-separated invocation forms; line-oriented extraction handles both without treating unrelated `name=` arguments as guidance. The focused fixture remains green at seven tests.
Files: clio.tests/Command/McpServer/WorkspaceTemplateGuidanceDriftTests.cs
Impact: Renamed, removed, disabled, moved, or syntax-varied guidance references now fail the shipped-template contract instead of silently passing.

## 2026-07-23 10:09 – Restore security unblock (#957)
Context: Required PR checks began failing during NuGet restore after new high-severity System.Security.Cryptography.Xml advisories were published.
Decision: Move the existing direct System.Security.Cryptography.Xml pin outside the net8-only branch, update the centrally managed Microsoft family to 10.0.10, and align Microsoft.IdentityModel.Tokens with the existing SqlClient minimum of 8.19.2.
Discovery: The net10 target had only the vulnerable transitive cryptography package, while a fresh audited restore also exposed a pre-existing NU1605 downgrade from Microsoft.Data.SqlClient 7.0.2. On net10, the cryptography package is supplied by Microsoft.AspNetCore.App, so the installed shared runtime must also be current.
Files: Directory.Packages.props, clio/clio.csproj, clio/docs/commands/mcp-http.md, clio/help/en/mcp-http.txt
Impact: Audited restores succeed for both clio target frameworks; full unit tests, Ring tests, and Windows x64 NativeAOT publish pass.

## 2026-07-23 12:16 – Handle read-only Codex toolkit cache files (#962)
Context: `clio update-toolkit` failed before invoking Codex because legacy cache cleanup recursively deleted a read-only Git pack index.
Decision: Keep the repair scoped to `CodexAgent` and route only its installer-owned legacy directories through the existing read-only-safe deletion path.
Discovery: `System.IO.Abstractions.TestingHelpers` reproduces the Windows `UnauthorizedAccessException`, so a unit test can pin both ordinary directory cleanup and nested read-only Git pack cleanup without physical filesystem I/O.
Files: clio/Common/Skills/Agents/CodexAgent.cs, clio.tests/Common/Skills/SkillAgentTests.cs
Impact: Codex toolkit install/update can refresh caches containing read-only Git objects without changing cleanup behavior for other agents.

## 2026-07-23 12:31 – Bound Codex cache cleanup at reparse points (#962)
Context: Comprehensive pre-PR review found that the first repair reused a helper that follows directory links and can spin indefinitely after deletion.
Decision: Clear read-only attributes only within ordinary directories, treat file and directory reparse points as leaves, then use recursive directory deletion, which unlinks reparse points without traversing them.
Discovery: The legacy delete primitive already preserves the link boundary; only its read-only preparation was missing. A substitute-backed unit test pins that no file or directory enumeration occurs beneath a reparse-point child.
Files: clio/Common/Skills/Agents/CodexAgent.cs, clio.tests/Common/Skills/SkillAgentTests.cs
Impact: The update handles read-only Git objects without following cache links or inheriting the existing unbounded post-delete spin.

## 2026-07-23 – ENG-92940 crt.ImageInput tooltip literal wrongly rejected
Context: page-schema-resources rule forced $Resources.Strings tooltip on crt.ImageInput, but the control renders the raw literal and never reads localizableStrings, so the mandated form showed no tooltip at runtime (dead-end: literal blocked at validation, resource empty at runtime).
Decision: Made the localizable-text literal rule component/property-aware via a static `LiteralAllowedTextProperties` map (crt.ImageInput→tooltip) in SchemaValidationService. Registry carries NO machine-readable literal-vs-resource flag (only prose .component.md + input description text) and the validator is a sync static path with no registry access, so a data-driven derivation is not possible today — hard-coded map with TODO(ENG-92940) to generalize once the producer emits a structured signal.
Discovery: All three tools (validate-page/update-page/sync-pages) share `SchemaValidationService.ValidateLocalizableTextLiterals` → `ScanNodeForTextLiterals`; the mobile variant shares the same scan, so the exemption reaches mobile too (mobile registry also has crt.ImageInput — deliberate). Component `type` is a sibling of the text props in the `values` object; derived up-front before the property loop (EnumerateObject is document-order, so tooltip-before-type must still see the type).
Files: clio/Command/SchemaValidationService.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Resources/PageSchemaResourcesGuidanceResource.cs, clio/Command/McpServer/Tools/McpToolDescriptions.cs, clio.tests/Command/McpServer/SchemaValidationServiceTests.cs, clio.mcp.e2e/PageValidateToolE2ETests.cs
Impact: Future per-component literal-vs-resource exceptions extend one map. Anchor test SchemaValidationServiceTests.cs `_MultipleLiterals_` (crt.Input tooltip+placeholder → 2 errors) proves resource-backed tooltips stay rejected. get-component-info doc untouched (already correct).
